Comment: This review is about the Fire and Water show - The dragon bridge itself is beautiful, especially at night. Done my research and best view from google is the dragon ball bar which i will review separately. Went to the bar 2 hours earlier to get a good seat. Great view secured. 9 pm came with great anticipation and at 9:02 we paid our bill as “the show” was over and left. We chatted to others in the masses that were leaving and not one person said it was good, let alone impressive. The marketeers have done a great job with this and the show will definitely be remembered for what it wasn’t. My advice , Rock up with 2 minutes to go on the riverfront and don’t waste two hours to get a good spot and go do one of the many great things in Da nang.

Dragon Bridge 'FIRE SHOW' only operates on weekends. If you visit mid week you will only see the Bridge under lighting. Don't be disappointed if like us you fail to thoroughly research every aspect of your trip.
Don't waste time on the fire show as it's just some fire spitting show with no music or lightings to enhance the show and some water sprouting after the fire spitting.
Disappointing.  The Dragon Bridge is beautiful, but it never puts on a fire show.  We waited for a long time, but nothing happened.  Maybe because it was the night of the fireworks show.
Dragon just breathes couple of times fire and leaves more black smoke and then throws water and it done. No sound effect, nothing at all....not sure what's there to see?

We went with expectations not very high and still disappointed. The dragon as such is cool, but up there.The fire and the water that he simply throws, does not deserve to go half an hour early to take place. The changing of the dragon's lights makes them all night and is nothing otherworldly. We looked foolish to see it was just that.

Honestly if you’re short on time give this a miss. We were in the area so we’re there for 2 hrs prior to the show. But at this time people had already started gathering and getting into position. After waiting this long and the amount of people we were expecting something magical. Unfortunately it breathed fire 6 times in 6 minutes and two squirts of water and that was pretty much it. Due to it being a dragon, Fire and Water i believe this is quite important to other cultures that visit so i believe for them this experience was more something that they would rate highly.
There was no fire and water show at 9pm on weekends. And there was no information on the cancellation of show. Disappointing.

This particular sight seeing spot was a complete dampner on our trip to Vietnam. The bridge closes for traffic a few minutes before the show begins and is packed with people (including a night market right at the foot of the bridge). The fire the dragon spews becomes a bit monotonous and one can actually feel the soarching heat and smell of kerosene if your close to the dragon head. The chaos begins when the dragon begins to spew water with a solid force and actually drenches all the people standing close to the dragon's head. And in the process there is pandemonium and that's when the petty thieves are having a field day. One of my friends had her phone robbed within a couple of seconds after the water jets were out in full force. While lodging a complaint at the police station we also saw another European lady arrive who not only lost her phone, couple of million vietnamese dong and also her passport. The thief threw the passport away which some good samaritan had handed over to the cops. The passport was soaking wet and completely trampled on. The poor woman was weeping in relief when she saw her passport.It is a complete nightmare..... Please avoid it!
Very crowded. Just 3 times fire and water came out of Dragons mouth. It wasn't much of a spectacle..

I was looking forward to seeing the dragon breathe fire from my hotel room on the opposite bank. The riverside was boring, with nothing particularly interesting happening during the day or night. During the day, it was just an ordinary river and bridge. At night, the dragon's colors began to change, and tourist boats gathered near the bridge.

We stayed at the SenRiver Hotel which is situate at the 'tail end' of the Dragon. The Dragon only breathes fire twice on Saturday and Sunday nights at 9.00pm. We were happily chatting away when the first blaze took place. We hadn't realised that it was 9 o'clock ! By the time we had set our camera's the second breathe had already happened and we didn't get a single shot !!A couple of rockets launched just before the event would have alerted us to the time and prepared us. Two 5 second blasts of flame hardly warrant all the hype. Don't bother making a special effort to see it. It's not worth it
Just a bridge with a dragon shape on the top.  If you are interested, seeing it on the picture is good enough.
very short display of fire & water on the weekend. Not worth your time spending on either on land or on water

The night we went to watch the display, we were left wondering as nothing happened, big crowd for a non event,  don’t know why it was cancelled but it certainly didn’t happen despite us being there early and staying for around one hour after the advertised starting time.

During the daytime the bridge just looks like curved pipes, nothing of interest.  As long as you are not too close by in the evening, it looks semi cool.  Not worth the effort of a special trip to see.

It's neat, but I am glad that I saw the show while I was eating and hadn't gone out of my way to see it.  If you are planning on grabbing a drink on a local roof-top to watch the show, arrive early.  By 10 minutes before the show, all the seats around us were full.
It's just a flimsy, cardboard-like bridge, not worth going out of your way to see. Seeing it didn't impress me at all. A completely tacked-on tourist attraction.
It is a bridge I see several times while passing through the city center, and it seems to have become a landmark of this city. I
We was so underwhelmed by this bridge we didn’t even stay for the fire and water on Saturday night definitely not worth the visit
Waste of time. We were at the roof TOP of a draft beer joint since 6:30pm, expecting an amazing display of fire and water display. Total waste of time...

We were disappointed that we booked a ticket to Da Nang to see a fire breathing dragon but all we got to see was a fireworks show. Wasted over an hour waiting to see the dragon breathe fire only for it to not happen. Of course seeing the dragon change colors was pretty cool but the main attraction for us going to Da Nang was a disappointment.
Bummed that the fireworks seemed to replace the fire and water on the dragon bridge. Took a lot of effort to get there tonight but no display.

It doesn't really seem like a tourist attraction. What is the point of packaging a place you just pass by as a tourist spot? It is literally shaped like a dragon, so it is called the Dragon Bridge... It does look pretty from a distance at night, though.
Pointless.

This is a free attraction in Da Nang. On Saturdays, Sundays, and holidays, there's a free fire and water show featuring a dragon. From this bridge, you can see many other bridges in the city, and it's conveniently located for getting to local restaurants and eateries.
enjoy a kitch golden dragon bridge with heart shaped eyes - there s nothing to do in this town apart from the marble mounts and beach.
Um... what can I say? Waiting 5 minutes for a giant fire-breathing, color-changing dragon
Do not waste your time.3 - 5 fireballs with black smoke polluting the city followed by sprinkle of water.The worst tourist destination ever. Fore show was over in 5 min, which was good.
The night view from the hotel overlooking the Dragon Bridge

After reading numerous reviews, we arrived two hours before this "spectacle." Hoping for an amazing show, we took uncomfortable metal tables on the observation deck of a cafe on the embankment. The drinks and desserts were terrible. Everything was tasteless and expensive. The worst part was the show itself. Two blasts of fire and three jets of water from a dragon's mouth. It was comparable to the wagons under the river in Shanghai. Everything was so beautifully described, but the experience was zero. The reviews are surprising. If there's nothing to compare it to, why write that it was magnificent? The biggest waste of time in our travels. We would have been better off just strolling the streets of this beautiful city.

Be very careful around this area during the fire show on Saturday and Sunday nights.  As my husband had his wallet stolen by a pickpocketer. The next day we were contacted via Facebook to meet someone who supposedly found the wallet. Then they wanted money before returning the wallet. Scam!

Please, everyone, do not go to Da Nang on a solo trip. You will get trampled to death by group tourists... The cost of living is also really high. Korea is just better. The Dragon Bridge is really tacky. The people are rough, and it seems like all they think about is ripping off foreigners...
I saw this bridge while passing by in a taxi. I heard it
I watched it from the Novotel rooftop.
Not really an attraction.  It’s a bridge with a dragon on it that shoots fire at 21:00 on a Saturday evening.  Pfff
It is truly spectacular to see the dragon breathing fire every Saturday and Sunday at 9pm. It is a very impressive sight to see.
It is just a bridge railing.
The bridge itself is relatively unspectacular. It is illuminated in different colors at night, and on weekends the dragon breathes fire at 9 pm.
An ordinary bridge plus the dragon's decorative
I waited at 9 to watch the fire show, but it looked
It is good if you watch it without high expectations. There is nothing particularly special about it; it is even insufficient to call it a fireworks display. You can see it if you pass by downtown.

I heard there are special effects like fire-breathing, but I didn't even go near it. I'm not interested in manufactured tourist attractions. The bridge was beautiful, with its colors changing every few minutes, and there were lots of locals on dates there.

I went because I heard it was worth seeing, but the show only lasted about 10 minutes. I was really disappointed, and finding a taxi on the way back was a real struggle. I think seeing it once is enough.

We traveled through Vietnam, among other places, with our two kids, aged 4 and 1. Our oldest son had been looking forward to the fire-breathing dragon bridge. Unfortunately, this dragon only does that on weekends (when we were obviously not there). Sadly, this was not mentioned in the Capitol, among other places, which we had with us as a travel guide.

Why this is an attraction is beyond me. Da Nang's lack of multiple places of interest has made the mediocre, at best, something to see or promote. It's a bridge that changes colors and is decorated to look like a dragon. Nothing about its structure or surroundings is unique or worth a boat tour.
'Fire show' - on every weekend. Recommended by hotel staff. Waited for 1.5 hrs to get a good seat at the coffee place. Bt was a real disappointment. No show. More of spit of fire. Wont recommend

On June 4th, 2017, we took a taxi to arrive near the bridge a little before 9:00 AM, but we were swept up in a wave of local motorbikes and couldn't get close. Even when we finally got out of the taxi, it was incredibly crowded. At 9:07 AM, water finally gushed out of the dragon's mouth, but it only spat water three times with awkward intervals in between. Huh? Isn't it supposed to breathe fire? Is that all? What is this? We shouldn't have come.

It's a cool looking bridge, but don't wait around for the fire and water show like we did. It literally sprays a little water and a little fire,  flame, spray, flame, spray, flame, flame ... flame .... spray, and for the finally a flame and a long spray.
In the shape of a dragon. Would you travel at all to go and see it ? Perhaps with children under 5.No kids ? Don't bother.

Traffic jam, accidents and definately not worth to see! We are talking about the "event" performed 9 PM Saturdays and Sundays. The "fire spitting dragon" did two small blows and then some steam. In the traffic afterwards we experienced a serious accident (motorbike vs car). We hope and believe that the man flying trough the air is alive today....
This fire-breathing show was so cheap! The special effects were like a couple of puffs and then the fire went out. A few puffs, then a long wait, and then they started spraying water…

As the title says, it isn't worth the trip if you are not staying around that area. The 'dragon' on the bridge breathes out fire and water for a grand total of 15minutes or so and so does the show ends. It would be better off walking around the city instead.
The bridge is like everything else... flashy: what a shame! The city is gradually losing its authenticity and it's a real disaster.

The dragon spits fire and water on Saturdays and Sumdays at 21:00. It's not worth waiting for. Maybe for small kids. We thought it would spit a larger amount of fire, it was just not very interesting...
The high life in Danang is Dragon bridge in night life ,if you not visit here that mean you still never came to Danang

Luckily we arrived on a Sunday night so we hotfooted it down to watch the dragon 'roar' at 9 pm.  It is terrific to watch. ..from one of the nearby bars. ..Fatfish bar was stylish.  This is an iconic bridge. ..a must see!

I visited Danang city as it was my first time here. Hotel recommended me the waterfront near the Dragon bridge.This areas has some coffee shops and restaurant targeting tourists, with obvious higher charges.Good panorama on the bridge but nothing much in this area. Cham museum is nearby for those who are interested.
I went to see a fire show. What I saw was a lighter. Disappointed. It's only for 3-5 year olds. There were tons of people. I don't get what the point is.
It's just an ordinary bridge with a dragon lantern decoration. You can take a picture if you're passing by, but if it's not on your way, you don't have to come. Google Photos are plenty of options.
not worth to the city tourists are very few taxi drivers chaos detour ended higher price waiters bad unfriendly city for tourists. Don't waste your time.
I went there with high expectations, but... how should I put it? It was just ridiculous. That’s about it. They just shot gas a few times, sprayed some water, and that was it. There was nothing there.
Too crowded too crowded too crowded!!! Full of local peoples, and traffic jam everywhere. Nothing special for the fire show. Very disappointed!!!
Just take a look if you pass by at night; there's no need to go out of your way to come here. Fire comes out of its mouth at 9 PM on Sundays, but it's really childish lol.

It's over in an instant. The content is exactly what you see on TV, nothing more, nothing less. Fire breathing, water spitting...it was so crowded it was overwhelming. Especially catching a taxi home was a real struggle.

Beautiful bridge ... a masterpiece of urban but eruption is environmental pollution (8-9pm night). So far from the sea to relax and choose. Nearby restaurants quite expensive. Han river brigde was the better choice!
The viewpoint looks very beautiful from afar... if you're passing through the city at night, it's worth going to see that one and others from the promenade.
Make sure to view the Da Nang Dragon Bridge from a distance.
I heard the night view is even better, but the Dragon Bridge in the daylight was nice too.

People kept talking about the Dragon Bridge, so I think I went back and forth more than 12 times during my 3-night, 4-day stay. There wasn't anything special during the day, just a bit like, "Oh, so this is the Dragon Bridge," haha. If you go to Da Nang, you're bound to cross it automatically anyway. If
It's just so-so lol. There are quite a few people during the fire show though. It's worth watching since you came all this way. You can just keep it in mind as a lighthearted place lol.
A bridge with super big long dragon snake. Pollution and noise...Good to take pictures. Particular style
Best seen at night, fun on occasion... I didn't get to see the dragon in action, just the color changes...

The dragon's design is beautiful, and it's quite spectacular to see it spanning the river. I heard it's even more beautiful at night, but unfortunately, due to time constraints, I only saw the dragon bridge during the day, which was also very beautiful. I'd like to see it again at night if I have the chance.

It’s a wonderful architectural piece to see in central Danang; showing creativity in what could have been a boring bridge. The ‘show’ at 9pm is worth seeing, although not quite the spectacle we were expecting.
A pleasant e ending of lighted boats and a 15 minute show of the dragon breathing fire and spewing water.

It's a major thoroughfare, so anyone going there will definitely pass by. It's quite beautiful at night with the lights on, and the dragon head breathes fire on Sunday nights. But I don't think it's a place worth making a special trip for.

On weekends, the dragon breathes fire and water from its mouth. It gets quite crowded and is very impressive. The amount of water is considerable, so if you get too close, you'll get soaked. The fire and water are spewed from the dragon's mouth on the beach side.

We visit the bridge at night after we have our dinner at a halal restaurant in town. We visited the city on weekend as well. We walked from the restaurant nearby our hotel to the Bridge/ Park and  it takes about 20 minutes . The bridge I think is more beautiful at night comparing at day time. There are some free dance culture show from the Da Nang community at the park. We spend there about an hour only just for the view.
Just a "dragon" on the bridge. Nothing spectacular. Best for phototaking in the evening, and a leisure stroll around the river.

It is a bridge that you cannot help but cross frequently, both during the day and at night. It was the first bridge I saw upon arriving in Da Nang, and it looks even more magnificent if you visit at night.

Vietnamese people do not speak English well. However, since they know what the Dragon Bridge is, you should keep it in mind as an important point when asking for directions or taking a taxi. There are also many Korean shops at both ends of the bridge, so it seems possible to ask for help in case of an emergency.
The bridge is extremely crowded.
I don't think you necessarily need to go out of your way to visit just for this night view.
It's not a sight to behold. Every trip to the airport or downtown offers a view of this bridge.
It’s a bridge in the shape of a dragon could even be a snake apart from the head and tail. Not too impressive in the day though.
There are dynamically moving dragon-shaped structures across the entire bridge crossing the Han River.
Visit during the night and you can see the dragon spitting out fire and water. Don't stay too close when it spitting water or you get wet.
It's a failure... I think you need to see it at night.

Basically you will pass by everyday but don’t notice that it’s of the shape of a dragon.Come here on Saturday night (9pm) for the fire show. Not so special but that’s nothing much night activities here so you may still go.
It was nice to see, but not special It is better at night with the lights. If you are in town and to swing by is ok , but I would not make a trip just to see this from Hoi An.

Worth a visit but very crowded.   Starts at 9pm and only goes for a short time.  Make sure you have an exit strategy with a taxi as it is difficult to get out.  Yes still worth going but don’t get your hopes up for a spectacular.  Just a bit of fun.

There are no bridges in Japan with fire-breathing sculptures. However, I don't think they're always breathing fire, and it's not something that's done at a specific time. I think it's more of a lucky coincidence if you happen to see it.
I liked it, but it's just a bridge, don't get your hopes up.
Our driver just show us this by driving pass this bridge in day time but he also recommend would be more nice in night time.
The bridge I encountered in Da Nang upon my arrival was splendor itself.
It looks good in photos. You'll probably see it while passing by, so it's fine not to go out of your way to look for it. It's not that great during the day, but you should see it at night.

I was quite excited to see the Dragon bridge breath fire and water -- which it does every weekend. When it finally happened it was a bit underwhelming, and didn't live up to the hype I created in my head. In terms of drivability, the bridge is ok, I prefer the bridge to the south, its bigger and more colorful. Way better than Thuan Phuc (aka suicide bridge) though. As they say, size isn't everything.
I heard they were going to breathe fire and water at 9 PM on Sunday, so I had dinner nearby and then went to check it out. It was incredibly crowded.
It’s a pretty nice bridge but it is just a bridge. Wouldn’t say it’s worth traveling to see but worth a slight detour if your in the area.
On my way back from shopping by taxi, I took some photos at the foot of the bridge and then walked across it.

It's appealing because of the water and fire effects. The water is nice, but the fire is a bit short, too quick. It's fine if you stand far away, but if many people get too close, they can't run fast enough when the water is sprayed.

The Dragon Bridge is usually docile, but on Saturdays and Sundays around 9am, it goes berserk and breathes fire from its mouth. If you have the chance, please go and see it sometime. I watched it from a distance.

When visiting Da Nang, you absolutely must see the fire and water fountain show, especially on Saturday and Sunday evenings. It's recommended to have dinner first, then take a bus to the riverbank to watch; there are vendors along the roadside where you can sit and wait.

The bridge connects old town and beach town and is designed as a dragon. During night it has many different lights which we enjoyed from our balcony. It is a pity that the owner did not payed the construction fully due to any reason.
The bridge lights up at night with alternating colors. Around 9 p.m., the dragon "breathes" fire and water. The spectacle isn't as interesting as it seems.
The dragon's fire show was over in about 5 minutes, and after that it started spitting water. It was over in a flash. I think it's best to watch it from as close up as possible.
It is quite funny to see the bridge. I wouldn't go specifically looking for it, but you will probably pass by or see it in the distance.
I saw the Dragon Bridge from the car window. The

It's so flashy that it's a great landmark even when you're driving by in a taxi! It was beautifully lit up at night. There's also a taxi stand near this bridge. It felt safe to take a taxi from there.
Quite nice idea to use the pillars to create a dragon  When you are here check it out but don’t expect muchIn the night nice light showJust have a look

The bridge is truly impressive, especially on Saturday and Sunday evenings when the fire show takes place; it's supposed to be a fascinating spectacle. Otherwise, it's interesting at night for a short time-lapse sequence, ideally with color changes. It's also worthwhile to admire the structure up close during a walk across the bridge, also at night.

I heard that the dragon was supposed to breathe fire at 9pm on Sunday night, but that the bridge would be extremely congested and it would be a real pain to get back, so I went to see it around 7pm. It was beautifully lit up in green. There were only a few people there at that time.
At the hotel's suggestion, we arrived well in advance on the rooftop of a riverside café to enjoy Da Nang's daily 9:00 PM "fireworks" event.
During the day, it is just a bridge decorated with dragons, but
Unfortunately, it was rather unimpressive as there were only lights on it. No display as such... We walked 40 mins to get to it, don't think it was worthwhile.

I went there after hearing about the fire and water shows at 9 PM on weekend nights. I timed my visit around 8 to 8:30 PM and found Time Coffee, a shop known for offering a good view of the bridge, and watched from the rooftop. I burst out laughing at the fire show, which was on the level of a flamethrower, and laughed again at the water show, which was more like a fire truck's water cannon compared to the size of the giant dragon. Please move carefully to avoid being bumped by the swarming crowds. The night view seen while walking across the bridge is also beautiful.
It was Saturday, and I knew I had to go see the dragon show.

We walked across. It's worth seeing on Saturdays and Sundays because it breathes fire. The next day, we walked across again. We were practically the only ones walking across. The dragon's eyes are heart-shaped.
During the day, you might think, "Ah, that’s the Dragon Head."
Da Nang has beautiful bridges and Dragon bridge is a beautiful sight anytime of the day. However, the water and fire performance (on weekends) is a let down for me. Be sure to find a good spot.

We thought we gonna witness something spectacular, but ended up disappointed as the so called fire & water show was so lame! If you really have nothing to do and you got plenty of free time or you just happened to be around the area, then no harm going to take a look. I don't advise to buy the boat ticket and see the show, is really not worth it!
Every weekend at 9 PM for about 15 minutes, there's a show where the performer breathes fire and water from his mouth.
It was right there in the middle of the city. It was much bigger than I had imagined. Its yellow color made it stand out quite a bit.
It's a large, beautiful statue with a golden dragon coiled around it, which lights up in changing colors at night. You can't miss it.
We booked a boat ride to view the dragon bridge, but a bit disappointed with the boat, anywhere view is nice, not bad.
It is simply a bridge, in the middle of the road. There is no photo-spot, or area for taking photos whatsoever.

The view of the river from the bridge in the evening is quite nice. There's a performance on Saturday and Sunday nights, but it's really just a dragon head spewing fire and water. There are a lot of people watching, so don't have too high expectations. However, if you want to get soaked by the dragon bridge, be sure to stand in front of the dragon head. Seeing hundreds of people completely drenched yet still smiling and greeting others is probably for good luck.

The bridges over the Han River in Korea are much prettier haha. Still, it is a bridge you are bound to see at least once if you come to Da Nang. When the weather is nice, it looks really pretty because the sky is beautiful, but when it is cloudy, there really isn't much to it.
Went there at afternoon and night, especially to see the night fire show but it was just so so. You don’t need to go there if you’re not a bridge lover
A beautiful structure that truly comes alive at night, especially with its lights.
Actually the view is good. But the 9pm attraction is too overrated. We came at 9pm to see the fire. Unfirtunately, the fire is only takes 2 minutes.

You don't necessarily have to go out of your way to visit; you might spot it if you happen to pass by. It is definitely better to view at night than during the day due to the lighting, and it is also nice to take a break at a nearby cafe while admiring it.

It is unique bridge, check it out at night time when lights are lit up.  My kids and I were underwhelmed by the weekend nightly show.   You should go and check it out to see what the show is all about.
The view is nice ...the view is better from the boat  the bridge is in the shape of dragon which blow fire and water in interval of time .

You need to see it from afar to enjoy. For that reason it’s hard to rate it so highly although it is a bit of a novelty.I’d drive past it and look but unless you’ve got the best wide angle lens it will be difficult to capture

While hanging out at Gold Pine Pub, I noticed the night view was pretty. It looked like the Han River, and at midnight, the middle of the bridge rotates 180 degrees. It is beautiful, but the river water isn't very clean.
I had a hard time coordinating my schedule so I could watch the Dragon Bridge fire and water shows and then stop by Lotte Mart.
Stopped whilst on a Vietnam tour. Bridge was okay but nothing special. Wouldn’t go out of my way to see it.

We came near the dragon bridge on our second trip to the Dragon Bridge to catch the water show and fire breathing on the Saturday we stayed in Da Nang, at correctly 9:00 PM, our family was excited for the show, but then we soon figured out that they only breath fire now and don't do water shows anymore, as we watch and counted, the dragon only breath 3 fire breaths and no more, I was terribly disappointed since I was expecting a great show like my last visit here.
The Dragon Bridge was much bigger than I expected 😁 lol I was thinking of walking across it to get dinner, exchange money, and buy souvenirs, but I changed my mind. I
I feel so proud as this is my first overseas trip with my son and daughter.

It isn't quite as impressive as the night view of the Han River in Seoul, but there are people fishing, so you don't really need to schedule a visit; you'll see everything as you pass by anyway. The fire show isn't anything special.
It isn't particularly spectacular. Since the city center is small, you'll see this bridge time and time again as you wander around. I don't think it's worth making a special trip to visit.
The bridge is one of the famous sights in Danang but we didn’t find it that impressive.. ideally go for a drink to one of the rooftop bars to catch a good view.

At night, it's beautifully lit up, and the dragon is said to breathe fire, making it a popular tourist spot. Also, the night view from the 20th floor of the hotel was beautiful, with the three bridges and gondolas lit up. I also walked across this bridge during the day to get to the old town, but it was quite long and tiring.

There's an event every Saturday night starting at 9pm, and the illuminations are beautiful, but the roads get extremely congested. The police have to direct traffic, so be prepared if you're planning to visit. However, the event at the Dragon Bridge, a symbol of Da Nang, is definitely worth seeing.
I don't think it's a place worth taking 7, 5, and 3-year-olds in hot weather just to watch the fire-breathing show.

This 666-meter-long bridge connects the city center to the beach, and the beach side is shaped like a dragon's head. At night, it's lit up and becomes a landmark. I didn't get to see it because I was there on a weekday, but apparently there's a show on weekends where fire and water are spewed from the dragon's mouth.
I went to see the fire that shoots out of the dragon's face on the bridge every Sunday night at 9 PM. I
I don't think it's really worth the time to visit. It looks good from afar, and there's no difference up close. Besides, you pass by it on your way to Hoi An anyway.

Went to the bridge hoping to catch the fire at 9pm but it didnt come on as scheduled. Probably due to the fireworks competition on that night itself. But its still a sight to behold esp with the lighting on it

Dragon bridge is a good place for photography. I went during a special festival and there were fireworks show. The river front is a good place to take a jog or stroll. The only downside was that during the show, It was a bit crowded and my friend's phone was pick-pocketed from her backpack. So i'd advice tourist to be very careful with their belongings, if you're carrying a backpack, carry it in front of you. We didn't stay for the water show at the dragon bridge later on since the pick-pocket incident dampen all our mood.
It's not particularly beautiful during the day, but it lights up at night and becomes quite pretty. There are events on weekends, so I recommend visiting.
I'd only driven past it a few times in a taxi during the day, so it was like, "So this is it!"

A beautiful bridge with unique lighting at night. We went out for an evening promenade and walked there. I wouldn't make a special trip from far away, but it's perfectly fine as a short evening stroll.
Best angle for photoshop especially at waterfront promenande. Fire and water spewing out of dragon during Saturday and Sunday 9pm.
It is interesting for a bridge to be camouflaged to look like a dragon.  However, it is just a bridge.

Was nice to walk around and see the crowds but its a quick little fire breathing and water spraying. Very crowded on the actual bridge so if you wanted to stand right underneath it you should go earlier then 9
I wanted to see the fire and water spouting, but it seemed to be over by the time I got flustered trying to cross the road...what a shame.

9pm on Sat and Sun there is a short fire and water show. 3 bursts of fire then water. Do stay clear of the head during the water show if you want to depart dry. Check out the direction of the wind first. Traffic is stopped for 15 mins.

May 1st, 2018 (Tuesday) was Labor Day, a public holiday in Vietnam, so even though it wasn't a weekend, the dragon on the Dragon Bridge spewed fire and water. We decided to take a sightseeing boat on the Han River to enjoy the view at a leisurely pace. It's nice to see it from a different angle than from the bridge itself.
You don't need to make time to go there; just check out the night view if you happen to be in the area.

Our accommodation was near the "tail" of the bridge and thus we always travel along this bridge.  We paid another visit on a Sunday night for the show.It was basically just fire and water coming out from the dragon's mouth, nothing very spectacular, and involved quite some waiting during the show from one fire to another/ fire to water, etc.Still, worth a visit not only for the 10-minutes show, but to enjoy the local vibes and scenery along the river.PS. it was hilarious watching people who originally stood on the bridge nearest to dragon mouth started running away at the water show as (i guess) they got wet. (Evil) Beware not to stand too close!
Will through the bridge around the city but we haven’t seen the special performance because only for SAT/SUN.
It looked a bit shabby during the day, but it looked impressive when the lights were on at night.

It's unique and beautiful at night as it changes color and lights up. The show wasn't much of a show, just fire and water shooting out of the dragon's mouth a couple of times. It looked like you'd get soaked on the bridge.
It's just okay, haha.
This bridge is for bikes and motor vehicles crossing. Nothing special about the bridge other then it glows at night time shaping like a dragon.

On Sunday evening, I watched the fire-breathing show from the riverbank near the tail end. It was quite a distance away, but the fire-breathing was short and infrequent, so it ended rather quickly. The bridge's lights changed color from time to time. I regret not watching it from the front end.

From afar, the supporting structures are made so to resemble a dragon.This is emphasized at nite time when it is high lifted by lighting.To me, seen it once, that's it... it doesn't do anything to evoke excitement/ emotion etc.When you travel over it. It's just a bridge structure!!!But, others may think different... rate... 4/10.. it's bridge!!
During the day, it was a yellow bridge, but at night it was beautifully lit up.

Went to the dragon bridge in the day, didn't quite feel the hype that everyone was talking about. yes, it is a bridge with a dragon shaped structure on the sides of it.... Didn't stay till night for the fire showcase, hopped over to hoi an instead. but it was nothing fantastic in my opinion.
It seems to be a popular spot, but I didn't feel it was worth making a trip to just for this place. It's something you might visit if you have the time.

This bridge has a suspension in the shape of a dragon; every Saturday evening around 9:00 PM, the dragon breathes fire, smoke, and water. Furthermore, the dragon changes color every evening via lighting, like many bridges in Da Nang. A nice sight, but not really special.

We watched the dragon bridge light up on a Sunday night. They were lots of people there but it was still easy to find a space to watch. If you do go don’t get too close to the bridge unless you want to get soaking wet - it sprays a heap of water and everyone on that side of the bridge gets wet.  Worth going to see if you’re in the area but not as exciting as you’d imagine.

At first, I thought the bridge was only for vehicles and could only be viewed from a distance. Later, I discovered that there were pedestrian walkways on both sides of the bridge, so you could walk directly on it, which I found amazing.
Lovely architecture, friendly locals and good mead. Bit of a dodgy place to go wandering at night though, We were sure some geezer has been following us around...
The bridge opens twice a week on weekends at 9:00 PM. By 8:00 PM, the entire area is packed with people. Everyone's dressed up, taking photos, and waiting.

You don't need to go out of your way to see this, its evident from a lot of places, and right outside the Cham museum. Makes you chuckle, and the dragon rising story is well embed in Vietnamese culture. Driving past is sufficient

This would have been an ordinary steel truss bridge. By putting on a layer of gold-yellow paint and added a dragon head and tail on each end.. what a piece of art design! It is hard to take a picture with the whole bridge if you want to be in it. I only saw it during the day time, I bet it would look even prettier when there are lights at night.

One of the most famous bridge among all the 11 bridge that connect the outland and inland of Danang city. Try arrange to visit it on weekend as they dragon will breathe fire over every weekend night 9pm.

Dragon Bridge is the most unique bridge of Da Nang with its design. The bridge that connects two sides of Hong Kong has a dragon design and it is changing the colours in the evening, creating an entertaining environment. So this is one of the bridges that watching from far distance is better than walking on it.
We walked to the Dragon Bridge after dinner and it was Sunday and there were a lot of people near the Dragon Bridge. The bridge was just a bridge to me but the ambiance was nice nearby.

It is just a bridge. Look at the lights on while taking a Han River cruise at night. If you view it with the leisure of a traveler, it is indeed pretty. I haven't spent a weekend there, so I don't know if they have fireworks.

I was expecting a lot more from this bridge. Although the idea behind it was great, it lacked the wow factor for me. The lights were pretty and looked good against the water but it was ultimately just a dragon head and parts of dragon back in the middle of the bridge. I wouldn’t go raving about this that’s for sure.

During the day this bridge seems to just "pop up". But of a night the lights and contours are magnificent. If one lived in the area and had this view from the window or terrace it would be magnificent.

It's a bridge with a rather interesting design, even from a distance. The idea of ​​it breathing fire is something unthinkable in Japan, but it's full of playful spirit and quite amusing. I went by bicycle, but the uphill climb was surprisingly tough, and I was constantly being chased by motorcycles, which was quite frustrating. You can really appreciate its size when you see it up close.

Completed in 2013, this is a relatively new symbol of tourism in Da Nang. It is one of the bridges connecting the city center and the beach area. As its name suggests, it is designed with a dragon and is lit up at night. The dragon's eyes are heart-shaped.
You won't need to try very hard to see the Dragon bridge... If you are in Da Nang you can't miss it. It's a 666 meter long steel bridge (woohoooo!) and it looks amazing by night.

Was given such a hype about this tourist attraction that is held 9pm Sat/Sun to which hundreds of people turn up. The bridge itself is lovely and looks like a dragon which changes colors - the surroundings are lovely also but all that happens is it breaths fire 2-4 times with really long pauses between them then smoke is exhaled again with long pauses - all up max 5-7 minutes - it’s ok don’t get too excited.
From a distance, the dragon on the bridge looked fascinating, and

it's actually the main road in danang town.  wont miss it. i stayed at hotel nearby and passes by  on my car ride to lady buddha, my khe beach etc.The attraction is the fire n water show. i get to catch ot on Sunday night.. at the river on the other side of the dragon head tho.. just a after dinner walk near my hotel along the river. the dragon lights up at night so the view is ok from far.
The feeling is different seeing it during the day compared to in the evening.
It's a pretty bridge!

I went to see it because they said it would be spitting fire and water over the weekend, but it wasn't the best view from the bridge. It seems like it would be better from a little further away along the river below.

It seems to be a famous tourist spot, but it has a comical and kitschy charm. It has a Gundam theme park feel to it, so if you have small children, they might enjoy the fire show on weekend evenings. It

Interesting architecture which really shows off at night when it's constantly changing colour.If you happen to be around for the fire and water show (on Friday and Saturday nights), I would recommend it, but not really worth making a special trip and waiting around specifically at those times or days just to see it.

Good to see the shape of the dragon in the day and is lit up at night with boats also lit up underneath a good view at 9pm sat and sun the dragon has flames and water coming out of his mouth do not stand on the road in front of the mouth you will get wet this does not last for long and probably not worth the journey to see it.
It lights up in the evening.
I've only ever seen it from the bus, but I think anyone who comes to Da Nang will see it at least once. 27 years ago it was a rusty, dirty bridge, but now it's beautifully lit up.

A must-go when you visit Da Nang, but it is just a bridge with nothing special. There are a few bridges in Da Nang too. This Dragon Bridge is only one of them. It is a tourist spot but nothing special after few minutes looking at it. In a morning, we witnessed many people stopped on traffic and looked on the edge. Turned out someone jumped over in an attempt to kill himself. Apparently this is getting popular due to the economics. Nevertheless, a must-go for pictures.

An ordinary bridge, but there is a dragon-shaped sculpture in the center of the bridge, the length is equal to the length of the entire bridge, separating the opposite reverse lane, in and out of the city center will pass this bridge, do not have to deliberately walk to.

They put dragon-shaped decorations on the bridge. It’s just a bridge, after all. I didn't see the fire show at the dragon's head (at the eastern end of the bridge) that they say happens occasionally, but it does look pretty convincing when the lights are turned on at night.
The Dragon Bridge erupts at 9pm every Saturday and Sunday for about ten minutes, and the color of the entire dragon body changes accordingly.

This is a view of the Dragon Bridge from the tail end during the daytime. Apparently, there's a show where it breathes fire and sprays water from its mouth only on Saturday and Sunday nights at 9 PM. It's beautifully lit up at night.

I used to pass by the Dragon Bridge often while traveling by taxi. I tried to go see it at 9 PM on Saturday night, but there were too many people, so I ended up watching it from a hotel rooftop bar. Passed Dragon bridge on the way to Hue. During the day it is just nicely decorated bridge with golden dragon 🐉. When gets darker it is more interesting to be lit and spitting fire 🔥
The bridge looks very nice and the fire and water show at 9 pm is quite nice but not really spectacular.

There's a large metal dragon in the middle of a big bridge leading from the city center to the beach. I crossed it to take a look. In the evening, the dragon lights up and changes between green, yellow, blue, and white, which is beautiful. You can also enjoy the changing lights of other bridges from the bridge. It's about a 15-minute leisurely walk. For the fire-breathing event on weekends, I recommend watching it from the top of the dragon's head, but don't get your hopes up too high or you'll be disappointed.
It's cool to see at night, but I think it's a shame the fire show is only on Saturday and Sunday, this provides limited opportunity for tourists to actually see it.

Obviously the best bridge in Da Nang, and seeing it as a quirky piece of the cityscape is always good. Not much to it if you don't visit when its erupting fire and water. Its still there to see if you are in Da Nang for a few days, so you may as well see it.
Da Nang has many bridges, the Dragon bridge is one of many. It is nicely decorated and at night has nice lights. But I would not say it’s worth while to visit.
It is worth visiting at least once.
I had heard that the bridge was beautifully lit up at night, but it was raining heavily that day, and I only drove across it during the day. It
I drove past it during the day and saw the night view...

We were lucky to visit at the right time on the weekend to witness the fire and water breathing dragon bridge. It was not as spectacular as we expected it to be. There was a huge crowd gathered and the atmosphere was pretty nice. If you are close by at the right time, it’s something you can enjoy. But I don’t think it’s a stop worth making time for unless you happen to be nearby.

The dragon Bridge is really a night time activity. It has cascading and changing lights it is nice to look at but I certainly would not make a trip just to see this on it own. There are lots of spots along the river for meals with a view of the bridge.
It is especially beautiful to see in the evening. It changes color and spits fire (we missed that).

the dragon tail is on the left bank of river while dragon head is on the righ bank.the dragon.head side dont much to see very limites cafe or restaurant while most nice cafe hotel are on the left side.
During the day the yellow dragon is out, but up close is not that interesting, at night when lit is when it and Danang come to life.

Our first thought when we got to the Dragon Bridge was that it didn't really look much like a dragon. Later, we realized we were on the tail end (on the Cham Museum side). The head end, which we drove past later at night, if much more dragon-head-like.Unfortunately, we couldn't be in Da Nang on a Saturday or Sunday night when the dragon head spews fire. We would have liked to see that. Otherwise, it's kind of a novelty bridge - fun to look at, but no biggie.

The Dragon Bridge in Da Nang, Vietnam is nice to see, although during the day it is not as nice as at night. I would suggest an early evening view as the light is fading and you can see the Dragon Bridge lights and the slowly darkening sky.

I heard the fire show is nothing special—they just shoot fire a few times and that's it—so I wasn't really planning on watching it. And then nothing came out for a long time... Don't go with high expectations; it's just the kind of show you watch while having a beer lol.

The Dragon Bridge, a famous landmark on the Han River in downtown Da Nang... Since it was right next to the Parish Hotel where I stayed on my last day, I took a walk after dinner and took a commemorative photo. It was great that famous restaurants, Han Market, Cong Cafe, and the Cathedral were all clustered near the Dragon Bridge, allowing me to explore them all at once.
It seems you really need to go during the time when the sun is blazing.
The glittering dragon is easily recognizable even from a distance.

A remake. That says it all. It's only worth seeing as an added bonus to Hoi An and the Marble Mountains. Near the bridge, there's an underground passage with graffiti, also a local landmark—and even more interesting.
A beautiful bridge that changes color at night: it's a shame I couldn't be there for the sessions when it was spitting fire :)
I saw the Pink Cathedral on my way back from my accommodation near My Khe Beach.

I had always wanted to see it since it's a tourist attraction, but I passed by it and saw it many times on my way from my hotel along Non Nuoc Beach to downtown Da Nang, so I didn't make a point of going there. I
The dragon bridge changes color

Besides the daily traffic, many people come to the Dragon Bridge at night to watch the dragon head spew fire and water. However, it's not as good as you might imagine. When it's time for the fire to start, the bridge is closed off, and cars are not allowed to enter. So you have to ride your bike here early to secure a spot. Then, many people start selling things on the bridge, and motorcycles fill both sides of the bridge, making it very crowded.

The driver said there would be a fire-breathing event every weekend at 9 pm, but the bridge connects the beach and the city, and I can usually see it every time I take an Uber, so there's no need to go there specifically.
Its ok, very crowded at the 9..00am show where the fragon emits fire and water for a 10 minute show.
The colour of  the bridge changes constantly and the bridge is very long. It is very convenient as it is opposite The famous fountain.
It worth to see it at night...during the day without the lights it can look just like a normal bridge with steel yellow structure.

One of the bridges over the Han River. It is pretty because of the dragon decorations, but it is not worth going out of your way to visit. At night, it is illuminated, so it stands out from a distance.

After securing a spot on the rooftop of Time Cafe by the sea at Yongdari Bridge on Saturday evening to watch the dragon head performance and waiting in the cool breeze, what we saw was four coughs mixed with fire and a five-minute dragon acupuncture shower. Had it not been for the bonus of the rooftop's ambiance and the excitement of the boisterous crowd, we might have actually felt a bit disappointed.

Very interesting but short visit is all that is needed it is in the town which you can spend hours wander through.We took a boat ride after, you may see weddings being photographed there as we watched two.
It's right in front of the hotel... You can see the bridge while having a cup of coffee in your room after waking up and having breakfast...
Apparently, the dragon breathes fire on weekends.+*:ﾟ+｡.☆
We've walked through it a couple of times. It's not exactly impressive, but it's definitely worth a few photos. But making a special trip just for this...that's definitely an acquired taste.

It's a beautiful bridge that gives the city a unique personality during the dark hours. Some days it throws fire but unfortunately we were in this city on a different day and couldn't see it. I wish I would had been able to see it, but... maybe next time.

The bridge is definitely a tourist attraction. If you’re in da nang you’ll want to come here. It’s right next to the harbour with eating spots along the way. The bridge breathes fire and smoke at 9pm for about a minute on and off. That’s about it. Nothing special

It is one of the representative images of Da Nang City. As one of the bridges crossing the Han River, it is shaped like a dragon, and a fire show featuring the dragon's head is held on weekends. It looks beautiful from a distance, and the Dragon Bridge viewed at night from the Asia Park Sun Wheel is also spectacular.
It is a quite a long bridge, and I think it would be quite beautiful in the evening when the lights are on and the Dragon will become a golden one. Good place to take pics

The show is available every Saturday and Sunday evening at 9 PM. We were at the head of the bridge. The fire-breathing part wasn't anything special, and it only lasted a short time. However, the water spray was quite large. I wonder if tourists standing on the bridge get wet...
I was hesitant about seeing the show since it was at night, but I'm glad I did. I was surprised that they used water in addition to fire, and it was very enjoyable.
It spouts fire and water at 9 PM on weekends.

Don't go to the shady New Mart supermarket in Da Nang, Vietnam.This shady supermarket is located at the intersection of Tran Hung Dao and Triev Viet Vuong streets, near the Dragon Bridge. It's opposite a very large boat. The store is called New Mart, and it has a pink storefront. The two girls in pictures 1 and 2 are the employees.We bought two boxes of coconut crackers, two boxes of minced meat and vegetable porridge, and one coconut ice cream. The girl charged us 359,000 VND. Then she refused to give us a receipt. When we asked for one, she initially said the machine was broken and couldn't print one. Then she said if we didn't want a receipt, she could give us a chance to win a prize.I felt the total amount she charged wasn't right compared to the price on the shelf, so I insisted on getting an invoice. She initially refused, then changed the prices on the computer. Printing an invoice should only take a few seconds, but she spent 15 minutes on the computer. I suspected she was just trying to inflate the price to 359K. Finally, she printed the invoice. Why did she say the printer was broken earlier, but now it was printing?Then I pointed out that the price of the minced meat and vegetable porridge was 20K, but it printed as 70K. She said we bought bird's nest porridge. We don't understand Vietnamese, and only after we got home and opened the package did we realize she was lying to us because we couldn't read Vietnamese; it wasn't bird's nest porridge at all. Then she charged us 80K for one coconut cracker and 75K for another, while the price on the shelf was 40K. She said it was because the price had been adjusted.We knew we'd been scammed by these two girls at New Mart, but we couldn't speak the language and needed to get to the airport. We didn't have time to argue with them and could only leave empty-handed. Later, I discovered that coconut crackers only cost 28-30K at other shops, and minced meat and vegetable porridge only cost 20K. New Mart sold them to us for several times that price, and even changed the prices at the last minute, refusing to issue invoices—very dishonest.But I couldn't swallow this; such deception is shameless. Many Vietnamese people I've met are very polite and courteous, but these two girls from New Mart really angered me. So I posted this online, urging everyone never to buy anything from this pink-clad New Mart; it's a scam.

This bridge is overhyped without reason. On weekends at 9 pm there is a water and fire show on the bridge wherein 2-3 times in a space of 10-15 minutes the dragon will breathe fire and water alternatively. And thats about it. A real damp squib of show not wroth the wait or the fact that you have to stand all the while as there is no seating anywhere around. Also if it happens to be raining then you might as well just go back to your hotel.

The fire/water show is on Fridays, Saturdays and Sundays at 21:00-21:10. The traffic across the bridge stops at least 15 min before and after the show which is really inconvenient. Watching at the bridge right next to the dragon head is not advised, as it’s too hot from fire and too wet from water that the dragon head exudes. There are many pickpockets around the place, many people lost money and phones there. Many Vietnamese arranged chairs in nice spots around the dragon bridge and will take your money to reserve the chairs and watch the show from there, and you will have to pay for some foods/drinks to only watch it within 10 minutes (not worth it).  The fire comes out only 5-6 times; water - 3-4 times, and that is all. Not spectacular and not worth wasting your time to arrive there in advance and book the spot/table/chair. It’s worth watching only if you are passing by and not going there on purpose
Saturday night 9:00.We are near the point and we see some flames coming out of the dragon's mouth.It lasts a few seconds.Then there are a few water jets.Overrated
Drove over the bridge during the day and looks like lumps of metal. At night okay with short show. Made as a tourist attraction but over hyped
Not very good. Disappointed. All the reviews were amazing but didn’t live upto the expectations. We waited over an hour for the dragon and it went for less than 15minutes…  Very very busy also :(

What a ridiculous over-hyped thing. The dragon shoots fire a few times than huge water. Big wool. Thousands of people gather to shove their way around, and local hawkers scream at each other and at passers by. There was some sort of unwritten code that you had to buy something to sit in chairs and then an older woman with a wad of cash would yell at people until they paid, even if they’d bought something from the local cart. Total mayhem and hard to get out of. Avoid and use someone else’s Insta pics.

Luckily I only stayed one night. A city without a soul, always immersed in fog. Many possibilities for excursions in the surrounding area, but for them I recommend using Hoi An as a base, very picturesque and pretty.

It is a bridge in the shape of a dragon, during the day its paint is golden. At night, it lights up with colors that change at regular intervals, orange, green, blue, green and we start again. On Saturdays and Sundays at 9 p.m., the dragon while changing colors as described previously, spits fire and water. It's not a spectacle or else a failure.

Having only been in Da Nang an hour or so, we decided to make our way and see the Saturday night show at the dragon bridge which, I believe, starts at 21.00.We walked and found ourselves outside a riverside entertainment centre, which had sumptuous 'pints' of draught Tiger (@30,000 đồng) and a superb view of the 'Dragon Bridge'.However, we felt a touch underwhelmed by the actual 5 to 10 minute display, the very beginning being the best part when the dragon snorts out the first burst of fire.This said, it's worth going to see, as the night market is right next door, and there's some interesting grub to be eaten, even though the stuff in the market are a bit mundane.
I had very high expectations because it was called the Dragon Bridge. I heard that
Pretty lame. I just chose to drive over it on my way back from the Hai Van pass. Just a bridge with a dragon head and body running down the middle.

The fire and water spewing dragon, was a disappointment. The show which comes on only on Saturday n Sunday night lasts for no more than 10 minutes. It is a novelty and unless you have nothing to do, you can give it a miss.

I went to see it because it's a tourist spot, but it was just average during the day. I walked from one end of the bridge to the other, but even seeing it up close didn't impress me. As I continued sightseeing elsewhere, it gradually became night, and when I saw the Dragon Bridge from another bridge, it was beautifully lit up.
I know this is the famouse face of Da Nang, personally I think it's kinda cheesy.  Walking around the river at night was nice though.

It was dispointed me about short demonstration of firing and water jetting in an awaitng weekly event, 9PM Saturday. I expected it was longer adequate to such big event of Danang. Beside that, many tourist boat approached too close to the bridge obstructing view from Love-bridge.
The Dragon Bridge is neither spectacular nor grand enough to warrant waiting in line.

The hotel staff told us there was a special performance at Zhenglong Bridge every weekend evening at 9 pm. We were really looking forward to joining in the fun, and the riverbank was packed with spectators. In the end, we found the performance to be quite boring; it was just that the dragon heads on the bridge spewed fire and water, the visual effects weren't very impressive, and it was also very short.

Went to go see this bridge on a Saturday evening to watch the fire and waterLots of people all around the are waiting till 9pm Lots of oohs and ahhhs when it start d, but lots  of people saying “Is that it .”?Unless your already over that way, I would just watch it from you hotel, save the crowded journey
Not much to say really. It's a bridge that is really busy with traffic. Good to see but wouldn't make s special trip.
On Saturdays at 9:00 PM the dragon on the bridge breathes fire and water, attracting thousands of people, the show lasts barely 3 minutes.
I watched it while drinking beer on the open terrace, but it was just okay, nothing special. It was crowded all around, so getting back was a hassle.

The Dragon Bridge, which I have seen so often on the internet. We viewed it from afar and also walked across it. As we tend to walk a lot while traveling, we crossed the Han River via the Dragon Bridge.
I almost always agree with the overall TripAdvisor ratings, but not this time! It's by far the most disappointing attraction we saw in Vietnam.
Just another bridge. Yes, it is according to the culture of Vietnam, as you see this motiv again and.again in shows amd it is very beautiful. But the bridge is nothing special

If youve seen online that the dragon breathes fire at weekends, it is NOT doing it on saturdays at the moment, it has been replaced by a firework show. The firework show was good but not really comparable to a bridge breathing fire! Our hostel said it still breathes fire at 9pm on sundays, though we were out of Da nang so cant confirm this.The bridge is still very nice especially lit up at night.
Waited until 9pm bustling through crowds and was not that great. The water did not work so we went home

There isn't much to do nearby, but it's a great place to sit in a cafe and enjoy the night view. The river smelled, so I only took photos, but the locals seem to enjoy it as part of their daily routine.
A bridge located in Da Nang, featuring a golden dragon 600m long. During the night it changes color every two minutes, and on Sundays there is a show where the dragon breathes fire and water.

Went to see the dragon breath fire and steam but after two quick bursts it was all over it takes longer to get the tourists boats in position  than it does for the flames to burn. It is all very pretty and ghd bridges are lit up the colours vary etc but it's one to rush out to catch. We took a taxi to a local restaurant  we were recommended to and had a short walk to veiw the bridge I would say see it but dont expect too much

It's worth a look if you happen to be passing by and the timing is right, but I don't think it's worth going out of your way to make it part of your travel itinerary... haha. From a distance, it is hard to tell if it's a dragon or a worm... but since it is a landmark of Da Nang, you should stop by if you have the chance.

The dragon was supposed to breathe fire at 9 pm on Saturday, and it did so several times, but then suddenly stopped. We thought it was over, but since no one had left, we waited. After about 1-2 minutes, it breathed fire again, just like before. After a few more times, it suddenly stopped again. We thought it would be the same every time, so we left. Suddenly, we heard a loud noise; it had switched to spraying water. Luckily we left, otherwise our camera would have been damaged. I'm very surprised that so many people were waiting for it to breathe fire.
I saw the 9 PM show on the weekend.
You will spot it as you travel from the airport to your accommodation or walk around downtown Da Nang.
It's easily visible from a distance, so it can serve as a landmark if you get lost. It's just a bridge you use regularly, and it looks quite beautiful at night when it's lit up.
Well, its a bridge. There is people on motorbikes and cars going over the river , using the brigde - 24/7.It's looks quite okay at night with all the lights but its more of a hype
You'll be disappointed if you go out of your way to visit.
Literally, it spews fire a few times and water a few times.
I think it would be enough to just take a look on your way to and from the airport and think, "Oh, so this exists."

There is no need to go out of your way to visit. It looks even more crude up close. Since you have to cross a bridge to get from the airport to My Khe Beach anyway, seeing this place as you pass by is sufficient. Alternatively, viewing it from a distance as a nightscape should suffice.
The nighttime illuminations were beautiful.
A bridge shaped like a dragon. There's a special fire-breathing ceremony at night. It's not particularly special, but it's a local feature. It's really not worth making a special trip to see it.
It isn't particularly impressive or worth seeing, but a quick glance while passing by should be enough. It appears that mainly locals come to look around the bridge.

It looks beautiful, but we went there to see it spit fire and the event was cancelled but they didn't tell anyone so everyone, so we ended up standing in the cold for an hour waiting for something that never came.

As of late October, evening temperatures are pleasantly cool at around 21 degrees Celsius. It is a great place for a walk after sunset, and since the area near Yongdari Bridge has been developed into a park much like the Han River, it is a good spot to spend time. In particular, there are several photo spots in front of the Riverside Hotel, so take a casual look around after dinner.
Oh my... I waited until 9 PM on Sunday evening just to see this. ㅠㅠ It just spews fire and water...

The Dragon Bridge fire show at 9 PM on weekends is incredibly underwhelming considering the massive crowds waiting in line. Rather than making a special trip to see the bridge itself, it is best to just view it as a landmark of Da Nang while passing by.
From a distance it looks like a dragon, but it's not that impressive when you actually cross it. It's lit up at night, and apparently water comes out at set times.

The dragon bridge on a Saturday evening at 9pm spouts fire and water and attracts many thousands of people. Don’t go for the spouting fire and water, go for the extraordinary atmosphere. Whenever large numbers of people get together there’s an atmosphere, here it’s terrific. Markets, bars, restaurants and every other type of outlet. Tourists and locals mingling seemingly without a care in the world.A joy but not because of the bridge!

Rather boring during the daytime it does come live a little in the evening. With the lights up it offers a nice entertainment for children. For adults it is more a curiosity, not worth a special trip. Just passing by in a car or on a bike is quite enough.

This is a difficult review. By day, the bridge's structure, so fragmented and serialized, has its share of originality. Even the "head" is fine; it looks like an architectural divertissement. It's all acceptable if it weren't for the insistence at night, when the "dragon" is illuminated with changing colored lights, comes alive for a few minutes, spitting flames, and is presented as a major tourist attraction.

We ended up in Da Nang by chance, as we had to change our route. The Dragon Bridge isn't particularly remarkable, much like the city of Da Nang in general. Apparently, it looks a bit more impressive at night, but anyone not planning on stopping in Da Nang shouldn't go there just for the bridge.
It looks pretty from a distance. Since you'll see it anyway while passing by, I don't think it's really worth going out of your way to visit. Maybe only if you're already planning to go to Love Pier?
The impression was a little too artificial. You don't necessarily need to go out of your way to see it, as it's one of the bridges connecting the town and the beach, so taxis will pass over it.

Viewing it while passing by in a taxi is sufficient. Walking along the Pier of Love at night to admire the tall and fancy buildings isn't bad, but the streets are crowded and heavily polluted, and it isn't particularly impressive.

Look, it’s pretty and it’s cool but you don’t need to plan your trip around this. If you see it, cool, if you don’t, it’s honestly no big deal. Under the dragon head, under the stairs there are some good restaurants. I recommend eating dinner and sitting there instead of waiting on the streets and stuff. Plus, there’s one restaurant that gives you free peanuts and quail eggs. Go for that.

Hi all,If you are in Danang on a Saturday or Sunday night at 9 pm the Dragon Bridge shoots out Flames and sprays water.The name Dragon Bridge comes from it's design. A Yellow dragon has been incorporated in the bridge design and looks great. You need to get there early along the shore line or actually on the bridges side walk itself to get a good Veiw.When the bridge does shoot out flames it's very impressive but also very disappointing, as it only shot out flames twice with two water sprays after that.I've now seen it once I wouldn't go out of my way to see it again.
I heard that the dragon breathes fire from its mouth
While i understand this is an iconic bridge in danang but honestly nothing spectacular about this bridge except for the lighted dragon. Good spot for some nice photos otherwise nothing much.

My mother in law told us we had to see the dragon breathing fire and water. This only occurs on Saturday’s at 9-9:15 pm. It was quite crowded, dark, people smoking everywhere. It was 20 second of breathing fire and 20 seconds of water. Was not worth the time and effort. Afterward it was so crowded to get out of the area. We don’t recommend standing on the bridge near the head otherwise u will be super hot from the fire and wet from the water.
I waited to see the show, but it's not as cool as I thought...
The bridge is nothing special. The fire/water show that takes place on Saturdays and Sundays at 9 pm isn't a must-see.
We took a taxi from the hotel to see the fireworks and flames that were supposed to be set off on the weekend.
It seems decent for viewing the night scenery, but the Dragon Bridge fireworks show is smaller in scale and not as spectacular as expected.

This bridge connects the hotel area and the downtown area, so I often crossed it when taking taxis. In fact, while driving, you wouldn't even realize it's the Dragon Bridge; it looks almost identical to ordinary neon lights.
Beautiful at night, lit up. Very scenic.
They say the Dragon Bridge
It wasn't as magnificent as I expected. The dragon did change color at night, but since it was such a slender dragon, it didn't really stand out.

It is a bridge in Da Nang with railings decorated with dragons, so I thought it was unique and different from bridges in other countries. I saw it both during the day and at night, but it seemed more impressive at night than during the day.
They say flames come out of the dragon's head... but it looks shabby... It seems like it's

I wondered if it was really necessary to go there at night or take a boat to see it. Since downtown Da Nang is very small, you are bound to see it while wandering around, so you can just check it out then... Don't do a tour... lol

You may pass through this Dragon Bridge not only when passing by but also when traveling. It is visible from a distance, and at night, the bright lighting makes it even more impressive. The bridge itself is worth seeing, but while it serves as a landmark, I don't think it is worth making a special trip to visit.
Our tourist bus tour took us across the bridge… unfortunately, we saw it from the tail side and not the head side. Not particularly spectacular; perhaps it's better in the evening.
Nice to drive over; not worth a detour. It is just a bridge over the water with an iron dragon across it.

Dragon decorations and lighting. I heard they have a fire show on weekends, but since I went on a weekday... It was sufficient as a landmark, but if you just pass by it during your itinerary, you'll simply think, "Oh, so this is the Dragon Bridge."

I saw it at the Brilliant Lounge lol. You really don't have to see it though. It's seriously nothing special, just take a look if you happen to pass by. There's no need to go out of your way to see it, and if you're tired, I think it's more beneficial to just rest at the hotel lol.
I don't think you necessarily need to go see this.
It doesn't look like much; it's just a bridge.
It wasn't as impressive as famous bridges in Korea like Gwangalli Bridge or Yeongjong Bridge. It feels like just tourism marketing.... It's too crowded and noisy.
There was nothing to do really..  Yea the bridge was nice, lit up but-don't know what the hype is about
If you've been to Busan, you'll understand.
A bridge... admittedly a bit unusual but well... if you're out and about in Danang why not... otherwise not worth the trip.
The night view from the riverside was nice. It's best not to expect the weekend fireworks show to the point of adjusting your travel schedule accordingly.
It seems like it became a landmark because there isn't much to see in Da Nang...
For the way everyone was gathering and talking about the Dragon bridge show we expected something amazing. Instead we stood waiting for a bit a fire and water. Not worth it.

The bridge, built to resemble a dragon, has a head that will spout fire and water.  Unfortunately, you may stand around with several hundred other people waiting for the show, only to discover the dragon mouth is not working.  Take your chances, the show is weekends only, at 9:00 PM.

We were underwhelmed by the dragon bridge 9pm show. The dragon on the bridge itself is something to look at if you're in the area, lit up at night, changing colours. The show itself was an anti-climax. Fire comes out of the dragon's mouth periodically and then water is sprayed periodically in a mist. The area gets absolutely packed. We wouldn't recommend making a special visit.
There isn't a compelling reason to go, but since you can't go anywhere else in Da Nang without visiting here, you absolutely have to see it at least three times.
A tranquil riverside atmosphere.
You can see it from anywhere in downtown Da Nang, and you have to cross the Dragon Bridge to get to Lotte Mart from My Khe Beach.
Go in the night to see the magnificient dragon bridge. Hai brought us to Hoi An via the dragon bridge where the views on our left and right were spectacular!
To put it simply, I shouldn't have had such high expectations (ーー;)

In all my life, I’ve never seen so many cockroaches. I’m terrified of bugs, so I couldn’t move forward and ended up bursting into tears. I was just planning to have a beer... I took a taxi straight back.

The Dragon Bridge Fire Show is a must-see at night. The Dragon Bridge is the main thoroughfare on the Han River, and its shape resembles a dragon. Every Saturday and Sunday evening at 9 PM, there's a 20-minute fire and water show, with the bridge's lights changing colors intermittently. Originally, I planned to take a boat trip on the Han River to see the spectacular fire and water show, but unfortunately, due to preparations for Vietnam's Liberation Day fireworks display on April 30th, the boat trips were cancelled, resulting in poor viewing spots. The overall fire show felt a bit weak at the dragon head, making me wonder why I even bothered going.
Unless you're just wandering around and looking, I completely do not recommend going there specifically to see this.
We went to the dragon bridge to watch the fire show and stoped at a coffee shop. The show is just a few bursts of fire and water.Not worth going out to.
A dragon-shaped bridge in downtown Da Nang.
It's not any attraction according to me. You can take walk along the river at evening after dinner, but it is not worth to go there specially.
Perhaps because I visited on a cloudy afternoon, I don't have any particularly memorable impressions.

The bridge is over the Han River.It is a generic bridge for cars and pedestrians. On my end of the bridge is the Cham Museum. The artwork at both end of the resembles a dragon head and that is how it got its name, but on closer look, it is just an artwork and so use your imagination to suit the name. It is well lit with different colours at night.Somehow, you will pass through this bridge or near to it while you are in Danang.
I heard they were doing a fire show
Nothing fascinating, just a normal bridge that has lights that changes colour. Good for pictures at night I guess.
There's a huge dragon attached to the bridge. Apparently
We were here during the day so we didn't see it all lit up or get to witness the dragon spitting fire which I can imagine would be very cool. But to see it in the day it's just another Bridge.

I stopped by on the weekend since it was close to where I was staying. I think the sight of a dragon breathing fire might be memorable for a young child... but I don't think it's really worth an adult to visit when they don't have time. The surroundings were a bit messy and noisy, too.

Beautifully lit up in the evening, I couldn't figure out where I could cross the road to walk along the river but I was tired and the heavy traffic didn't give me the impression it would be a relaxing walk
Around 9 PM, it was absolutely packed with people. I watched from near DHC Marina, so I had a great view of the dragon, including the fire and water.
i couldnt find anything impressing there. it might look better at night, but in day it was jut a normal bridge with a skinny dragon in between, the view over bridge is nice though
If you walk, you'll just breathe in exhaust fumes.
There really isn't anything there other than the bridge. I wasn't
Just a bridge with a dragon, not fantastic. Can take a look at it if you're around the corner, but not worthwhile to make a deliberate trip down to see it

It's hard to tell during the day, but the dragon's eyes are heart-shaped. The color is yellow, the natural color of the material, but at night it changes to blue and green, giving it a different feel.
If you ask me if it's worth seeing, I'd say no.

Locals and foreigners alike talk about the Dragon Bridge show, but to put it bluntly, it's nothing more than spraying fire on the bridge, then spraying water again, with little lights on, and that's it. There's really no way to understand what's so good to see; there are no surprises, and I definitely don't recommend going there specifically. If you happen to pass by at night and want to see it, it's recommended to allow more time because it's incredibly crowded. The real advice for the area is to take a walk along the river; you'll find bars of different styles that stay open late.

We went from Hoi An, which cost about $25 AUD to get there, and we were expecting a fireworks show from what we had been told and read about. You need to be near the head of the dragon where it breathes fire and water, but there is no fireworks. It is pretty to see but it wasn't worth the expense to get there from Hoi An just to see it. Make an afternoon of it, the restaurants along the beach are good and you can watch the fishermen pull in the nets and then stay for the show.
I didn't have high expectations, but... it wasn't very good.
The nighttime illumination is beautiful, but during the day, the Dragon Bridge is the kind of bridge you just drive past without really noticing.

Everyone is waiting in a cafe without air conditioning just to watch a fire show, all for what is so special about that dragon? But it just spewed fire artificially a few times and that was it... I usually say everything is good, but this wasn't very good.
