It’s not a place worth visiting during the day. I haven’t been there at night so I don’t know, but I thought Cheongdam Bridge was much cooler.

The dragon bridge is just a bridge. I have to admit it is a nice bridge, but at the end of the day, it is just a bridge. It has some lights. On special occasions, huge crowds gather to see the fire and then water spew from the mouth of the dragon. Not really worth the wait and crowds. I had difficulty understanding the big deal.
The bridge itself is best seen at night when it's lit up.

Unless the 9pm fire spouting is convenient for your schedule, don't set aside time for this. It's not like a flamethrower, it's three little (literally little) bursts of flame about 5 seconds apart, and lasts for a few sets. The reviews are very misleading too, it really underwhelms. Sorry to be a killjoy.
There are large dragon decorations along the bridge. At night, they are illuminated with dazzling lights and look beautiful from a distance. Perhaps they are not meant to be viewed up close.
I took a cruise ship with my child to catch the fire show, but

It isn't exactly a place worth going out of your way to see, but seeing the Dragon Bridge while traveling by taxi made me realize once again that I was in Da Nang. Although there is a pedestrian walkway, I wouldn't recommend walking across it due to the severe exhaust fumes. It was so hot that I didn't even dare to cross.
Even when asked "What do you think!?", all I could say was "It's a pretty interesting bridge."

The building looks beautiful when lit up at night. I went to see the fire and water show on Saturday night at 9pm. The performance lasted about 10 minutes, with 5 minutes of fire breathing and 5 minutes of water squirting in between. It was so lame that I couldn't help but laugh!

Nice bridge but it's just a bridge. I saw that it is really famous so I was half expecting to be impressed... But it's just a bridge. Don't come all the way just for it, but if you are alrady in Danang, go and have a look.
A place where a lot of Vietnamese people gather, and lots of foreigners too!!!
I dont know what to say. I think its beautiful but still its just a bridge. But there near is nice places to take pictures and just relax or walk.

It's one of several bridges connecting Da Nang's main urban area to the coastal area (New Quarter), nothing particularly noteworthy. Just take a look if you're passing by. I haven't walked across it; it's quite long. Walking isn't highly recommended in Vietnam; it's quite tiring. Taking a taxi is relatively cheap.
It's just a bridge. If you go around 6 PM, you can experience a massive wave of motorcycles heading home from work. Loud honking...
Luckily, I went on the weekend and
Sent to see this Saturday night at 9pm. At that time the dragon changes colour and breathes fire. It's pretty cool to see but I wouldn't go out of my way to watch it.

Nothing awesome. It didnt turned out as what we have expected. The atmosphere was boring. Litetally there was nothing much, just fire and water coming out of the Dragon's mouth.  Alot of locals crowding along the bridge w their motorbikes and before the show offically ends, everyone took their bikes n rode off causing traffic jam.

We went there because it was recomended by the hotel .You go there and you wait until 9pm , that it's the show on weekends . We were expecting a light or firework show . Time arrives and you see for 5 minutes only fire and smoke coming out from the dragon.Very dissapointed Go if you have kids , they will love it
Nothing to see about this bridge. It's not as expected. The only thing special about this one is there lights on in evening.
It looks beautiful on the photos but it was a woah thing to do in Da Nang.Every sunday, according to the hotel staff, at 9.30pm the dragon wiill spit water and fire. We didnt make it...
They say fire comes out of the dragon's mouth on weekend nights... but normally, the color of the lighting changes. It's nothing special.

Dragon Bridge. I had high expectations before the trip, but it's just okay. It's a regular bridge. I was looking forward to it because I heard the dragon breathes fire on weekends, but that was just so-so too.
The fire-breathing dragon every weekend, free show but it is advisable to arrive early enough to find a good spot.

IMHO the locals "have been sold a pup". Others will surely disagree. It's a nice enough bridge and, I am sure, a feat of engineering as are all bridges covering any span. A few bits of metal added on and painted yellow to give the impression of a dragon. At weekends and special occasions it spits fire and water. Perhaps I was not best situated to view this but I was not at all impressed. Going by the enthusiasm of some local youths, they would disagree.
Tourists can only admire the Dragon Bridge from afar. In reality, it's not as impressive as it looks in photos. A new attraction in Da Nang.
More beautiful to see from a distance (from another nearby bridge).
not that big a deal. Looks nice at night, just drive past on the way to somewhere else. ....."..............
The dragon is supposed to breathe fire according to the videos on YouTube. However, when we were there on November 15, there was only a water fountain three times, for a total of 5 minutes.
The dragon bridge is fine to drive over and take pictures but it is not a destination. It is OK to see on your way to the DaNang markets.etc.
Perhaps it's because I saw it during the day rather than at night, but despite its immense size, the sculpture itself is rather shoddy.

It's considered a landmark of Da Nang. You can actually walk across the bridge without it taking much time, as it connects the two sides of the city. There are coffee shops and bars along the bridge, so it's practically impossible to visit Da Nang without passing by it.
We waited for several hours for the light show that turned to be average. We were told later that about 100 litres of petrol is used each weekend for the fire...what a waste....

The dragon blew fire and water at 9pm only Sat and Sunday. It drew lots of tourist, but it wasn't impressed much. It was worth of hangout by river and drinking coffee.  It was good idea for attracting tourists.

The bridge really does spit fire and water but I would only go if you are staying nearby or if you happen to be in the neighborhood. It only lasts for a few minutes and it ultimately isn't that spectacular. But hey, how many people can say they've seen a bridge shoot fireballs?
Very fast show and we imagined more but is still worth to visit if u are close to this place. Show is avaiable every saturday and sumday at 9 pm
It would have been even better to see it at night, but it was impressive since it was my first time seeing a bridge like this. I heard that a dragon breathes water and fire on weekends. ^^
O it's nice to visit with kids , they ll see the fancy in a dragon going up and down the river . Lovely pictures being taken
We have a fireworks show every Saturday at 9 PM.
Dragon bridge is just a long bridge with a large wavy yellow Dragon in the centre. On weekends at 9pm the Dragon head breathes fire. Good to look at and perhaps take a photo.
This is one of the most amazing snow bridges with classic architecture. You should come here, come to Vietnam!

Unfortunately, we missed the fire and water spectacle, as we crossed the bridge to the Cham Museum during the day. Anyone visiting Da Nang should definitely see this bridge; it's well worth it. Only satisfactory because we were there during the day.
This bridge utilizes recent technologies, and at the same time, the bridge's support structure allows for a very interesting dragon shape.
The night view is charming, and walking along the river is nice. However, it is noisy because there are too many motorcycles.

At 9pm on a Saturday and Sunday the bridge breathes fire. If you are in the area then pop along, but I would not recommend a special visit just to see this. The area gets very busy from 8.30, the day nang food center overlooks the bridge. So if you get a seat around 8pm, you should get a good view. The boats that go up and down the river stop by the bridge to view this as well.
The Dragon Bridge is one of the famous landmarks that comes to mind when thinking of Da Nang.
I don't feel it's worth going out of your way to see it. You can see the colors change slightly at night, but it isn't particularly exciting.
This is a bridge you will frequently come across while traveling around Da Nang by taxi. There are many cafes nearby as well. There isn't much to see specifically, but it is an iconic bridge.

It seems like you could just pass by it while taking a taxi! It’s a unique bridge, so it’s interesting, but that’s about it, nothing more, nothing less. Still, since you’re in the area, I recommend taking a look, even if just from a distance!
The bridge looks like a dragon swimming through the river, something different. Kids love it, it is pretty at night especially when lit up.
As you walk around downtown Da Nang, this iconic bridge
It's worth seeing, but aside from the dragon-shaped bridge,

I was so excited to see it at 9 PM on Sunday, so I waited at a nearby cafe. And finally, it was time! They closed the bridge to traffic a few minutes beforehand, and lots of tourists had come to watch. But when it actually started, I was like, "Huh? Is that all?" There was fire breathing and water squirting, and I think the show lasted less than 10 minutes, haha. Apparently, many locals have never seen it before.

Not more than a regular bridge in weekdays. There’re light decorations, but uhhh. Anyway, if you don’t have other engagement on a weekday night, it’s nice to have a cup of coffee in shops on riverbank, and wait for the fire and water show. It should not be a must-go item though.

A dragon-shaped bridge, illuminated at night. Worth seeing, but not a special trip to Da Nang. However, if you're staying nearby, there's a very nice area with restaurants and shops, and also a sort of replica of the Lovers' Bridge.
This time, I stayed at the Mercure Danang Hotel.
I think it looks pretty when viewed from the Novotel. When you go up close, it's just... like a landmark, I guess? ㅠㅠ
It was cool, not amazing, I think I liked more the excitement for the show then the actual show. We seen it from the Riverside Hotel roof terrace.
This bridge is a symbol of the city. It was beautifully lit up at night. I was looking forward to seeing it spit fire on the weekend, but the show was canceled due to the APEC summit. What a shame.
It isn't particularly special, but it is a decent place to enjoy the night view in Da Nang. The colors are pretty, though. However, I have doubts about whether the water being sprayed is clean.
Nice Bridge, especially if you are there over the weekends when they have the fire show in the evenings.
I found out about the Dragon Bridge when a taxi driver told me about it while I was out for a meal nearby.
We walked from Art Hotel Danang and see the Dragon Bridge from far. I think Han River Bridge much more spectacular and colourful than this Dragon Bridge.
It wasn't anything special since I couldn't watch the fire show.
When passing through Danang, you can't ignore this distinctive bridge. It certainly has a different effect when lit up at night; during the day, it's its originality that's most admired.

The show was scheduled to start at 9 PM, but my shopping at Big C took longer than expected, so I didn't get into a Vinasun taxi at the taxi stand until 8:50 PM. The taxi driver also realized we wouldn't make it in time, so he sped off, but as we approached the Dragon Bridge, the traffic increased, and we couldn't move forward easily. As we were crossing the bridge, we saw the dragon in the distance spewing flames. We could see it from inside the car, but the driver didn't give up and smoothly drove along the road beside the river, telling us to go and see while he waited, so we got out of the taxi and headed towards the river. There were many tourists, so we were able to see the dragon, but it was the last fountain. When the show ended and we returned to where we got in, it was packed with people, and other passengers were negotiating with the taxi driver who had brought them in. But the driver didn't pick them up, which was a relief. I'm grateful to the Vinasun taxi driver.

This is the famous Dragon Bridge... You don't really need to make time to come here; you can just take a look as you pass by during your trip to Da Nang haha. It is a little prettier at night, though.

Pretty a bridge, almost every day the journey will pass. It looks nothing special during the day, but at night it turns into a giant dragon that changes color. It is a landmark to be able to take pictures by the river.
A lot like Blackpool, it really is just a flashing bridge by all accounts that you will guaranteed see if you are in Damage without having to pay it an official visit

On Saturday night, the dragon head on the Dragon Bridge breathes fire and smoke, attracting many boats to watch. But after the performance, I felt a bit empty... from a distance, it really didn't feel like much.
Its best to go to the bridge on the weekend when they use a flame thrower to make the dragon breathe fire

The arched bridge, with its minimal artificial embellishments, holds commemorative significance, but its design isn't particularly unique. The nighttime fire show attracts tourists and boats, but the sound and light effects are mediocre and the show is short. You'll likely pass by it on your way to or from Da Nang city and the coast, so there's no need to make a special trip to it.
It's an amazingly designed bridge. But I think the best view is of the Dragon Bridge at night, from a distance. It's truly Da Nang's iconic bridge.
Lovely bridge and clever design but advertised in a lot of places as fireworks at weekends. It’s not, the dragons mouth spurts fire for about 10 minutes.

At night, this bridge is shiny bright with various colors of light (keep changing). But it is not good as expected, just some crude decoration. But, this place represents this city's young people's night culture and attracts lots of people to itself.Don't bring a car any reasons and be careful tremendous traffic of motorcycles.
Everyone kept talking about the Dragon Bridge, so I wondered what it was... but

Hi thereThe Dragon bridge quite impressive especially at night. There is a fire show on Saturday and Sunday at 9pm. Not exactly fireworks I would say more like a dragons fire. Still looks good but don't blink or be to far away you might miss it. Still worth seeing. Something different. Cheers
Opposite the Champa Museum lies this bridge in the shape of a dragon. Unfortunately, I didn't see it lit up, but it is an eye-catcher in the city during the day as well.
This bridge is a famous landmark in Da Nang.

I personally think it's a very ordinary tourist attraction. Actually, you can pass through this bridge from the city center or the airport to the beach. Maybe it's because I didn't see the fire-breathing scene, but I think it's just a bridge and nothing special at all!

The dragon comes alive sprouting fire and water at 9 pm every Saturday and Sunday night. All traffic from either sides of the bridge is closed during the five minutes of fire and water sprouting display.Advice not to stand too close facing the dragon’s head as you will be blessed by the sprays of water from the dragon.The entire promenade on both sides of Han river adjacent to the Dragon bridge is most active and colourful on before and after the fire and water display.I wish the dragon and the display could be installed on Han river instead of the bridge.
It's a cool looking bridge... That is all. Lots of couples go here to see the romantic city. Does have a cool view of the river and buildings though. Not mind blowing though.. It is a  bridge.
A bridge I kept seeing while passing by in a taxi for three days! I just think it contributes to the night view. I don't know the details.
About 9pm you can see the fire and water coming from the dragon mouth it can be seen from a distance or experience close up you don't have to go on a tour to see and can get a taxi cheap enough.
The bridge, like other objects along the river, is bathed in colorful lights in the evening. It's quite impressive, but not a must-see.
It's near downtown and looks beautiful at night. It even spews fire on weekends, so it's definitely worth seeing. I only viewed it from my hotel, though...

One of Da Nang's landmark attractions, it's not particularly special except for the night view, but there are many restaurants, bars, ice cream shops and cafes nearby, making it a good choice for an after-dinner stroll.
I saw the Dragon Bridge(?) once while passing by.
It looks worth seeing at night because of the lighting, but
There were no people crossing on foot...
It's just fascinating and big. It's prettier at night.
You'll definitely see it anywhere in the city; the Dragon Bridge at night is quite unique and is the main bridge connecting the two sides of the river.
I don't think it's worth going out of your way to visit. The city center is too dangerous and full of motorcycles, making it too risky for tourists.
It looks great from a distance, but it is better to view it from a hotel rooftop or something similar. I don't think it is worth going out of your way to see it.
The dragon on the Dragon Bridge breathes fire on weekends and public holidays. I was so happy because I arrived on a Saturday, giving me two chances to see it!
I walked across from the dragon's head to the tail.

I watched it as part of a local tour. I was watching from a cafe terrace by the nearby river, but the flames were surprisingly small and it was over in no time. If you have too high expectations, you'll be disappointed.
Dragon Bridge, one of Da Nang's representative attractions, had a rather ordinary atmosphere. Compared to something like Korea's Gwangan Bridge, it looks a bit lackluster.

The Dragon Bridge is a bridge over the River Hàn.During night, you can see the dragon is with different colour (the LED lights) compare to the day.On weekends at 9pm, the Dragon will breath fire + water.

From reviews I read I was told to go early to secure a spot by 8pm. That is not true. I went at 7.50pm and waited till 9pm for a 5mins "performance" of fire and water...Not worth my time since the weather is unbearable.Tips: if your hotel faces the head of the bride watch it from your room in comfort.If you have no intention to get wet, do not be on the bridge as the dragon shoots water (probably from the river)The best vantage viewpoints are one of the stalls set up around the bridge on the lower level.The area is bustling with activity from both locals and tourists alike. I recommend coming at 8.50pm instead.If you do have time take a stroll along the river, its more cooling but there's a smell. Good area for some nice night photos too. Can also see the locals dance, saw an outdoor art exhibition and performance too.
Nice to see the lights changing at night for a change of pace.. we walked the bridge as well as along the shore.

If you want to see the fireworks, please choose to come on the 5th, 6th, or 7th of the month. There are no fireworks on the 1st to 4th. It's a great choice to come on a special evening to see the scenery. Many people gather together at night, making it very lively.

arrived in danang on a saturday noon and checked into a hotel just next to the dragon ( head side). read that the dragon will spew fire and water at 9pm on weekends. from 730pm, sat at a coffee place with a good unblock view of the dragon (cannot recall the name). the dragon had litted up as well and changes color periodically.from about 8pm, the main road closed and a parade of songs and dances begin. there was a huge crowd of locals as well. at 9pm, the dragon began by spewing fire, 3 times of interval of about 1.5 minutes. follow by a spew of water. it was indeed a spectacular sight. but in all, lasted only less than 10 minutes. nice sight but gets abit boring after awhile. perhaps should just be there 10 minutes before to take some pics, albeit amongst a huge crowd
The bridge is pretty, but it's just a bridge. It's not a must-visit place, and since you'll see it a lot while traveling, just go ahead.
It spews smoke and sparks, its 1/4 of a mile long, its a bridge, what more can I say. I watched the show and night and walked it during the day, fun way to dress up a bridge.
It's big and impressive, but I don't feel like there's anything more to it than that.
Really it's just a bridgeA quite stylish bridgeA must visit for people who are lovers all things bridge!

The experience is quite different during the day and night; I recommend visiting once during the day and once at night to experience the different atmospheres. There are fireworks at 9:00 PM at the dragon head, and traffic control will be in effect on the bridge.

The bridge changes to various colors when lit up at night. It's usually yellow, but when it turns green it looks like a dragon. I've crossed it several times by taxi, not on foot, and once you're on it, it's just an ordinary bridge.
The area around the dragon that breathes fire and water is
A bridge shaped like a dragon, according to our guide the largest metal dragon in the world... nice to see, but don't make a special trip just to see it.
The night view is nice, but... it's just a bridge lol. I thought
It is very crowded and only on weekends.  The dragon head would have fire and water springler at 9.00pm.  Great to take picture with changing of colors on the entire bridge.

On Saturday night, a large crowd gathered about 30 minutes before the fire-breathing demonstration. However, when it began, the flames were smaller than expected, and there was a considerable gap between the first and second bursts of fire before it was over. After that, they repeatedly blew water (steam) for a long time. It was so anticlimactic that I was disappointed. I
It is simply a bridge with a dragon model.
It's a bit tacky, but when turned on in the evening it has a slight effect! Then I happened upon it one evening where it spat fire ;)

The fire only shoots out on Saturdays and Sundays. This time it was a Monday, so we couldn't see it shoot out fire. But at night, the lights change from one color to the next, so it was beautiful just to look at the night view. Up close, it also had a kind of cuteness to it.

When I saw it from a taxi, I was impressed by its unique shape even from a distance, but after looking at it for a while, I lost interest. It would probably look good as a background for a night view photo.
A bridge that shoots fire and water out of its dragon mouth on the weekends. Its kind of cool to see but I wouldn't say its somthing you must see.
Apparently, it only breathes fire on weekends, which was a shame. Well, I did manage to take some photos.
It's nice and interesting to bring the bridge to life. However, the dragon's fire only occurs at 9 PM and, at least when I was there, it was very weak. I was disappointed with the final result.
View it from a distance and take the opportunity to photograph it. Only from afar can you truly see the dragon's appearance on the bridge. From the bridge itself, this effect is lost.
The bridge with its lights is certainly beautiful to see, but I was disappointed by the fireworks displays, which I found rather short. However, the water jets afterwards were truly spectacular!

So I mean ultimately this is just a big bridge for traffic. There is a path for pedestrians but I wouldn't recommend the walk. The view from the bridge is nothing special (better at night when da nang is lit up) at 9pm on Saturday and Sunday the dragon head gives out a few puffs of fire and smoke. That's cool but I wouldn't go out of your way
Bridge is beautiful. The highlight has to be the fire and water spitting every weekend. But tbh that's not really impressive.
It's just a bridge, designed like a Dragon. Pass by and crossed the bridge a few times, but didn't bother to take picture.
It was a bit bigger than I expected, which felt strange, but it was definitely a dragon.

It was an average fire show, but I enjoyed watching the people under the dragon run when the water comes out. That was just worth it in. Show only goes for approx. 5 minutes Huge crowds. But its still an amazing and different bridge.

I was so excited at the idea of fire coming out of the dragon's mouth. Unfortunately, it just spat out several fireballs which weren't that huge. Not such an exciting display of fire as I expected. It also sprayed water. That's about it. If u wanna have your dinner as you watch the show, the restaurants in that area mostly only serve drinks. Honestly, I thought the Dragon Bridge fire would've been more impressive. There was a huge crowd gathered to watch the Dragon Bridge though.
Da Nang has several flashy bridges that connect the city center to the sea, but this one has the most unusual design. Is it

This was explained to us as a periodic fire and water spray show. Draws a HUGE crowd of people watchers.The show itself was a dud and in itself not worth the bother of navigating the crowd.Except that the DaNang folk are totally chill and very friendly. And good way to mingle and try to catch the local scene. Plus, the bridge itself is a nice piece of engineering- as is a number of the other bridges.

Didn't get to visit at night but its a lovely sight in the day also! We crossed the bride on foot. From the middle there is a nice view of the two neighbouring bridges and city. The traffic wasn't too heavy so we made it a little more exciting by running along the dividing strip, under the dragon structure! I wouldn't cry if i never saw it again, but 'when in Da Nang', why not? :) If you are staying on the coast, you will most likely cross it in the taxi on the way to the airport anyway. Keep a look out.
On the advice of French people living in Da Nang whom we met during our stay, we went on Sunday evening at 9 pm sharp to see this event and the many onlookers around.
Nice to have seen once, but certainly don't expect too much from it. Short but sweet and a few beautiful photos, but don't go to too much trouble to visit it.

We saw the crowds that gathered for this show on the internet so decided to watch from a distance. It was interesting to see the cruise boats all gathering around below the bridge when as the time draws to 9pm. The shows are held Fri-Sun at 9pm.

From my point of view as a European, such attractions are absolutely nothing special, while many tourists find this incredibly great. All right, I can chop this off now. And will certainly never go there again, since unspectacular and somewhat banal. But apparently many think this is great. Insanely many people of course, a cruel crowd and after 15 minutes everyone pushes away again and 10,000 people are looking for a taxi at the same time.... Do not go on the bridge, but for the obligatory photo it is best to stand next to the bridge below, take a snap, go home.....

Everything you’ll read/see about Da Nang will include the Dragon Bridge. It’s a nice spectacle at night as the ‘Dragon’ changes its colour on a continuous loop but the fire breathing ‘spectacle’ (Fri/Sat/Sun) is more Puff the magic dragon than Godzilla…After the fire show my partner looked at one another with a bemused ‘is that it?’. Top tip. Book a table on the balcony of The Riverside Terrace restaurant for one of the best views of the Dragon and some great food.
This is a unique bridge in the shape of a dragon. We were lucky to see the dragon "in fire" during the day as they were testing it.
It was okay.  We did have fun watching it because we had young children with us.  Worth a visit if you’re already in da nang. Kids had more fun riding on the nearby go karts.

Fun to see but not worth explicitly planning around. The show is okay but very short and could have been better integrated with the lighting of the bridge. The rooftop bars around the head of the bridge are not really worth it in my opinion.

Took a boat tour with 7 star cruises. It was ok going up the Han River seeing how beautiful the lights of Da Nang are at light. We stop off at 9pm to watch the lion bridge fire show with the dragon head shooting out flames. Then at the end it sprays out water. So if you were walking over the bridge near the head you would get wet.
There is some fireworks and water show at 9pm from Friday to SundayIts only for about 5-10 minutesnothing to rave aboutThe dragon bridge does look pretty at night

This was one of our main reasons for coming to Da Nang and unfortunately it was very disappointing. The dragon breathes fire, and later water, but there are long gaps in between each breath and we were expecting more to happen. We sat at the side of the river near the bridge to have a drink and arrived about 20 mins before, it did get full for the seats we were at but overall we wouldn’t say it is worth making the trip for.

I enjoyed the 9:00 PM fire show on a Sunday night.  However, it is not that spectacular, so manage your expectations.  We watched from the ground away from the bridge because we did not want to get wet.  We stayed dry, but even from as far away as we were, we could feel the heat from the fire, so that was kinda cool.  He breathed fire a few times, then shot water over everyone on the bridge.  It is a neat thing to see if you are in the area, but I would not go out of your way to see it, or miss out on anything else for it.  For example, I was feeling sick Saturday night when we had planned to go, and I felt bad that we had to go to bed early and miss it.  When we saw it on Sunday I realized I would have been so mad if I had gone sick to see it since it just wasn't that special.
During the day, it is an ordinary bridge.Only on Saturday night, it seems to blow fire from the mouth of a dragon.The air is bad and you can't see the distant view, so it's not worth seeing.

We went to see the fire show and I think we were expecting a spectacular show so we were pretty disappointed. There’s no fancy lights or music. All that happens is the dragon “breathes” fire a few times and sprays water a few times. Cool to see if you’re in the area already but don’t go out of your way to see it.
If you are in the area it is worth jumping in.If you have to do 5 km to get there it is not worth it.The show is on the side of the bridge looking out to sea

Disappointed but not sure really what I was expecting only last 15 minutes but if you have children I'm sure they will love it. The night market is very close so I would combine the two together. So glad we did this and not on a boat watching.
In the evening maybe impressive but nice to see during the day but no must see. Lady Buddha was much more impressive
Spectacular bridge with very heavy traffic. We walked over it several times both day and night. Every Friday, Saturday and Sunday at 9:00 PM the dragon head spits fire. Awesome!
Can definitely give it a miss.Not worth changing, waiting or adjusting any plans to see this. The night market near the bridge was good

This is probably one of the most popular landmarks in the city and visitors to Danang will not miss it, whether day or night as you will surely pass by it somewhere somehow sometime.The long bridge is lit up at night and it changed colours within minutes and looked nice especially from afar. If you come on Friday-Sunday night at 9.00pm, there will be the dragon performance where the dragon mouth will spit fire and water. Quite spectacular at first but after a while it's boring because it's the same repeated action that lasted 15mins.But there will be gathering of big crowds or people standing by the riverside or on top of the bridge and if you wanted to have a good view, you may like to go there early to chope a good place. For us, we stood along the riverside that also offered a good view but we didn't find it anything special, maybe thinking that our own Merlion back in Singapore that spits water is nicer and more iconic.

It looks awesome. The smell of Kerosene, over powers the visual impact. Video looks great. We were not prepared for the water ejection or water spray.  We caught off guard, people kept running to avoid the water droplets. One  girl slipped in the steps of the stairs.

Fun show but please keep your belongings VERY close. We got pickpocketed and that soured the experience. After it happened we filed a report with the police who told us it's a place where a lot of pickpocketing happens. Hope to help someone with this review. Have fun!
Dragon Fire Bridge:
Very short show and over within 15 minutes.Not as spectacular as suggested or as we expected. If you’ve done Disney, you’ll be disappointed.Gets very crowded for the night show at the weekends.
This is a busy bridge for motor vehicles and not for visitors because of the waste from vehicles. It is to be seen at night time from a distance.
It's a bit much of a tourist trap. But a night market is good. A lot of people. The bridge closed at 8:55 p.m. and finished at 9:15 p.m. We love children!!!

The fire and water-spitting Han River Dragon Bridge is one of the things one has to see as a tourist in Da Nang. If you want to watch this spectacle from the convenience of your hotel room, know that the dragon head faces towards My Khe beach and the tail is closer to the Cham Museum. It's good some government officials thought about building this Dragon Bridge because it's become an iconic symbol of Da Nang. It's not an exciting thing to do, but watching it as a tourist, why not?

The best way to watch this ten-minute spectacle is to relax and watch it from the Draft Beer Bar not far from the bridge. There you can eat something in peace and drink a delicious beer. In high season you should reserve a seat in the front row.That way you can enjoy it in a completely relaxed way. The show ends after a few minutes with three rounds of 7 to 9 bursts of fire and two rounds of water bursts at the end. You can also watch this from ships, but you don't have to. Have fun.
Nice show but very short and not as spectacular as we expected. We had changed all our travel schedule to watch and would not have done it if we knew what it was
The fire was spit only for a few seconds. We were all quite shocked. However it’s still quite spectacular as we watched from our hotel room for 2 nights. Was expecting more though. However it’s okay.
It’s nice to see and say Ive seen it, done that  but apart from changing colours it’s not much to write home about to be honest. You can walk along the river but nothing to see.
I was underwhelmed with the 'show'. I'd suggest watching on youtube and deciding if you can be bothered. They really should try and do more with the show with what is a pretty cool structure.
Was expecting more. Dragon only breathes fire for a few mins and the flame is very small. When we were there the bridge did not even change color.

A bridge, like any other. I luckily stumbled upon a fire show that had attracted a large crowd and promised to be a fascinating spectacle, but... nope. Periodically, a stream of fire erupts from its mouth, and that's it.

We were at the night mqrkets, so we thought lets check it out love how the bridge is made to resemble a dragon but the show was missing sound and they could have made use ofthe lights along the dragons tail
At 9pm, there’s a show where it pisses some Fire and Water and … Uh that’s it. I guess, at least the bridge has some design..

it was nice to see you could have it on your list to do , its no  SYDNEY harbor bridge  but ok  the strip next to it is good lots of coffee shops and the night market for food lovers is close by you can do both

The dragon bridge adds life to an otherwise just a communication link. It shows a dragon flying towards the sea. No doubt it is an attraction of Da Nang. With the show of fire and water spraying performance. it is something to witness. The performance takes place at 9 PM on every Saturday, Sunday and holidays.
Is a nice dragon bridge but not really amazing for me. Just past by while in the car.  As there are many great bridge around the world.
This Dragon Bridge spans the Han River, which flows east of the old city. The bridge's railings are designed to resemble a dragon. It's even more dramatic when lit up at night.

I was away for a long time this time, and unfortunately, I didn't get back before the weekend, so I missed the dragon's water show. At night, the bridge is beautifully lit, and the view from the Han River or the bridge area is quite good. The hotel I stayed at was also near the Dragon Bridge, but my room didn't offer a view, so I couldn't see what the night view looked like from afar.
Not sure what the attraction here is.  Better of just looking from afar.  Nothing special really up close that I can remember.

Nice to look at from a distance at night. But just an average bridge that acts as a main thoroughfare through the city. Nothing special when driving over it. But makes a nice backdrop from one of the many roadside restaurants that surround it.

The Dragon Bridge was built between 2009 and 2013 across the Han River. It really is just a gimmick but still fun. In Chinese culture, since the dragon is a symbol of power, strength and good luck, we of course had to visit and take a picture.
It's worth seeing, it's nice but not extraordinary. The dragon changes color, it breathes fire and then water.

This is a nice novelty bridge in a nice area of town along the boardwalk with statues and locals fishing. It is lit up at night and on weekend the dragon breaths fire. It’s nice to look at from the boardwalk.
I saw Dragon Bridge while taking a walk around the city at night, and it was magnificent.
It wasn't as impressive as it looked online, but I decided to walk across the bridge anyway.
Only on weekend at 9pm. Be there early to get a best spot. You can see the dragon breathes fire then water. Bring an umbrella if you gonna stand at the dragon head area.

An ordinary bridge. There is a fire show, but it was just okay. It is not worth changing your route or adjusting your schedule just to see it; it is worth watching if the timing happens to align. It is better to view it as you pass by rather than actually crossing the Dragon Bridge.
Don't expect an extraordinary show!
Dragon with fireworks and water cannon on weekend 9pm. Lots of local and tourist. Please do not wait underneath the dragon mouth if you do not want to get wet
I went last night to watch the fire and smoke show. Nothing amazing and was over in minutes. Worth a look if in area on Saturday and Sunday nights 9pm.

It is a bridge you can easily see while traveling around downtown Da Nang. Even in the evening, you can simply glance at it while passing by in a taxi. It is not a place that is absolutely necessary to visit.
As you can see, it is the Dragon Bridge.

It is a bridge. nothing more and nothing less. We visited this several times during our stay in Da Nang. Illuminated at night and changes colors. During the Chinese New Year, lots of people gather to watch the fireworks and celebrate the new year.
I didn't go there to see it in person, but I saw it while driving past.

Nice sight to see in the city centre. No-fly zone for drones so restricted shots for photos & I found it awkward to take a photo of. It's impressive when lit up at night but during the day it's so-so.
Not a must go but for those who like to take pictures with family, there are lots of spots for photos with the bridge as background. Besides it is a walking  disdance from the Da Nang Cathedral.
From a distance, the bridge looks rather dull and doesn't inspire much desire to go, but I stopped by as it was on my way through the town.
The Dragon Bridge was praised for spitting fire on the 3rd day of Tet (Lunar New Year), but we waited and waited and the dragon only spat fire 3 times :)

The Bridge cool to see if you happen to pass by Da Nang. But don't bother waiting for the so-called show. It's not worth as it only spits a bit of fire and water and it is all over within less than 5 min.
When I walked across the Dragon Bridge during the day to see the Fire Dragon, it was so close that I just thought it was a strange bridge.

The bridge itself is goof... It's a good bridge with dragon decoration... The show was just ok. It was very crowded and really nothing special.  There is a night market next to the bridge (on the city side of the bride) if you are into night markets.

Quick stop for photos on our trip from Hoi An to Hue.  Visited during the day and looked pretty cool (for a bridge), but can imagine that it is quite impressive when lit-up at night.  Worth a look if you are in the area.
The weekend shows are definitely worth seeing, but it was difficult to find a good spot to take photos.
This is one of the bridges crossing the Han River in Da Nang.
We waited with anticipation for the fire and water dragon bridge display to begin and whilst I have never seen anything quite like this before, I was left feeling a little disappointed.

My stay here was very pleasant staff was very helpful food was good staff even bought me an American hamburger the pool was nice and clean Room service was amazing Room was very clean we help doesn't no matter what we needed hotel car to take instead of taxi we had a very nice day

I recommend going when there are fireworks; it's much more spectacular then. When I went, there were no fireworks, only lights. It's a very large bridge, and there are many drink shops and bars nearby, suitable for young people to hang out. You can also take a walk on the bridge; I saw many couples on dates there. The night view is nice, and the atmosphere is different at night, but personally, I wouldn't consider it a must-see attraction.
Unfortunately, I wasn't able to see it spewing flames. When I asked the driver, he said it wasn't a day for it to do so...
The nighttime illumination is said to be a kaleidoscope of colors, but during the day it's just yellow. The undulating shape of the dragon can be clearly seen from the adjacent bridge.
Folkloric and distinctive in its shapes and colors,

There are only fire and water shows on weekends. If you come on weekdays, there are only lights on. There is also the Golden Dragon Night Market next door, so it is more suitable to visit both together.
The bridge looks goods better from far as it gives you a complete view of the bridge, rather then when you are on the bridge. We availed it to visit the main city restaurant, for our dinner.
Really cool bridge. If you are nearby go and check it out. Looks lovely at night too with the lights.

A very great, original design of what would otherwise be a very ordinary bridge.  It made for a nice photo-op, but if you are not in the area or not a bridge enthusiast, it is hardly worth going out of your way to see.  Although we were there during the day, it was clear that at night the area around the base of the bridge would be full of active nightlife.  So, given the opportunity, it would be nice to go in the evening.
Best time to view in the night. Missed to see the bridge over the weekend. During holiday season they should keep the fireworks on weekdays too
The dragon breathes a little fire on weekends around 9 pm. The children are fascinated. It adorns itself with colors at night. The surrounding area is lively.

I heard from many of the locals that during the weekends at 9pm the dragon on the bridge would have fire and steam come out of him, therefore I decided to go see it. I walked from my hotel (monarque), took me about 25min and arrived a bit earlier to check out the night market nearby, which was quite nice. Around 8pm, I decided to find what it seemed to be the best spot to see the whole bridge (at 8pm because I read online you should start guaranteeing a good spot at least an hour earlier). I recommend you not to stay under the dragon nearby the bridge of love as water comes out around 8:30pm, and if you’re under it, then you get a free cold shower (yes, that was what happened to me). Anyway, the show itself is nothing special, there’s nothing too grand about it, so don’t go there expecting too much. For me, I saw it once and I don’t think I would go back as it’s just fire and steam coming out not too often and not too grand. Be prepared though to see a lot of people!
The bridge looks nice but nothing special. We expected more from the show at 9pm on Saturday/Sunday - the dragon spit fire couple of times and that was it - very poor.

The Dragon Bridge is 666 meters in lenght and the longest bridge in Vietnam. The bridge was opened in 2013 and the Golden Dragon spits fire. The dragon Is a symbol of nobility, power and good fortune.

Nice enough bridge at night time when it’s illuminated. We were there on a Saturday night, when at 9 PM a fee futile attempts are made to copy the spectacle of a Rammstein show. But still, pretty awesome that the bridge is a fire-spitting dragon. Points for originality!After recommendations from both hotel and restaurant, we were slightly disappointed with the few fire balls and the few water geisers, which is all one gets in the 5-minute display on Saturday and Sunday.

I was actually disappointed when I passed by the dragon shape in a taxi during the day, thinking it looked a bit ridiculous, but looking down at it from a tall building at night, it was quite spectacular.

Before arriving Da Nang, the Dragon Bridge is the most talked about “attraction”.  Over the four days stay, we travelled on, below and next to it during day and night.  Other than a metal structure that decorates the bridge in the shape of a “dragon”, we cannot quite understand why so many people talking about it. Yes, the lights will change colour and hence making the dragon looks different over time but that’s about it.
If you can go yeah why not but don't have to. The fire doesn't take long, it's busy of course. Good for the kids.

I heard that if you go on the weekend, the dragon head breathes fire and shoots water haha. But I didn't have time, so I just took a taxi and passed over the Dragon Bridge. It looks exactly like the pictures haha.
Best viewed at distance and at night.Changes colour ever 30secs or so.Not a place you can really stay long.
We walked over the bridge and across the promenade to Han River bridge and back on a lovely weekday evening  No fire and water but very pleasing. Both bridges are lovely.
I crossed the Dragon Bridge during the daytime. Walking across it is great exercise!
I think it's prettier to focus on the night market or attractions around the Han River in the evening rather than the Dragon Show
I don’t find the bridge really special or unique that is worth traveling to visit unless you do happen to pass by

You can opt for the cruise scheduled 8.30pm (purchased from Klook with less than USD5) to have the best view of the dragon bridge fire show only at 9pm every Saturday and Sunday night!. Cruise will stop right before the shows start where you can enjoy the view without crowded tourists. More crowded area to enjoy the free show would be around the bridge areas but you may want to go earlier to get a better view.

A 666-meter bridge spans the Han River, shaped like a dragon that changes color at night and even breathes fire on Saturday nights. A Cham museum is located almost at the foot of the bridge on the west bank. A lively night market is situated on the east side. Enjoy a pleasant evening stroll with the lights illuminating the riverbanks and the river itself.
Normally it's just an ordinary bridge

Indeed something new since the same dragon could breathe fire and spits water.  The dragon need intermediate break. This was funny. The crowd is overwhelming on bridge as well as below. Interesting but will not return to watch again.

It’s not that great in the morning, so be sure to go at night. The dragon's color changes at regular intervals. I watched it from a rooftop bar near the Dragon Bridge, and it’s worth seeing. Photos taken just from under the bridge are pretty too.
I heard the road would be closed, so I went an hour early. There was an empty table on the second floor of a nearby restaurant, so I decided to watch from there.

It was down as one of the must-see attractions of Da Nang. It was interesting but there were thousands of people there and it only lasted about 10 minutes - would be great if you had small children and is worth seeing if you are in the area but I wouldn't make a special trip to see it.

As far as bridges go it’s cool. It looks like a dragon, It lights up and changes colour at night and then shoots fire and water out of the head end. We saw it at 9pm on a Saturday but not sure what other times the fire and water show happens. They stop traffic going over the bridge a little while before the show and there were a lot of people gathered to watch at the head end. The show lasted about 10mins.
We sew the bridge when we where flying into Da Nang  it looked fantastic you can also walk over the it. Love the way it lights up a big attraction.
It's neither better nor worse than you'd imagine; it's exactly like the photos. Discerning Japanese people will probably only think, "They really tried hard to create a tourist attraction."
not worth a special trip to Da nang but if you're there then it's worth a look. On saturdays it spits fire at 9pm - we didn't see it but heard it's not bad. the view from the bridge is also pretty

The bridge is something different and looks especially amazing at night. When we were there, we were able to watch "the show" in the evening. The dragon breathes fire three or four times, followed by some water. The spectacle was a bit underwhelming. It could have been enhanced with music or light effects, especially since several hundred people had gathered to watch.
It only takes place on weekends. Da Nang, Vietnam, doesn't have many other tourist attractions, so it's recommended for a meal.
I was watching from too close... and
It was only as i was riding around that i came across the bridge.   I saw it during the day but I'm impressed with the architecture.  Im sure the lights at night would be a great view

If you have low expectations you’ll enjoy this bridge at night on the weekend. We arrived at 8.58pm, there were people everywhere, crammed onto the bridge and covering every square foot of the ground below. Two minutes later there was fire coming out of the bridge and lots of oooing and ahhhing from the crowd. His carried on for about five minutes and followed by a lot of water coming out the dragon/bridge. Great fun if you’re in the area but not really worth a specific trip to see it
If you're in the area at night, go see it. And if you catch it when the fire starts, even better! I didn't get to see it!
The dragon fire-breathing show that takes place at 9am on Saturdays and Sundays.

We overlooked the bridge from our hotel room so escaped getting soaked and covered in fumes from the Dragon.More fun watching the milling crowds on and around the bridge and how quickly it was all over and the area cleared of so many people.
This is my second visit to the Dragon Bridge. It's quite impressive up close. I

You have to choose whether to go for photos in the evening before the dragon sprays water, or at night when the dragon is spraying water (which it doesn't do continuously, just for a short time). There are a lot of people taking pictures, and lots of tour groups. There are two photo spots: one on the side of the road where you can see the entire dragon bridge, and another next to it is the Merlion statue spraying water, similar to Singapore's.

This is one of several bridges that connect the beach and the old town of Da Nang. Since my hotel was right on the beach, I crossed this bridge by taxi every time I went back and forth to the old town. It's a large bridge with a golden dragon winding across it. The dragon's head is on the beach side and its tail is on the old town side. The golden dragon appears to be constructed of metal, and you can see its structure when you get close. I heard that on weekends, flames shoot out of the dragon's mouth, creating the spectacle of the dragon breathing fire, but that didn't fit into my travel plans this time. I thought I'd try to go on a weekend next time.

We had a tour that included seeing the bridge at night. We were on a boat quite close to the bridge when the bridge came alive. There was fire and water that came out of the dragons mouth. It was nice to see. A once off visit.
I went to the night market and then waited to see the Dragon Bridge show.

Apparently, it breathes fire on Saturday and Sunday nights, so unfortunately I couldn't see it since it was a weekday, but the surrounding illuminations were beautiful. If you view it from the main area where Da Nang Cathedral and the market are located, it's on the tail side, so it might be better to view it from the other side.

I heard that the Dragon Bridge only flies sparks on weekends, so I went to see it. I searched for Han Bridge and Dragon Bridge, but ended up at the next bridge, so I walked for about 20 minutes from there. It was the middle of summer, so it was incredibly hot and I almost gave up, but I couldn't find a taxi. The sparks weren't that impressive. On the way back, it was extremely crowded and I had a hard time catching a taxi. I feel like I've seen it once and that's enough.
It is an iconic spot in Da Nang;
2 photos are more than enough, we had the day in Da Nang and we passed through there, but if you give up you don't miss anything

We were there on Saturday, mainly lots reviews recommend it. Mm..I would say good to have such a nice bridge. With regards to LED lightings..not bad. As for fire and water, I could see it from a distance. Unexpected crowd, too many of their locals. Please do not set high expectations about the 9pm show.We took a cab there, and there was a traffic jam which we expected it. We  walked slowly around 5 mins to the Bridge because it was very crowded. We walked along the Bridge after that, lots nice photo spots. Not too hot at night, good to stroll.
The bridge in itself was awesome to see but the "show" wasn't much of a show. My partner and I have been to much better, we were expecting spectacular things and left feeling disappointed
Dragon bridge is not a bad looking bridge but that’s where it stops. As for the dragon spitting fire what a waist of time.Would rate as one off the worst shows of fire I have seen.
The bridge is lit up every night, and a fire show takes place on weekends starting at 9 PM.
The brdge is crossing the Han river. The fire will start around 9pm. There are crowded people around there. After the show, massive traffic jam at the nearly roundabout, walking recommended

Just another bridge. The Dragon design is ok. The Dragon spitting Fire was too short. Me n my ftiends waited for almost an hour for a few puffs of fire and smoke. Overall a must see if you are there but not a reason to go Da Nang.
There wasn't really anything worth seeing. It didn't catch my eye during the day, and it was just so-so in the evening. It wasn't a view worth going out of your way to see in the evening.

like the hundreds of other tourist and locals we made our way to the head end of the Dragon bridge  to watch thew big weekly Saturday night 9pm show. what can i say, it was almost laughable, not very impressive for sure but worth a laugh while having a beer and then you can wonder through the local market right there which is a bit of fun .if you are there, great , enjoy just dont expect too much but i wouldnt drive from far away 'just to see it'. you can do better elsewhere

The Dragon Bridge fire and water show is held on weekends at 9:15 PM. You should go early to avoid traffic, around 8 PM is best for a good viewing spot. It's difficult to get there, but the show is truly disappointing. The dragon's head spews fire accompanied by thick black smoke, which looks very polluting. The performance is very slow and lacks sound, making it uninteresting. There are periods of silence between the fire and water displays, causing interruptions.
The Dragon Bridge looks pretty cool and makes a nice photo opportunity. That's about it, though. It's just a bridge with an extravagant design.
The Dragon Bridge will喷 fire and water at 9 PM on Saturdays and Sundays

The view of Longqiao Bridge is better at night than during the day, but there's not much to do on the bridge itself. Therefore, it's not recommended to make a special trip there; just take a bus past it and see it from a distance.

I timed my visit for a weekend evening to see the Dragon Bridge fire show, but the head of the bridge was already packed with people... Fire and water came out of the dragon's mouth three times... and that was it... It was different from the fire shows I've seen in Korea, so I was somewhat disappointed.
You shouldn't expect night views or scenery like those in Korea.

The dragon bridge seems to be the place to be on a Saturday night at 9pm. There were incredible crowds there, with vendors selling novelties, drinks and food. The fire went for a couple of minutes and there is a water sprayed after the fire. Found it interesting that there were so many locals there for something that happens every week.
I went there during a break from a business trip.

If you go on the weekend the dragon blows fire and water at 9pm just for a few minutes but ridiculously busy. If you are taking a taxi home walk away from all the people for a good Km as traffic is not moving. I would prefer to go on a week day evening without the fire water and people.Sit by the river with a drink and relax
Ranks highly as thing to do in Da Nang-huge crowd of people waiting to see the dragon breathe fire. Ok but nothing overwhelming.

The area is beautifully lit up at night, but I think it's best to see this bridge only as an afterthought while you're in the area for something else, rather than going there solely to see it. Also, the river over which this bridge is located smells terrible.

At night, the bridge is illuminated, and the lights change into seven different colors. Both banks of this bridge are also places of relaxation for the citizens, and you can feel the power of Da Nang, which will continue to develop and become even more vibrant in the future.
Quite a special spot to be honest. Worth a look. Kids enjoyed it. Nothing much to see if you are driving over it, or during the day!
My accommodation was nearby, so I ended up seeing it while going back and forth. Up close, I can't really tell if it's a dragon or what. It looks much more impressive when viewed from a car.

Perhaps because it happened to be the day of the International Fireworks Festival, the fireworks didn't burst out even though it was the weekend. The illuminations were beautiful, but the bridge itself was just average. The night view of the entire Han River was beautiful!

Don't get me wrong, the bridge itself it a sight to behold, but we heard so much about the weekend light show that we made time out of our holiday schedule to attend it. My wife and older daughter stayed on the bridge to get close up videos while I was on the riverbank with my younger girl to snap pics and videos from a distance.So 9pm comes by and the dragon starts belching fire which was pretty cool to see. Can imagine that it was super hot on the bridge as I could feel the heat almost 500m away.. Anyway, after 3 fire belches, I expected an actual light show with lasers or something, but what followed was water.. The dragon started spewing water.... And it drenched everyone on the bridge that was near the mouth. No one on the bridge was expecting it and there was pandemonium and almost a human stampede.. Authorities only cordoned off traffic, but didn't do much else. It was pretty bad management imo.. All that said, go visit the dragon bridge at night and get some really nice snaps of it, but you can probably give the 10min "light & water show" a miss.Where: Dragon BridgeWhen: 9pm, Sat and Sun

Vietnam is intersected by many rivers which has made the Vietnamese engineers, specialists of bridges some of which has become special attractions for the tourists. Da nang is one of the most popular city among the visitors with its amazing seashore, flashy line of hotels along the coast and Ba Na hill attractions.The Dragon Bridge (Vietnamese: Cầu Rồng) is a bridge over the River Hàn at Da Nang, Vietnam. It is one of the night time attractions of Da Nang city as the bridge is intervened with a gigantic dragon from one end to another. It looks way more attractive in the night as the dragon continuously changes its color. The lighting is magnificent and the dragon emits fire and water time to time. There are probably light and sound shows on Saturday and Sunday evening. But there is no need to book a tour of the bridge unless you want to enjoy a river cruise. Just take a taxi from your hotel and you can easily spend some nice time by the side of the river enjoying the nice breeze and the colorful bridge. Along the side of the river there are nice siting places. There are also night markets on the both sides of the river with multi cuisine food places. It really is a fine place for a night stroll.
Perhaps because I live in a city of bridges, I didn’t like this structure at all; I think I wasted my time visiting it.
Da Nang Dragon Bridge.
It’s a really funky bridge don’t get me wrong but that’s it, you literally don’t have anything else to do but see the bridge.
As a standalone, there is not much to it. But with the Love Locks Bridge behind it and Marina, etc, worth to spend 15 minutes there for a few photos.
Nice bridge in the shape of a dragon. Not too much during the day. In the evening, a fun fire display and all kinds of activities around.

The Dragon Bridge fire show itself was a bit underwhelming compared to what I expected... so I wouldn't recommend taking the cruise just to see the show. It's hot, so I'm fine taking it to enjoy the night view, though. You can pretty much skip the fire show if you want.

Can consider visit this Dragon Bridge at night if you want to take a stroll after a full dinner nearby. The fire breathing and water spitting session starts by 9pm on every Saturday and Sunday. So, prepared for a large crowds by the then as both tourist and locals will be gathering around that area.
We didn't notice anything remarkable... maybe because it was daytime?! The neighboring Golden Bridge in Banahills is a completely different story))
Lovely views.  But a bit overrated. No need to sit in a restaurant to view the dragon breathing fire.

The bridge is impressive during the day. As we were only in Da Nang for a couple of nights, we were not lucky enough to see the dragon breath its flames. That only occurs on the weekend so we are told
If you are in the location yes its a great sight and an interesting walk over to the other side.But its only a bridge,

This is the yellow bridge that spans the city. It's crowded with tourists even at night. When I visited, it was a weekend, but unfortunately, the fire was being suppressed in obscurity for the president's death.
We had a hotel 2 minutes walk from the bridge so going there was easy.  If you are close by, the bridge is worth a photo or two!

The bridge is beautiful, but that's all. It's not worth a special trip. We were expecting a show at 9 p.m. on Saturday. We waited 15 minutes and left without seeing it. The embankment wasn't impressive either.
A must-visit spot if you come to the Han River in Da Nang!
The Dragon Bridge in Da Nang features daily performances at 9 PM. The bridge is beautifully illuminated at night. Nearby, there's a night market with food stalls and shopping opportunities.
A nice view for photo taking. There is fire show on weekends, not worth to see, just 3-5 mins. I won’t recommend to watch the show, but it’s ok if you just go to have dinner in the restaurant nearby.

This is a bridge which is decorated to resemble a dragon. The dragon is painted in golden yellow, as in the tradition color of a Chinese dragon. Nice are for a quick photograph to fill up your Facebook, Instagram...etc

Legend says that a dragon will spit fire at 2100pm on every Saturday and Sunday, on a bridge in DaNang, Vietnam ....Well, I missed it on this Saturday because of other major event in the country. Waited for a good 30 minutes, but the disappointment could be avoided if the city authorities announced it earlier. Hundreds, of not thousands were disappointed tonight.But it show that a flame throwing bridge can be attractive, and there are plenty of small shops selling stuff and food along the wait ..
Rather than making a special trip to see it, you're more likely to stumble upon it or pass by it while sightseeing.
Hmm... the lights changed in various ways, so it was pretty just to view from the hotel rooftop.

It is quite a pleasant walk along the pathway leading to the dragon bridge. The light changes and was quite a pretty sight. I only spend 15-20minutes there taking photos.  It can be quite busy with motorcycles in the daylight.
There really isn't much to do in Da Nang,
Dragon Bridge is best viewed from a distance.
Is this a place you have to walk across?
Very quick show, not too spectacular, great to watch from other side of river at Bamboo2 barEntire show lasts about 7 minutes but only shoots about a dozen fireballs
Apparently, it's lit up at night, but it was far from the hotel, and I heard it gets extremely crowded on weekends due to events, so I didn't go.

We were lucky enough to be in Da Nang on the weekend at 9pm when the dragon show is on and the dragon's head spits balls of fire into the air. We took a boat cruise to watch and the river is definitely the best area to be to see the show. When the dragon breathes out smoke it is actually water spraying so be prepared to get wet if you are on the bridge under the head. Definitely something different to see. Only lasts about 5 minutes but worth making the time to be there. Boat cruise was just over an hour.

We went there just for the 9pm fire and water spit but it's a disappointed. It's not even a show or anything but just the dragon spit and water and fire and it's small and just few minutes. No need to go on the bridge and just wait on riverside is good enough. Better if getting dinner around there and wait til then than just going there to see what would happen.
The bridge you always pass when going between My Khe Beach and Han Market
Make sure to go on a Saturday or Sunday to see if he show. We didn’t realise and were a little disappointed to miss it

I went to see the dragons that were supposed to breathe fire at 9 PM on the weekend. They only breathed fire for a few minutes, and they also spit out water at the end. I was watching from a distance, so I didn't feel any sense of awe at all. There were so many tourists, and I doubt it's worth going to see them while being pushed and shoved. I do think the bridge's design is interesting, though.

Every Saturday evening at 9 pm, the dragon breathes fire (and water). We arrived early for the spectacle. There's good parking right in front of the Da Nang Riverside Hotel. 10,000 yen. We had a good view from Time Coffee, which has a rooftop terrace. You order at the counter downstairs, and then it's delivered to your table.
The area around the Dragon Bridge is one of Da Nang's symbols, and beyond the bridge, there's an area where you can enjoy browsing stylish cafes.
The bridge itself is designed like a dragon dancing. Daytime looks a bit ordinary. But at night with lighting on, the dragon looks vivid and eye catching
I was surprised that it breathed fire, but it wasn't as impressive as I had expected. What surprised me more was the sheer number of people who had come to see it.
The Dragon Bridge is visible when passing by, whether during the day or at night.
I watched it from my hotel room. I was 1km away, but I could see it spewing fire. It was interesting, but personally, I don't think it's worth going all the way up close. It's over pretty quickly.

Night time the dragon beach will have fire show. Think many places nearby it can view it clearly too since the bridge are big enough. But we bought the package with boat ride which the boat send us near to the bridge and watch the show. On the return trip the boat sail along the river have some relax time for us to view the city with different angle.

We've driven across the bridge many times, both during the day and in the evening. It's nice, but nothing special. We thought we'd be wowed by the show, but we were disappointed. Crowds of tourists line the embankment, the show lasts a mere five minutes and consists of the dragon periodically spewing fire. It

During the day, it looks like just an ordinary bridge with a dragon's head on it, nothing special, but at night the lights change color one after another and it's quite beautiful. However, it's not worth making a special trip to see; just driving past it is enough.
On Saturday, Sunday, and public holidays at 9 PM, the dragon 🐉🐲 breathes fire from its mouth, then water!
We visited Da Nang during the week and were unable to see the fire-breathing dragon show at 9 pm on Saturday and Sunday evenings.

We decided to go see the weekend show where a dragon supposedly breathes fire from its mouth. When it was time for the show, we waited, and lo and behold, the dragon started breathing fire from the mouth on the opposite bank! I waited with my camera, expecting them to take turns, but it only breathed fire from the opposite bank twice, and all I could see was black smoke.
Yes, it's something you have to see. Not incredibly beautiful, nothing you haven't seen before, but somehow quite nice.
Every Saturday and Sunday at 9 PM, there's a dragon light show and a fire-breathing and water-spitting dragon performance.

it's nice to see in the night time when it is dark it looks quite impressive in the day time. it just looks like metal pipes. but beware crossing the roads they don't care and they won't stop it is quite scary
A bridge is a bridge, but the design is noteworthy. Beautifully illuminated in the evening. Nice to see. I didn't consider it a must-see.

It’s not really worth making a special trip for; you’ll see it constantly as you walk around downtown. Since most people eat and hang out near Bach Dang Street, you’ll see it every time you pass by, so it’s not really worth going out of your way to see. However, if you want to see it properly, I recommend booking tickets at the front of the Novotel Hotel to time your visit for the fire show at 9 PM on Saturday and Sunday evenings and taking a cruise.
It’s not really a place you need to go. It’s just crowded. I don’t understand why structures like this are so popular. I do like the bridge design, though. It’s just that kind of place.
It looks good from afar with changing colours. Will spit fire at certain timings in the evening.Pointless to go to actual bridge, because it looks really ugly at close proximity.
This famous dragon-shaped bridge is very unique and is photographed by everyone for its unique beauty.

The Dragon bridge is a nice thing to see in Da Nang. With its unique style that really makes you feel that you’re in Asia. Besides the fact that we drove through the bridge several times on our way to the hotel, we also went there on Sunday evening when the dragon breathes fire and spits water on a show that is nice to see. But you have to take into account that the promenade around the bridge is packed with viewers at that time so you better find a nice terrace with a view over the bridge and stay there until everybody leaves. After the show it is really impossible to leave (by car, scooter or even walking). But the nicely illuminated city and bridge are worth the wait.
It's a bridge. All be it an imaginitive design.  Look out for the sea life murals at the head side of the bridge.

Wonderful experience in Da Nang taking photos along with thousands of other tourists and locals.  A few food vendors along the promenade selling local snacks.  And some walking around selling balloons for the kids.  You can also see the fake 'Merlion' at the DHC Marina. Good for some photos.  The Dragon spits fire a couple of times and then sprays water out from its mouth.  Tip:. Do not stand directly near / in front of the dragon head, you will be soak!  Worth to experience once but get ready for a warm sticky evening and huge crowds dispersing to all directions once the 10 mins show is over.

Took a day trip to the Ba Ha Hills and made a stop here on the way back, it was alright, just a bridge with a gold dragon, heard the light show on Sat and Sun night is meant to be good but very busy. Sadly didn't have time for this
The dragon bridge is active with a fire and water show on the weekends. It’s a bridge that is well lit and it’s decor in the shape of a dragon.

This is my second time visiting Da Nang. I didn't come here the first time because I brought my parents this time. I didn't feel like there were many people in Da Nang, but here it was ridiculously crowded. I guess all the tourists come here to visit!

We drove over this bridge several times and we also walked over it one evening. Beautiful view from the bridge. We waited one evening for the dragon to spray fire and water. It wasn't that special. It takes a few minutes and can be nice to see if you have the means to get there, but if it's a long detour you can skip the spectacle.

This is an iconic bridge in Da Nang. It's a busy bridge with heavy traffic. While the bridge itself is shaped like a dragon, it's not particularly impressive, but it's beautifully lit up at night. It's easily visible from the Sky36 bar.

We saw how the bridge changed color etc. It wasn't anything particularly remarkable. Lots of people at the bridge abutments. There were very nice flowers there and everyone naturally wanted to take a picture with a beautiful background. However, we will go there on a Saturday/Sunday at 9:00 PM because then the dragon head will spew fire and a little smoke.

There really aren't any iconic structural buildings etc. In Da Bang city to shout about.However, the yellow-painted Dragon Bridge that spans across the Han River is quite striking.There are other bridges as well, but this one is worth taking a shot of.At the tail end of the dragon, you can get to the beaches along the coast. At the head of the dragon is where the buzz is...cafe, restaurants and a local covered market.Enjoy!
We passed by by this bridge in night. Very nice to just enjoy looking at the shape and take some cool pictures as the bridge is lighted up in the night.
It’s a bridge. Looks like a dragon. Period. The Trip Advisor system insists that I write more, but there is nothing more to be said. It’s a dragon shaped bridge.
After viewing the bridge from a distance, I crossed it.
It's just a regular bridge. We were there on Monday, so there was no show, and the fire wasn't set off.
I've seen it dozens of times while passing by, but it's just so-so.
I crossed the bridge when it was decorated with lights, but if possible, I would have loved to cross it when it was spewing flames! ✨ Apparently, it

First passed by the bridge on route to the hotel and returned at dusk to see the light up. A nice location next to the night market. Suggestion : to come after 7pm to see the lighting as well as the night market. Do not go too early.
I actually prefer the Han River bridge further forward than the Dragon Bridge! They have a fire show on weekends, so
It not the most interesting thing I had never seen ;-)But at night the is nice if you're not far from this bridge.
This bridge connects the old town to the beachside. It's decorated with dragon motifs and is therefore called the Dragon Bridge. It's beautifully lit up at night.

The fire and water show at Longtou Bridge is only performed on Saturdays and Sundays, and I found it quite ordinary. The daytime performances at Longtou Bridge are even more unremarkable. Missing this attraction wouldn't be a big loss; instead, sitting in a coffee shop on either side of the river and enjoying the night view is much more pleasant.
The best time to see this bridge is at night, when it's illuminated.

Haha, it was a bit cheesy for us but we still enjoyed the Saturday evening show. Place gets crowded so try to come a bit earlier. The show lasts for around 8-10 minutes. In the cooler months, it is a lovely evening walk around the area.
It is a structure that stands out when you come out into downtown Da Nang.

It is just a bridge with a dragon on, it's cool to look at for a few minutes but not spectacular. It is lit at night but I found the colours not bright enough to really stand out from the rest of the waterfront.
Drove over it by day, drove past it by night, something different to see. The night view is better due to the lighting.

This bridge is advertised online as a major Da Nang attraction, practically a must-see in the evenings. We regularly saw it in the evenings from our room on the 30th floor of the Muong Than Luxery Da Nang Hotel and often drove across it, so it's safe to say the hype is exaggerated. It's not worth rushing to see. The idea of ​​decorating the bridge with welded metalwork symbolizing a dragon and the various illuminations at night may be a bit original. But to claim that this is a major attraction that tourists will regret not visiting is TOO MUCH!

The whole of danang has many amazing bridges to connect the the city over their waterways.dragon bridge quite pretty especially at night.lovely carnival atmosphere around the bridge with little food stalls and vendors. Woudnt go out of my way to see this.but if you in the area

I crossed the bridge several times by taxi, but the rainy weather prevented me from stopping and enjoying the view properly. Apparently, there's a fire-breathing show on weekends. I think the nighttime illuminations are beautiful.

Visited Dragon bridge on a Sunday evening as recommended by our hotel. On weekends they have a firework display at 9pm.Nice atmosphere but the fireworks was a bit disappointing. Basically a blast of flames from the Dragon's mouth followed by a jet stream of water. Not truly a firework display. Lots of restaurants in the area and some live street music. Not a must see..

It is not strictly necessary to visit, and you can see it at least once while passing through downtown Da Nang. You should probably view it simply for its symbolic significance. According to a friend living in Da Nang, the scale of the water and fire show is rather lacking, consisting only of water and a little fire. It is best not to have high expectations.

The dragon bridge is neat, but once you've seen it, you've seen it.    It's really no different than in pictures than it is being there in person.   I wouldn't exactly call it an architectural wonder, but it is cool.
It's just a bridge, so it seems suitable for a quick look while passing by. I don't think there is any need to include this bridge as the main purpose of your travel itinerary.
If you choose a hotel along the beach, you can see it every time you take a taxi around town.

The design is fantastic. I saw so many great photos before the trip that I went looking forward to the Dragon Bridge water and fire show. However, the show was much weaker than I expected, so I was a bit disappointed. But everything else about Da Nang was wonderful, so I love Da Nang! I will go again!
This is a bridge I crossed many times during my stay in Da Nang. Its basic color is yellow, but it was lit up in several colors. It has several arches, and at the end are its head and tail.
