If you're in Da Nang at night, it's cool to visit. The lights change color and it's pretty, but nothing special.
It was a frequently used bridge.
Saturdays and Sundays at 9 pm. Please be on time, it only lasts 5 minutes. Beautiful colors.
Aside from the events on weekend nights, it's just a regular bridge. I walked across it after getting out of a taxi during the day, and it seemed like a very ordinary bridge during the day.
Nothing much to write about. The fire show on weekends is few minutes long, nothing awe inspiring. Can cause a stampede especially for those on bridge itself.

A bridge shaped like a dragon! Its charm is even greater from a distance, especially at night. You will see it frequently while walking around downtown Da Nang, but it doesn't evoke much emotion during the day. Whether you are on a package tour or traveling independently, you are bound to come across it as part of your itinerary!

Unfortunately, my trip didn't coincide with a weekend, so I was determined to at least catch a glimpse of it. However, since its head faces the sea and its tail faces the town, all I could see from the windows of the bus or taxi while traveling was its tail.

It's more spectacular at night than during the day, but waiting for it to shoot fire a few times isn't really worth it. I suggest canceling this trip if it's not on your route, as it's quite ordinary.
Better to see at night when it’s all lite up and on Saturday and Sunday nights at 2100 local time the dragons mouth blows out fire.
Luckily, our trip coincided with a weekend, so we were able to see the fire and water spouting scene at the top of the Dragon Bridge at night. The area
I went to see it at night while taking a drive. It was

The bridge is beautifully made, nicely made! On weekends (Sat-Sun) at 9 pm, there is a 5-10 minute "show" in which the dragon's head spits fire and water. It's funny to watch, but don't expect too much from it.
Yes, those who like it will find it beautiful. The Asians certainly found it very exciting when the bridge spewed fire and water.
Hi Everyone. If you are at Dining Saturday or Sunday evenings at 2100hrs go to Dragon Bridge. Everyone is there for a few minutes to see the dragon.
It was alright at night when the lighting came on, but nothing special in the day. Would rather go spend time somewhere else.

Every Saturday, and I believe also Sunday, the dragon on the Dragon Bridge spits fire and water at 9:00 PM. It isn't very spectacular, but it is funny and definitely worth watching. If you arrive on time, you can grab something to eat and drink on a nearby terrace with a reasonably good view while you wait. Tip: If you decide to stand on the road at the top of the bridge, bring an umbrella, because when the dragon spits water, you will get soaking wet.
The locals and tourists anticipated for the Saturday night "Fire & Water" show from the Dragon, however lasted only less than 5 minutes.
To get a good spot on the riverbank, you definitely need to arrive about 60 to 90 minutes beforehand, as all the surrounding bars and restaurants are packed.

The Dragon Bridge is definitely cool to see at least once — especially in the evening when it’s lit up and during the fire and water show, which happens on Fridays, Saturdays, and Sundays at 9:00 PM. It’s an impressive landmark and very photogenic.That said, be careful around the water, it sprays out unpredictably, and it’s hard to tell where it’s coming from. I wouldn’t stand too close if you don’t want to get soaked or surprised by a sudden spray.Overall, it’s worth stopping by if you’re in Da Nang.

The dragon-shaped bridge in central Da Nang is open for a fire show at 9 p.m. only on weekends. I was encouraged by the hotel staff to go on a pleasure boat and watch it. When flames and water came out of the dragon's mouth, the ship also stopped in a position that passengers could see clearly. It was nice to see the fantastic night view!

Its a little cheesey but its fun. On the side of the bridge where the head is there is a night market, and there are bars with rooftop viewing. Stools are set up in the parking lots and sidewalks, yes there is a minor cost in post places, but they are serving beer and food to make your wait a little more comfortable. You can find plenty of places to stand without paying anything if you are so inclined. There is standing up on the bridges sidewalk, but then the water starts to get sprayed many were trying to take cover, so keep that in mind.Is it a must see? No, but its quirky, low cost fun for an hour or two, if you do the night market, because the show itself is probably 15 minutes if even that.

If you’re in Da Nang when the fire/ water show is on, you’ve probably got to go. Just temper your expectation’s. It goes for about 10 minutes and they thankfully close the bridge to everything other than pedestrians for the duration. Pro tip - unless you want to get drenched with water, don’t stand under and within about 50m of the head
Best to connect in the evening with the night market, makes a nice impression and is something else. But we haven't seen the fire spitting

One of the most overrated and underwhelming tourist attraction in the world. On Friday, Saturday and Sunday at exactly 2100 = 9p the Dragon Bridge will shoot fire and water for about 10 minutes. Locals and tourists alike lined up the street and trying to get a spot. I suggest to get there early to get a great spot. Also expect a chaotic scene right after when everyone trying to leave the area.

Watched the fire and water show recently on a Saturday evening. Very busy , ' show ' lasts about 15 minutes but is quite spectacular. The river looks lovely all lit up. You can also book a river cruise and observe the 'show' from the water.

On Friday night, I looked at the bridge from the promenade along the Han River.Lights emanating from the Dragon Bridge and passing pleasure boats lit up the river, creating a fantastic and beautiful scene.The weekend night "Dragon Fire Show" draws so many people that it might be a good idea to look at it from a distance.

Username: From budget hotels to luxury hotels
Comment: Last time I only saw it from the hotel, so this time I walked across. It faces the sea from the city. You've got it all wrong. Look at the tail? I thought so.

If you happen to be in Danang on the weekend, this is worth a stop. You need to get there early to secure a good view but luckily there are many places on the bridge, adjacent to it, below the bridge on the riverfront boardwalk, and nearby restaurants to see the short show. There are tour buses that will bring lots of tourists around 8:30/8:45 and the fire breathing starts around 9pm (only on weekend nights.) It is quite short, maybe 10 to 15 minutes. When we saw it, the dragon let out six fire bursts then a few water shoots. It's true that you will get soaked if you are anywhere near the dragon's mouth on the bridge. Lots of Grabs waiting on the main street after the show - pick a good (and easy) pick up location!
Eaten near the dragon bridge then show to see on Saturday or Sunday...fire and water top tourist bridge restaurant la cabana..

It is one of the bridges of the Han River that crosses the old town of Danang and the coastal area. It has become a symbol of the city since its completion in 2013. As the name suggests, the dragon is located in the middle of the two-way line, with the tail to the west and the head to the east. There is a special illumination event that turns into multiple colors at night, but it's hard not to see the head-side show on the weekend night.

The fire display was interesting for a few minutes but is it worth a special trip! Hmmm. Probably not.Start your night at Son Tra night market then grab a spot where you can see the dragon’s head. Once is enough.

Unfortunately, we only saw the bridge during the day. It's quite interesting there, but you don't have to see it. But it must be great to look at when it gets dark and it is illuminated and the dragon fire spits.
Very cool bridge within the city, we went on a weekday though so we couldn’t see it breathe fire. If it works for your trip try visit Da Nang on a weekend instead.
Construction of the Dragon Bridge
Fire & water show - fun experience next to night marketWell worth visiting easy viewing from riverside. Dance show before display

We went there on a weekday as part of our city tour. Amidst the sweltering heat we just got photos. It is said to be most appreciated during weekend on a nighttime. Anyway , till next time Dragon bridge.

We took the option of jumping on a boat, very cheap around $8 Aussie $$’s. The actual light show itself was,well, quite and uninspiring the was also a shower of water from the dragons mouth to finish it off.The cruise gave you a better sense of the buildings all lit up so that’s a big bonus. Beer was cheap around $2 and you can also take up a banquet option! There’s heaps to choose from so just google your preferences. Overall a good night, but don’t think the dragon breathing fire will steal the show.

It is a bridge in the center of Da Nang with a huge dragon-shaped structure that blows fire and water only once a day on Saturdays and Sundays. When it's time to breathe fire, the bridge is closed to traffic and overflows with people. If you go too close, you will get soaking wet.

A bridge named as Dragon bridge because the lighting is made as dragon shape. Its a bridge connects the city on the other side. Huge one and crowded one in the evenings. Quite breezy and cool place to spend time with family and friends in the evenings.

Enjoyable night along the river Got to see dragon breath fire & shoot water The kids that were there at the time we were there seemed to really enjoy it There was so many tourist so be prepared to pushed a little All up a good experience but I don’t need to do it again
It was full on busyBe careful not to go on road too close as we watched many people get soaked Very difficult to get grab after

It's a nice bridge, the show starts at 9 p.m. on weekends. We were standing on the bridge but behind our heads, so we weren't sprayed with water. From the bridge you can enjoy the beautiful views of the water and the ships that brought tourists to the show, they glow very beautifully.  The show is short, first fire comes out of your head, then water jets. Those down on the waterfront were all wet at the end of the show. After the show, you can come down from the bridge and go left to the night market. There you can eat delicious and varied seafood for cheap.

We only saw this bridge in passing as we travelled over it in our tour coach.  Difficult to take a photo while moving but from what we saw it looked rather interesting.  It would be great to see the dragon in action.

On our Vietnam tour, we specially stopped in NANG, to see up close this spectacular construction that crosses the Han River and serves for a fast connection from Da Nang International Airport to the main places of the City.

Love the dragon bridge at night with the light.The show at 9pm fri-sun is very short. We thought it would be more of a light show in 15 min. Here they can certainly improve. The night market is next to the bridge and a tour/dinner at the market before is to recommend.If you like to get wet you can stand on the bridge next to the dragon head.
Great area to take pictures. In addition to the Dragon Bridge, there is a dragon fountain, a green area, and some very cute shops, and (of course) lots of coffee shops.
To clarify-the show DOES occur on Friday nights at 9 pm.  Saw it last night, 2/14/2025.  Saw in various places that it was Sat and Sunday.
The bridge, which looks like a dragon, is said to have been built according to the recommendation of a Hong Kong watcher and is priceless to protect the port from typhoons.

The bridge represents the dragon from head to tail over the Khan River, so you can see it from a distance. We perched the bus in the square below and went near the bridge to get a glimpse of its size. I didn't get to see the dragon show because it was in daylight, but I'd like to see it at night once.

We could see the whole of the Dragon Bridge both from the Roof top bar at the Haian Riverfront Hotel as well as from our Room there . Which was great as on Friday Saturday & Sunday nights the Dragon Breaths Fire & Water for 10 Minutes at 9 Pm . The Bridge is illuminated at Dusk , changing colours from Gold , yellow , Red Blue Purple & Green . Fire & Water show attracts huge crowds each night who gather in the Parking area Beside the Head end of the Bridge .The Bridge's Construction began in July 2009 and was opened in March 2013 . The Bridge is a 6 lane Road & Pedestrian Bridge , with the Dragon up the Centre . The Dragons head is closest to the Sea end of the Bridge . The Bridge is 666Mt long and 37.5 Mt Wide and cost 1.5 Trillion Dong (US$ 88 Million) to build

One of the major and probably most known bridges of Da Nang. While the bridge itself is rather orthodox, the stylized dragon in the middle of the bridge looks incredible. A perfect symbiosis of art and functionality.
Long interesting bridge Looks especially well at night Simple design but looks good Easy to walk across In city centre Have a look
I visited in mid-2024.It's a good time to get together with a bucket of love. What season is 9 p.m. on the weekend? There's a fireworks festival.But it's just a leg.
Great beautiful bridge very characteristic for its dragon shape, it is a recent construction crossing the Han River

Visited Dragon Bridge last night for the show. Great atmosphere and very chilled and relaxed with everyone behaving in a friendly manner. Unlike some venues in Australia where some people are drunk and obnoxious. The show started at 9 pm and went on for 10 - 15 minutes. Plenty of restaurants and cafes around with a night market only 50 meters away.  It was a nice and relaxing time. Great opportunities for photos of the bridge and also the boats on the river with their lights all on.

The dragon is bridge does spit out fire and water on weekends, which is a great thing to see. They close down the bridge for the cars, and people stand around the bridge close to the night market to watch. Though it is a spectacular thing to watch, I wish the park had music throughout the area that coincides with the fire-breathing. It would help enhance the experience. And, if you're close, be prepared to get wet when the dragon spits out water.

The bridge itself is beautiful,  the design or the dragon blending with the bridge is unique and seeing the while area at night with the lights is really nice. It does get crowded around the time for the show, a few rounds of the dragon spitting fire and after a few rounds of the dragon spitting water, if you stand on the bridge you'll get wet. There is a market near by and lots of restaurants.  I've never seem something similar on this scale and for kids it would be super exciting

Dragon Bridge is one of the bridges across the Han River. During the day, it is a bridge with a dragon shape on the bridge, but every weekend at 9:00 pm it will transform into a fire dragon and water dragon. Many tourists have been waiting at a favorable location by the river an hour in advance.

The splash is more amazing than the flame. Fire and water are sprayed from the dragon's mouth for about 10 minutes from 9pm on Friday and Saturday nights. You can sit and watch from the night market next door. It's worth seeing. If you are right under the dragon on the bridge, you will be splashed. It also seemed like fun. If you haven't seen it before, I think it's OK to go and see it at least once. On the way back, the road is crowded with crowds, scooters, taxis and grabs, but after a short walk, it feels like no normal person is walking at all.

I came here to see the fire-breathing dragon, but I went on the wrong day; it was already asleep. However, strolling around the area was very enjoyable. The night market was a bit boring because there wasn't much food or merchandise worth buying.
We walked across the bridge returning from the Cham museum. It’s an impressive structure that we are sure looks even better at night and when not raining.
Stopped here on way to airport.  Took some pics, walked up and took more.  It is a pretty good dragon and kids would love it.  We missed it at night so can't comment on that.

We were so thrilled that we made it to Danang on the weekend! This show is only given on Friday, Saturday and Sunday nights. Lots of people were crowded on the bridge itself but we enjoyed the show from below and it was still great. Take in the night market while you wait, the show is at 9.

The bridge has an interesting and original design. The fire and water show only happens over the weekends at around 9 pm. Combine it with a visit to the Son Tra night market. In addition the nearby road is shut off to traffic at that time and there is dancing and music on the street.

I wasn't here at night so can't comment on the breathing of fire and water.However, it is a very nice looking and well designed bridge and worth a look even in daytime if you are in the area.  Certainly one of the prettier bridges in Vietnam and worthy of a couple of photos.
The view is amazing. Choose proper day so that you don't miss the fire show, otherwise also daytime visit is excellent. Nice

Very cool must see thing in Da Nang BUT the dragon only breathes fire on weekends and holidays so plan your visit accordingly.  There are lots of little bars and restaurants within walking distance, especially on the ocean side of the bridge (that's the end with the dragon's head).

The Dragon Bridge is magnificent both during the day and the night. It connects the city and busy and bustling 24 hrs. If you are in Danang you can never miss it. Should you wish to walk along you can do as there is a side walk for visitors. The dragon only breathes fire in the weekend nights between 9 to 9.30 pm. So if you want to witness that, be there well before time to secure the best place for photography/ videography. Else the dragon changes its colour in frequent intervals throughout the evening. A beautiful sight to behold.You can have a nice evening out in the expansive promenade and spend a good time shopping in the night market.

The dragon is very important for the Vietnamese and this bridge inaugurated in the early 2000s is proof of this. Even on the weekend at 9pm there is a sort of little show with the fire-breathing dragon. Next to the bridge there is a beautiful green area with small sculptures also dedicated to dragons and at the end the Champa museum.
Beautiful light show, especially on Fridays and Saturdays when the dragon breathes fire. It is close to the market, so you can combine the visit.

The Fire and Water show is Friday, Saturday, Sunday. I read that best place is on bridge. It is if you want to get wet from the water show. I think that better view is on the roads beneath the bridge as better perspective of dragon. Doesn’t last very long but good fun.

It is undoubtedly one of Da Nang’s iconic bridges. You can walk under it, close to it, or on it as it has a wide pedestrian pavement connecting its head to its tail.You get a good view of the water and other bridges while on it, and on weekends at 9 pm, there is a mini event where the dragon spits water and fire, so can be fun to watch. Make sure you are at the dragon head end when the show starts to get a close feel of it, being at the tail side makes you quite far from the action.Worth a quick visit while in Da nang.
Very nice !The bridge and looks are very good and appealing......... Everyone coming to Da Nang must visit it once.... !!

We stay just @ the tail end of the dragon, and watched the show from that end first time. It is too far away to get a good experience. Second time we saw it from the front and that made the show much better.So go watch it but do it from the head end.
We combined the nearby night market with the dragon bridge. The dragon shoots fire and then water from the dragon's mouth on weekend nights at 9 pm.

The Dragon Bridge fire and water show is a little gimmicky but a lot of fun. Alongside the river, underneath the bridge, is the best viewing spot unless you're keen to get wet. There's inexpensive street food available and as the 9pm time for the show draws near, a real shared camaraderie and party atmosphere develops.

Tickets are not required for this free experience. 😄We had so much fun laughing. The fire was great, but the water spray was even better.It's an exciting time when the clock hits 9 p.m. and everyone starts cheering.

Try to go when the dragon breaths fire, it’s pretty cool, better to stand a little a way rather than on the actual bridge or you’ll get hot then wet as it breaths fire then water.  Great free activity.

Da Nang is an extremely beautiful coastal city in Vietnam. To travel in and around Da Nang like Ba Na Hills, Golden Bridge, Hoi An use the local taxi services. Extremely inexpensive.I used an English speaking, always smiling and reliable taxi driver called Hoang ±84 936423833. He has a clean Mitsubishi SUV and we availed his services to drive to Dragon bridge, Golden Bridge, Ba Na Hills, Hoi An. He is very reasonable and reliable in terms of time and scheduled appointments.

We were lucky enough to be in Da nang on the weekend, they told us that on Saturday the bridge spews fire and water from its mouth.This is correct, every Saturday and Sunday at 9:00 p.m. the bridge puts on a small 10-minute show where the dragon breathes fire and water. In the surrounding squares and on the bridge itself, people gather to see it.It was a nice experience.Just a 2-minute walk away you will have a night market where you can eat and buy some souvenirs if you feel like it.
I went for a walk and looked at it from the tail. It looks messy up close. I recommend watching it from a distance and watching it breathe fire.

You would have to check locally but the fabulous “ Dragon Bridge “ does a show on certain evenings at 9pm 🕘 Where the dragon comes to life and lights are in abundance and fire 🔥 flames are breathed ( safely ) from the dragons mouth 6 times then water 💦 there after 😃The experience near the night market is something different for all ages and worth seeing whilst in Da Nang

Sat-Sun- 9 pm for around 15 mins show... The crowd around to see the show, the atmosphere and the real show- all are worth watching.The dragon head emits fire first and after a gap, water.... You can plan to visit the night market nearby before/after the show, so plan accordingly. If you have to wait for the show, take something along to put on the field, sit and wait for the show to begin.

A landmark in Danang. You can’t miss it as many  places we visited are at both sides of this bridge. We actually went to a restaurant which is walking distance from this bridge to explore the promenade. It seems like an ideal place for people to spend their evening with family members along the Han river.

The bridge is built  across Han River from Nguyen Van Linh Street to Vo Van Kiet Street  It is about 2km from My Khe Beach, and about  5km from Danang International Airport. During night time the bridge comes to life due to unusual and changing light color and looks amazing.

Stayed at the Val Soleil Hotel in Da Nang which was within easy walking distance of the dragon bridge. We could also see the dragon bridge from the rooftop bar. The bridge changes colour at night, varying between reds, greens, golds - very attractive. We went to the bridge on a Saturday evening at 9pm when the bridge gets closed to vehicles and the dragon breathes fire and then water - great experience if you cam.

It was a long bridge spanning the river in Da Nang with a wave shaped Dragon constructed above the road way.  At night there were multi color changing lights that illuminated the entire bridge.  Sometimes the Dragon mouth would breath a sort of Fire!

Yes it's gimmicky, but that doesn't really matter. It's a huge dragon that spans the length of the bridge and every Friday, Saturday and Sunday night breathes fire and sprays water. It won't be the main reason you go to Da Nang, but it is worth seeing.We had drinks at a little sky bar to watch the show. (Just spent 30 minutes on Google maps trying to locate it without success.) It was close to the bridge, served excellent - but very strong - cocktails and hardly had anyone else there - which was perfect. If you can find a rooftop bar close by, this would be the ideal place to watch it from as the road gets very crowded and everyone gets wet! lolThe show doesn't last long so don't get there late.
We took a ferry to view the river scene inclusive of views of Dragon puffing balls of fire at night and changing colours from golden orange to jade green

This is a "normal" traffic bridge which has a dragon which extends the length of the bridge built into it separating the lanes going each way.Every Sat & Sun at 9pm however is the time to visit for the fire and water show. The bridge is closed to traffic and the dragon lights up various colours and spits fire, after which it spits a large plume of water. This will soak you if you are in front of the dragon on the bridge trying to get a good view and photos.You can  stay on the riverside beside the bridge for a good dry view, probably the right side as you look at the dragon head is the best.There is also a large night market, food and other goods about 100mtrs away to visit either before or after the show.

The Dragon Bridge is very beautiful. If you come to Da Nang, you should go at night; it's amazing with the lights. Going on Friday, Saturday, or Sunday is even better, as you'll get to see the Dragon Bridge breathe fire at 9 PM.
Spanning the Han, the newest bridge crossing on the waterway features a dragon that lights up and changes colors at night…and spews Fire and Water on weekend nights at 2100.
Did a day visit to Dragon Bridge. Spectacular view of the bridge and surrounding area. Was not able to witness the night show. however walked over the bridge.
Artificial Dragon that change colour and breath fire and mist at 9pm Fri. Was on a boat watching the show. A lot of koreans was visiting Vietnam this time and making lots of noise on the boat.

It is worth a visit to admire the unique beauty at night and see the fire and water show which is at 9 o’clock on Saturday and Sunday nights. Remember to stay away from the dragon’s head or you may get wet during the water-breathing show.A minus point is that it takes much time to be back to your hotel after the show because of the trafic jam.

A great fun experience as the busy Da Nang traffic is stopped at 9:00pm for the Dragon to do its thing.The dragon blew fire and water for about 10 minutes, very spectacular and pleasing the very large crowd.Well worth going to but recommend arriving at least 30 minutes to ensure you get a reasonable viewpoint.

The bridge is shaped like a dragon; it looks amazing at night with the lights. There's a small show (every weekend at 9 pm) where the dragon breathes fire and water. If you miss it, it's not a big deal; it's not anything extraordinary.

Really cool to see. Go half hour earlier and walk the bridge to the other side and see the views over the river. Da Nang is beautifully vibrant at night and lit up so well it’s stunning. There are places for coffee or drinks nearby however it gets so crazy busy. Everyone is happy and great but wildly over crowded. Not to let this deter you but just to be prepared. There’s a night market right nearby which people come from and then go to after the Dragon Bridge show. If you stand on the bridge or underneath on either side you can get soaked. Otherwise there is a huge standing area a bit further down the street that is a perfect viewing point without getting wet. Would recommend for the light show and if you have kids. The show lasts about 10 minutes in total. Started with fire then ends with water.

A nice bridge most of the time but turns into a spectacle from Friday to Sunday for the Fire and Water show.It gets pretty busy and traffic is chaotic so try and be there about 15-20 minutes early.The night market is just there so there is plenty to keep you amused and fed.
We went to Dragon bridge on a Saturday evening along with many many others. It’s obviously a huge attraction for the local people as well as tourists. The city lit up at night is very pleasant.
It only lasts 5 minutes, but it's worth the wait to get a good spot to see this spectacular light and fire show at night. It's beautiful.
Worth a visit on way to night market, spits fire and water at 9pm on weekends, nicely lit up at night.
We were there on a Sunday evening; there's a fire/water show at 9 pm.
Dragon Bridge being lighted up with different coloured lights at intervals at night. A popular tourists location. Surrounding areas of the Dragon Bridge during daytime is also nice
It's a symbol of the city. The fire-breathing, water-spouting dragon shows at 9 PM. It's worth seeing once, but nothing particularly special. You have to stand on the east side of the Han River.
Impressive construction. Fun with the extra show on the weekend that sprayed fire and water. There was good standing space to watch the show.
It was a nice bridge, I guess even better at night and when you actually see the dragon spit out fire. But I never did since I left on a Friday.

The gathering of hundreds, even thousands, around the bridge on a Saturday or Sunday from about 8 pm onwards has almost a festival atmosphere. Hundreds sit on plastic chairs, stand on the bridge, or along the riverbank, waiting for 9 pm. We thought, "Goodness knows what event we're about to witness!" Right on cue at 9 pm, the dragon's head erupted, this was repeated several times, and then followed by a water fountain. Nice to watch, but not really a highlight. Still, it's part of the experience when visiting Da Nang.
A good engineering marvel .  Worth a walk from one end to another. On the other end is the Cham museum which is worth a visit.

We were lucky to be in Danang during the weekends as the fire performance at the dragon bridge was only available at 9pm on weekends. The place was very crowded with both locals and tourists, awaiting to see the dragon show.

Staying in Hải Châu i have crossed this bridge a few times to get to the beaches. It is probably the bridge that is most synonymous with Da Nang. I unfortunately will not be here for Friday night or this weekend when the dragon spits fire. Hope to see this next time.

The Dragon Bridge is a fascinating place to be on weekends(Sat & Sun) with the mouth facing the Night market emitting fire...PLEASE NOTE THIS as the dragon has a mouth on both the sides(no tail) & you need to be on the side where it faces the ocean/night market to view the spectacle. We weren't sure abt the right side, so assuming we were on the fire side, we waited for the spit, but at 9 pm nothing happened, we saw it spitting from far away & had to rush to the other side to watch it...was a comic spectacle but we managed to reach by the time it ended.Few understand English, so don't assume you would be guided as we found out the hard way stranded on the other side.
A very distinctive feature of Da Nang, it actually sprays fire and water on Saturday and Sunday nights. You should definitely go see it if you have the chance.
Best the view the bridge at night when it’s illuminated. If you’ve got some basic skills using a camera you can get some really cool shots of the bridge and surrounding area.

This is great in that it is a free thing to do on a weekend evening and brings in a lot of people so good for local businesses. Plenty of space alongside the water to view it easily enough, prior to the show starting we watched the bridge change colours plenty of times, and to be fair that was good to see but the actual show onky added the fire and water element and not much else, it would have been more impressive with a music background. The area is rather bust afterwards and to catch a grab you need to navigate back through the market to a few quieter streets where the cabs will be able to stop
What an odd feature to have in the middle of a city but I enjoyed seeing the bridge "breath fire".  Go in the evening

A uniquely designed bridge. During the day, it is not much impressive because of the daily traffic. Better to appreciate at night with the lights on. A very good photo op for tourists. Must place to see
It could be said that Da Nang's tourist city is centered around this place. It is located in the middle of the Han River, and
nice bridge. many local tourists. from the bridge you can see nice han river and Danang city. remember come before 8h45 to see the dragon they put water and fire out
Look beautiful but especially from a distance. Obviously a bit more crowded in real life but still quite a sight to be seen.

Unfortunately, I didn't get to see the fire or water show. But, even just sitting there during the day, the bridge is really cool to see. The head of the dragon points to the sea. Honestly, if there's no show - it just sits there. Danang is pretty dead right now (late December 2020) due to the rain and the lack of international tourists. But they still light up the dragon at night. So, when you make your way down to the night market, take some time to watch the lights change on the dragon too!
It's an interesting landmark and the show in the evening it's quite nice to watch. A dragon bridge breathing fire is definitely something I haven't see before so it was cool to see.
I stayed there for several days
A Saturday Sunday 9pm eventWoo hooNot that exciting, having seen it at ground level.Best observed from north facing rooms.Children would enjoy  if they remain awake!
An amazing sight, looking from our hotel windows and watching the colours change as this dragon bridge lights up and is reflected in the river below.

This bridge is spectacular and has lights on it every night but Friday and Saturday from 9:00pm does a water and steam show.  Well worth seeing just stand back fro the water - go over the bridge to the head end for best view stand along promenade  at distance so as not to get wet.

An interesting piece of architecture, particularly for this part of the world! It's a fairly nice walk along the river and it changes colour. If you are aiming to see the fire and water display get here EARLY! It can be likened to central London on firework night/new years eve, the crowds are HUGE and tightly packed everywhere for miles. It is really cool though although quite brief.
When you think of Da Nang, you think of the Dragon Bridge.
You have to see it at least once. It's best viewed from above.

The old building, after several renovations, has been given a new look and purpose. The hotel layout is simple and clean, and the accommodation prices are reasonable. However, the parking lot driveway is winding and steep, and there's only one lane for entry and exit, which feels like a less than ideal design!

This bridge, spanning the Han River, is a symbol of Da Nang. As its name suggests, it's shaped like a dragon, but its official name is apparently Long Bridge. It's beautifully lit up at night, and there's even a fire and water-breathing show on Saturdays and Sundays at 9 PM.
The spectacle is nice to see as the bridge lights up and the dragon breathes fire. Something you would never see in N. America or Europe. Worth a watch if you're there over the weekend.

It was artificial, but the lighting was beautiful. I walked there from the Mike Beach side on Saturday, and as expected, it was crowded. I wonder if everyone was waiting for the flames to be blown out? It was crowded on Saturday night, but it was normal during the day. I think it's a spot that's better viewed from a distance.
We visited the DaNang night market first then found a seat near the head of the dragon to watch it blow its fire. A beautiful sight.
Went to see the flames at 9pm.Nice but a little underwhelming. The spectical only lasted around 5 minutes and then everyone disappeared
If you're there it is worth a  look. It is better at night when it changes colour . supposed to  spit fire at the weekend around 21.00

The fire and water show takes place at 9 PM on Saturday and Sunday nights. It's not anything spectacular, but it's a major event in this city. Tourists and locals alike gather there. You could say it's nothing special, but somehow it feels just right for Da Nang right now. When there's no show, the dragon is lit up in various colors at night and looks quite beautiful, and it looks great against the city's night view and the surface of the river. Oh, and I don't dislike the heart-shaped eyes either; they have a slightly retro, Showa-era feel to them. The

Love this bridge please make sure you go and see it on the day it has its fire and water breathing show it may only be 5 minutes but it's a fantastic site on the edge of the river. Located right on the river it is In the perfect location no matter which side you are staying. There are 4 bridges in total on the river all lit up and creating a grey view and photo opportunity.

In a city with many bridges the Dragon Bridge stands out. Great design and duing the night its beautifully lit. Then every night at a certain time it breathes fire. There are tour boats on the river which give you the best view

This is right by a great night market. Dragon puts out fire and water show on Saturday and Sunday night at 9pm. Lots of people and the close the bridge for about 15 minutes. Dragon changes color. The whole area is really nice at night. Take a stroll and see the beautiful lights along the river. Note that after 9pm the buses don't run but there are plenty of taxis to get you home. It's very inexpensive for taxis here to get you anywhere you want. Recommend to go here, especially if you can on the weekend to see the dragon show.
You just don't see structures like this in Germany. The bridge is truly special and definitely an eye-catcher both day and night.

An original bridge, beautifully illuminated at night. You don't necessarily have to watch the "fire show" at 9 pm on weekends. It lasts about 10 minutes, you have to squeeze in with thousands of people at the viewing point, and there were 3-4 bursts of fire from the dragon's head followed by 3 jets of water – rather lame. Be careful not to stand directly at the dragon's head on the bridge, otherwise you'll get wet.
I was looking forward to seeing the famous Dragon Bridge.
Doesn't last too long but worth a visit to see the display of lights and fire. Was extremely busy so need to be early for good position.
The 666-meter-long Dragon Bridge is a dynamic bridge designed to resemble a dragon swimming on the water.
Evey Saturday night at 9pm the colourful bridge comes to life with 2 separates fire breathing. This is followed by 3 separate water breathing. A lot of fun but very quickly over.
The color change is terrifying.
It is a dragon going through the bridge... I mean... I really don't know what people expected here, but oh well...

Nice bridge, even better to see it at night with the changes of lights. We were told about the "show", I was expecting much more to be honest haha (not for it to last 2 minutse), but it was still nice!
Absolutely nothing wrong here but a bit of a gimmick. Cool enough but nothing special. Don’t go out of your way, chances are you’ll see it anyway.
If you're a really impressionable person, then you'll probably like it and even see something incredible in this structure. But for everyone else, it's a bridge with an iron dragon and that's it.
Nothing exceptional, but a beautiful bridge that changes colors. However, there's an interesting nightlife nearby.

You can enjoy it both day and night, but unfortunately, since you're driving past it, it's difficult to get a good shot of the whole view. They say it breathes fire, but it's hard for tourists to time their visit to coincide with that.

Da dragon fires first fire than water for 5 mins every Saturday and Sunday at 9 pm.They will close the street for that event and a lot of people come to see this.Be ready to fight your way to the front for a better sight.The dragon Bridge is good reachable from the son tra night Market where you can go eat before the event
This is a very unusual bridge. It's definitely worth seeing. There's a park next to the bridge where you can rest. The bridge has an underpass, allowing you to safely cross a busy main road.
Very unique bridge. The Dragon head  blows fire on weekend nights only. Nice to watch. Can cross the bridge by foot, and see the Han river and other amazing bridges of Danang,
The bridge was built with the image of dragon. It is very colorful with light and crowded with people at night.

I pass by the Dragon Bridge several times almost every day; the heart-shaped dragon is truly a landmark. The bridge spews fire and water on Saturdays, so allow some time for that if you're going to the night market. I suggest not watching from the bridge itself, or you'll get soaked. January in Vietnam isn't too hot, and walking across the Dragon Bridge at night isn't too far, offering views of the nightscape below – it's quite pleasant.

The attraction of Da Nang is the dragon-shaped bridge, it attracts attention especially in the evening when it is illuminated and changes colors, and it is best to go there on the weekend because then the dragon breathes fire at a specific time.
Nice and colorful bridge in the evening. We sat at the boat nearby and had our dinner and watched firing and water squirting. It's great for kids since kids were awed and enjoying it.

Not many cities will dress up a bridge with such decoration to attract tourist. It's the effort Danang government put in to enhance the tourist industry and a credit to her. The design of this dragon looks impressive if you support the idea. There wasn't much to impress during daytime but after evening. It is worth the 2 hours to spend walking around the area to watch the so called performance if time allows. Before you move close to this bridge, you should walk around from distance to appreciate the full scale of the bridge.
This was a fun bridge to look at from a distance and so we decided to ride our rented motorbike over it. Quite exciting.

I passed by several times during the day, and it didn't seem that impressive then, but when I saw it at night while strolling along the promenade, it was incredibly cute. The illuminations look beautiful against the nighttime background. If you walk around it once, you can see the whole view from the front and back. It's a good idea to wait for the colors to change and take several photos.
I saw it up close on Saturday, but it was difficult to see because I had to look up at it.
We passed by there both day and night; it's definitely much more beautiful at night. A typical tourist spot in the city.
A must see at night on Saturday or Sunday 9pm as they close the rd and the dragon breaths fire.Night market below bridge so you can combine the two together

We got to experience the area during Tet where they had created a number of floral displays which were utilised by the locals to take photos in their traditional dress which was something to see. We also walked the bridge which is lit up and being their on the first day of Tet we got to also experience the fireworks which incorporated the bridge

I was only in Da Nang for a short time and I was able to visit this bridge, but it was in the day time. I can only imagine what it would be like when it is lit up and breathing "fire". I would love to return to see this. Even in the daytime, it is pretty and there is a nice place to take a short walk and get a snack.

Located right in the heart of the old town, this is a very striking bridge shaped like a dragon. It's beautifully lit up at night. You can see the whole thing by walking along the park by the river. Apparently

This has become a landmark of Da Nang. It breathes fire at night, but only on Saturday and Sunday evenings. Even when it's not breathing fire, it changes color: gold, blue, green, and white. Standing beneath the dragon's head, its grandeur is even more striking.
The most famous attraction in a city that has very little to offer. On Saturday and Sunday evenings, it spits fire from its mouth.

A beautiful bridge but miss the  firework which is only on weekend. There is a night market no far away from the bridge with street vendor selling affordable and variety of seafood and local delights and souvenir a place to visit in the night
Very ornate for a bridge... but at the end of the day...its a bridge...Novel but don't plan your trip around it.

This is worth taking the kids to have a look. 9pm it started breathing fire, but the stories you could tell little ones would be a treat lol. Makes for great pics at night also. You can view it from the rivers edge which has seats and no bikes driving up and down.

During the day, we toured the Imperial Palace in Hue and the bustling city of Da Nang, which was filled with preparations for the New Year. The city was lined with flowers, mainly yellow chrysanthemums, and national flags, and was bustling with people eagerly awaiting the New Year. In the evening, we went to the night market and the Dragon Bridge. Since it was a weekday, the fire and water weren't blowing, but the dragon was adorned with yellow, blue, and green illuminations, and the illuminations on the sightseeing boats, Ferris wheel, bridge, and buildings created a magnificent spectacle.
If you're heading down to Danang beach from Danang train station be sure to catch a taxi that will go across the Dragon Bridge. Very cool...nose pressed against the taxi window cool...

The bridge looks impressive and as the show starts everyone gets very excited but by the time you start enjoying the show ends abruptly. Its a really short show with nothing much but the night market near the bridge is really a must visit. Lots of Vietnamese street food, exquisite fruits,slushes,smoothies to try.Overall a good place to visit.
A large bridge which crosses the river with a large dragon which spans the length of it. At night it spews out fire and smoke. Quite a spectacle to see.

We could watch the night show from the balcony of our hotel room. It involves fire and water squirting, and apparently it's a show that takes place at 9 PM on Saturdays and Sundays. There are also tours that take you to the base of the bridge by boat to watch it. We enjoyed watching it from our room while having drinks. Normally, it's just an ordinary bridge.

Each weekend you can enjoy the Sin Tra Night market and some nice local music entertainment at the entrance of the Dragon bridge in Danang. We were there in December so it was probably busier than usual.The Dragon is lightens up in various colors and around 9 pm there comes fire and water out of the dragon’s head. It not super spectacular, but great to experience. It can be very busy so it’s good to arrive earlier to find a right stop to be able to take nice pictures and videos.

Only has the fire/water display a couple of times a week over the weekend.Around 9pm. Arrive early to get a decent spot to watch the show as it is very popular and can get extremely busy.A great free activity if you’re in Danang later at night over the weekend. Our daughter found it very impressive and enjoyed watching the dragon breathing fire and water.
I visited on a tour. Since it was a weekday evening, it didn't breathe fire. However, it was beautiful and enjoyable to see the colors change one after another up close.

We went during the day. This is a unique bridge with a golden dragon design. As you would read from most reviews in TA, people would prefer to go during the night when the dragon spews out water and fire. But, we went on a day when the weather was cool, windy. So, we really enjoyed the open area next to the Dragon Bridge. We clicked photographs of the bridge in the background. We spent around half an hour there. Whatever the reviews might say, we enjoyed going there. We also learned that there is a night market around the bridge. Also the fact that during the water and fire show (at 9 pm) from the dragon mouth, traffic is stopped on the bridge for 10 minutes. In and around the bridge there is a very nice promenade to walk about.This was part of the itinerary of the 1.5 days private tour we did with Rubicon Tours Vietnam (please see my separate review of the same).About Da Nang- Wow what a lovely place. We have decided, next time we come to Vietnam we will come straight to Da Nang. It is a very touristy place and very unlike Hanoi and Ho Chi Minh City. Hanoi you need to go just because if you want to do the Halong Bay cruise which is fantastic (please see my review of Unicharm Cruise for that). Ho Chi Minh City you can avoid. Da Nang is well planned, modern with wide roads and beautiful. And you don't have millions of motor cycle riders like ants trying to run over you like the way in Hanoi and Ho Chi Minh City. This city has a very up market feel to it. I have a feeling that this will be the next Bali of South East Asia very soon.- It has a fantastic beach - My Khe beach which runs for what I am told around 10 kms. Please see my separate review of that.- Da Nang is the base for Hoi An and Ba Na Hills (please see my separate reviews for those) which are very good places to visit and do touristy activities.- Da Nang also has the Marble Mountains, Linh Ung Pagoda (with the famous Lady Buddha statue), local markets like Con Market and Han Market. Please see my separate reviews of these places.- For 2 days we did a private tour with Rubicon Tours (please see my review of the same). First day was whole day trip covering Linh Ung Pagoda, Dragon Bridge, Con Market, Marble Mountains and ending with a trip to Hoi An and back (lunch and dinner included and a private car with a driver and a tour guide). The 2nd day we did a half day tour to Ba Na Hills (lunch included and a private car with a driver and a tour guide). I would say Hoi An and Ba Na Hills is a must visit when you are in Da Nang. Please do not miss them. Please see my detailed reviews of each of them.- We noticed some of the most fancy hotels on My Khe beach. Whoa!! Such lovely big hotels and resorts which are located one after the other on My Khe beach. Next time we thought we will stay in one of these fancy hotels which offer lovely bungalows next to the beach. But, all the big hotel chains are located on My Khe beach one after the other. Quite impressive. This we saw on our way to Hoi An from Da Nang.- Da Nang is full of spas and karaoke bars. Literally full of them. We are not interested in karaoke bars. But, yes we are interested in the spas. So, we decided next time we go, we will do many spa sessions. This time we could manage only one with TinTin Spa (see my separate review of that).- We noticed that Da Nang has some of the biggest sea food restaurants. Huge ones with each of them having some 70-80 tables per restaurant.- It has got nice warm, tropical kind of weather. It was a relief for us coming from the cold weather of Hanoi.- People here are very nice like all other places in Vietnam. They are honest, good at heart, intelligent and enterprising. And they have the ability to give excellent service (the best I have come across wherever I have traveled). They will go out of their way to give you good advise and tips and they will get attached to your family in a very unique way. I really admire them considering that they have been plundered and colonized by the western people for ages. I feel it is there time to rise and do well for themselves. And this shows from the rapid tourism interest for Vietnam. And the rapid development being seen here.- It is better to carry and use the local currency, Dong (VND) here. Many places supposedly accepts USD. But many small places only accept cash and VND. Earlier we were advised by few people to carry USD only. But, then somebody else advised us to carry VND and we went with that advise. And we really feel that you should carry the local currency which is accepted everywhere. Even if in a place they ask for USD, they will accept VND by a quick conversion. And then there is one big advantage of using VND. You can become a millionaire there. For e.g. a decent meal will cost you VND 1 mill!!!! And you will not hesitate to pay the VND 1 mill!!!- Da Nang to Hoi An by road is 45 minutes. Da Nang to Ba Na Hills is also approx. 45 minutes by road. Hoi An you can cover for 1 evening. Land up there by 4-5 pm and hang round till dinner there and come back. Ba Na Hills can take almost a full day (weather permitting). It has an amusement park + the famous Golden Bridge (the bridge being held by the 2 hands) + a French themed village + another pagoda with a lady buddha (there are 3 in Da Nang) + lovely gardens + lovely eateries. But, we did Ba Na Hills for half a day as we did not have much time.- Grab (e-hailing) is available in Da Nang. But, we just used it once from Da Nang airport to our hotel. For the rest of the times (2 days), we had a private tour with Rubicon Tours and had a cab with a driver and a tour guide all the time with us.- There is good souvenir shopping in Hoi An. So, please prepare accordingly. And you must buy a lantern from Hoi An because that place is lantern paradise.

If you are interested to watch a short fire show and you don't mind the crowd, give it a go. The show happens on weedends, 9pm. The dragon head on one end of the bridge will blow fire, followed by smoke, then water. We went there on a Saturday, stood under the DHC Marina statue, crowded with people and smelled bad water. Nothing happened because we were unlucky, someone important to Vietnam passed away and so the show was cancelled to pay respect to him. We went back the next evening, the crowd got worse, every tourist was there to catch a glimpse of the fire dragon. We decided to just stand by the roadside to enjoy the show. Something unique I shall say, worth to watch or not, I leave to you to decide. I think it worth, not my fam though, they say no more such silly shows.

Best seen at night when the dragon lights up. 9pm every Saturday and Sunday it blows flames from it's mouth. Many people have a boat trip on the river to watch. We had a rooftop bar and enjoyed a drink and meal at 7 bridges brewery quite close to the bridge.We also walked over the bridge (there is a footpath either side) in the daytime for a different perspective.
Original and spectacular, an emblem of the city visible from everywhere, there are 3 bridges, all a magical display of multicolored light once the sun sets
Every Saturday and Sunday night at 9pm, there is a dragon fire-breathing and water-spouting show at Dragon Bridge.
As our hotel was located opposite han river which oversee the dragon bridge, we have the luxury to see it during day and night. It is something unique that I have not seemed in other countries!

With ample pedestrian footpaths either side of this iconic landmark, it gives access to the coast and city of Da Nang. Accommodating thousands of vehicles on a daily basis this bridges is never quiet, which gives it a character of its own.This bridge in the evening provides opportunities to view Da Nang surrounded in neon lights. It also has great great eateries either side to dine in.

This is a cool bridge in Danang that looks like a dragon.On weekends, it is lit with different colors and has a brief fire and water blowing show at 9 pm, quite cool don’t miss it. (Short though)Have taxi drop you at head of dragon side of the bridge, otherwise you will end up staring at his lifeless tail all through the show :)))If you find this review useful, please like it so encourage me to keep posting.Thank you!
We stayed for four nights in a hotel along the river and in the evenings we enjoyed walks along the banks, the local life, and the lights of the bridges. Things to do

There's a show of fire and water breathing dragon on the weekend at around 9 PM that you can pretty much see from any distance. And nearby there's Son Tra Night Market, which is really great if you want to get some gifts or souvenirs to bring back home and there's some street foods as well.

If you're going to see it, it's definitely at night. It's a distinctive bridge, so it's nice to see it during the day too, but at night it's lit up and looks really beautiful. The colors change depending on the time, so it's fun to watch, but unlike the Han River Bridge, the illuminations don't change, so you can't enjoy watching it for a long time.

The Dragon Bridge is in the heart of Da Nang city with stunning view upon the Han river. If you rent a hotel room that is nearby then you definitely are in the most exciting location of the city. The Dragon spits fire and water every Sat and Sun evening at 9PM for about 15 mins so try not to miss that special moment.
We visited this bridge on the weekend the fire and water breathing lasted about 15minutes over very quick but really impressive

One of 7 bridges, we saw it first driving in from the airport to our hotel in Hoi An. Part of a package tour which we booked specifically on a Sunday because it is only then that you will see the  very brief (9pm for 10 minutes)  'fire and water show". Driver dropped us off by the dragon's head, you walk up a staircase and we had a great view. The fire is REAL, the water is REAL. It is very crowded and our guide kept warning us to be aware of pick pocketers.

At 9pm on weekends the dragon spits out fire and water. The dragon on its own is very pretty with its changing colours. It spits out fire a few times, takes a break and then spits out jets of water. It ends with spitting fire again. People line up on the streets to watch. You can sit at the street side restaurants and order snacks and watch the show or you could just sit on the wall. It doesn’t matter which side of the dragon bridge you sit at. Just don’t sit in the front! We saw people standing on the bridge to watch too but I think you get a better view from the road below. The whole thing takes 10 minutes and is free. Worth it!
Nice bridge to visit. Was told that there will be fire/water coming from the mouth during the weekends and on special occasions.
Spectacular bridge in the middle of Danang. On Saturday and Sunday night at nine PM the dragon shoots out fire and then water from its head. It gets very crowded at this time worth the effort

Visiting the food market near the bridge for a pre fire show feest was great, so many cheap choices of local and western food to satisfy most foody travelers. The fire and water show was good but could be much better with a little imagination.

We were around the area on Christmas eve and we decided to see the Dragon Bridge from distance during day and crossing it and much better to have the most amazing view from a top bar of one of the surrounding buildings during night.During day is nice but not something to be so amazed. If you try to cross it bear in mind the heavy traffic, noise and smoke of the cars and scooters passing next to you.Near the river you can take very beautiful photos but especially from the roofs of the buildings around. During night is a show of lights and colours, not only the bridge but the whole area.It worths a visit especially during night.

There are alot of restaurants around this bridge. The show starts around 21 on sat and sun and any red days. The bridge close for traffic. If you want to see the fire and water show I think you have be near the head area. I dont think the boat rides see the show any better. But it's a nice light show in the dark night.

The bridge was beautiful at night. We took a ferry ride and it went pass the Han Bridge and Dragon Bridge and the lights and view were quite beautiful. I would recommend booking the ferry ride through Klook. The price seems reasonable. But just remember the hygiene as the ferry inside is a bit dirty.
If you happen to be in Da Nang, go to this bridge on Saturday or Sunday evening and you will see a show with a fire-breathing dragon. It is a bit of a beehive, but well worth it.

in the city of the seven bridges, this one is the most famous - even if not the nicest in my view. but it is interesting, by day and by night. the weekend activities with fire and water however I did not find specially exiting.
During the day, it's a normal bridge, but at night it's a very beautiful sight, especially if you cross to the other bridge and there isn't too much traffic to drive at a crawl.
The Dragon Bridge is a very impressive structure that lights up in different colors at night. Around 9:00 PM, the dragon heads breathe fire.
The Dragon Bridge is beautiful and very crowded on weekends. It has fire and water displays every Saturday and Sunday. It's near the Love Bridge.
Best time to view is on weekends in the night as there will be full lightings and Fireworks and Songs.  In the day, you will see just the Golden Colour of this Long Dragon Bridge.
we watched the fire and water works.. so cool.. if u visit da nang, u should make time to watch that.. we were lucky we could..
It's an ordinary bridge, but nicely lit, next to a pedestrian zone. It's perfect for a walk. It doesn't breathe fire during the week.

The Dragon bridge display is known throughout the city during weekends, so it starts to get packed about an hour before the display starts. Despite the area getting packed, the traffic isn’t affected at all, which I find impressive. There are also a lot of nearby restaurants

Nice for night photography with the backdrop of the dragon bridge all lit up. Get a good view from the top of Molly's cafe (al fresco) which sells mainly drinks. The love bridge and night market are within walking distance. Crowded. The show at 9pm just has the dragon (head) spewing fire and water 3 times each.  There's no accompanying laser or music. It was over very quickly. Not quite worth the wait and the crowds. Just go for the night photography and night market experience nearby.
This is surely one of the biggest attractions of town.The color of the bridge changes every 45 seconds at night and its quite a sight.On Saturdays & Sundays at 2100 hrs the dragon spits fire !!
Walking along the bridge during the day isn't very nice. I recommend going at night to see the beautiful nighttime atmosphere with the lights on the bridge.
If you want to go to Dragon Bridge, don't go during the day, but in the afternoon or evening. The dragon-shaped bridge is decorated with lights that change color.
An iconic landmark in the city centre which breath fire and spray water in the same time.Lucky to have seen it before leaving!

A beautiful bridge, an iconic landmark of Da Nang. It's best seen on Saturday and Sunday evenings around 9:30 pm when the dragon's head breathes fire and water. The city is also magnificent at this time of day, illuminated and bustling with life.
I guess it is a must see, when you visit Danang. The best time is during the dark hour where the brigde is illuminated.

The Dragon Bridge is lit up and changes colours nightly.  On Saturday’s and Sunday’s the Dragon spits out fire & water at 9pm.  At the foot of the bridge, there’s a night market that opens at 7pm.  There are lots of stalls that sells all sorts of goodies and street food. A good place to visit for souvenirs and grab a quick bite to eat.  If you walk along the river it has a beautiful view of the other bridges and in the evening the city comes to life.

Vietnam currently boasts numerous tourist attractions, but today I'd like to recommend another one: the Dragon Bridge. Its unique and striking design, combined with the stunning nighttime view of the river, makes it an unmissable visit.

Amazing bridge! I only went over it on the back of a motorbike heading to Hoi An, but this bridge is spectacular! Unfortunately I didn’t get the chance to see it at night, but if it’s better than the day, enjoy!
Okay, it's just a small jet of fire and then water, but for the festive atmosphere, the lights and the night market, it's still worth the trip, I think.
Wonderful bridge, especially at night when it changes colour a sight to see , even from a distance not to be missed

There was a fire show at the Dragon Bridge on the weekend, and there were so many people. It was nice to watch it amidst the crowd after browsing the Son Tra Night Market, eating something delicious, and walking around a bit.

Dragon bridge is one of the iconic Bridges in Da Nang. When it is lit during the night along with other lights in the background, it's a soothing views. In some days, e.g weekends, the dragon throws fire, a fantastic view

On weekends, this dragon will first roast you with fire, and follow that up with quenching spurt of water!One of the best way to see this spectacle is to join a boat cruise on the Han River. The boat will position itself at a good vantage point when Showtime draws near...the dragon will be lighted up and undergo lovely color changes. Then at 8pm,you will see flames shooting out of the mouth of the dragon, after which comes water. It's quite a show. And it's all free.Take note it's only on Saturdays and Sundays.
Invigorating walk along the Dragon bridgeNice view of Han River and nearby bridgesStrolled to the nearby DHC Marina

It is a marvellous piece of architecture. It is lit in the evenings. The best part of the bridge is the fire and water show which happens on Saturday and Sunday nights at 9.00 pm local time. Worth watching
The Dragon Bridge is a real eye-catcher at night because of the changing lights. On Saturdays and Sundays, this is further enhanced when the dragon also breathes fire and water.
Beautiful construction on the bridge of an iron dragon that breathes fire and sprays water on weekends (Saturday and Sunday, always at 9:00 PM) - beautifully illuminated in the evening.

Definitely very cute! Not one of those essential sights, but I think you should definitely take a look while walking around Da Nang! We were lucky enough to see the dragon on Sunday, since the fire-breathing show is on at the weekend.

On Saturdays or Sundays, around 9:00 AM, about 15 minutes before the bridge is already full of people and vehicles. You need to allow time for the dragon head to breathe fire. The whole performance lasts about 10 to 15 minutes. After breathing fire three or four times, it starts spraying water. If you stand in front of the dragon head, be careful, you will almost get completely soaked. The atmosphere is great and it is well worth a visit.

You must come at night to witness the dragon's color-changing abilities, and on some nights there are water and fire-breathing shows that create a spectacular sight for viewers. The atmosphere is great, with a comfortable outdoor seating area to enjoy the breeze. Nearby, there's also a night market where you can shop for various goods.

The dragon bridge looks beautiful both day and night. On holidays, there's a show where traffic on the bridge stops and a large jet of fire and then a jet of water shoots out of the dragon's head several times. The show lasts no more than 5-7 minutes, but it's definitely worth watching.
A delightful surprise as we crossed, and saw it as we departed. Wow, what a Golden Dragon!This is not to be missed

I went to see the Dragon Bridge fire show. Before going, I read reviews that said you get soaked if you get too close, so I tried to stay away from the edge. I got completely drenched and cold, so I went back to the hotel lol. There
On weekend the dragon bridge will blow fire and water display at 9h pm. Its so interested. Also night market near by, there are a lot of people gather around.

One of nice attractions in Danang which caters to all tourists for free. Every week end this Dragon Bridge which connects the 2 districts of Danang is a site to reckon with. Its spits fire to the delight of the audience. You can view it from near or afar at your own comfort area. Its location is accessible to all means of transport or even by walking. This is the number one event that Danang City is very proud of, that one should not miss.
The Vietnamese love the bridges of Da Nang. It's almost funny! Haha, at night they're even more beautiful and brightly lit. On weekends, the dragon breathes fire. It's cool to see!
Many Vietnamese people also come out onto the streets here at night. Although

Actually, unless you see it from a certain angle, you won't appreciate its beauty. It really is just a bridge; you can probably stop and take a picture and that's it. There's no need to go all the way over. Taxis sometimes pass by anyway.
Visited on a weekday morning, there was nothing special about the bridge, but there was no other place to go, so I went to it.I
This dragon has heart-shaped eyes.

We went down to the bridge early on a Saturday night and found a good spot to watch the action!! Glad we did....never realised how crowded it gets. Truly is a sea of bodies!!A 9pm the dragon blew fire then water/smoke. It doesn't go for very long, but is really pretty to watch.
The Dragon Bridge is magnificent, especially its show where it breathes fire, water, and smoke, each color representing a different element.
Good show, never seen another bridge do this, well worth the effort to attend, starts at 2100hrs and was well attended, take the time to see!

There is a show on the Dragon Bridge itself at every Sat 9pm last 15 mins to watch the dragon spray out fireball and water from the Dragon Bridge.All the roads and bridge will be packs by visitors, motorists and travellers to watch this significant dragon bridge fireball event and nearby there is a night market selling foods and some souvenirs from Vietnam
Dragon Bridge is located in the city center and is easily visible. It's a very distinctive bridge, and anyone visiting Da Nang will definitely cross it. It's a very iconic landmark.

Every Saturday and Sunday at 9 PM, a show featuring fire-breathing and water-spitting creatures takes place on the Dragon Bridge. Even from a distance, the bridge is fascinating as it changes color, but it is closed to traffic during the Saturday and Sunday shows, and it gets incredibly crowded, so it's best to arrive about 15 minutes beforehand.

The bridge itself was pretty cool. But the cruise we took to watch it was truly unfortunate. Not the bridge’s fault, the fire-breathing part was truly worthwhile, so if your travel plans include a weekend, make sure to be near the bridge at 9:00 pm.
Fun and unique bridge to go and see just at end of night market. Unfortunately we were not there the night of the fireworks display.
Please research the best places and times to visit.
Very colourful and busy at the end where the dragon breathes fire and water. It only goes for 10 minutes but a good Look. Make sure your at the West end for entertainment.

At the hotel we stayed had a excellent view of this bridge from our room. During the day the dragon bridge is yellow with two lanes of traffic each side. At night it comes alive changing a variation of colours.  On The weekend Sat and Sun even better it’s closed to traffic for ten minutes so it can breath fire then water.

I went for a run and cross the bridge. It’s nice and they have a show every Sunday night where the dragon spit fire!Plan something else and pass the bridge on your way, no need to go there specifically

I first passed over it on my way from the airport to the hotel, but I was more captivated by the sheer number of motorcycles than the bridge itself, so it didn't leave much of an impression. Later, I went back during the day and at night, and it was very beautiful when lit up at night.

My visit to Da Nang felt all too short. Before heading to the airport, I enjoyed some leisure time at 'Highlands,' a cafe popular with Vietnamese people, and then went on a cruise to enjoy the Dragon Bridge and the illuminated night scenery of Da Nang. If you want to capture memories of Da Nang, I highly recommend seeing this bridge.
Perhaps a bit kitschy, but fitting for Danang and pleasant in its own sort of way. Best viewer at a distance.
The fire show at the Dragon Bridge on Saturday night is the most memorable thing you'll see in Da Nang. The bridge at night, combined with the neon lights, is absolutely amazing.
It's just a bridge, so there's nothing particularly special about it, but it's bigger than it looks in photos and has become a symbol of the town.
The bridge is a symbol of Da Nang. At night, it's illuminated, and the night market at the foot of the bridge attracts many people, including tourists and locals. It's fun to walk across it.
By walking along the river you can see this bridge which has been designed and lit up as a dragon. its a beautiful sight as night. nothing too extra ordinary though. Just another structure.
I walked across it at lunchtime. It was hot, but the view was amazing!
The dragon bridge changes color at night and spits fire and water, putting on a show for the people who go there to watch.
The famous Dragon Bridge is worth seeing both day and night.
The Dragon Bridge is truly dazzling at night under the lights. On weekends, watching the water and fire show is a delight.
Ok to visit in nightSomething new to seeNight market also near by Far view is much better then near so just visit night market and see from far

I think our experience at the market next to the Dragon bridge is worth going there. Just be ready that its a wet market so its not like a sit down dinner but the seafood is cheap, delicious and lobsters are just big and wow!  So come with your appetite.
Dragon Bridge is Da Nang's calling card. It was built in 2013 and is 666m long. Performances are held on weekends at 9.00 p.m.

Wow, we are so amazed! It is our first time in Vietnam and seeing the Dragon Bridge in Da Nang is really a dream come true for the whole family. The size of the dragon architecture that bridges the river is really unique and very majestic to look at. We were so excited waiting for the night show, and we were not disappointed because we watched the spectacular fire and water breathing! When he started spraying water, he really looked like a giant monster character coming out of the river, so cool and intense! We were really excited, especially the kids, because the cinematic view of water and fire in the dark. The vibes here on the riverfront are perfect, the atmosphere is so happy and lively, like we were in a movie. The lights that change color are also so beautiful, every corner is really Instagrammable! This is definitely the highlight of our Da Nang trip, and we will never forget this moment.

The Dragon Bridge breathes fire and water on Friday, Saturday and Sunday evenings at 9:00 p.m., most weeks. Stand at the end of the bridge near the dragon's head on the north side of the bridge if you don't get want to get wet. Super fun!

Waaah, this Dragon Bridge is absolutely massive and looks so steady even during the day!But the real magic is the fire-breathing show on the weekend—you must watch it.My kids were screaming with excitement when the dragon really spat fire, then water!It was so dramatic and a totally different feeling compared to fireworks, lah.The whole family loved the atmosphere; so many people, so vibrant.A must-see attraction in Da Nang, it's free entertainment best!We were super happy we waited for the performance.

Dragon Bridge — Cầu Rồng — is one of Đà Nẵng’s most iconic landmarks, and it’s impossible to miss its bright golden dragon stretching proudly across the river. By day, it’s a striking piece of architecture, but by night it becomes a real spectacle, glowing with colourful lights that reflect beautifully on the water.If you’re in Đà Nẵng on a weekend, the highlight is the dragon’s fire and water show, usually on Saturday and Sunday evenings. Crowds gather to watch the dragon breathe real flames and spray water — a fun, lively experience for families, couples, and anyone seeing the city for the first time.The bridge is perfectly placed for a riverside walk, with easy access to cafés, night markets, and viewpoints along both sides of the river. Whether you cross it on foot, drive over it, or admire it from below, it’s a must-see symbol of Đà Nẵng’s modern, energetic spirit.

If you're in Da Nang, it's definitely not worth passing the Dragon Bridge. It looks like a bright yellow arch over the Han River during the day, but is particularly impressive in the evening. Every weekend at 9:00 p.m., there's a little show: the dragon releases fire and water. First, a few volleys of flames, then a stream of water. The atmosphere is very unusual: a lot of people, a lively mood, everyone is filming what is happening. There's one thing - if you stand too close, you can get caught in the rain and get wet. Therefore, it is better to choose a convenient location in advance: closer for vivid emotions or a little further away to watch the show quietly. It's worth coming a little early to take a good point. And on weekdays, the bridge is illuminated by neon lights, and in the evening it looks very beautiful too. It's one of those places where you really feel the atmosphere of the city - bright, vibrant and memorable.

Da Nang is a very beautiful city, very modern and so clean. My family was very impressed when we came here.The main thing everyone must see is the Dragon Bridge. It looks like a real dragon over the Han River. At night, it has many lights and looks amazing. The river itself (Han River) is nice to walk next to. You can see all the bright buildings reflecting on the water.The city has a great combination of new buildings and the big sea. It is a good place for family trip because everything is easy and safe.We enjoyed the fresh air and the modern atmosphere. Da Nang is truly a good place to visit in Vietnam for a modern city experience.

Danang not only famous for beaches but also for golden dragon shaped bridge. It is most famous bridge and has a lovely and a beautiful view over Han river. It is the World’s largest dragon - shaped steel bridge. whenever we travel within Danang, we tend to cross the bridge or it is viewable at a certain distance. The shape of the bridge makes it so beautiful and attractive. It is illuminated in beautiful colors at late evening and night time to enjoy It's real beauty. If you go for walk or visit to Night market which is very famous and huge market place in Danang to shop and buy unique and memorable items, you would get beautiful close view of the dragon shaped bridge in multiple illuminated colors. If you plan to visit Danang during weekends then you would get a exciting chance of bridge (dragon head) breathing fire and water over the beautiful riverduring night time and so you can enjoy the real beauty of the bridge along with fire & water works to the fullest. Speciality of the dragon bridge design is that its ability to breathe fire for 2 minutes and water for 3 minutes.

Arriving in this central Vietnamese coastal city, our group was completely taken aback by the sheer scale and ingenuity of the Dragon Bridge. It's absolutely massive, dominating the Han River skyline! As night falls, the atmosphere transforms—everyone leisurely gathers along the riverbanks, just soaking up the cool breeze and enjoying the beautifully illuminated view of the winding dragon.The real excitement, of course, is the moment the dragon comes alive! Waiting for the weekend fire and water show built up such a fun, buzzing anticipation among us. When that magnificent head starts breathing fire, it's genuinely a spectacular and thrilling sight, followed by a cool spray of water.
