It was such a unique, unforgettable piece of evening entertainment and a perfect, exhilarating memory for our whole travel crew.

My first visit to the Dragon Bridge in Da Nang was really struck by its unique and modern design. During the day, the dragons on the bridge take off like the sea, full of power and momentum; at night, the colorful lights shimmer, making the whole city look particularly charming. My favorite is the dragon fire-breathing and water-spraying moment, which is spectacular and attracts many visitors to stop and watch.

Following our incredible Northern adventure, we flew down to Central Vietnam for a three-day, self-guided escape in Da Nang and Hoi An. It was the perfect contrast to the structured tour—a chance to relax, explore freely, and enjoy the beautiful beaches and city life.Da Nang itself is a dream, but the Dragon Bridge is an absolute marvel that truly exceeded all our expectations. This architectural masterpiece is even more captivating in person. Seeing the dragon's head breathe fire was the undisputed highlight of our nights! My friends and I were completely mesmerized, shouting with excitement and filming the entire spectacular sequence. It's an utterly mesmerizing sight that completely transforms the city atmosphere. This fantastic show is a must-see, and the fact that it's a free attraction makes it a perfect, unforgettable experience in the charming and beautiful city of Da Nang.
It was very nice, but only to see Friday, Saturday and Sunday. It's worth it, though. The market next door can be used immediately.

It's definitely an attraction. Especially in the evening when it changes color it is beautiful. If you are in Da Nang on Friday, Saturday or Sunday remember that in the evening at 9.00 h it breathes fire three times and after a while water, always 3 times. And of course the crowd on deck at that time is incredible. Shortly before the bridge is closed to vehicular traffic to give the opportunity to pour on the bridge to take photos and videos of this 'show'. In the end it's a nice memory....
beautiful country of exceptional people another culture France has much to learn from Asian countries in all areas.

A fun weekend experience in Da Nang. The Dragon Bridge breathes fire on Friday, Saturday, and Sunday evenings at 9 PM. It's best to stand at a distance for a better view. Avoid getting too close as it can cause burns and is dangerous. The bridge is usually closed about 15 minutes before the fire-breathing show. There are large crowds, so please be careful.

Dragon Bridge isn’t just a bridge — it’s one of those things you’ve got to see while you’re in Da Nang. It’s bold, bright, and yes, it actually breathes fire and water. It might sound a little over the top, but that’s what makes it fun. If you’re around on the weekend, definitely swing by to catch the show.It doesn’t last long, but hey — it’s free, and it makes for a cool little experience.While you're in the area, stick around a bit. The night market’s nearby, the Cham Museum’s worth a visit, and a walk down Bach Dang Street is always nice — it’s all within walking distance.It’s easy to get to, doesn’t cost a thing, and honestly? It’s a great way to spend an evening in the city.

When travel from Hoi An to Hue, I passed by The Dragon Bridge in Da Nang.  It’s relative new, opened to traffic in March 2013.  The modern bridge was designed by the US-based Ammann & Whitney Consulting Engineers with Louis Berger Group.It’s super grand, bright in deep gold color!  The Dragon Bridge (Vietnamese: Cầu Rồng) is a bridge with an arch-beam-wire combination steel structure in the shape of a dragon crossing the River Hàn in Da Nang, Vietnam. It is the world's largest dragon-shaped steel bridge.  Super awesome.
It was so nice to have done the massage so coolly and perfectly. It also filled the time well and was the most satisfied of the massages received nearby

It is the most important bridge , connecting eastern and western sides of Da Nang on Han River. It's a great piece of architecture. It is world's largest dragon shaped steel bridge. It is illuminated with 2500 led lights in the evening.Every weekend at 9 pm, a spectacular fire and water show is held here for fifteen minutes. One hour before that, the bridge is closed for traffic. Thousands of people gather here and occupy each and every corner on and around bridge to watch this great fire breathing and water spraying show. Please be careful after water spraying, the people standing on bridge can get wet.
The highlight of our trip to Vietnam! Sunday night at the huge night market followed by the dragon bridge show which breathes fire then water !

I was only expecting to see dragon spit fire but not water ( 3x each ). A large crowd of us arrived there to watch it as it will start at 9pm from Fri to Sun only. So for those of you who wished to stand on the bridge to catch a glimpse of it, remember to bring your umbrella too. 😆
It only lasts 15 minutes but is a site to see. Do you recommend it?Get a grab, look around, and wait for the show..We found a good spot just of the side.. and we walked over the bridge as well.

Visiting the Dragon Bridge in Da Nang was truly a memorable experience. The atmosphere was vibrant and full of energy, especially in the evening when the city lights came alive. The bridge itself is a stunning piece of architecture — bold, creative, and beautifully illuminated at night.One of the highlights was the fire and water show that happens on weekends. Watching the dragon breathe fire and water right over the river was thrilling and unique, drawing crowds of excited tourists and locals alike. The reflection of the colorful lights on the water added a magical touch to the whole scene.Walking along the bridge, I could feel the cool breeze from the Han River, which made the experience even more relaxing. The surroundings were lively yet peaceful, with street performers, local food vendors, and friendly people all around.Dragon Bridge isn’t just a crossing — it’s a symbol of the city’s spirit and creativity. I’d highly recommend it to anyone visiting Da Nang. Whether you go for the spectacle, the view, or just a peaceful evening stroll, the atmosphere is something you won’t forget.
The Dragon Bridge in Da Nang is absolutely worth seeing. Especially in the evening and on weekends, when it is illuminated and the dragon spits fire.

This majestic bridge was built by the Eiffel Company during French Colonization. It has been named for the 3 arched dragon body and head alongside the actual bridge. Spectacularly lit up at night and during the weekend spews fire from its head in front of a huge crowd. Couldn’t make that show , but understand it is quite the spectacle.
What a wonderful occasion so well managed by the city to see the fire & steam show of the dragon at 9 pm. Fun family atmosphere & close ti the night market which is superb

The dragon bridge is very good just to look at, and then if you are there on the correct days Friday Saturday, Sunday it shoots out fire and water.  Nice to stroll along the river, watch the boats and the dragon show
Every Friday and Saturday night, there's a water and fire show!

A fire and water show on Fridays, Saturdays, Sundays and evenings at 9 p.m.You can see it for free from the top of the bridge.The bridge is normally closed to cars and people, but only during this time. It's a 15-minute show, but it's a very dynamic show.Under the bridge, there's a night market where you can eat seafood very cheaply.
Trang was wonderful as a tour guide. Friendly and always helpful, she was full of knowlegde with a dash of humour! She ensured that everybody was comfortable during the tour.

In January 2025 to China-Vietnam Harbour, one evening is a boat trip to the Korean River, the Korean River is the most prosperous and lively place on both sides of the Korean Harbour, there are many bridges over it, among which the Long Bridge is the most beautiful and most representative, especially in the evening, the light changes, plus the dragon fountains will erupt and fountains, in the evening to take beautiful photos. It is worth a boat trip to Hong Kong to enjoy the beauty of the Long Bridge.

Dragon Bridge is one of Da Nang’s most impressive landmarks, featuring a striking golden dragon design. A special highlight is the fire and water show at 9 PM every Saturday and Sunday, attracting many visitors. The surrounding area is always clean and spacious, providing a comfortable and enjoyable experience for tourists.

The Dragon Bridge, a symbol of Da Nang, is talked about by everyone and many have crossed it numerous times. On my last day, I had the chance to stroll around the bridge, participate in a few activities, and take some photos. However, it wasn't as lively as Ho Chi Minh City or Hanoi. It's a city with a slow pace of life and friendly people.
I love Da Nang. I'm a local of Da Nang, and I visit the beautiful sights here every week, but I never get tired of it. It's truly beautiful, and I'm always proud of that.
Bridge symbol of the city famous because every weekend at 9 o'clock it breathes fire and water for about 10-15 minutes. If you are visiting these days definitely to see

Dragon Bridge in Da Nang is an iconic landmark known for its impressive design shaped like a dragon. It lights up beautifully at night with vibrant colors. On weekends, the dragon breathes fire and water, attracting many visitors. The bridge offers stunning views of the Han River and the cityscape. It’s a must-see attraction in Da Nang.

This iconic bridge in Da Nang boasts unique architecture. In the evenings, it features water fountains and offers stunning river views. Furthermore, the bridge is conveniently located near the night market, making it easy to get around and find food.

We were staying at the Vanda Hotel which is a Highrise that sits just off the river overlooking the Dragon Bridge, although you're on the tail end of the great lizard. It's still a great view from the rooftop bar. I think everyone is aware that the head breaths fire and steam out of his mouth on weekend night. It really becomes an occasion on those nights. We elected to book a ride on a river tour boat to view the fire action but it gets very busy with all the boats jockeying for position. If I had to do it again, I would have watched all the activity from shore. Actually, it might be a better view and you won't have a a boat in your way spoiling the view. Recommended.
Fire show happens on weekends only at 9pm. It's the fire, then water. Important not to stand too close or else you'll be drenched during the water show :) A lot of fun.

There are several eateries around, as well as a night market. On Friday, Saturday, and Sunday evenings at 9:00 PM, the dragon performs a fire and steam spectacle. It takes them around fifteen minutes to close the bridge.

Amazing structure. Go on the west side into the park to take pictures/video of the Dragon changing colors every night after Dark.Go to the east side North or South sidewalk along the Dragon for expected pictures of the show at 9:00 p.m. Friday Saturday and Sunday...  you should be in place by 8:15 because it gets crowded.The dragon spouts fire but when to catch it? Just before the fire erupts, there is a small fire at the mouth that is used to ignite the big fire. That is your key to start the video. Shortly thereafter the flame will come forth, but only for about four times...Then there's a pause, and I believe it fires up again. But after that, it is going to spray water all over people below in its path.So set your viewfinder with the dragon head to the right ( if you are on the North side) so that the spray will show in the left three quarters of your viewfinder.You will not be able to get the entire scene of the dragon head and the water spray, and the crowd getting wet below.  You will have to go down the stairs below to get a further back shot to encompass all I described above... the next night...
We saw this from about 100 meters..With the hudge flame and the colors we where happy to watch it from across the river, with the building lit up in the background.Very cool

Another nice bridge in Da Nang city centre.  It's a yellow metal structure crossing the river. During the weekend the dragon's head spits fire. Unfortunately we weren't there for the weekend but we crossed the bridge watching the entire structure. Nice!!!

The view was amazing. It was drizzling but worth the wait. The fire/ water show is only on weekends so best plan accordingly. Taking a cruise on that day maybe a good idea as the boats stop at 9 pm to view the show though we didn’t do that. Best viewed from love bridge as you can see the entire dragon.
Wow!!!!Amazing show. It was crouded, but it worthed.We suggest to go early and enjoy the night market under the bridge, before the show.
Dragon Bridge is fabulous.The bottom part goes down the stairs and there are beautiful dragon and photo ops.A stunning bridge and marina.Great at night with personality.
Nice show) starts at 21:00 every Saturday and Sunday. Stand back better, not on the bridge, as you will be burned by fire and doused with water.

The Dragon Bridge breathes fire on weekends, which is a great experience. There are many shops and restaurants around the Dragon Bridge area, and the Son Tra night market is right at the foot of the bridge.

What a spectacular bridge.  So clever to turn a bridge into something that is  not inly practical but beautiful.  On a Friday, Saturday and Sunday night at 9 o'clock the dragon breathes fire and then water a 3 minute spectacle that is a must see.  At night the bridge lighrs up and changes colour it is gorgeous.

Very nice installation in the shape of a dragon in the middle of the bridge. Especially in the evening which is illuminated in various colors. At 9pm on weekends the dragon breathes fire and water from its head on the east side of the river.

The Dragon Bridge's fire-breathing display is very beautiful. It's best to stand far away to avoid getting wet, as it sprays water after the fire. We sat at Simor Cafe waiting for the fire to finish before walking over to watch. It was quite convenient and nearby.
Very amazing creation, beautiful beautiful view once you start cruising in the river, it was a wonderful experience at all
This bridge is spectacular.  A great way to make a bridge look wonderfulOn a Friday, Saturday and Sunday night it breathes fire and water.  At night it lights up and changes colour.

Worth the visit at night if you're here during the weekend. We went on a Friday night to see the fire/water show. They close the bridge about 5-10 min before the show. It's free to access the show. During non-show times, you can freely walk/drive across it anytime.
The view of the bridge is already really great during the day, but really spectacular in the evening and an absolute tourist magnet.
I love the view. The night view is beautiful!D.HOANG-san took care of me and I was doing my best! The meat and salad were delicious!

I was lucky to be in this area on the weekend as the Dragon fire is only set off during the weekends. How lucky am I right? It was really beautiful. Nothing I had seen before. And the excitement from both locals and foreigners? Was worth the time. Some people tend to dance after the water splash from the dragon LOL such a beautiful experience. If you happen to be in Da Nang on the weekend? Don’t miss this please

The bridge spews fire and water every weekend evening; I watched it from a nearby restaurant, and it was truly spectacular. Standing too close to the bridge, you can get wet when the dragon sprays water.

Danag is very beautiful city of Vietnam. Best attraction is Dragon bridge where place in center of Danang city. It make water and fire at 9pm every weekend. Be sure when u come Danang city, u have to visit here for first time.
Amazing bridge design and very cool area to walk along the riverfront from either side.I'm sure bridge builders in other countries are saying "how cool, why didn't we think of that!"
I loved the fire show 🔥 The kids really enjoyed it. And there were fireworks this time too. Absolutely gorgeous 😎 !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
Amazing place to visit. Nice atmosphere. Very unique friendly safe place to spend good time with your family. Highly recommended
It's a must-go in Da Nang, iconic! I can see this symbolic bridge from my hotel at a distance. During my visit at daytime on a Saturday, there're extraordinarily many police constables.

To explore the city best way is to book a motorbike tour.And we booked city tour with Áo Dai Riders,the female bikers.We stop mouth of the Dragon with Fire and Water Show,that is thrilling experience ever.I love to seat behind the Bike to observe the riding moments along the bridge as well.

Cool bridge worth checking out! Really busy on the weekends during the fire show, best to get there early!Great experience in Danang and no cost. Get there about 45 minutes before it starts for good spot

The Dragon Bridge connects the two banks of the Han River in Da Nang. The idea of building a bridge in the shape of a dragon is very interesting in itself. In the evening, when the Dragon Bridge is illuminated, it makes a very nice photo opportunity. At weekends (Fri. Sat. Sun.) at 9 p.m. there is a fire and water show. The dragon spits fire and then water. The show is actually very interesting and spectacular, even though it is short. The area around is always full of people waiting to record the show with photos and videos. I would not recommend standing on the bridge because you will get wet when the dragon starts spitting water. A "must see" in Da Nang.
Great free Fire and Water show. If you go to Da Nang make sure you go on Friday, Saturday or Sunday when they have the Fire show.

We went on a cruise down the river on the 8pm sailing.  It was well worth it.  Fantastic photos and videos of the dragon breathing fire on the bridge.  Amazing sight.   Do book though because when we went it only breathed the firecand smoke on Friday, Saturday and Sunday nights 9pm
We loved watching the Dragon Bridge breathe fire and change colors at night. A unique and entertaining sight! 💕💕💕💕

During my recent business trip, I found that Da Nang is truly a livable city as rumored. Here, my favorite thing to see is the Dragon Bridge spewing water and fire. I didn't think Da Nang was such a beautiful place at night. So, I am sure that I will come to Danang to live for the rest of my life

Tips only.. you didn't have to get there early..Maybe 15 minutes in advance, when it is close to 9 they choose the road.. when that happens, make your move closer. A night market is at the South base of the bridge enjoy the deals thereKeep in mind the dragon shoots fire AND WATER.. judge where to stand feeling any slight breeze

I first crossed this beautiful bridge when travelling from Danang Airport to my hotel in Hoi An. Crossed it again when driving back from Hoi An to my hotel in Danang. The staff at the Danang hotel opened my window curtains and showed me that I could see it from my room.Because I visited on a weekend, I was able to watch its short fire and water show that began at 9:00 p.m. Took many pictures of this unique, beautiful bridge!

The Dragon bridge crosses the Han River at the Le Dinh Duong/Bach Dang traffic circle, providing the shortest road link from Danang International Airport to other main roads in Danang. The bridge was designed and built in the shape of the 666-metre long dragon and to breathe fire on the weekends after sunset. Tourists and locals would not want to miss the pyrotechnic show of the dragon's plumes of fire, followed by hissing clouds of water vapour at 9.00 pm during the weekends.

The atmosphere of the Dragon Ball at night is very beautiful. For those who love perfection at night, this is a great place. I find Da Nang, Vietnam, one of the most livable places in the world. I love Vietnam very much
The performance was excellent😍😍😍😍😍😍😍😍😍😍😍😍😍. We really liked it. High recommend for you whenyou come to DaNang🥰🥰🥰🥰🥰🥰

Fun activity to do on our first night here, luckily the weekend so we saw the dragon in full action. Lasts about 15mins. We watched from down on the plaza to the left of the dragon as you are facing it and got good viewsStarts 9pm sharp
Very cool experience! Dragon Bridge with fire and water show afterwards. Absolutely recommended at 9pm! We just stood on the bridge, that's where you had the best view
A great way to turn a simple bridge into a nice experience.  Well worth enjoying if you're in the area.  Other than that it does what bridges do best in that it gets you from one place to another.
Attractive and a great symbol of Da Nang city in particular and Vietnam in general. Come and experience this place with your friends and relatives!

After spending time at Son Tra Night Market, I moved around 8:45 and took a seat, but the view was good and the space was nice.It takes a while to get a Grab after the end, but it's a good place to see while spending a night in Danang. If you don't have too high expectations, you'll be satisfied.

Great location to visit. Lots of restaurants and a night market close by. There is a fire and steam show from the dragon on Friday, Saturday and Sunday nights at 9:00 PM. They close the bridge and it lasts approximately 15 minutes

Beautiful!! I really like this bridge that I biked across herea sevral times on my day trio to Danang, just to admire the beauty. Anyway I went on Thursday so I couldn’t watch the fire performance. What a pity :(

It was the first day of Lunar Chinese New Year on 10 Feb 2024 which is also when the Vietnamese celebrate Tet. Conicidentally it was a Saturday where the Dragon comes to live at 9pm.  Moreover it is the year of the Dragon and it is every reason to be there at the Dragon Bridge head to celebrate the first day of Chinese New Year and Tet to see it spitting fire in 3 sessions and spraying water in 3 sessions.  We stood at Tượng Cá Chép Hóa Rồng platform (a statue like a fish fountain). By 8:30pm the place was already quite crowded and by 9pm everyone was excited enjoying the dragon's performance.  There are many ways to watch the dragon from near or afar or from decorated boats if the timing is right. I didn't manage to book the boat during the last minite with the right timing because it was all sold out but I was glad we didn't because it is better to watch from the harbour side anywhere you like.  If you are in Da Nang during weekends, this activity should not be missed.  This dragon shows the prosperity of Da Nang and Vietnam.
On Saturdays and Sundays at 9 pm, Dragao releases fire and water, it's worth checking out. Tourists and locals celebrate. Crossing the bridge by car is really cool
There are fire and water spray every Friday, Saturday, and Sunday at 9pm. Best location to see the whole dragon bridge is near Marina Coffee area.

We were 6 days in Da Nang, crossed this iconic bridge many times, but to get the most of it be there on weekends at 9 PM.10 min before that time the bridge closes for traffic. At 9PM starts a show when first the Dragon spits fire and after that sprays huge amounts of water (watch out where you stand, we ended up being totally wet, returning after to our hotel to change).This performance lasts around 10 min and is totally worth coming.

Visiting the dragon bridge on any given night is quite spectacular. But if you happen to be in Da Nang on a Friday or Saturday I would definitely suggest checking it out around 21.00 when it does the fire/water performance. If you decide to climb on top of the bridge to see the show. Then don't be a fool like me and pay attention to the direction of the wind. You DON'T want to be standing downwind  from it because you will get wet, not just a little wet, no, you will get soaked! But definitely worth to check out.

If you are in Da Nang on the weekends this is a unique event. The bridges are so picturesque and add to this beautiful city. The Serpent Bridge is spectacular. 9pm on Saturday and Sunday they stop traffic on the bridge and a crowd amasses and the serpent breathes fire and finishes off by unloading a breath of water. It’s a lot of fun and quite a sight, unique to Da Nang

I loved this! Atmosphere was great. All along the river the lights were amazing before the fire show. The market right beside the bridge has great cheap food , especially the seafood. Also it's one of the cheapest markets for souvenirs I have been to yet (better than hoi an & Saigon). Also wonder along the river to the love bridge where you can get a padlock engraved and leave your mark.  The dragon breathing for was impressive and the water spout was also good. Doesn't last for long but is worth seeing. Yes it does get busy but we stood on the river side and watched  and didn't actually stand on the bridge. We could feel the heat on the ground so would have been hot up there!What other bridge in the world can do this??

A must see for hen in Da Nang. We should really close to the dragon and could feel the heat from the fire and never anticipated the little surprise. I’ve been completely showered with water at the end.
It was amazing.. This is on Saturday and Sundays only.. Exactly at 9pm.. Lasts for 10 minutes... Beautiful idea and a place for the tourist to go
An ideal place for a walk, you should come experience it on weekends from Friday, Saturday and Sunday. Watch the dragon breathe fire and water. The atmosphere is very bustling.
Very safe with Cau Rong spraying rice and water, I don't know how to do it. Minh and his friends have a beautiful relationship here.

This is the most beautiful bridge in Da Nang. It spits water and fire every Friday, Saturday and Sunday night. It is the worth to come here, enjoy the show, walk over the bridge, to Love Pier and the Carp here. Pro tip: reserve a balcony table at near by La Cabana steakhouse to have dinner and watch the show.

The location : Sontra Night MarketTime to visit : weekend sat and sundayCame here around 7 PM to have some seafood and souvenir. Find a seat infront of night market order a fresh juice and ready for the dragon breath show

Friday night, and I passed by here. I was wondering why there are a lot of people around, and I just realised the dragon bridge show. So I stopped to watch. It was worth it. And it's free. Just don't stay near the head of the dragon if you don't want to feel the heat from the fire and if you don't want to get wet with water.
I couldn't imagine that a simple bridge with such a construction could look so great, totally inconspicuous during the day but really something special at night.
We went to the bridge to see the Fire and Water from the dragon and it is definitely worth a trip and you can look around the market before or after the dragon show.
The dragon show is amazing. Worth getting there at least 30 minutes beforehand to get the best view. Unless you are prepared to get wet stay below on the concourse
must-visit place when you are in Da Nang. it looks amazing when the night comes, great place to take a photo too!

Must visit place if you are in DA Nang . Plan to be at this place during the weekend like Fri,Sat or Sunday as there is fire and water show in the evening after fully stopping traffic movement, which is spectacular and is worth seeing.
Please visit this Dragon Bridge on Weekend Sat  and Sunday 9 PM to evidence the life coming to Dragon ejecting Fire and Water. Must visit if you are visiting on Weekend to Da Nang in Vietnam

Impressive site to see. At 9pm on Friday, Saturday and Sunday the dragon breathes fire and sprays water which is very cool to see! Its also right next to a large food market and riverside restaurants. The area is extremely busy around the time we went at 9pm

Our taxi driver told us about this event when he picked us up from the airport .. so naturally we had him collect us and take us to the fire spitting dragon 🐉 Wow ! What a spectacle  we found it absolutely fabulous and what a crowd puller!! Typically a  tourist "must see" but don't stand on the bridge facing the dragon or you're going to get a soaking of water!I can imagine that it  could be an "inconvenience " for local people because they shut the  bridge, but please do not forget that the tourists happen to come to your city with many Dongs!!! There are quite a few negative reviews about this  fire spitting dragon ... to those who say "don't bother"  I say in return "Bah Humbug"   Get over it!  And I'm sure that people can make up their own mind..
We visited a nearby bar DRAGON BALL ROOFTOP BAR, an ideal place to watch the fire-breathing dragon performance and the panoramic view of Da Nang city.

I wanted to see the Dragon Bridge breathe fire four years ago but unfortunately spent the night in A&E.  This time I managed to see it and it was everything I wished to see!  Another thing ticked off my extensive bucket list!
The large Dragon Bridge is beautifully designed and is a highlight of the city. Whether taking photos in the morning or at night, they have different beauty.

visited here on 10 September Sunday,came to see the golden dragon spraying fire and water at 9 pm, before 9 pm, many visitors already came here, and picked an observing point in advance to watch the dragon’s fire and water show. be noted that you should stay away from the dragon’s head, or else you might get wet during the water-breathing show. the show usually lasts for about 15 minutes. the dragon head will spray fire first, and then water. after the show, many visitor will take photos at the both side of the bridge, i also took some pics with the dragon head.the show only held on Saturday and Sunday night, if you want to see, make sure you come earlier to snag a good spot, cuz it might be very crowded.

A visit to Da Nang is also a must to see the Dragon Bridge. If possible, you should be on or near the bridge at 9 p.m. on the weekend. Then, in addition to the amazing lighting, there is also a fire and water show. We took a boat trip to the bridge for little money and watched the spectacle from there.

Excitement that will surprise you! Don't just watch this dragon fire and water show from afar, but head to the bridge 30 minutes before and wait for it to start, drenched in sweat. The moment you are disappointed by the first fire performance, you will understand the terror of water (lol). It's a rare surprise show these days. Recommended!
I really enjoyed this trip and the host very much. I felt it was well worth the money and again a great guide.
We went on a Sunday night to watch the show, it was fun entertainment to experience as a one off thing to do.

It was a fun show but it gets really crowded! I personally preferred to watch it from below the bridge on the side, rather than on the bridge due to the crowded place! If you stand on the bridge near the head of the dragon, be ready to get waves of heat and to get wet with the water!
A bridge unlike any other. Not only does it actually breathe fire, but it's also beautifully illuminated at night.

Walking along the bridge was clean and good sea view and river view, in the evening the the Dragon lighting was up and attractive. It was safe to walk around the surrounding area from heavy traffic along the riverbank with very wide footpath.
Fun time on the riber cruise in Dananag and watching Dragon Bride fire show. After Las vegas show at Mirage this comes next best show
Unusual bridge. Better to explore ot by legs, it is not that long.Note: locals say every Friday and Saturday night they use fire to show dragon breath.

Lovely spectacle of the city and it’s free. It operates at 9pm at the weekend and lasts about 20 minutes blowing both Fire and Water. It attracts huge crowds and the bridge is temporarily closed for the spectacle to take place. It’s a must see when visiting the city if you’re lucky enough to be there at the weekend.

Username: Ajay Arora ( Food N Travel Diaries )
Comment: This is the heart of Da nang. Incredible being here at night with all the lights on. Weekends 9 pm the bridge is closed for the dragon to breathe fire and water which is a must view spectacle. Luckily we were there in a Sunday and just managed to reach around 9. Even during the day, the bridge stands majestic. Lot of space to hang around and soak in the views

We drove through this iconic Dragon Bridge across Han River during the day . Although we did witness the intermitten changing colors from led lights on the dragon in the evening , its a pity we could not manage our time to watch this gorgeous Dragon breathing fire and water show at 9.15 pm on Sunday,  as we had to leave for our dinner. Maybe next trip !!!
Beautiful bridge to take a look at if you happen to be in Da Nang. Especially beautiful after dark. On weekends at 9:00 PM, the dragon breathes fire and then water. Nice to see :).
It was a great experience, nice attraction, nicely done and definitely will return again soon. Wi are planning to get back
Argualy the best bridg to walk over in da nang .best at night when it lights up, all different colors.
It's centrally located and very beautiful at night, changing colors and creating a charming atmosphere.
A must go. The dragon bridge is so beautiful. You must go before 9h pm . They have a night market near by . Fantastic!
I went to Da Nang and visited the most famous bridge there. Every Saturday evening, there is a fire and water show on the bridge.
A beautiful bridge that continuously changes colour. The Dragon only breathes fire from Friday to Saturday nights followed by large water/mist bursts at 9 pm. Easily seen from the Night markets.
Looks lovely at night  good place to use as a landmark, also near the GB Yatch where you can have a drink and look across to it
One of the iconic bridges of Da Nang. Great pedestrian walkways and views of the river and other bridges. The dragon itself looks beautiful both up close and from across the river.

It’s a memorable trip with my friends to visit Dragon Bridge this weekend, we waiting for the Dragon Show start at 9pm and very very happy to enjoyed it under the head of dragon, of course we can record a lot of videos and pictures about this show. Next time I’ll try dinner cruise through the river 🥳🥳✌️

Nice Bridge which is mainly used by motor vehicles and very few pedestrians walking on the bridge though there are pedestrian paths on either side. Nice experience to walk the entire length of the bridge and have a nice view of  both sides of the river bank.
Firstly it’s in middle of town and everytime you go you go through this bridge , on weekends they have fire show and lights keep on changing . There is night market near to that and garden also

All kinds of meat here, such as beef steak, lamb, and pork. I opted for a steak with a salad. The steak was properly cooked to my specification: medium rare. It was very tasty. I washed it down with a glass of wine recommended by my server.
It has the fire and water show on every Friday, Saturday, and Sunday. Quite crowded here, you can take a chance to visit night market as well.

Dragon Bridge is an iconic place in the beautiful city of Danang. I had the pleasure to see the lights change ever night, as my 180 degrees hotel room faces the dragon. So cool to watch the dragon as I sip my green tea at night.  The dragon breathes fire every Friday, Saturday and Sunday at 9 pm.
Massive dragon themed bridge in the heart of Da Nang, situated near to a night market. If you go on the right night at 9pm the dragon let's out flames and water spouts.

Passed it during the day and couldn't see what the fuss was about. At night was a different matter, it's a beautiful golden lit, leaping dragon. We were not there the day and time of the fire show. Hopefully one day we will!
Beautiful view in daytime but the real dragon is on weekend nights . Mesmerizing view. Great crowd. After that visit the night market near by for cheap shopping

One of the reasons that we stayed in Danang on a Saturday was to see the dragon Bridge. Saturday and Sunday at 9pm the dragon breathes fire and water, each for a minute or two. The bridge attracts many visitors but do not stand in front of the dragon as you will stand a good chance of getting wet. Under the bridge several people gather for ballroom dancing.

seriously look like da nang city planner wants their city be the best and they try to put all efforts to make city beautiful and every attraction is unique, dragon bridge is one of the best place to visit and specially at 9 pm on Saturday and Sunday when you can see the fire and water from dragon  face
If you’re visiting DaNang, try to go to the Dragon Bridge on a weekend and catch the Fire and Water show at 9pm.  You will not be disappointed! Careful where you stand unless you want a splash 😜.

A dragon-shaped bridge, it may seem like any other bridge during the day. On Saturday and Sunday evenings, crowds gather to watch the spectacle of flames shooting from the bridge's mouth, followed by sprays of water. The show begins around 9:00 PM on the right (east) side of the bridge.

This is an iconic bridge in Da Nang. Do remember to catch the fire/water performance every weekend at 9pm. There will be quite a crowd waiting for the performance, so do arrive slightly earlier if you want to get a good spot.

The scenery on the bridge is beautiful, especially at night. As the lights come on, standing on the bridge not only offers scenic views but also a refreshing breeze. However, the wind picks up as the night goes on. If you go in November, I recommend wearing a light jacket in the evenings to avoid catching a cold.

Dragon Bridge in Danang in Central Vietnam is in the heart of the city, a vital communication, broad, big and the best part of it is its design, instead of just being another suspension bridge its makers made a dragon shape on both balustrades and from far and near it looks like a long big dragon lying across the river with vehicles going on it, the day time motorbikes fill the bridge along with other heavy traffic, at night on weekends and Fridays at 9 pm dragon spits fire and water alternatively, traffic is topped and a lot of people line up on both sides of the river and on parallel bridges to watch the spectacle, unfortunately, we missed it though we saw videos, this is the best part of the bridge shown a utility bridge was converted into a tourist spectacle by brilliant imagination. Another famous bridge in Bana hills s the Hands bridge again a semicircular walk-only bridge from a cable car station in a curve with very good views and with co0ncrete hands holding it , it's so famous n social media that Danang has more visitors due to this picture

One of the beautiful check-in spots in Da Nang. If you visit the Dragon Bridge, you can combine it with a Han River cruise to experience the scenery from the river. It's affordable and worth the experience.
1 very beautiful bridge, many colors. It's a pity that I couldn't capture it when I stood far away...
Impressive show starting at 9pm Saturdays and Sundays . The dragon comes to life. First spitting fire and after water.
Watch the dragon spew Fire and Water! Impressive and magnificent, the display only happens on Fri, Sat and Sunday at 9pm sharp. When in Da Nang, make it a point to catch this impressive show!

Visit danang on fri/sat/sun.. it is when@9pm, dragon fire show is there on dragon bridge..  Best way is to take a cruise, which is parked very near to where dragon fires.. after the cruise, go to novotel sky bar on rood top..Not to be missed!!
After having a delicious meal at Coba Pho, a famous restaurant located in downtown Da Nang,

A breathtaking and totally unique concept. Just having the idea and putting it into practice is incredible. The whole surrounding area is pleasant and offers a complete change of scenery. You really feel like you're in Vietnam.

We stayed in Da Nang for Sunday to catch an early flight. And the dragon show is for Friday, Saturday and Sunday. It might be different according to the travel season. The time for the dragon fire was sharp 9:00 PM. The whole place was lit up and there was a big crowd to enjoy the scene. There are a ton of vendors on the banks do the river. You can also book a ship and enjoy the view from the river. But we had a great view from a little far from where the complete dragon was visible.

The dragon bridge was awesome!! I stood by the Love Lock bridge and watched the show. The flames were impressive and I enjoyed the changing colours of the dragon through the night. There were lots people there so get there 15 minutes before to get a good spot :)

Wow! I was great to see the Dragon Bridge spraying fire at night, so we took a lot of memorial photos for our holiday in Vietnam. Maybe this is one of the places we like best in Da Bang, won't forget!

Visit the bridge at 9pm on Saturday and Sunday to watch it breath fire and water. Eastern end.On Sunday young adults play under the bridge different games and dance.Near the Eastern End on Saturday and Sunday night there is a night market.
This bridge is right across from the Vanda Hotel.  Our room offered an excellent view of the bridge and was spectacular at night with the changing lights.

My son and I have come down both nights of the weekend to enjoy this event. The bridge is closed a few minutes before 9pm and the Fire Blasts begin promptly. They are blasts every few minutes, and followed up by water blasts that convert to steam. All the locals still come to enjoy it as well. Food and drink is available. The Night Market was still working on the Beach side of the river. Getting a taxi home was bedlam in the first few minutes after the show finished (15 mins) so we walked home.

The city's iconic landmark is the Dragon Bridge, facing the sea. It's a symbol that every resident knows and has crossed. It's similar to many other bridges, but its location runs straight from Da Nang Airport to the beach. At night, the bridge is illuminated with beautiful colors. The biggest attraction for tourists is the dragon show every Saturday at 9 PM, where the dragon breathes fire and sprays water. Traffic is blocked for 15 minutes to allow visitors and locals to enjoy the show safely. I love Da Nang!
It was so much fun to see the fire-breathing dragon on the bridge for the first time. The dragon breathed fire, then finally sprayed water, causing people below to scatter in panic :))

The new landmark of Da Nang, you should visit on Saturday and Sunday evenings to see the water and fire displays. The bridge's walkway is spacious for pedestrians. It's also a great place to take some memorable photos.
The Dragon Bridge was a nice show with fire and water.  Restaurants for Vietnamese food were great.  Even had a really nice meal of sashimi.

Thanks to the taxi drivers who took me here without asking the cost. One of the best thing here was the night market under the bridge, I walked around here and see the music live show at there. And the city offered the Fire Show every weekend at 9pm. That was funny once the the Dragon watered, everyone got wet.
The night view around the Han River in Da Nang is fantastic!
Sharing with everyone a great spot to watch the Dragon Bridge spew fire and water – it's close and you don't have to worry about getting wet. Plus, you can even take selfies with the dragon!
On the weekends at 9pm. Don't stand too close if you're on the bridge because you'll get wet.  We took a boat tour to see it which was really fun

Walking along the Han River at night, the view of the bridge is beautiful. However, it's a pity I've never had the chance to see the dragon breathe fire. I'll definitely try to ambush it sometime before I leave.
Walking across at night, the bridge is beautifully illuminated with constantly changing colors of lights. Standing on the bridge, you can admire the romantic Han River.
If you visit Da Nang, the Dragon Bridge is a must-see. For local specialties, don't miss Quang noodles. And for souvenirs, don't forget the pandan and jasmine tea.
Danang possesses various beautiful bridges that's why somehow this city called The city of bridges.Nice to visit Dragon bridge
When I traveled, the Dragon Bridge looked very beautiful from afar, breathing fire like a real dragon. I hope the pandemic eases so I have a chance to go again.

The Dragon Bridge in Da Nang is most beautiful at night. It's especially stunning on clear nights. Unfortunately, the dragon didn't breathe fire tonight, so I couldn't take any pictures of it. Just across the bridge is a night market with lots of food. Walking along the Han River is also a great experience when you visit Da Nang.

The bridge looks very beautiful at night. I'm a second-year student in Da Nang, so I often cross the bridge, especially at night when I take walks with friends. It's a must-see if you come to Da Nang. I often have drinks at Nguyen Van Troi Bridge, looking towards the Dragon Bridge at night; it's truly stunning.

The Dragon Bridge has a very unique and attractive architecture, with a beautiful view, especially towards the Han River Bridge. On weekends, you can watch the bridge喷 fire and water, which is very attractive to tourists and is a must-see destination in Da Nang city.
A must-see spot when visiting Da Nang, especially on weekends, is watching the dragon breathe fire. Stand on the bridge and admire the romantic Han River.

There are several beautiful bridges over the Han River in Danang and Dragon Bridge is one of the most famous and attractive. Our hotel was in Hoi An and we were sorry that we did not have time to visit the Dragon Bridge on the weekend at night and experience the light show, when traffic stops, the dragon stretching the entire length of the bridge shines into the darkness, changes colors and also "breathes fire". We saw the bridge several times on the way to the airport and back. We saw the "Dragon" in all its beauty and length when we had an afternoon flight to Hai Phong and we drove along the side Cau Nguyen Van Troi, which is the main link across the Han River to the airport.
I traveled a long distance to visit the Dragon Bridge and found it even more stunning in person than in the photos. It's definitely worth a visit for tourists to explore and discover.
My family went to see the Dragon Bridge breathe fire on the weekend. It was even more beautiful than we expected. There was also a rain shower, which was really unique.
Take your time to walk over  and watch , it is worth , was there on a rainy day and could observe real Vietnamese life

We went for a drink in one of the rooftops clubs just to see the dragon bridge by night and Everything stops just to let this spectacle to happen and it was worth it really. Definitely something never seen before to must experience if you visit the beautiful city of Da Nang

The scenery is beautiful, with picturesque rivers and waterways. The Dragon Bridge in Da Nang, along with the night market and the Love Bridge, attracts visitors who come to admire the view, especially to watch the dragon breathe fire on weekends.

Username: Quang Nga Motorbikes Rental Da Nang
Comment: Beautifully designed bridge.
Da Nang is truly a livable city – modern, civilized, clean, and safe.
| can say this bridge was my favourite due my travel in Vietnam, specially every Saturday night when the fire dazzled every single person in Da Nang.
Danang has a lot of beauty spots. It's easy and convenient to reach the destinations, airport, beach

stayed close to the bridge last year when travelling to Da Nang in September.i had many chances to admire this beautiful dragon bridge,especially at night,the dragon will change its colors,very charming.on saturday and sunday nights,the dragon head will spit water and breathe fire from its mouth from 21:00 onwards,which is a nice experience.worth a visit(cars and motorbikes are not allowed before the performance).in the morning,there is another scene,the bridge is busy with motorbikes and cars.along the Han river,you can see cruise ships(at night also).you can sit at the waterfront area,enjoying the beauty of the river,the dragon,the bridge and the carvings along the river.
The Dragon Bridge is very beautiful at night, and there's a night market nearby, making it convenient for eating, having fun, and admiring the bridge at night with family.

In the evening, we took a ride with Xiaoqi to Longqiao to see the water and fire fountains. It was truly amazing; it was the first time we'd ever seen anything like it, and everyone was quite surprised. We also strolled around the nearby night market. Being in a foreign country, everything felt new and exciting.

Went for the Sunday 9 PM show. Started on time. Show only goes for 5 minutes.Noticed there were a few ferries on the river that pulled up for the show. Looks like a good idea, dinner in the boat and a little dragon show for entertaining:)
Best Burger in Danang.
the dragon beach is beautiful at night and day. in night which i haven't seen yet has a lot of lights. while in the day, it has an exotic feel

The Dragon Bridge is very beautiful at night when lit up. Every weekend at 9 PM, there's a spectacular fire and water show lasting about 15 minutes. You should stand at the beginning of the bridge to watch and bring an umbrella because you can easily get wet when the water is sprayed.
My child was incredibly excited to see the fire-breathing dragon, and best of all, when it sprayed water. It takes place at 9 PM on Saturdays and Sundays, I think.

This is one of special bridge in Vietnam 🇻🇳- Dragon bridge. One of famous symbol of Danang city. It owns unique design and display. Every Saturday evening, alot of local people and tourists will come surrounding area of this bridge to see Dragons breathing a fire 🔥 and water 💦. It is amazing.
Beautiful bridge especially at night. Hard to believe the police allow you to park on. All kinds of night life along the river.
The visit to the dragon bridge was amazing. How it changed colors. Too bad we didn't get to see how the fire roared. But also the city of Da Nang in general. I would like to go back there someday ❤️

I was unaware of the displays which occasionally take place and initially walked to the bridge to take photos of the changing colours. My hotel then informed me of the display which was taking place at 9pm on the forthcoming Friday evening.I found a good place along the river to see the dragon breathing fire after the traffic had been stopped but was not prepared then to see the the spraying of water. It appeared that I was not alone in this respect as those people who had gathered on the bridge were then were made to scatter quickly after the spraying started in order to avoid being drenched.Well worth seeing.
A very pretty bridge to see, especially at night when it's illuminated. The dragon changes color, making the bridge a very beautiful sight.
Very quick display but pretty cool to see. Nice walk along the river with plenty of food to choose from. Great night out.
You should go there on Saturday and Sunday at 9 p.m. The dragon exhales fire and then water. You should not stand to close the dragon or you will be roasted or wet. Should be there!
I loved it with sparky neon and colourful lighting. It has flame & water fire from dragon head every sunday. You can see it directly or choose a nice view from coffee shop around it.
By day or night this is quite a structure. Typical of South East Asia. Not more that you can say really..
Watching the Dragon Bridge breathe fire at 9 PM is a must-do when visiting Da Nang.

Dragon Bridge is undoubtedly one of the best highlights Danang has to offer. It's built over the mighty Han river which itself empties into the South China Sea. During evening, it illuminates the whole city and draws a huge number of crowd.

We scheduled a visit to dragon bridge and came to know every saturday and sunday they got a fire and water show from the mouth of dragon shaped in the bridge. It was amazing experience to witness this amazing view for both saturday and sunday this march 2020.
Spectacular. Especially in the evening, with all its lights changing color. On Saturday evenings at 9:00 PM, it puts on intermittent fireworks. Absolutely superb. A recent construction.
This is the second time I came to Da Nang City and I always come to Cau Rong at the to see fire-breathing Dragon. Its really really niceeee. People is very friendly in here. I love it !!!

The Dragon Bridge in Da Nang is truly beautiful both day and night. We were fortunate enough to visit and walk across this bridge, and the locals told us many stories and facts about its history. It's a source of pride for the people of Da Nang and the whole country. Even more special, we witnessed domestic and foreign tourists excitedly watching the "dragon breathing fire and rain."

This is a beautiful ornate bridge but it looks spectacular on an evening when it is dark and it is lit up. It's lovely to watch the boats floating down the river with the lanterns. You do get a bit pestered though to take a boat trip.
The Dragon bridge is the most interesting place to see in Danang. Breathes fire on Saturday and Sunday at 9h pm. They will close the street for the event, a lot of people come to see
Fab bridge day or night. At night it is lit up and changes colour. Situated near a night market which is worth a visit.

The bridge was cool to see for the first time just passing over the next bridge in the daylight, however at night when it’s lit up it’s great. I’ve never seen a bridge like it anyway!! Saturdays and Sundays 9pm it breathes fire and water. Word of warning - Don’t get too close to the head!!! ☔️

Beautiful place; on Saturdays and Sundays there's a dragon show where it breathes fire. Near the bridge there's a huge boardwalk for walking, and in front of the boardwalk there's a river. The place is safe and pleasant, as well as well located. There are several souvenir shops and cafes.
The show, which takes place every week and even involves closing the bridge to traffic,
Make sure you get there on a Friday or Saturday night at 9pm to watch it breathe fire and water on to those stood below!
Taking the river cruise up to the bridge and watching the fire and water eruptions from the dragons head is well worth it.

Hired a car and driver to discover Da Nang.  Drove over the bridge very slowly several times during the day to take up close photos.   Then stopped by the bridge on the side by the Dragon’s Head to take more photos to include the length of the Bridge.  Then you realize that the body of the Dragon goes under the bridge.Do NOT MISS the fire show from the Dragon’s Mouth.  It starts at 9:00 PM.  When the fire show is over, the Dragon then spits out Water.  It is an amazing sight.  Lots of tourists, so make sure to go early for a good spot to view this unique show.

the dragon bridge has 10 minutes show on Saturdays and Sunday from 9pm.. it gets really crowded by locals and tourists.. if your in danang it wouldn't hurt to check it out.you have some shows that take place by the riverside and also a huge market set up by the side of the bridge.. you got food custom key rings live portrait drawing  and many more.. it's worth a walk.
Take the time to get to this location. I believe on the weekends it breaths fire. Night time is the best to see the bridge and take some wonderful photos
The bridge is very beautiful. In the evening, everything lights up and changes color.

I highly recommend people to come here on Saturday or Sunday since they have a fire show from the dragon around 9pm on these two days. Grabbing a drink to enjoy the fresh air and watch the gorgeous bridges is the best experience in this city!
Beautiful! Unfortunately we were only able to go on a weekday, breathes fire on Friday & Saturday nights, but still very cool to see while here.

This is a famous bridge in the beloved city of Da Nang. In the evening, it changes color beautifully, and especially on Saturday and Sunday nights, there's a dragon show with fire and water at 9 PM. During the show, the bridge is closed on both sides so that visitors can enjoy it safely.

Dragon bridge was a cool thing to see. A very unique bridge that's worth a visit. If you do go, go on the weekend because the dragon on the bridge spits fire and water on ther weekend.  A nice ten minute show, nothing super fancy but it's free. Besides where else in the world can you find a gir breathing dragon bridge
There are several bridges crossing the river.

I’ve never seen a more artistic bridge.  The bridge itself is a normal passageway over the Han River.  However, the flare of having the structural supports take the magnificent, undulating form of a Golden Dragon are exciting to see.  On Saturday nights at 9 the dragon breathes “fire”
A very nice bridge, which lights up at night. We saw the fire show on Saturday, it was magnificent!

Every Friday and Saturday there is a show where the dragon spits fire - around 9pm. It changes color continuously so it's a sight to see anyway. Watch the show at the dragon head pillar. Market right next door with lots of good food.

The Dragon Bridge is amazing. I highly recommend walking across it at sunset. The views of the city and the river are beautiful. The Dragon breathes fire at 9 pm on the weekend. It lasts about five minutes. They also stop all traffic on the bridge. It’s a nightmare if you get stuck in traffic.

This place is the best for relieving the boredom of the night. It feels mysterious, as if there really is a dragon here. During the day, its location was great as a landmark for walking tours. It was such a welcome sight when I came across it while wandering around trying to find my way back to the accommodation. It felt like it was connecting the waterfront and the land in Da Nang City. Especially during rush hour when motorcycles pour out, it is overflowing with vitality, like a bridge of living life.
The Dragon Bridge was built in the shape of a golden dragon =))) The feeling of riding a motorbike across the bridge at night is really amazing. February has a slightly chilly feeling 💓

Impressive and creative suspension/arch bridge built over the River Han in Da Nang, it is difficult to take your eyes off this amazing architectural creation.  Constructed between 2009-2013, the Dragon Bridge appears to be gracefully snaking over the water below, and is colorfully illuminated at night.  On Saturday and Sunday evenings at 9:00 pm, this imposing creation actually breathes fire for approximately 15 minutes.  Our group toured the bridge during daylight, so unfortunately missed the bridge in its finest regal glory.
The bridge is beautiful both day and night.

If you find yourself in Da Nang Vietnam, it's really worth seeing this bridge at night when it is fully lit up and changing colour. if you are there on a Saturday or Sunday night at around 9pm, you will witness the Dragon breathing *fire. Many people gather on the bridge and surrounding areas to see this. * After the fire comes water so you may get wet !

Even in daylight, this is an amazing bridge, but, at 9:00 pm (2100 h.) on the weekends it becomes SPECTACULAR when, first FIRE, then WATER, erupts from the "dragon's" mouth.....for those 15 minutes all traffic is stopped. Go to the "head" end of the bridge and walk around to a good vantage spot as it becomes jam-packed. If you don't mind waiting, I recommend getting there at about 8:00 pm and find your favoured spot.....have your camera ready to capture the dragon in action.At night, the bridge constantly changes colour...... but the fire/water display is very spectacular.
A stunning architecture of a beautiful suspending bridge across Da Nang Han river..in a beautiful coloured dragon style shape....especially by night
Also worth seeing is the beautifully illuminated Hong Sen Bridge, with its multicolored lights.

I was so beautiful and amazing. We just walk along the river edge looking at all the light. I was amazing how they change color the site was magnificent. It was so beautiful to just walk along the rivers edge and take a seat and sit watch it.
The best time to visit the Dragon Bridge is around night time where the light change color. Would be back again
Why aren't all bridges created in such an imaginative way? Make sure you're there at 9pm on the weekends when they close the road for 10 mins!

This bridge is amazing, but looks way better at night when it is all lit up and changing colour. Also every Sat & Sun it breaths fire and spits water. Brilliant experience. Warning - you may get wet. lol
What an amazing bridge. It looks like a dragon and at night it changes colour. Really fun. What more to say.
Even by daylight this bridge is impressive, but it's beauty comes out at night! Every weekend at 21.00 the bridge spits fire and water. It is spectacular!

I never imagined Da Nang could be so beautiful! The view of the Han River is undeniably stunning. The Dragon will be spitting water and fire every Saturday and Sunday evening. But choose your spot carefully to avoid getting splashed, or if you want to be safer, go down to the Love Bridge at the foot of the bridge – you can stroll around and watch at the same time, it's simply amazing... Along Bach Dang Street at the end of the bridge, there are many cafes, bars, and pubs. It would be wonderful to choose a place with a high vantage point; the soothing music will give you an indescribable feeling. Alternatively, if you want to enjoy a cruise on the Han River, you can book a boat tour (listed price 150k/ticket, or possibly cheaper). Have a wonderful trip!

If you are in Da Nang on the weekend on Saturday and Sunday, must do visit the Dragon Bridge in the evening to see the show at 9h pm where the dragon will breathe fire and water. nice bridge, great view at night with change colour dragon.
The bridge dominates the landscape and helps the visitor to immerse themselves in the magic of the Vietnamese atmosphere.
It’s really nice and look like a picture. Should come here morning time with the clear sky. And bring follow the jacket 😀
Definitely need to go early and find a suitable place to watch it ..despite the show being okay...its the dragon changing colours that make it unique

The view is beautiful from both sides of the bridge. The tail side is a walking area with flower gardens perfect for photos, and it's a popular nightlife spot for Vietnamese people with plenty of bars, pubs, and coconut ice cream shops. The head side has a water-spouting statue and food stalls along the river. It's a relaxing walk across, the weather is nice, and the views are stunning. I highly recommend the Son Tra Night Market on the head side; it has cheap seafood, especially the lobster – a must-try! Also, try the shrimp pancakes (the thin, crepe-like pancakes) and the durian-filled bread.

I’m really not quite sure what’s special about this show… the bridge itself looks quite nice. Came 1hr early to get a nice front row spot to view the show! Only happens Fri-Sun at 9pm. However was utterly disappointed. The dragon only plainly spit Fire and Water several times, with intervals in between. Really not worth my time.

So, the bridge comes to life every Friday- Saturday- Sunday with fire & water show at 9 pm.
We reached there at 8:50 only to watch the shit show. It only happened twice & we wasted so much time there.
Catching the taxi was incredibly difficult that we paid 2 times the fare to get to the restaurant and not to mention there were only hand full of options available considering everything closes at 9:30. Can skip.

Something I don’t understand what’s the hype about this place when all you see is a dragon that blows fire 3 times 1 minute or more in between. And it only happens 2 times. But the people who are there is a lot, like it’s something real nice. For us it’s a waste of money.

The 9pm show isn’t worth it. There’s a few puffs of fire then you nothing for a few mins. That repeats 3 times then the same happens with sprays of water. Overall lasts 10 mins.

People start getting to the bridge 1 hour before to get good spots. There’s cafes on the right of the dragon which charge 30k/40k for a drink and a seat to watch the “show” but the view is impaired compared to the free spots on left side of the dragon. If you watch from the bridge itself you’ll get wet from the water.

If you’re around the area then sure go have a look but don’t make a big effort. Definitely don’t just get a night cruise to see it either.

Disappointing. We spent an hour there, crammed in with a lot of people. We were expecting something not spectacular, but entertaining—some music, special lighting, a bridge for that moment—but no, it only had two or three bursts of flame and a jet of water, which is basically similar to what we see when we go to the bathroom... nothing special.

Like many of the one star reviews here, I also don't understand the hype here too. Shoot 4 times fire and 3  time water and that's it.

But if you have never seen fire and water, please go ahead go there and spend money on double priced restaurants, river cruise and cab rides there and back... 😬

One of the biggest scam Da nang is offering.

Spitting fire for 10 seconds and pausing 1-2 min after the fires comes out again.

Ending it with water is just funny.

If you want to sweat and squeeze between crowds do it. What a disappointment
Far too crowded, cool if you’ve never fire or water before! Careful where you stand as the water spray goes a long way! Would recommend standing away as the crowd after it’s finished is chaos!
Nice bridge however the fire show isn’t worth the hype.runs for about 7 minutes, Hard to get a taxi & they ask to much money because you’re stuck in a situation to get out of there

Most over-rated attraction in Vietnam.  Stay away!  Take photos from a safe distance.  Wretched burning gasoline fumes are the by-product of the dragon flame.  Afterwards, the water cannons blast  from the dragon’s mouth, soaking visitors more than 60 meters away.  Do you wish to be cold and wet and miserable until you return to your hotel to change all your clothing?  This is the place!  Maybe it is more fun in the summertime if you wear flip-flops, swim suit and a tee-shirt.
Very much hyped by public. We were waiting and nothing much happened 🤨☹️ utter waste of time. …

Don’t waste your valuable time and money

Visited three times during my stay in Da nang, and waited from 08:30pm to 09:30 pm
No show at all

Dates from 23,24,25th May (Friday, Saturday and Sunday)

Really disappointed

⭐☆☆☆☆
One of the biggest letdowns of my trip.
I’d heard so much hype about Dragon Bridge in Da Nang  how it breathes fire and water every weekend night, how it’s a must-see attraction. Let me save you the trouble: it’s not. I showed up early thinking I’d beat the crowds and get a good spot. Instead, I ended up standing in a sea of people, surrounded by honking scooters, selfie sticks, and total chaos.

After nearly an hour of waiting, the “show” finally started if you can even call it that. The fire display lasted less than 3 minutes, and all it really did was spit out a few bursts of flame followed by clouds of thick black smoke. It looked more like the dragon had indigestion than anything remotely magical. Then came the water, which just sprayed from its mouth like a broken pipe. That was it. No music, no lighting effects, no storytelling  just smoke, fire, and water from the river.

To make things worse, traffic comes to a complete standstill on the bridge while this happens, so you’re inhaling exhaust fumes while being shoved around by the crowd. There’s no real crowd control, no information about when it’s actually starting, and the whole thing feels like an underwhelming afterthought.

Honestly, I’ve seen better entertainment from a barbecue grill. Definitely not worth planning your evening around. If you’re expecting a dramatic, unforgettable performance  lower your expectations. A lot.

I visited the famous Dragon Bridge in Da Nang to catch the fire and water show, which starts at 9:00 PM and runs for about 15 minutes in staggered intervals. Despite the rain, many tourists, including myself, were excited and patiently waited to see the dragon breathe fire.

While it was expected that the bridge would be closed to traffic during the show, it remained open, which slightly affected the viewing experience and safety.

A few tips if you're planning to go:
- Bring an Insta360 or action camera for great footage
- Wear a raincoat or bring an umbrella (rain is quite common)
- Wear proper shoes as the area can be slippery
- Get there early for a good spot

Overall, it’s a unique experience and definitely something to tick off the list when in Da Nang. However, for me, it’s a one-time event , I personally wouldn’t go back again. Still, worth seeig Once

One of the major but over hyped attractions in Da Nang. It is a modern bridge crosses the Han River at the Le Dinh Duong / Bach Dang traffic circle, providing the shortest road link from the Da Nang International Airport to other main roads in Da Nang city, and a more direct route to My Khe Beach and Non Nuoc Beach on the eastern edge of the city. The bridge was designed and built in the shape of a dragon and to breathe fire and water each Saturday and Sunday night at 9 pm.

The main span was completed on October 26, 2012. The bridge was opened to traffic on March 29, 2013, the 38th anniversary of the capture of Da Nang City by North Vietnamese forces (known as the Liberation of Da Nang in Vietnam) during the Vietnam War (known as the American War in Vietnam)

May 2025 Dragon Bridge is considered one of Da Nang's must-see landmarks. In fact, most people travel across this bridge when visiting this city. It's beautiful during the day, but even more stunning at night when illuminated. The lights can change color.

There will also be a Merlion display, similar to the one in Singapore, but this one has a dragon on top and a fish below, yet it sprays water just like the dragon.

I just came once to see it, so it's not something to the point where I'd say, "Wow, that's amazing!"

We went over the weekend, it was too crowded, and it was hard to get to by public transport. Honestly, it's not worth planning to go see it. If you have time, it's good that it's next to the night market, but I recommend going to the night market first, since it gets crowded afterward and it's impossible to enjoy it. Not particularly special.

You'll pass by in a car.

They breathe fire on Friday nights and holidays.

It's not a huge deal, but it's a culture that exists in this country.

Starting at 9 p.m., there are several fireworks and fireworks displays.

Finally, water is sprayed, perhaps for a ritualistic purpose, as a form of cleansing.

The amount of water sprayed is surprisingly large, and it's enough to cause artificial rain within a 100-200 meter radius.

The Dragon Bridge itself is impressive.

There's a night market in Longqiao, and I thought it would offer a wide variety of food, but I was a bit disappointed. From one end of the street to the other, it was all seafood or meat barbecue, and you had to haggle over the prices! So tiring 😖. Those who love barbecue will find it heavenly, but those who don't can only order Grab delivery from their hotel 😵😮‍💨

For me, it's not a big deal. First, the dragon breathes fire a few times, then the water pours down. We watched it from the quayside, drinking juice and beer. I think the drinks were the best part of it all.

The whole thing lasts 10 minutes, you can see it, but there's no real wow factor.

It was surprisingly underwhelming, lol.
However, the people who were eagerly awaiting it were full of energy and excitement.
As part of a fun festival, it was acceptable, lol.

It was cool, though...

After pushing through the crowd to find a spot where the dragon wasn't obscured by trees to see it,

you have to endure the struggle to catch a Grab taxi to get back to your accommodation.

No Grab available... If your accommodation is nearby, I recommend walking.

Otherwise, just giving up is also an option.

This iconic landmark 666m long Dragon Bridge spans over the Han River in Da Nang, Vietnam. The dragon breathes fire and water, and there's weekly show at 9pm on Saturday and Sunday only. The best place to view is from the 2nd floor restaurant at the DFC Danang Food Centre, besides the Danang Riverside Hotel.

Linger around the head of the dragon as that's where the fire will shoot out of. Mind you, it's not a continuous spewing of fire. Then, the water is sprayed out of the mouth from which the fire was spewing a minute ago.

As an adult, it was kinda uninspiring and underwhelming, but if you're a kid, I can see this being super exciting and awe-inspiring.

Still, the bridge is a Danang icon and the show is a part of this amazing city, so at least, go see it once.
