Then at 8.30 the tours start (we were warned about this and we were fine with it)  but what we weren't expecting was loads of tourists to try and open our room door and banging on our door. The staff however were lovely!!

50% under construction, not sure when it will complete but definitely not within this 3 months time. VND40K per entry for foreigner. It's just alot of walkway criss crossing around the premises, quite a number of souvenir shops inside, hence it felt more like a shopping area then a recreational area. Apart from that, I personally think it's quite dangerous as most of the walkway barrier is below waist level and super narrow. Imagine you have people coming in another direction. Probably not ideal for kids.

Went here and was thoroughly confused with its twists turns and steps. Can be a little tricky so be careful if with small children. Construction going on and no barriers or safety rails. Interesting to see but think arrows for directions would be helpful so you don't miss anything (and can find the way out!)
If you have seen Efteling, this is not necessarily a must-see. However, the entrance fee is low, so if you have time to spare, you can always stop by.

Myself, fiancé, his mum and younger brother all booked in to stay at the crazy house for 3 nights, we lasted 2.  This place is really unusual and fun to see and different to experience but only stay one night, the beds are comfy and fires are a nice comfort in the room, the hot water does not last long, and often everything feels damp. Admittedly the weather (wet) would not have helped. You CAN NOT nap in the afternoon as tourists constantly try to open your hotel room doors or bang on them... so very little privacy from the hours of 08:30 - 19:00. Check in was complicated and our wait for the room lasted 40 minutes (even though we should have been able to check in by this point) the staff members were very friendly especially those near the doors doing a good job a of security! A specialShout out to the night shift guy, he was so so so lovely!!! Worth a visit not a stay!

Strange house which is worth a visit. Basically a fantasy house which is still being added to. Nice to spend an hour or less. Entry fee was 40,000vnd (around 2usd). 5minutes on motorbike from centre Da Lat. Many coffee and refreshment shops around entrance. Thank price is increasing to 50,000VND soon.
Gaudilike house with special rooms where you can even stay for a couple of nights. Be careful with kids since they have some high level crossings between buildings with low fences.
Nothing special, but if you're in the neighbourhood, do stop by. Under construction at some spots...

The house looked interesting, but it tended to be crowded. We went with a tour guide, who said it would be less crowded in the morning. Although I'm Chinese myself, I have to say there were a lot of Chinese people there, and they were the only ones making a lot of noise.
There's not much to see in Da Lat. The crazy house is one of the few tourist attractions, but it is a true tourist attraction. A fun time for children and teenagers, and nothing more.
This eccentric building might seem somewhat gloomy and dilapidated with its asymmetrical windows and both real and imagined cobwebs.

But I knew it wasn't thing from the start.  Architect is a woman in her late 70's and has been building for a few decades.  It was cool too wander around all the stairways.  Staff spoke excellent English and took the time to investigate answers to things they didn't know offhand. Similar to the City Museum in St Louis, MO, USA but much, much smaller and had much less variety. I went with a guy from my hostel who works in construction and he was endlessly fascinated.
This crazy house is an interesting attraction where you can climb through interesting and narrow corridors and sit at the bar by the "spider". But don't expect more.

Crazy House is a controversial place, and few will like it. The architecture and concepts are stunning; creating such a complex requires a great deal of imagination. It's easy to get lost there; every corridor is unique, the staircases and paths are intricate and terrifying, and each room feels like its own little world.

A unique hotel. There's plenty to see. The architect and owner get 5 stars. But with so many Asians jumping on each other's heads and yours, it's hard to see anything. Especially in the half hour the guide gives you. You might need to go there yourself.

We just went for a tour here and didn't stay. The entrance prices were fine for a tourist attraction, and the only complaint I have is that there were so many tourists! The place was almost like a zoo and no direction for people to all go the right way and then trying to pass people on the tiny bridges was a little dodgy. The place itself though was pretty cool, reminds me of Hobbiton. The views from the upper areas our over Da Lat are stunning! You could also get a glimpse of the rooms to stay in and they look really cool, I can imagine it would feel strange to stay there at a tourist attraction, but they're super quirky and it'd be fun! Overall it was pretty good, just maybe try to go at the start of the day before the tour buses arrive as it's a bit small and gets overwhelming with all those people crammed together.

It's very famous in Da Lat.  When we were there, there are a lot of constructions still going on.  A lot of tourist, little chaos.  Be careful when you purchase the entrance tickets, they tended to charge 1 or 2 people more when it's a big group.
This isn't my first trip. Nothing's changed in eight years. It's best to go in dry, sunny weather, but that's hard to find in Dalat.

It's basically a hotel constructed like a maze of trees. As it's practically on every tourist itinerary, it can get really crowded so as a hotel, not an ideal restful place. Suspect the owners make their money from tourist entrance tickets than hotel fees. As a tourist attraction, it's a maze for people to climb up and down, looking into uniquely decorated rooms. And some steps can get steep and narrow so need to be cautious especially if wet. I'd give it a miss if my itinerary is tight.
It was fine and funny but it's not one if the best thing to do in Da lat. By the way if you are fan of architecture or with kids you'll enjoy it.
I was amazed by the architecture here. As an architecture enthusiast, this was a memorable experience for me. It was fantastic.
My boyfriend and I visited the crazy house this afternoon while visiting Da Lat. Very cool concept, very brief tour.. We stayed for about 20-25 minutes. 40k each entrance fee.

The house itself is nice to walk through. It wouldn't look out of place in Efteling. If you want to see everything, expect it to take a while, as there are many Chinese tourists taking a selfie after every step and the high paths aren't wide enough to overtake. Go as early as possible, then it won't be too bad.

It's a deceptively large place that is, well, a bit crazy.  Not too much to do but wander around and look at the crazy architecture.  Some of the walkways are pretty high and not 100% safe, but had no trouble getting around, there are a lot of steps though.
Crazy is the right title for this house. It's more for Kids to be explored, than for adults to be visited. For those, who like Disneyland, go there. the other won't miss a lot if they don't see it.

Somewhat clearly resembled Gaudí's houses in Barcelona, but those, for all their atypicality, were very cleverly made in terms of engineering and the convenience of residents. Immediately, the house was indeed "crazy" and was clearly built with the desire to surprise and shock the outside viewer and was clearly not intended as a place of residence. So the few hotel rooms here are clearly not shiny and are designed, rather, for people as empathetic as the author of the project herself. For a change of course, it is worth visiting, but for the special sake of this "miracle" it is definitely not worth going here, especially if you have seen Gaudí...
A truly odd building with access between buildings often by what seems to be stairs build onto tree branches.  The imaginative architecture makes the admission cost worthwhile
Very nice pastime. You always discover new corners and paths. Should definitely visit if you are in Da Lat.
Complex of original architecture. Space is limited but the whole is high. Note that some passages are narrow and impressive for people afraid of the void. To be included in a city tour

Name of the place really says it all. House is crazy and is an interesting building to visit because it clearly shows how genius the architect was. However it’s not a place for everyone and some people won’t find it interesting as it’s not related to vietnamese culture, spaces are rather small and there is a lot of stairs.
What a ‘ crazy’ place !!  Must have taken years to build. Very quirky and a bit of fun exploring for an hour. Lots of narrow staircases to quirky little rooms.

Not particularly impressed by the place, the beautiful views from above, but very many people, the narrow staircases upstairs with the distrustful railing, was even in places scary. But you can watch it once.

Atypical construction that deserves a visit, very busy place and guaranteed scramble.  Many groups but the decor is really very special and friendly. We had a preference for the very kitsch sea floor. Right across the street there is a small cafe selling wafers to fall.

Unfortunately we choose a very busy time when a few tours with loads of people were there. However, we still managed to explore this amazing property be it slowly as the Asian tourists don’t seem to understand that you can move out of the way when other people want to come past…🤬🤬 Like me they wanted to take photos but for goodness sake respect other people….rant over.Would have loved to have stayed here, the rooms are eclectic to say the least, every one of them unique in their own way. It’s a must if you are visiting the area.

A new experience to be able to visit one of the inns designed by one of the architects from Vietnam. And a house concept that is indeed made out of the box, because when entering the house, we might get lost, because every corner is confusing. And there are some areas where the stairs are getting smaller and the stairs are in a fairly high position. So you could say you have to be careful too.In addition to being a tourist destination, we can also try staying here. Because it is indeed designed as an inn too.To enter here there is an entrance ticket fee.If you are visiting Da Lat, make sure to include it in your travel plans.
A very interesting house & definitely a perfect name.  I would recommend it.  Not sure I would stay the night there for all the tourists ( like us).
the house is impressive the way its built. makes you go crazy going around it.overall worth a visit to see

A much better place than expected. The individual quality itself was not high, but I liked the various mazes, the unique atmosphere, and the clear sky and view. However, you have to get the timing right because tourists from the mainland were so noisy. Noise + cutting in line + roadblock 3-piece set. If there are many Chinese-language tour buses standing at the entrance, it is recommended to enter a little later.

The architecture and imagination of the designer/owner of Crazy house is brilliant. A crazy maze of walkways leading to different areas such as an underwater scene. Well worth a visit to this quirky place especially if you have children. We were surprised to learn that you can actually stay here. Think it would be quite eery at night!!

The Ghost House or Crazy House is, in my opinion, one of the most creative locations in Dalat.This is not just a house, but a whole labyrinth.The only small negative is the crowds of tourists) Maybe I didn’t get there very well)Overall, I recommend visiting at least once.
The villa is very beautiful, the most beautiful scenery in Da Lat that we visited, the architecture is amazing. Along with the most magnificent buildings in Da Lat.

The entrance fee is VND 60k. It's the work of a Vietnamese female architect who studied in Russia. The buildings are quite quirky, independent yet connected by stepped bridges, going up and down. It had just rained heavily, and even I, an older person, found it a bit tiring.
The toilet in the crazy cafe is the most disgusting toilet I have ever seen. The rest of the crazy house is great
A very bizarre experience. What must the person who designed, or rather conceived, this have been thinking? There is so much to see! Truly crazy. Be sure to read the story behind the design.
Really enjoyed my visit - even though I am terrified of heights and found  many  of the passages too scary for me. Well worth an hour or two to visit.
I went to Da Lat and was introduced to this very special place. Climbing to the top, you can see the whole city – a place worth visiting.

A lot of different paths to take to explore all the rooms & spaces.  It wasn't too busy on the day we visited so at times we felt like we were the only ones there.  Visiting Crazy House reminded us of some of the architecture in Barcelona.
When  imagination meets wherewithal you get a wacky place like this. By far the standout was the aquarium room. Would have been better with a guided tour to explain The Who behind the creation.

Much bigger than I anticipated.  Was great to see how an architect's imagination, undoubtedly combined with a bottomless budget, results in something quite unusual.Don't think I would want to stay in any of the guest rooms, as they're rather small , dark, and you'd have visitors walking right past your entry door (and peering in if you haven't shut them!).Onsite booths selling coffee, tea and snacks as well as separate souvenir shops.
The space is unique. If you're bringing children, be careful as the railings are quite low. It's located in the city center, so it's easy to get to.
Not a must see, but if you're staying in Da Lat you should go, because it's a unique house full of art and creativity. And you can actually stay there over night as it is a hotel at the same time.
This place was really fun to explore! It’s really cool - def not really safe for kids as it’s easy to fall - but super fun for us to explore. Cool architecture.

We visited the crazy house with our 2 children (3 and 7 year old). The idea and infrastructure of the houses are certainly unique and a wonder for the eyes. We enjoyed exploring the different levels of the houses but at points it did not feel particularly safe with our youngest. Great fun though.
The crazy house is truly crazy. A unique place. Everything is outside of construction sites. Very nice to see the vision of the developer. Highly recommended.

This place is worth the detour, especially with kids. I was a bit worried to watch them walk around the crazy place with all its slippery stairs going up to the roof and then down to the ground... did not feel safe at all... but they loved the tour.

If you don't like buildings with angles and straight lines, you'll love this place. It's like a trip through a hobbit house or an Alice in Wonderland adventure. Lots of stair climbing - some of them go to other rooms, some don't seem to go anywhere at all, and none of them are traditional stairs. They also have rooms for rent overnight - there's a bear room, a kangaroo room - the rooms are just as strange as the main structure. It's a really quirky place and you could easily spend a few hours getting lost in the many caverns and winding passageways before you find your way out.
Worth to visit this architectural monstrosity. It is like a giant tree house and play ground combined with spiraling staircases on the outside.
In addition to the hotel, there's a space you can visit if you don't want to stay there. The rooms are quite unique, rustic, with animal motifs. The underwater-themed lounge is spectacular.
Spent about 40 minutes touring the house on a day there was no traffic.  The owner is 80 years old and lives in the house in the center still.

Crazy house look really crazy, the design all look very odd and it is some thing very unique by itself. Must visit if you go to DaLat. If it is crowded just be careful because the stairs are very narrow.  If you are carrying babies or very young children you need to take extra caution especially after rain and it can be slippery too. Many design inside  and outside the building.

We visited crazy house during the quieter period in March ‘20 at the time of corona virus. It was quiet and we happily paid the 60,000 VND each entry.This place is crazy, and there are some really cool stairways and paths to follow. You can spend at least a good hour in here exploring. Beware if you don’t like heights some of the routes may be a little intimidating.Definitely worth a visit if in Dalat.
A must-see on Da Lat. It takes a good hour and it's fun to wander between the different paths.
I like this place, it second time visiting there, last time was 14 years ago, much more building . it more interesting  now.

During the tour, we visited a unique house. We were pleasantly surprised by the lady's imagination. Construction continues on the vast grounds behind the house. There's plenty to see inside the complex.

Not bad for the price, a big maze-like house. It'll be fun for kids and everyone else. If you've got a million or two to spare, you can rent a room. It should be awesome. I liked the honey moon house the best…
The Crazy House in Da Lat is worth a visit. It has winding paths and nothing is straight. You do need to have a good head for heights, though...

We explored many areas of this place and must say I was impressed with the architecture. The designer had an authentic vision and more is being added to it today. The admission is cheap enough so go and explore it. You can look into some of the rooms that aren't occupied to decide if you would like to book a night here. Pretty awesome place!

This is a really unique experience.Very clever idea of a person to build such a house in Dalat.It is built in the most unusual way and has to be seen to be believed.If you are in the region do go and visit as words alone can’t describe this place.
This hotel, I think you should definitely visit in Da Lat. You spend about an hour there, but it's worth it. It's fun to see how they've thought completely outside the box in terms of design.

The Crazy House was a fun little activity. We weren't expecting such beautiful architecture. The site is very artistic and very large. Much more impressive in person than in photos! Ideal if you have young children.
Interesting place to visit. Remind me of  Goudi building in Barcelona but of course different level. Awesome.

I wasn't too sure about visiting the place or not, but I had a few hours left to fill and being curious I decided to go for it. I didn't have many expectations, but I thought it was really interesting to see and explore. It's a bit like a maze, wandering through the stairways and peeking into the rooms, finding new entrances and exits and parts of the house which one was not aware that existed. Especially the mermaid room was super cool, so many little details...! I am very glad I visited, it's something completely different which is hard to describe if you haven't seen it - so if you are up for an exploration, definitely go for it!

Pretty good place especially for families but also interesting for everyone else too. 50 000đ per person at the gate to stay for as long as you want. I was done in 30 - 45-mins but a family might kill half a day here (along with some kid energy).

A cool complex best explored early in the morning. Many quirky buildings and corridors that are difficult to describe in words. Unoccupied rooms can also be viewed. A must-see when in Da Lat. Unfortunately, it gets very crowded from around 11 am onwards.

A very interesting and quirky layout, like Alice in wonderland meets Labyrinth! It is very crowded but sometimes it's good cause it makes you slow down. Costs about 60,000, which isnt the worst if you have the extra cash.
You have to see this place (once and not for too long). It is crazy and maybe one day I will try to see the sense of it (after my headache is gone)

Our day began at The crazy house, we had heared a little bit about The place but didn't knew what to expect. When we walked in a nice lady told us about The place at what we will see. Very helpfull otherwise we would be lost in there. We didn't stay there overnight but we wished we did because it looks like it adds something to The whole experience.

We visited this house during sunset. Its a cool designed house. Do not park your motorbike in the small street, a bit further you won't have to pay for parking your bike. Its a nice thing to visit in Dalat.

The aptly named house is a labyrinth of stairs and paths that interconnect and soar between the buildings in the complex. We had fun retracing our steps to try and cross every path. However, we went in the afternoon and it was very crowded. In most places the path is only wide enough for one person and many spots don't have much of a guardrail, so it was nerve-wracking trying to pass other people. I recommend going early to miss the crowds. They are building a new section in the back that looks like it will double the size of the complex. I was pretty done with it after an hour so I don't know that I would want to do more, but maybe that will help with the crowding.
Certainly the architect was thinking outside the box. Quirky to say the least.Cheap and cheerful. Good for an hour or so.

We appreciated getting a brochure and some instructions in how to proceed as we entered. But after the first building we were drawn everywhere and not understanding where we were. Maybe that’s the point. Quite amazing with steps and bridges everywhere. I give the architect/founder credit...so well built and not one 90 or 60 degree corner except in the rest room!

It’s a very odd place , but certainly worth a look. It has gaudi and Picasso vibes going on, and seems to be eternally under construction. It’s often busy and full of tourists taking photos for the ‘gram and generally being irritating- but still worth it!

Basically a maze for adults. I have a small fear of heights so found some of the higher steps and narrow bridges a little hard, but I did them so not impossible. A little expensive for what it is, but it’s one of a kind,.. so why not.
We weren't quite sure what to expect before arriving. We were pleasantly surprised by the size of the rental. It's a unique place and one of the few worth visiting in the city of Dalat!

This place designed by artist/architect Đặng Việt Nga who's 80 and still adding to this place. She acknowledges the Gaudi/Dali influences but seems to take to new levels. I'm not a fan of that surrealist stuff but got to admire this woman's dogged determination to get it built, and it's still growing. Problem with metal, concrete, paint as building materials is you'll need a full time maintinance team repairing, touching up, especially with several hundred ppl passing through daily, which by looks of it, I don't think they have.There's also rooms you can book but again, no quiet snoozing on balcony with half of DaLat passing thru.Definitely worth a trip though ...

It was a surreal facility that felt like a combination of individual interpretations of DisneySea's Ariel's Playground, Harry Potter, and Jack and the Beanstalk. It seems that it can also be used as a hotel, and I'd like to stay there next time...! This facility is still under construction to expand the facilities, but the main area can be enjoyed as a little activity by climbing up and down the maze-like stairs.

Any visitor will be unlikely to leave the place unaffected.It's surely a one of a kind design.If you hv time, like us then do visit.A pleasant enough place which will be memorable because its rather unique. However if you hv limited time, can skip it.

This is another place in Dalat to take lots of photos but there is no single theme expect for 'crazy'. When walking up the steps outside, take care to hold on to the side railings, for your personal safety.
What an amazing, crazy looking house to visit. Bought souvenirs at the shop and it was well priced. Would recommend.

Lots of tourists but it was very unusual and a good place to take photos.  Worth the visit. Cheap entry cost and cute cafe. The upper levels made me a little scared of heights as there is no proper railings.  I liked the changing design to incorporate different aspects of nature. Would recommend.

The place is really cute, crazy and cool buildings. You can walk around there for hours and still not reach every corner of the buildings. We were there for an hour and exhausted. The place is quite crowded with tourists but a great experience!

Our tour continues from riding cable car to strawberry farm then exciting visit to the famous Crazy House. The entry ticket is cheap and the place is quite small but lots of thrilling floors to climb in order to look at the amazingly cinstructed rooms and attractions.on each floor. At the highest (3rd floor- I was dustracted to count correctly) we took some memorable pictures in groups.However, our visit was shorter than expected because the place was too crowded with tourists so we headout earlier to go for our late lunch at 'Kampong Pandan'.restsurant Da Lat (Halal food).

Crazy House is exactly that- a crazy house! It is listed as one of the top ten weirdest constructions in the world, and for good reason. It is super bizarre and whimsical, kinda like a Dr Seuss meet Tim Burton story come to life, mixed with a generous dose of Alice in Wonderland, and sprinkled liberally with the Magic Faraway Tree. It has themed rooms, secret passages, rooftop walkways, winding tunnels, and strange gardens.
This is a place worth visiting on a private tour of downtown Da Lat.
Quirky design architecture expressing nature’s form - trees, cave like staircase and part are done up as guesthouse.Worth the visit.

There is usually an onslaught of tourists there so it is a bit annoying, especially those who like to take pictures in every possible corner. But if you survive that you will discover a beautiful and thoughtfully and wonderfully designed place where each space has its own uniqueness. However, if you have other things to do instead of visiting this place - do not hesitate.
It's a crazy house, everything about it is strange, the architecture is bizarre but very beautiful. I recommend visiting the place, you won't regret it. The ticket price is reasonable.
The house in under construction but still you can enjoy the rest, you cannot see some like it somewhere else, everything is unnormal you can take a lot of pictures there and enjoy a coffee

Guess the best way to describe this place is free form architecture overcomes functionality. Stairs that are so narrow, two little people cannot pass. Paths that are not the shortest way between 2 points. No direct paths in or out or up and down. Shops with one entrance because the other doors are locked. Steps that are intentionally uneven, or small, or occasionally large. Whimsical meets flights of fantasy in design. No overarching theme I could discern. Rooms seem a little on the small side for overnight stays if compared to a normal hotel.

A really nice and absurd experience in the crazy house of dalat. Only cost 60 000 vnd and we got some short information when we entered the building. Alot of fun walking on the narrow paths and bridges and finding our way to the top of the building with a very nice view of Da Lat.
Like it said ,crazy house. The way they design it ,kids will love it. More like photo opportunity. One of many attraction which is a must in Da Lat. But get ready to climb a lot of stairs
We liked it a lot, but the maze bar (I guess it's by the same architects?) in the center of town is even cooler.

Based on some reviews on TripAdvisor, we weren't expecting much. But we were pleasantly surprised! It's very unique and something you have to see at least once. The name "Crazy House" is very fitting. You definitely need a head for heights in certain places! The construction and the height of the railings are by no means up to European building standards. Our only criticism is that the theme inside the house isn't very authentic. You won't find any typical Vietnamese props. A few information panels about the architect or what motivated her to build this house would also be great! However, the view over Da Lat is absolutely stunning!

Quirky place to visit in town if you want to while away a couple of hours. I don’t regret the 60 000 vdn entrance fee ($2). You might want to skip some of the narrow bridges if you suffer from vertigo ;)

The unique architecture of this place creates a special atmosphere where you want to wander for hours, looking at the interesting rooms and halls...🙂A must-see for those who are in Dalat for the first time!

Interesting background and worth an hour or 2 to visit. I must say the design of the house is like nothing I have ever seen before. Should have gotten a private guide to the place , that would have been much better, unfortunately alot of info inside are in vietnamese,  they could do better by having them in english too.
Nice place for tourist. If you have kids traveling with you, do make sure you hold and accompany them as you traverse from building to building.

Our guide suggested crazy house as a way to pass some time before our flight in da lat. It’s one of the top weird pieces of architecture in the world. It’s definitely entertaining to walk around and the climb to the top offers beautiful views. Not for the faint of heart though as the railings are low and you’re quite high.
Stopped here with friends for around 45 minutes. Had lots of giggles and fun photos taken in this wacky place. Its quirky and strange but worth a look.

I thought the attraction would only be suitable for children, but visiting the crazy house is charming and spectacular - it is a creative site rich in visual and experiential content for the whole family. The only reason I took one star off is safety - the routes on the site are a kind of roller coaster on foot and reach significant heights to the railing that is too low and I was afraid for myself and the children. Highly recommended for all ages!

Spent around 45 minutes here wandering the bridges etc. Great views of the city. For 6000VD you have a visual experience of Tim Burton meets Salvador Dali architecture. A bit of fun but not a must see. Go late afternoon to see it in daylight (we did not) but catch the setting sun.
A very interesting place. The buildings are even more spectacular than they appear in photos. Climbing up and down, I felt like I was in a fairy tale. A place well worth visiting.

Nice visit, construction style similar to the building on Hayarkon Street, nice for beautiful pictures and on rainy days, suitable for children, although you should note that some of the railings are low and can be dangerous, recommended if it is part of a trip

Crazy house, situated within a busy district. Surprisingly, when you really walk around inside this crazy house. You will tell yourself is really crazy. Good to see how they really do it but not for the weak legs. Quite dangerous if you walk up those small high passage way. Is really crazy.Try it, good to explore.
What a cool place, we had fun exploring.  Very clever designs. Architectural pleasing.   The extensions look great as well

If you enjoy photography and have a vivid imagination, this place will be your paradise. It's not large, and the vine-covered stairs are fun to climb. The restrooms are clean. It would be even better if there were English explanations of its historical background.

It is fun and surprising to discover all the buildings – if you are lucky, you can also view the (unoccupied) hotel rooms, each with a different theme. Go towards the late afternoon/evening so you can see the whole complex both in daylight and illuminated. After your visit, you can enjoy a nice meal at the restaurant Le Chalet opposite.
Very interesting architecture, and definitely worth a visit. It gave me the impression of being in a Gaudí house with an Asian taste.

The design of the Crazy House is definitely crazy inside out! Be careful when taking the stairs as most of them don't have proper handrail. Be extra cautious if you're visiting when it's raining because the stairs are narrow and slippery. Some parts of the house are still under construction. Overall, I had great time there although there's nothing much to do, but the architecture really amazed me.
Da Lat is a beautiful mountain town. During my time here, I became particularly enamored with its clouds—playful and adorable clouds.
The place is beautiful, but very unsafe... The bridges are very narrow and the railings sometimes don't even reach your knees. Not suitable for people with vertigo...

This truly bizarre area is located about 1.5 km on foot from the city center, making it easily manageable for a stroll. Entry costs 50,000 VND, which is just under €2. Winding paths, stairs, and staircases lead through whimsical formations and rooms, some of which can be booked in advance as accommodation. However, considering the hundreds of people scurrying past my room, I would think twice about staying there. The steps are very slippery, and some of the railings are only knee-high. Caution is advised, especially with children. All in all, it's a fun experience that is constantly being expanded and developed.

It was raining hard when we visited so we did not able to spend most of our time checking every corner of the place due to the rain. The place is cool and artistic, just a bit expensive for the entrance fee. And you can't walk around without getting soaked in the rain, but I like the place. If you are in Da Lat, Vietnam make sure to pay a visit.

Yes it really is a Crazy House, and the 80 year old architect still lives here with her family. You can stay overnight if you want. The “house” is based around 4 tree trunk like structures with many nooks and crannies, many staircases ( some of which gave me vertigo), rooms themed by the environment ( eg the ocean) and by countries ( Kangaroo room, bear room, eagle room, etc) It’s easy to reach on foot from DaLat town centre. Pay entrance fee at the door and listen to an info session about the architect and her inspiration. While there are a lot of tourists, it’s such a big complex with so many pathways that it’s not a problem. I would not recommend visiting on a rainy day as I think the stairs would be quite slippery. My favourite is the ocean inspired area. Great views of DaLat from the upper levels. Children and adults will find interest in the Crazy House.

We went to take a look here on a rainy day. Every corner is different. Sometimes you can peek into one of the hotel rooms. We were there with a group. Some thought it was really nice to see, others didn't care for it at all. Tastes differ. You know best how you feel about it. You can walk on, through, and past everything. And they are expanding. Keep in mind, you are not alone there, even when it rains.

Definitely worth a visit for how strange the architecture is and how fun it is to explore the different paths, stairs and tunnels through this house. Parts of this house are under construction to expand the property but there are still many rooms to walk around.
The design is safe; it's a place worth visiting once, not multiple times! It's a little dangerous if many people go up there.

We were not sure about this place, but someone on the plane recommended it to us so we gave it a go.  The kids absolutely loved it.  They walked all over and took photos from the very top.  If you are worried about heights you may want to give it a miss.  An easy couple of hours here though.

Felt that the intro by one of their ambassadors was really well done. She was very knowledgeable and sweet. The place itself really is crazy - great views, very interesting. As with any attraction, it was busy particularly with tour groups. Finally, some safety officers might have something to say about the walkways... but worth it for the views!
One of the must-sees in Dalat was the Crazy House.

Crazy house is one of the most interesting and strangest places to visit in Da Lat. Words such as remarkable, mysterious, unconventional, supernatural and queer  come into one's mind as we see the images of the buildings. Eerie  and sinister  feelings cannot be avoided for those who are not used to weird and bizarre buildings. A must visit for those who are adventurous., curious and inquisitive.

Wouldn’t stay in the hotel as all the visitors walk around the rooms, but it’s an interesting place to visit, very different. If you are already in Dalat, I think you should give it a go, because you probably won’t see anything like that anywhere else. Maze of staircases, hidden rooms and funny creatures. Some of the stairs are pretty high (at least for people afraid of heights like me), but offer great views over the city.

An amazing piece of architecture, you might love or hate it, much bigger than I expected, a Gaudiesque experience of winding pathways, cave-like rooms, nooks and crannies, surprises everywhere. Definitely worth at least 30 minutes visit. The guest rooms are all different and hidden away but you can look inside if no residents. It would definitely be an interesting experience to stay here! Quite a lot of tourists wandering about, lots of photos!  a few gift shops but all the same and not much in good souvenirs, to be honest, it's all a bit tacky. There is a cafe on the ground floor. 50k entrance fee is worth it to see this adventure in architecture, really fascinating! Would be slippery if raining.
Very crazy and a fun place to spend a few hours wandering up and down all the weird staircases while peering into the different rooms. A unique experience and worth seeing!
Amazing building. Kids really enjoyed it. Good for an hour. Kids said if given a choice between crazy house or 100 roof cafe they would pick 100 roof cafe

This place is amazing! Creative, colourful and dreamy! It's bit of pity because lots of place and shops are under construction 🚧, would be look great after this, having nice coffee in `aquarium ', relaxing and enjoying place

We had read reviews saying it was boring and that they regretted paying for entry. We got there and you can’t see much from the street so we decided to pay to go in. Totally worth it! It’s $2 for an adult and like $1.50 for kids..... if that’s ridiculous for an hour or so of entertainment and amazing views then traveling might not be for you. We spent probably 50 min here and the kids loved it! Yes they are still adding to the house so there was construction going on but it’s amazing regardless.

Pretty cool/crazy house but extremely touristy. A lot of people and hard to get through it all easily. Should check it out but don’t need to spend a lot of time there. Enjoy a beverage in the porn aquarium. You’ll know what I mean once you’re there.

Definitely worth the visit. Only a small fee to get in and have a look around. All the windy corridors and steps make for a great wander around, there's so much to see and every room/area is done so well.Amazing piece of architecture. Gutted we didn't actually book to stay there, the rooms looked awesome!
Full of walking. Not suitable if you are afraid of height. But you can always get something to grab on. They offer accommodation but it seems less privacy to me.
Its a nice place to spend the afternoon.You can get lost there, but thats the nice thing to do.And if you want you can book a room there, crazy rooms.Entrance fee: 50k dong
The designed and style of this place is so crazy! I enjoyed walking around at the height level. There is many spot to take photos. In front of this place had a good yogurt you should try as well.
There are winding passageways, small nooks and crannies, and hidden rooms inside. There's also a viewing platform at the top. Those who are afraid of heights might not dare to go up.

If you have a fear of heights, please reconsider visiting. There are more exposed stairs on the outside than inside the building. My legs were shaking. The building is unique, but the interior design is a bit childish.
Cool place, we walked around there for about an hour

Hằng Nga guesthouse, popularly known as the “Crazy House,” is an unconventional building designed and constructed by Vietnamese architect Đặng Việt Nga in Đà Lạt, Vietnam. While it’s called a guest house and has bedrooms and there are bookings available at various online travel sites, I did not see any evidence of actual guests’ belongings. Furthermore, I did not see any bathroom facilities for the various rooms, so I don’t know how practical an overnight stay would be.Nonetheless, visiting the Hằng Nga, commonly known as “Crazy House” in English, is a fun adventure. The building’s overall design is supposedly a giant tree, with vines, caves, flora, and fauna. The owner has acknowledged influences of Antoni Gaudí (Catalan architect) and his organic style of buildings in Barcelona, Spain. That said, the house also feel “Disney-esque.”Some of the facades of the various building that make up Hằng Nga (the Crazy House) appear to draw on macabre faces, with other structures look like gingerbread houses.Nonetheless, the narrow, steep exterior stairwells (with low handrails) caused quite a few cases of vertigo among our group. If you have a fear of heights, this might not be the best attraction for you. But if heights do not bother you, it’s fun to spend an hour or so climbing the staircases up and down (inside and outside the structure) and exploring the various guest rooms and gift shops.

Place is cool, full of stairs leading to interesting rooms with drawings. Also great view from the top floor. Problem us the stairs are not safe at all and on a rainy day you can easily slip and get hurt.

Surprising for the special and borderless architecture such as under water world, hotel room with theme such as kangaroo, honeymoon etc. The site is currently  under expansion and believe more unique buildings will be built in years to come. 1 thing to take note, in my opinion those staircases are meant for 1-way passing as it is very narrow .

What can one day about this odd structure.  I liked the reef section the most.  Lots of narrow stairways to navigate through the property.  Very imaginative site with more construction underway to expand the experience.

It was fun but not a place for tall people or those afraid of heights. I’m kind of tall and I had to duck a number of times. This place kind of reminded me of something you might find at Disneyland. It’s worth a visit.
A visit before sun set is a good time.. the attraction is nice.. we had a great photos from  the top that looks so good!
The Crazy House is one of the 10 most creative houses in the world.
We visited the crazy house at the very end of our tour and although we were tired we enjoyed it! It’s so good to see how different it is and would really recommend for anyone visiting.

Places is cool to visit and the design of it is really sweet.It’s 50K which isn’t bad but it’s not massive.It’s also a hotel which I can’t imagine is great as you would constantly have people peeking into your room.
Crazy house is crazy.  But it’s fun to explore the building and amazed at the unique design.  Some walkways are narrow with low handrails. But plenty of spots for photo taking.

I was told it was a must see in Da Lat, and it definitely was amusing. There are lots of stairs taking you all throughout the uniquely constructed house/hotel/building. From the top you get a very nice view of Da Lat. It is fun discovering the different corridors but a lot of it was under construction and parts were unsafe. (Notably a tarp covering wet paint on the stairs which was rather slippery and no way to go around it.) I would recommend going if you are in the area but be aware it’s not complete and use caution walking around if it’s raining.
If your in the area this is a must. It’s just so strange and interesting to look at. Spend as little or as long as you wish here

The place itself is truly unique and fascinating; I want to explore it all alone and understand every twist and turn of this marvelous construction project. Construction is ongoing, so you'll literally have to climb, and definitely not alone; there are tons of people there. Bridges and walkways embrace and connect buildings at different heights, and the railings are very vague (some might find them scary). The buildings themselves house a hotel with small rooms (with windows; don't expect panoramic ones). Each room has its own style and is named after an animal. There's also a café and several souvenir shops, accessed via intricate corridors and staircases. The routes are so confusing that you might start your walk on the second floor and end up on the first floor through the fourth floor, in the same building, even though you were heading to the next one. The architect and ideological inspirer, a wayward and obstinate lady (already in her years), formerly from the Vietnamese bohemian class, who lived in the USSR for two decades, lives here.

One might wonder what's going on in his head, but one thing's for sure: compared to 15 years ago, the owner has a newfound sense of purpose. Before, he offered guests the option of sleeping in one of the rooms; now, he's taken this madness of excessive room construction to a whole new level. Each room is unique, as are the staircases.

The villa has many different themed areas, and there are rooms available for overnight stays. It looks really beautiful in photos. 😃 When I visited, the villa was still under construction, so I'll probably come back for another visit at the end of the year. This is a picture taken in the bear-themed room.
A really fun experience, definitely worth a visit. The entrance fee is cheap, and it is very fascinating to walk around and look at the building.

The villa's architecture is very unique, unlike any other style in Da Lat. A drawback of Crazy House is that during peak season, even with a ticket, you may not be able to see some of the unique rooms because they are already booked.

It was better than I expected. It isn't small and is designed like a maze, so it's worth exploring. The high places are a bit dangerous, so I wasn't sure if it was suitable for children to climb. There were photos and materials from the creators displayed in the middle, which were impressive. Recommended!

Well, I must admit there are many places similar to this is Europe or America. What makes this place so different are the different theme used to build the house which can also host people since there are rooms for accommodation ! From scary high raised building theme to under ocean theme, this place is lovely. And knowing that the place is under construction and new sites added makes it special.

This is an offbeat travel attraction. There is a nice place to eat across the street, as well....It is a particular vision, and it is crazy. I cannot say it is attractive, it's sinuous and vine-like...
One of the best sights in the area. Recommend visiting both at day and at night as they have an array of coloured lighting after dark which changes the experience.
A must-visit spot in Da Lat, perfect for young people. There are many photo opportunities, ideal for taking pictures with themed backdrops. It's a slightly adventurous and fun trip.
Basically, it's a crazy hotel. The rooms look like hobbit houses, and everything is connected by small paths, bridges, and stairs.
If you love Gaudi this house is absolutely worth a visit. We visited Cracy house with our two children and they just loved climbing the staircases and finding new cracy spots.

A surprisingly interesting 'Gaudiesque' pastiche structure that functions as a guest house and popular tourist attractions on one of the hills of Da Lat. A rollercoaster of walkways crisscrosses roofs 3-5 stories up which makes for a precarious walk above internal courtyards and high gabled rooftops. In areas this can feel both exciting but also unsafe, as the soaring walkways are narrow and the concrete railings very low in some places. Nevertheless, this mad- hatter's-tea-party of house is a good way to spend a couple of hours.
If you are in Da Lat and have a spare hour to kill that’s not a bad place to do so. Cheap to get in, cool architecture and some nice views from the top !

Definitely worth the visit and the entrance fee as a place to see. However could not imagine staying there as a hotel guest with so much noise from visitors day round and really very inconvenient communication routes in the place. Somebody's dream was materialised. Is it good? Is it bad? Definitely it is not my kind of arts. But it was the owners' vision and they have all the rights to put that vision into reality. And for that they should be appreciated. A visit before sunset is a good time allowing the viewing of the place in different types of light. Can be pretty crowded... Still, do not miss it while in Da Lat...

Not seen any currently operating hotels that look this... Crazy...? Winding and meandering walkways or corridors to your rooms and having to live with tourists gawking into your rooms through the day. Still... a must visit becoz of its uniqueness... Designed by a Vietnamese lady architect trained in Russia (among her many other accolades) but it could be more tasteful in my opinion. 😊 Don't miss the 3D trickery photo opportunity of being swallowed by a shark 📸😉
We visited Da lat for 1 day so we decided go this house .it was funny they design very good .some of them can make you scary .we also go to water fall.next time we will stay here 2days .

The crazy house (aka Hằng Nga guesthouse) is definitely a must see cultural artifact in Da Lat. Architecturally it’s quite unique and reminiscent of works of Gaudí and the striking Hundertwasserhaus in Vienna. The space has an organic quality, which integrates nicely with the hilly views of Dalat’s urban mesh that is intertwined with farms and pine forests.We found the information on its architect quite interesting: her family had strong ties with the party.There are a lot of tourists there so be prepared to wait for people to take photos while you try to get from point A to point B.
Its really crazy.. and stil under construction :D entrance is Imho overpriced but worth it.. No safety be carefull on kids... ;)
Very Gaudi-esque. Well worth a visit. Includes a restaurant and also serves as an hotel.Good views over the town.
The Crazy House in Dalat is an unusual building where you can book a room. It's a visit that's off the beaten track.

A funny house, built and expanded by the owner after her studies in Moscow. The house has countless quirky nooks and structures. It is very ostentatious and over the top, but that is exactly the point. Very nice, but extremely crowded with tourists.
And although we were a bit mentally prepared, as we'd been at the 100 Roofs bar that evening... Crazy House still struck me as odd.
The house truly lives up to its name!

If you are a little interested in art and architecture, we recommend visiting the crazy house in Dalat. The house is reminiscent of Gaudi's architecture in Barcelona and is a small labyrinth of stairs, rooms and viewpoints over the city itself.

It was way bigger than I expected. Didn't really expect a lot, I thought it might be a bit like the theme park set, but it was more detail. It was fun to explore of all those different styled buildings and to see the history & art of this area. Can't compare Gaudi's architecture, but it has its own color.

Crazy House Here I am in DaLat with the cool breeze of the night from the bus station until the hostel. This morning while having breakfast, I asked Wynn at the Reception, what's best to do for the day, he gave me a map with 3 various places I could visit by walking.The weather is very friendly and at first I decided to go to the nearest one, the 'Crazy House'. Well, it's another challenge for a Senior Lady to follow the direction and luckily with my amusing and intelligent way I found it easy!At my first sight from the gate the name of the place defines how it looks like. I was mesmerised..what an amazing place and it's a job done by a creative genius. You pay 50.000 VD to go inside.It's a funny building/place but interesting how it was built and surely nice for children. I felt the challenge to go up as there were several staircases wind across rooftops but the railings were very low for people like me especially for kids so after few steps up I went back down. There's  a nice café on the ground floor where I sat down checking the photos I've shot.It's a very unusual place so worth to visit and something that should not be skipped while in DaLat-Vietnam.

A hotel you probably wouldn't want to stay in. It's definitely worth a visit, as an adult attraction. Admission is $5. Everything is jumbled and confused, but overall interesting. The main thing is to be careful not to fall. Visit the Marine Room.
This house is huge and completely crazy; you go up staircases that lead from one house to another, with magnificent views of the city at times.

It's a house with no fixed shape, with winding staircases going up and down all over the house. Some spots are high and a bit scary, but I didn't dare go up. However, my friend who went with me was a little scared because she felt unsafe walking around. It would be very scary if there were a lot of people and they were cutting in line or walking past each other. Some parts still looked like they weren't finished construction.
We went in for a wander around. It was very cleverly designed. My only concern was that the railings were not high enough for people like me who are a bit scared of heights and young children.

They close half of it and you can't reach the top after 9pm Was still fun and whoever designed that place has issues! Note: you have to buy a drink before you enter. Fun and Trippy! Must go if in dalat
A very fascinating structure, but there are still construction sites in the middle. It's worth seeing, but I find it a bit overpriced for Vietnamese standards.
Great architecture. Certainly one of the most dreamy destinations in Vietnam. You should visit if you are in Dalat. Here you could have a great stay. You can also go on a day tour.
A bit disappointing when you see how often it is mentioned in the guidebooks.
Worth visiting , you can easy get lost in here with so many different passages. Some bits are really pretty.
It is an interesting visit and very unusual place. It does not take long to visit and it is quite central so worth a visit, but not something that cannot be skipped.

Better than expected. Thought its going to be an half hour photo shoot session but ended up spending 1.5 hour inside. Rather interesting to walk and climb around for photo shoots. Looks like they are expanding with some constructions going on. Think would be more interesting then. Good for photo shoots and can see the Dalat city view. There are rooms available for stay but i think i won't do so with so many people walking around the premise. Can visit if you are in Dalat.

Travelled from Nha Trang to the Crazy House just for one night, we were very much looking forward to this experience, the place is very much a tourist attraction and run as if they are distrusting of the clients which is a shame and a couple of the staff could have used a more friendly attitude....The place itself is amazing, and you can spend hours wandering around, its like Dr Seuss and Gaudi combined (very surreal),   our room was great (Pheasant Room) quite romantic and definitely suited for a couple only, the bed was very comfortable (actually the best sleep we had while in Vietnam) and we were able to get someone to light the fire in our room which was nice....My Friends stayed in the Land Eagle Room which was large and had two large beds in alcoves at opposite sides of the room (I liked this room very much) and it is suitable for two couples or a family, I would have loved to stay longer maybe in another room just for the experience...I would recoomend if you are looking for a crazy surreal place to stay, different from anywhere else..... Fun and definitely a crazy house!!!!.

Amazing buildings, we didn't know where to go because of the many stairs in every direction, we walked around there for an hour and it was worth it, we really liked it, recommend going! 50,000 per person for entry. The only thing is that the place is undergoing renovations and expansion, so it has reduced the quality a bit - in any case, it's worth going! Enjoy

So much fun exploring buildings that build beyond imagination. Unique and stairs connecting to each blocks you will never know where you end up with the next one. We have such a great time having fun  to the fullest. Credits to the architect. 👏🏻
Really fun to walk around and really lives up to its name! Spent a good 90 minutes here walking around! Worth the visit

A very nice place over all. When we just entered we were approached by a guide pictured below who had really good English and explained a lot about the house. It’s a shame that they don’t have signs showing were to go because once you take one wrong turn you’re lost for good but I guess that’s part of concept. Beautiful garden beautiful architecture and atmosphere.
The interior design lives up to its name: strangely crazy, perfectly suited for teenagers.

The Crazy House is incredibly creative; exploring it feels like navigating a maze. I wonder how many drugs the creator must have taken to create such a unique place. You can even stay inside; the guest rooms are located within the Crazy House itself. It's definitely worth the time to visit.

What an interesting place. Inspiring from and artists point of view. Would of been interesting to try to spend a night in one of the funky rooms if our travel schedule allowed but honestly never heard of the place till we toured it. Kids loved it but very young kids would definitely need to be keep on tight leash because of fall hazards.

The Crazy House is an enjoyable diversion in Da Lat.  You are able to stay, but wouldn't recommend it as your accommodation is on display.  Entry otherwise is paid, and you may wander through, and on top of, the 'house'.  Built by/for an architect and his family, the buildings have narrow, winding interconnection through and over the twisted paths.  The exterior looks like trees and rocks have been formed into this weird house, but the concrete facade belies this and hides an eccentric layout.  You can literally walk over the top of the buildings for some enjoyable views or wind your way through the various labyrinth-like rooms.  Be careful if you have a fear of heights.  The construction continues, probably funded by the entrance fees, but it is worth a visit.  We spent about two hours taking in the scenery and marvelling at an eccentric vision.

Usually, when I visit places like this, I don't get a particularly special feeling even if I have high expectations, but this place was fun. I think it would be fun to go with children, and the cafe inside is affordable and delicious.

This was actually quite impressive, with its Gaudi like stairs and spires, though the lady architect - who still lives in the building and who is still planning further expansion - was trained in Moscow.The rooms and grounds have whimsical touches, and some of the rooms are rented out to guests.  However, if there are too many busloads of tourists at one time, it is difficult to move around the narrow staircases, especially those that head towards the roof.
It’s a really crazy house; it’s even more crazy when one realizes that it’s a hostel or guest house, in which one can rent a room. Sooooooo insane...but lovely
It's cool to see crazy architecture like that of the Crazy House in Da Lat, especially if you can go inside.

I have been here since last year. This place was pretty cool and the architecture was great. I really like the designing of this place except for the time when I was here, the construction was still under working. But yes, highly recommend to anyone when traveling to Da Lat to see this great architecture though!
It was interesting to see what an artist has in mind :-) I wouldn't want to sleep in that room though... worth seeing, but also very touristy and crowded.
We visited this hotel with its very "Disney" style decor.
The visit is worthwhile even if it has nothing to do with the country's culture or anything like that.

A really odd place to visit but it’s most certainly not like anything you’ll see along your travels. Worth the entrance fee to explore the madness 🙈 can get super busy so maybe visiting earlier would be better :)

This place was great to stay at for one night. It is very busy during the day with loads of day visitors (until 7pm) who try to open you door so you have to keep it shut and locked. Be careful with young children when climbing the structure - it’s very high and the railing low.

The Crazy House makes you feel like you're walking into Alice in Wonderland's world. There are a lot of pathways to walk through. The level of detail is impressive. There are quite a lot of tourists due to the many photo ops that are here. Guests can stay in uniquely themed hotel rooms, though it's hard to imagine they have much privacy during the day. Some of the paths do seem a little precarious to walk on, mainly because the side walls are short. There seems to be constant construction or projects to expand the estate. If you like art or architecture, this is the place for you.

This place is truly a unique feat of architecture and engineering, it was really fun to explore all the different areas, tunnels, caves, interiors, exteriors and rooftops. More than once we got lost and found ourselves in a section we hadn't seen before, the attention to detail is mind boggling. The only downfall were the incredibly narrow paths so quite often you'd get stuck in a selfie-shoot traffic jam.Well worth a visit if you're ever in Da Lat. I would also recommend the maze bar/ 100 rooftops bar. I could be wrong but I think it was built by one of the apprentices/ builders who worked on this trippy treasure.

My first impression of the house was just average, looking from the exterior of the building. However My view totally changed as I went inside the house. What an awesome place!The artwork was very intricate and detailed. Every inch of the house was well-planed and designed giving visitors the feeling of being in a fairy-tale world.It is a must attraction for one who visits Dalat, Vietnam.

It is fun place to explore but beware that the crowd might make it difficult to fully enjoy. It is definitely worth once to visit. Construction is apparently still going on, not sure if and when it will end.

This place is truly amazing to see.I think staying one night would be awesome, but having hundreds of people walking past during the day, may mean you have to go out for the day. 50,000VND entrance fee. Very cool architecture and so many levels/rooms to see. The walk ways over the roofs are very high (5+ stories). Under big construction so would be cool to see it again once it’s done. The cons:- When the weather is bad, you miss out on a lot of the views from the ‘walkways’. - No information given on the construction, time, layout etc. and what the idea behind it was.
Very weird, precarious but worth a visit. Felt like a kid in a playground. Look forward to seeing it when completed.

The house has a very unique and beautiful design. Especially the Ocean Room, designed to look like you're in the middle of the ocean, very vibrant and perfect for photos. The winding pathways above resemble a dragon encircling the entire house. It's truly an ideal destination when traveling in Da Lat.
I didn't stay overnight, but it is a tourist spot worth visiting at least once. It is pretty decent. There are many spots for taking photos, but it is a bit dangerous for children.
A very interesting place to visit. Enjoyed exploring.. it was great that we could just roam around to explore :)  Quite a number of tourists visiting at the same time too.

Although some of it is still under construction, the prepared rooms and especially the ocean room is a work of art which everyone can enjoy.The tiny roads on the rooftops leading from one room to another are thin (1 person barely) and very confusing but for us it just added more fun to the house.

The only word that can be used to describe this place  is "crazy". It's design is unique, creative and crazy. It will make you feel like a kid again  and I can only imagine how children would would react to this place. It could keep them entertained for hours. Well worth a visit, but be prepared for lots of stairs up and down. My legs were burning by the time I finished exploring.
Loved my visit. Lots of stairs and amazing views of Da lat. Awesome restaurant across the road. Quaint litle rooms would be cute for overnight if you leave earlybefore the general tourists...

This architect was under heavy influence when he designed this crazy house. Nevertheless the result is astonishing. The kids ran around for over an hour and had lots of fun. The only thing to note is that some of the rooms are under renovation/construction Go Go Go
You will amazed of the place, there are some area that are under renovation they said it will be finished after 2yrs.
You must see this unique house to believe it. I felt like Alice in wonderland! The imagination of the owner/architect is unbelievable. So many twists and turns

You need to pay a small fee to go in; suppose for maintenance and upgrading works. I was told that the Crazy House is always a work in progress as the lady owner is still exploring more and more ideas. A place that you can also stay - cheap accommodation (but I will pass it as there are too many visitors). An interesting, colourful and it reminds me of childhood dream of wanting to built a tree house and hiding in there. A good place to visit and have some fun.
My parents like there very much ..we stayed there for over 2 hours for taking photoshop Luckily it was not raining that day .

For most other tourists, the reason for coming here is simply curiosity because it's "unusual and unique," and I came here out of the same curiosity. But during my visit, I realized that achieving this "uniqueness" and "unusualness" required immense dedication and a long process of nurturing the impossible into the possible. It's a work of high artistic value, carrying profound meaning that each person will surely interpret in their own way.

While visiting this place, I thought i would have loved to run around this definitively crazy house as a kid with a group of friends.  The adult thought someone was on a trip...Not too sure about the point of this house but does there need to be one? Spoiled daughter of party apparatchik's indulgence or amazing Gaudi-like talent? Jury is still out but I would be lying if I said I didn't have fun visiting the crazy house...and isn't it what it is about?😊
Really cool place - it’s much bigger then it looks from the outside. Don’t miss it - even if it might be raining.
The images are very unusual and beautiful, the style is truly unique and crazy.
The children loved it. It seemed like a kind of maze where you kept seeing something different crazy or fun.
Coming from Ireland, Crazy House was pretty cool and something we hadn’t seen before. Its a good use of half an hour. Certainly worth checking out while in Da Lat.
We stopped off here as part of a tour we took. It's such a bizarre place and is definitely work checking out!!

Really cool place to visit. If you’re planning on using this as your hotel and staying here then be aware that during the day it is very noisy and people will constantly be trying to get into your room and open your door as they explore.

It's only about £1.50 entry and a strange but amazing place. Designed by a Vietnamese architect. There's quite a lot of construction going on and it looks as if they're expanding it a lot so some parts look like a building site. There's a small gift shop and a cafe which weren't open when we got there at 5pm

One of the amazing hotel we've seen, looks like Gaudy visited Vietnam for few years,  and had outperformed...Takes less than an hour to explore thiz crazy house, and the view of Dalat city from the top was remarkable.
We came here on a rainy day, pay fee for one guest is 50000 VND and then, we had an excited experience!!!Here are the craziest architecture in the world I ever seen! Highly recommended for everyone!
Nice place to take picture . A small sum of fare need to pay for entrance. They are still building more at another block. Hope I can see more next time when I return to dalat again
You should go with your friends so as to take pictures. A lot of spots you can choose. I highly recommended to go on the roof.

Fairly cheap, 50,000 dong, quaint place to visit and spend 45 minutes or so climbing throughout. Probably not that good for small children as there are steep stairs with low handrails. The bakery stall across the street has fantastic coconut cake though, so try that! (39,000 dong)
Strange house, yes it was crazy house with many special detail, they also have sample of honeymoon room, bathroom... you need the ticket to go that house around 50,000vnd

I'll be honest - we read a lot about this house before the trip, but once we got there, we weren't particularly impressed. Yes, it was cool and unusual, but honestly, I didn't want to live there - the atmosphere was very oppressive, and finding our room would probably be incredibly difficult.

The building is quite small, with only nine guest rooms, if I'm not mistaken. A tour, however, takes at least half an hour. The space, both inside and out, is tangled with a maze of stairs, not always easy to navigate.

For those who like something weird, and rather artificial, this is a place for you. A little fun climbing up and down, left to right, all around, you will never get lost here. Don't expect anything else more.

We visited as part of a tour of Dalat. 30 minutes wasn't enough. It's a shame the rooms can only be viewed through windows. Construction is underway. The stairs are slippery in rainy weather—a child fell on them. Beautiful photos.

In March, during the honey-gathering season in the Central Highlands, I visited Da Lat. The "Crazy House" was on my itinerary. The architecture is unique, unusual, and harmonious. From the rooftop, you can see a panoramic view of the city all the way to Lang Bian Mountain.
If you are into architecture and art, you will love a visit here! Many interesting features, lots of mushrooms and animal art built into the construction. Very unique.

My friend and I came here in a sunny day and this place was absolutely crazy of weird architectures, buildings, mazes, rooms for staying there too,...This is one of places should come when you're in Da Lat.I recommend this!

Be sure to set aside at least 2 hours for this. A beautiful adventure through the winding paths, corridors, and bridges. The rooms are very beautifully furnished. There are also fantastic murals in the newer sections. Furthermore, work and expansion are still ongoing. An absolute must, especially with kids.
Perhaps because I don't have a deep understanding of art, I found this house interesting only because it was unusual.

This place is very interesting and good to have an explore for a couple of hours.Try to go early on as it was full of tourists trying to get the perfect Instagram photo when I went around 4pm so it made it quite difficult to walk around.The view from the top it great too.

The houses have been transformed into various forms, including an ocean world, a treehouse, and winding ladders that go up and down – those with a fear of heights should be cautious. There's also a hotel for accommodation. The whole place has a fairytale-like feel and is constantly being expanded.
This’s one of the special place as you’re in DL, close the central, good views but expensive for a crazy design house!

Some pretty neat history behind this place which they just keep adding on to. You can actually book a room here which would be interesting. Fun, wacky, nice restaurant across the street. I am glad I went and checked it out!

We enjoyed our time looking around the crazy house, it definitely suits its name with each building and entrance taking you into a whole new world. I can understand that it may not be everyone's cup of tea but I really appreciate the attention to detail and creativity that is shown in every themed room.It was quite confusing that you can just walk into the parts of the building which are still just concrete and under construction and might have been better if they had been closed off as it took away from the atmosphere of excitement when walking through them.Otherwise we really enjoyed looking around.

We'd done some previous research and the Crazy House caught our attention for something not-too-expensive and a bit different so we thought why not? It's not too far from the main area of De Lat and has pretty cheap entry at 50k VND.It is a very cool experience, gives you a sort of Alice in Wonderland feel. The staff are friendly and give you a short introduction on how it all came about which is a nice touch. There is then an information room at the start for you to read a little more into it.The Crazy House also has quite a fun element to it as it very maze-like inside and you find yourself with several paths to take during the experience.All in all, glad we went and had a look around and would recommend it to others.
A very enjoyable activity, visiting a place with extraordinary architecture; however, the site is relatively small, it will take you less than an hour to explore it.
For those with an unconventional, open-minded outlook on life, it'll be fun.

We didn't have time to tour the entire building. There were so many people there, you had to follow the crowd. We did manage to look at a few rooms. Some were closed because they were occupied by guests. Honestly, I wouldn't want to stay in a place like that—endless crowds, no privacy. But it's worth a visit once.

I was here nine years ago, and then found myself in Dalat again. It's quite a fascinating place; it could have been dreamed up by someone with a rich imagination or someone high on drugs; the evidence suggests both. :) I don't know how much fun it is to rent a room here under the "supervision" of tourists, but it's fun to wander around. We rang the gong as we climbed the narrow stairs to the second floor and peeked into all the open rooms, but the house with the "honeymoon" sign was closed—apparently the newlyweds decided to celebrate their wedding in such an elaborate way.

This is another place that people recommended, but because some areas were under renovation and expansion during my visit, I couldn't see much inside. This place isn't very suitable for people who are afraid of heights.
I think this place got some idea from Antony Gaudi . Very fun to walk around , some bridge is narrow . And can take nice shot of photos .
Cleverly done. A bit scary, but the bridges are well secured so not dangerous. Beautiful paintings, especially in the aquarium.

A weird place but interesting. If anyone has the slightest fears about ‘health and safety’, forget it, there’s none! The steepest steps, with the flimsiest of barriers to prevent you falling 30 feet! However you don’t come to Vietnam to be lectured by the hi viz brigade. This place is bigger than it might at first appear, but the winding paths and stairs lead you higher and higher until you end up perched amongst the avocados with views across the town.If you’re so inclined you can actually stay in this ‘real life’ Alice in Wonderland  adventure haus, but with plenty of visitors poking their noses around you might opt to visit only.
A good stop if you have an hour, truly eclectic and doubles up as a Hotel as well. Easy to get lost but great fun. Enjoy......

This is a guest house, come tourist attraction with themed rooms everywhere in what could only be described as a treehouse structure. You can wander up any one of a series of windy steps to explore what this “structure” hides inside as far as its rooms. Not sure about your privacy if you stay in one of the rooms, but it’s worth the small entrance fee to visit & explore, but perhaps not if you aren’t good with small & twisted steps!

Available for stays in the guest rooms, open to visitors during the day. Modest (pretty cheap) admission to visit - less than $2 at the time of the writing of this review. The creator / architect is Đặng Việt Nga, daughter of Trường Chinh. Wow what a fantastic vision. I enjoyed walking, exploring, observing - just seeing. Awesome!
Good to know about the idea of designer, it seem to be a part of Alice wonderland story but displayed by the local style.
Bit like gaudi stuff in Barcelona Was part of a 1/2 day tour which is recommended and will cost about 800k for up to 7

An interesting city landmark. The hotel is constantly being renovated/updated/expanded. It features interesting staircases, various passages, rooms, and more. If you're afraid of heights, this will be scary, as the passages are quite narrow, barely room for two people to pass each other.
Madhouse, built in 1990 in Dalat, influenced by the work of famous Spanish architect Antonio Gaudí.

All lovers of fairy tales, fantasy, all things magical, crazy, mad, bright and fantasy, creepy and attractive at the same time, lovers of Tolkien, Potterian, creations of Gaudí and Montaner, are encouraged to visit this place. The site, created by the architectural genius Dang Hang Nga, a female architect who had studied in Russia for many years, was a keen admirer of Gaudí and sought to leave a similar mark on the history of architecture. Thus came an unusual house that is so unlike other Dalat architecture, and not only Dalat. The Crazy House is now considered one of the most striking landmarks in Vietnam, one of the top ten most unusual structures in the world, and at the same time the country's main long-term development, stretching over three decades.

This is a MUST VISIT in Da Lat. The architecture is beyond your wildest dreams. So many paths and routes to the top where you can enjoy wonderful views across Da Lat. It has what looks like an aquarium inside, also the most magnificent rooms where you can stay for a night. It was worth coming to Da Lat just for this experience

Crazy House in Dallat is really... well, crazy! 😅 It feels like walking around in a fairy tale or in a Dali painting. The corridors wind everywhere, stairs that seem to lead nowhere, and bizarre shapes of animals and trees everywhere. You literally lose your way, but that's what makes it fun. I especially liked the view from the higher terraces. Definitely a must-see if you’re in Dallat, even if it’s more art than “comfort”

If you have a penchant for different phobias, heights, crowding, confined, curved spaces and surfaces, you don't need to be there. But if you don't, you'll just get an unspeakable feeling. All these crossings, high narrow bridges, it's hard to see where the leading paths are. Wild colors, hands, feet. teeth, heads... The brain at the end just stops working in harmony, in unison with consciousness:) And actually it's a hotel, How do you live in such a...?:) The creators got their way, give them credit.
