A real madhouse!
I’ll say it this way. Don’t make a special trip all the way to Da Lat just to come here, but if you’re already in Da Lat then go for sure!

The Crazy House is now considered one of the most striking attractions in Vietnam. This hotel in Dalat, which is one of the ten most unusual buildings in the world, is still growing and new unusual buildings are being added to its grounds. All the buildings on the property, which is a functioning hotel with ten rooms where you can stay overnight, are built of concrete and painted with bright colors. As interesting as the story of the creation of the Moon Villa, which was the original name, is the story of the owner of this unusual structure herself.

Surprisingly fun to visit. It's a crazy thing. A mix between Efteling and Gaudi. What an architectural masterpiece. You're out of your mind. The rooms look super nice too. If it's booked you can't view it and otherwise it's open and you can look behind a gate.
It's incredibly fun to come here and admire some truly "Crazy 🤪" designs. A unique and very famous house in Da Lat.

Just spent 3 nights here and have thoroughly enjoyed it. I was nearly put off by some of the other reviews about having to leave your room open etc but so glad I wasn't. So this is a review just about starting here

If you're in Dalat - this is the place to visit.I would also stay here for a night.The rooms are unique and unusual. The place is bizarre in a good sense. I only don't know where they put the visitors luggage for a day. Because during the day the rooms are half open - so that visitors could take a look inside. No suiitcases, no luggage, no personal belongings inside. Where do they put it?
Very unique experience staying at Crazy House, interesting architecture, and story behind it. I would say if you don't have the time to spend a night, take yourself there for a look 😆

The architecture is unique, there are many attractive sightseeing areas, and it's great for taking photos. The fish with its mouth open was a bit scary though. It was a bit crowded since we went on the weekend. I will visit again.
The house  So so crazy. We are come there and enjoy with this house , there have room for rent also .... Everything from here so special
Surprisingly interesting! I was expecting a tourist trap but found an art experience! Recommended to anyone visiting Da Lat!

It was nice to do something a little different. And this place is definitely that. If you like artcitecture you will love a visit to this unique house. aND you can even stay. I peeked into some of the rooms and they looked half decent. Not sure of the cost though?
Crazy House Dalat: A whimsical escape in Dalat's hills, offering surreal architecture, themed rooms, and enchanting experiences for all.
Very unusual house, really good fun , definitely worth a visit,good value for money,it was  crowded when we were there due to Tet ,but it didn't spoil our visit
Fun excursion. This exceeded our expectations.  Very creative. Need to wear good shoes Children would need close supervision. Climbing many steps.

Crazy House in Dalat is a mesmerizing wonderland! The eccentric architecture, tree-shaped rooms, and winding paths create a surreal experience. It's a must-visit for those seeking an unforgettable adventure in Dalat. The staff are friendly, and the views are breathtaking. Highly recommended!

Crazy House is a fascinating labyrinth of creativity and imagination, with its unique architecture and enchanting ‘under the sea’ section. The whimsical setting, complete with a charming café and intriguing rooms, makes it a truly captivating experience.

This place is amazing a lot of work and intrigue and very unique.it has a lot of stairs but is very interesting especially the under the sea part. There is a great little cafe among the quirky setting and it appears that you can also stay here- rooms set up but not sure. This is a real whimsical place- love it

We happened to stumble on this historical museum as we ventured onto our various escapades in Vietnam. Crazy House is a unique magical place to be. The attraction was designed by architect Dang Viet Nga who is the daughter of Dang Huu Phuc, who was the vice president of Vietnam from 1992-2001. The features of the Crazy House resembles that of a fairy-like structure. You have to be physically there to experience the twisting staircases and detailed designs of maze-like corridors, fantasy rooms and hidden breathtaking landscapes. It's just impressive being immersed in the creativity of it all. A great place for tourists, architects and art lovers to explore unconventional designs. The entrance fee is quite affordable.

Dali and Gaudi inspire Dalat in a visual psychedelic Esher web of stairways, airways, and byways of a labyrinth of the out of this world imagination. Words cannot describe the dazzling architecture which proclaims next time in Dalat, and perhaps this, here is where I rest my head for the night for it is a night to remember from the road.

What an amazing and unique place. Like nothing I have ever seen in the world. Some tried to compare it to Gaudi but I would disagree. They are not the same. There is something different to see around every corner and totally loved the aquatic area.Great family activity which will last two to three hours. Watch the kids on some parts as there are low or no handrails

I would never expect such a wonderful experience from a house.I strongly recommend to visit this wonder place unforgettable experience is guaranteed.If you’re single or couples or a family with children everyone will find this place amazing.Highly recommended The WOW guaranteed!!!

Very cool and interesting place to see. Nothing like you’ll ever experience and full of so many details to discover. Best seen from the highest stairways but plenty still to see if you don’t like climbing. Def not to miss and worth the 60,000 entry fee.
Loved Crazy House a fanciful mix of Dali meets Disney with a great back story. I had my most relaxed night sleep in this wonderland
Lots of fun! Very intricate! Could spend forever here!!! When you think you’ve been through all the rooms, think again! There’s more!! Art!!

Great and unique houses connected by stairs and paths and two great gardens. Everything is lovingly designed and very beautiful! We were there twice and stayed overnight, we can highly recommend it! A very special and creative place!Also great Halloween decorations and several cafes on site!

Indescribable terrain with houses of all kinds. The stairs connect the houses on different levels and are an art in itself. Sometimes fear of heights is inconvenient, but you should overcome it because the view is worth it.

Despite the cloudy and occasional rain, the serene architecture, pulsating with magnificent and vibrant curves and colors, blended seamlessly into a stunning, weather-independent backdrop, resulting in many photos I really liked. Even if you have seen this content in many other photos, don't miss this opportunity to get your own unique and unexpected shots.
This was an amazing quirky place to visit. Loved it. Very clever architecture and really beautifully designed. Worth a visit for sure
We thought it was an amazing place! If you have kids with a good imagination, then they will love it. Our oldest was 16 (boy) and he absolutely loved it. Highly recommended ☀️
It is a very interesting and unique building.
only 70.000 VND for international tourist and you can see beautiful view of Da Lat city from the top of building.
it’s really a very amazing place with great architecture. All the people who complain it’s dangerous hav no idea about that natural wounder.
The architecture is simply amazing, and it's really fun to explore the layout of the house. Some

A trip to a world created by a very imaginative person. It would be a little uncomfortable for someone who didn’t like heights. There are many stairs and elevated pathways with low handrails. For that same reason I wouldn’t bring young kids unless you understood you wouldn’t let go of them when you weren’t on the ground. But I don’t want to dissuade you. This is a must!

I initially thought it would be boring. But it was totally worth it. Up and down, back and forth, up and down. Fantastic! The architecture is very impressive. However, it wouldn't pass TÜV inspection in Germany.

This "house" is so fascinating. It's actually several houses and building elements connected together. Fantastic is the best word to describe it. For example, you can walk across the roof of the witch's cottage via a normal staircase (without a high railing). Children, in particular, can discover endless things here.

All of the positive reviews on Crazy House are so correct. It’s a trip to stay here and a must if your in Dalat. We stayed in a “normal room” and it was fantastic, spacious, clean and comfortable. Wide open windows bring in the smell of the flowers and the grounds are immaculate with beautiful flowers amidst the craziness.  My review relates to owners and the staff at the hotel. I left my passport in Crazy House in not realising until I arrived in Mui Ne. Once contacted Crazy House told me not to worry and enjoy myself and they would liaise with the staff at Serenity by Sea….(another must hotel) and get my passport delivered. It arrived 2 days later. Losing a passport is a major worry when a flight home is imminent. Crazy House staff were amazing and Crazy House reflects them.  Thank you for a beautiful stay and for treating your guests the way that you would want to be treated. Great team, Great place. book it.

I was initially hesitant to visit, but it really struck me. The house is also a hotel; in fact, there are even rooms that can be visited from the outside, in the same incredible style as the rest of the house. Personally, it struck me like Gaudí's buildings. You can spend over an hour wandering through the passageways that climb up to the rooftops. At sunset, it's truly incredible.

You must stay here if you are going to Dalat. A very special and exciting hotel, but with only 10 rooms.. Clean and nice. Fantastic staff.. Nice and helpful. This is the most visited place in Dalat, and here we stayed.. for 3 days. Booked the room about 10 months in advance.. And we discovered new exciting things here every day.. You must stay here.. In Dalat.. Crazy House Dalat
Surrealism at its best!. What an imagination the wonderful architect had. It's a maze in itself and definitely worth a visit.

Thoroughly enjoyed visiting, amazing creativity. Worth a visit. I spent atleast two hours there and felt it was something unmissable in Dalat. I didnt have the food there though. They had almost no vegan options or perhaps they were not interested in sharing. There are plenty of options in the vicinity and hence this was not an issue. I went there for only a visit and felt it would have been a great idea to stay there for a night.
Very stylish construction. The rooms are spacious with charming design. Can definitely recommend a visit here. Pleasant and service-minded staff. We had a great time!
crazy halses yo I love them so much mack them in astrayla ther are no poo homses i wont a homes like that
We stayed at Crazy House on 29th May 2022, It was a great experience,  They had cooked special vegetarian breakfast for us too

It reminded me of something out of Lord of the Rings or The Hobbit movies. It is amazing to view and explore.  If you had kids, they would love this place for hours. The architecture and design is amazing, alot of creativity and thought went into this place.

Really different place to stay. The fireplace in the rooms was a nice touch. The only slight problem is that tours begin at 8.00 so you may get woken up then by loud children but this only happened on one of the days! Breakfast was great.

An experience that definetly deserves the word "crazy", something very unique I haven*t seen anywhere before ( And I did travel quite a lot already ;)) It's worth a visit, you get to climb around the house and discover the crazyness and if you take a look inside, you'll see the rooms, since this is also a hotel, the architecture is simply amazing and it's really amusing to see, it's quite big so take your time and check this crazyness out for yourself- and find the crazy side of yours ;)
A creative and unconventional space in Da Lat. A famous spot that anyone visiting Da Lat should see at least once. The entrance fee is also quite affordable.
I think you have to see this on your visit to Dalat, it’s so famous. It only takes an hour or less but it’s amazing to explore. So much creativity and artistry. Definitely worth a stop.
It was quiet the day we visited so we had an amazing time exploring with our children and friends. There's so much to see and explore. The architect is a very interesting lady . Very worth a visit.

Went to this place after others had been there just to see how crazy it was. It really is a crazy house, fascinating and much dedication has gone into this place by owner and she has seen her vision and dream become a reality. Not practical to stay here though!
This place is completely out of the ordinary. They build a real beautiful labyrinth and it's getting bigger and bigger year after year !
Spend a couple of hours exploring this labyrinthine wonderland. Some very slender high bridges with low handrails add to the thrill!

Great place to marvel at how concrete can be used to make strange shaped buildings. An architectural cross between Gaudi and Darli and a must to see in Dalat. Best for kids of an age that can be trusted on low railed walk ways. I loved it and it's my second visit to the 'Crazy place'.
It's truly a house worth seeing; it's magical, fantastic, original, a really great time for both children and adults.
Great fun at the Crazy House. A maze of staircases and doorways so easy to get lost but good fun trying to find each other!

A great attraction, but an even better hotel experience! The rooms have a crazy layout, but they're fantastic. If you don't want a bed with such an unusual shape, don't go to the Kangaroo room; otherwise, the rooms are individually stunning. Each room is unique, and the fireplaces are themed around either a bear or a kangaroo. The staff is incredibly friendly and helpful, whether it's about the room or finding lost cell phones in a taxi in the middle of the night. Wonderful attraction and a great hotel. We stayed in the Kangaroo and Bear rooms, and it was fantastic. We loved both rooms. If you need a normal-sized bed, choose the Bear room; the Kangaroo room is really crazy. All rooms have their own fireplace themed around one of the room's animals.

We visited this establishment on a tour with Sergei Kuropov. Construction of the "Madhouse" is ongoing. It will be very interesting to visit in another five years. Although Sergei said it was designed in the style of Tolkien's works, his wife insists it's based on Russian folk tales, as there are Matryoshka dolls, a hut on chicken legs, and so on. This is not surprising, since the architect studied in the Soviet Union.

So glad we visited the Crazy House. It feels not just like an architectural must-see, but also like a playground. You never know where the bridges or staircases are going to take you. Fun, and worth allowing extra time for it.
So crazy! Architecture is cool and intricate - so many different bits to explore. Was definitely worth the money
The Crazy House was one of the highlights of Dalat, a recommendation for every design/architecture enthusiast!
A unique architectural design. The service quality is excellent, the road to get here is easy, and the view is beautiful.
Fun experience and worth the visit. Lots of pathways to explore. It’s like visiting the world of Dr. Seuss.

We found it incredibly exciting to explore the house and every nook and cranny. It's so much fun to walk around the houses, through them, and along their sides. You also get glimpses into the accommodations and rooms. Definitely worth a visit if you're in Da Lat. If you arrive by moped, you'll need to factor in a small parking fee, but it's really minimal. I think it was 8,000 Dong.
Wowwww!!! This place is crazy and the whole design doesn't make sense. Spend some exploring the place and you will really enjoyed it.Love how it blends with nature:It is beautiful!!!

After reading about Crazy House e expected something special but it was really above and beyond those expectations. A must see in Da Lat! You should combine a visit with lunch @lechaletdalat which is just over the road.
A mind-blowing house born from the fertile imagination of an architect who broke free from all conventions. Walking in, on, and around this house feels like a dream.
We actually visited this as we had run out of stuff to do that was open. It was amazing. So insane. The architecture is unbelievable and there are surprises in each room.

Myself and my girlfriend came here without knowing what to expect and we were pleasantly surprised. Very unique place to have a wonder and get lost in the amazing design! Dont go if you're scared of heights as it feels relatively unsafe because of the low railings but just tred carefully and you will enjoy yourself! ( I did it though and I am very scared of heights!!!)

It’s quite a big site- Lots of stairs but well worth it as the architect really let his imagination run free on this space that challenges you to re-examine what constitutes a dwelling,a note of caution people with kids under 12 might thing about giving it a miss as some of the many charms are low barriers fine if you are grown up but maybe not so wise with small kids

I came two years ago and the legend is true- this place is always changing. Definitely worth the 40,000d entry, I didnt pay for a tour but there is a room with alot of history and building plans that I enjoyed.

I absolutely loved this crazy house, and I wish I could have stayed a night or two. You get lost among the bridges and tunnels; it's amazing. It's like a house straight out of Grimm's fairy tales and Alice in Wonderland, a fantastic house straight out of a fairy tale 🥰 It doesn't have much to do with Vietnam, but it's still worth a visit!
We enjoyed this more than we expected.  For a bit of light hearted fun this is worth visiting at a cost 60,000 vnd each.   Very whimsical!

This was my forth stay at the Crazy House and it just gets better and better.  You have to realise that this is a very eccentric homestay, it is not a hotel and for that reason it's a bit rough and ready around the edges.  The staff were really helpful and their English was brilliant and they were really helpful and good at informing me about the history of the building and the life of the architect.  Kids would absolutely love this place.
This was my first time admiring the beauty of these houses and buildings, which are considered bizarre; this is a must-visit destination when traveling to Da Lat.
A short trip that's well worth it! A quirky house with countless things to discover; you just have to take your time and explore every little winding path. Don't forget the garden in the back...

Surprising, funny, whimsical, but original. A building unlike any other, but well worth a visit. A maze of interior and exterior staircases leads from one room to another, each more original and beautiful than the last. I highly recommend it.
we like it, been there many times and renewed Crazy House very good we like it.Every year it's getting be better and better! Bravo!
Much larger and cooler than I'd thought. Definitely worth a trip. Take your time and explore each stairway and crevice. There are little hideaways and also a cafe in the bottom.

This is one of the most creative buildings that I have ever seen.  It is like stepping into a story land with interesting characters and interesting twists and turns. Do read up on the history of the place and learn about the perseverance fo the artist who created the place. You can actually even book a room. Don't forget to enjoy some of the coffee in the region.

The visit is quite irregular. The architecture is strange but amazing. The idea behind the carved passes between the rooms is attractive. Unlike Gaudi Sagrada Familia, here you can also travel inside the house and feel it intimately.
Must visit the crazy house. It's interesting & quirky, fun & quite a thrill to climb those stairs, all without banisters or with rather low ones.Cool place to visit!

Me, my brother and mum came here for a couple of hours. Suggested by locals. Just incredible. It looks like there is no structure, but yet there is, to this place. Has accommodation rooms, dance area, souvenirs, good views of Da Lat, and some sections still under construction. Also some stairs and passages are extremely narrow and high up, so if you have no fear or are mobile, don't do them.

An incredibly intricately constructed house (or houses), connected over several floors by narrow corridors and staircases. Especially just before sunset, you can take beautiful pictures of Da Lat from the very top. Highly recommended!
Nothing normal here. Plenty to explore the nooks and crannies, up and down narrow staircases. Certainly an IG spot. Worth the entrance fee :)
Lots of walking up and down.  Good exercises. Selling cheap and good cookies!  Loved it. 1.5 to 2 hours can  be spent there till your legs are tired!

Well worth £2 entry fee. Whacky eccentric and your mobile will take so many photos. We visited mid afternoon and it seemed quiet. There is nothing to prepare you for this. Gaudi or Dalhi artists spring to mind. Yes the steps are narrow but well spaced and an easy gradient.

An absolute must-see in Da Lat. The architect is the daughter of Ho Chi Minh's right-hand woman and has truly created a monument of architectural mastery here. We recommend going early to avoid the crowds.
Totally mad concept, beautifully executed!! Worth a visit just mind your step as there isn't much in the way of barriers to stop falls! 😃
Spent about an hour here, and still didn't find all the rooms.. Kids will love it as well. Go carefully, steps and stairways are narrow.
Nothing like I’ve ever seen before. Vietnam take on Gaudi would be the closest to compare. Only 60,000 VND entry so very reasonable.
For the price I thought this was an excellent place to spend a few hours and take a million pictures. Great view from the top and the sea life room you enter through a giant shell is awesome

The house is so unique it’s hard to describe but it’s definitely worth a visit. However if isn’t for the faint of heart because there are incredibly high levels with low banisters. The architecture is without a doubt fascinating and imaginative.
we loved exploring the ins and outs of this place. it was super fun to climb above everything. definitely check this place out.

Way better than I imagined.  I thought it would only be interesting from the outside but there are many surreal rooms inside with crazy pathways linking different parts.  One room displays much information about the owner and the evolution of this Crazy House.

This was incredible!! I didn’t expect much and was so impressed- all you could hear (in 100 languages) was ‘wow-how did they get up there?’ And it is great to know you can still stay there too. It’s 60k vnd to get it. And I spent over an hour!

I cannot compare this place to anything I’ve seen before. You’d think that the title is pretty self explanatory but not nearly. It is a maze indeed and all the whimsical rooms send you into a strange fairytale. There are some themed rooms all connected by a labyrinth of ups and downs. Probably if you feel dizzy at heights, you should stick to safe paths, there plenty of those. Some stairs are straightaway scary and breathtaking. Loved every minute of being there. It was a bit crowded during Tet holiday but I don’t mind the crowd. You could still take great pictures and explore as much as you want.

I adore this place! I've recommended it to everyone, and I still do, even though I spoke to someone who absolutely hated it. Our last visit to the restaurant was under construction, we saw the artist's work, and knew we'd definitely visit again when it was finished. What's more, we even met the owner herself there! No more, no less! When she found out we were from Russia, we were the only ones in the huge crowd who allowed us to take a photo with her, and that's it! I now have such unique photos! You can wander around there all day without getting bored. I wonder what the guests feel like? I'd love to stay there for a day one day (I can't take it anymore! )))
We could not emagine that it would be so interesting. Crazy design, interesting forms, great ideas!It worth wisiting!
I always make sure to visit Crazy House when I'm in Dalat. I really like this place. It's constantly being built and expanding. It's usually very crowded, but you can always find a spot for a photo.
Very cheap tickets and worth the visit. We were like kids. Dalat was not what I expected so, this was the best sight in the city. Go in the afternoon and wait until they turn on the lights. ;)
This place blew my mind. I didn’t think much of it when I first walked in, but the more I explored, the more I loved it...then I walked into the dancehall and.....wow!
A huge investment has been made, get here when the doors open as it gets very busy, some cultures are not very good with waiting and simply push through...

This is not my first time there. Was few years ago,  so many new things and places. Tickets prices for adults 60k children 20k. Have good coffee bar, free wi-fi,  view point at the rooftop.  My recommendation for visiting time around 5 pm. Enjoy evening city and sunset.

The Crazy House is a must-see when you're in Da Lat. A tip: check out the restaurant in the complex, which is in a separate building. Very few tourists go there, and the coffee and smoothies are fantastic!

This house is a maze ! I loved it ! A little introduction is given at the beginning and then you are left on your own adventure inside this crazy maze. The design of the house is supposed to connect the human kind with nature and you can feel it as you go from place to place as each of them are related to an ecosystem.

It's an interesting place. You can get lost. And this attraction is still being completed. Well done to the architect who came up with this idea. Unfortunately, my wife and I weren't able to stay here. It's worth a visit.
A unique building, a clever architect. It's hardly a convenient hotel—there are crowds of tourists. :) It's constantly expanding, adding new rooms and amenities. One of Dalat's must-see attractions.
A visit is a must; it's the most visited tourist spot in Dalat. You can see the entire building, comprised of various rooms, in about an hour.
Entrance fee is 60 000 VND per person. Was great fun and super interesting. This place is good for all ages. I think it should be on your list to see in Da Lat.
Crazy house is an amazing place to visit and spend a few hours being amazed by their creations. It’s really hard to explain the place to people who haven’t been. It’s just WILD architecture really...
The unique, unusual, and elaborate architecture makes it an ideal place to visit and explore. Located on Huynh Thuc Khang Street, Da Lat.

More of a crazy maze with a few rooms hidden and scattered along crazy passageways snaking all over, under, sideways, wherever. Colourful, multi themed complex of several connected buildings. GREAT FUN!!!

Crazy house is an understatement. A great place to play hide and seek... amazing place to spend an hour or so wandering around the different pathways throughout the various buildings. The steps at the top are narrow and a bit disorienting. Amazingly, it is also a hotel... and they are still constructing more craziness.

Very interesting expereince to see a talented thinker thinks of how a crazy look like and built it for us to experience...........it's really out of the box experience that is fun. There is the 'Goudi' in Spain but this one has more absurdity to expereince.You got to come and see this if you are in Da Lat.

We visited this unusual and, mind you, still functioning hotel on a private tour. It's a truly extraordinary architectural structure, a must-see to understand the possibilities and imaginations architects can bring to life! It's difficult to describe; you have to see it for yourself and, if possible, stay overnight, but that's for those who aren't bothered by crowds of tourists during the day! There's the Sea King's kingdom inside, complete with stunning 3D paintings, a multitude of intricate staircases, a building shaped like a termite mound, a hut on chicken legs, and a matryoshka doll. If the rooms are empty, you can peek inside, but you can't walk in. In short, everyone will have their own opinion. We loved the crazy house and took lots of interesting photos, especially in the Sea King's kingdom with its 3D paintings.
Guest House with amazing architecture reminiscent of a tree trunks in the city of Dalat. Is a must to visit for tourists. Unique structure built place and good place for pictures.
The space is very unique, the architectural style is truly crazy, and the staff here are very attentive and provide excellent customer service.
Just a side seeing place, crazy house with all kinds off rooms and and walk way - you can get a bit lost weaving and bobbing around
Interesting place to see, definitely worth it if you are staying in dalat for a few days, great value for money as well.
It's just a hotel with a very unusual layout and decor. The most interesting part is climbing to the very top, where you'll get a wonderful view of all of Dalat and the surrounding area.
It's incredible! The architecture is absolutely wild, the themed rooms are sublime, and the decor perfectly reflects the spirit of the house. If you're ever in Dalat, it's a must-see!

Although we were there on a rainy day, it wasn't ideal...but the crazy house is truly crazy! Weird, beautiful, bizarre, cool, crazy, confusing...are some of the descriptive words that fit the place...definitely worth a visit!

I guess by the crows that nearly every tourist visits the Crazy House so even after 20 years of continual work the project certainly pays fr itself ad there is an entrance fee to visit. The owner started the project over 20 years ago and it is still ongoing. There are narrow staircases, bridges , sculptures and guest rooms scattered in this strange building. Souvenir shop too !!! However i would not like to be a guest here as tourists walk by your doorway throughout the day. We definitely enjoyed our visit to this unique building so spend the $3 on the entrance fee and join the crowd.WARNING   - this building would not pass safety rules in the west so be careful if you have children as some of the paths only have railings 2 feet high and easy to fall over. I am a senior citizen myself and on one occasion i had to turn back as i did not feel safe
A popular tourist destination, with affordable entrance fees, stunning and unique architectural structures, and an ideal spot for taking photos.
It's an ideal tourist destination, with a cheap entrance fee of 30,000 VND per person and boasts unique and eye-catching architectural structures.

Spent the day running around getting lost all over the place and taking wicked photos. If you don’t take a photo pretending to be eaten by the shark on the floor did you even experience it properly? So much fun and so creative!

Actually a pretty fun place, even for adults. I think kids would find it especially cool..with stairs up and down, across roofs and mysterious paths and corridors...they are making the place even bigger. It takes about 1 hour and costs 60,000

It's really cool to walk around the Crazy House. Everything is incredibly detailed and beautiful to look at. With all the different corridors, you have to watch your step to avoid getting lost, but even if you walk past some places two or three times, you still discover something new. They're currently expanding it even further.
It's really interesting and beautiful, especially considering that there isn't much to see in Dalat.
Fun place to visit with an astonishingly cool things to see. Not sure if we could book a room but such fun to see and walk through

After visiting the Gaudi buildings in Barcelona and Hundertwasser’s Museum in Vienna, I thought I’d be better prepared. This structure has various level and an intricate number of passages, some quite narrow. Spent an hour here and it was well worth the 70 VD, even if I bumped my head moving into one room.
This place is really unique and one of a kind. It is also a mini hotel and you can rent rooms in the house. You can get lost in its maze of passageways and it is quite intriguing.

Who would have thought that concrete construction could be so much fun? This whimsical and eccentric cluster of buildings is great fun to explore. Just follow the walkways as they lead you. No point in trying to navigate your way - I could see the Crazy Coffee House but never managed to find my way there. The aerial walkways are narrow with low handrails, a trial for the vertigo-challenged. I have never dropped acid but this is what I'd expect an acid trip to look like - an unleashing of the imagination. Best to avoid the main part of the day, when coach after coach disgorges its load; go early or late.
This house is beyond words and it is also so beautifully made you have to see it so beautiful and crazy when you are in Denmark

We weren't sure what to expect but after 2 hours exploring the various levels and rooms were impressed by the creative thinking and ideas that have taken place to make this unique! Even in the rain this was a great place to visit.

Simply this, just go to Crazy house, let your inner kid out to play as you explore the house which is like a maze of stories and designs... no more to say, better to simply go and enjoy, not even the pouring rain dampened our fun and sense of wonder here. Excellent value, what an amazing women to have designed this house
Wow! You should defenetly go there.Lots of different rooms and views.And also you Can visit the crazy cafe.

It’s a super cute place. We visited in 2 trips, 2012 and 2014. Today in 2019 we went to Gaudi’s “Park Güell” and kept wondering why it looked so familiar. Then we realized it’s because Da Lat Crazy house is inspired by Gaudí. Frankly, Crazy House was a lot cuter and more fun than Park Güell. Highly recommend a visit. Then go uphill and have coffee or tea at the adorable place with a garden. And finally hang out at the Da Lat Bao Dai palace.
Fun, cheap. The place at the top has an amazing view of the city. It's touristy but so is everything else you're gonna do in Vietnam!

This was a really great time. There were people everywhere and as my wife said they were all making  happy noises. Constant expressions of surprise. Designed and built by architect Dr. Dang Viet Nga using what appears to me the looks and feel of a mature tropical forest.

My friends and I visited this place twice and really enjoyed it. There's a lot to see and do. The rooms are unique, but they're lit up at night, and it's wonderful. It takes on a unique atmosphere after the tourists have stopped visiting.
The experience offers an exciting sense of discovery, with unique and creative architectural designs that combine both originality and the bizarre.

I visited the Crazy House Hotel this evening! It's truly different! It's impressive. The colors are completely different, the mushrooms glow under the fluorescent paint, and you can even see the outlines of other animals! I recommend coming back in the evening too :)

Haa haa I almost got lost inside there, mesmerizing, it couldn't get any more crazy than that.  So many levels, corners and crazy stairways.  I was in Awe that someone could build something like that.  Wow. A must visit.

I spent at least an hour or 2 running around the entire place by myself. There were large groups of people, couples and families - which makes picture taking so much more fun. A part of it is actually under construction, I was told there will be more to see next year. It’s fun if you like having a fantasy like experience, but I could see it getting boring for people who aren’t into that kind of stuff. It reminder me a bit of Gaudi’s architecture work, but of course a less finessed and detailed version. It’s very whimsical and artsy, there are multiple paths and rooms joining one another. My favorite part was the sea creatures, it’s very detailed and quiet beautiful.After talking to some locals I realized this is the kind of place only tourists go to. It’s 60k vnd to enter and I’m sure it’s not worth the money for locals who work very hard for that much money to spend on a maze like building experience. But if you’re a tourist with money to spare and started running out of things to do in Da Lat other than going to yet another café, then why not get a little lost in the creative structure. It also gives you an amazing 360 of the city with the mountains in the background because some parts of the building get pretty high up. Worth a visit!

The architectural style is quite unique, not following any particular style, the service quality is quite good, and the rooms are decorated in an extremely impressive way, attracting tourists from the first glance.
nice place to visit when you are in Dalat.it's very Dali style and huge, plenty of tourist but still nice. still under construction but so many things to see.
Crazy House is a fun and exciting new accommodation option for young people, definitely worth experiencing when visiting Da Lat.
A house with a crazy, strange style, decorated without any particular system, but very distinctive.

Crazy House is totally different  than anything we have seen before.  Spent over 3 hours exploring. Totally admire the woman whose dream this was,  and the building of it. Would thrill children as well as adults.
Was trippy as! Unlike anything I have seen before! Insane! A little scary (I'm scared of hights) they are still building more!

Although it was raining a bit, we had a pleasant visit at the crazy house. It's nice to walk the different paths through the house and take a look at the strange and unique buildings. Some rooms of the hotel were open so we could take a look, every room has a different theme. When you're in Da Lat, you must visit this place! I'm sure children would love this!
The place was stunning. Bit challenging for someone scare of height with all those stairs, but once got up, the view was beautiful. Definitely worth a visit!

It's smaller than I expected. However, the inside was worth seeing. The stairs are narrow, so you need to be careful, and since it's not something you can enjoy for a long time, you should plan on doing it with other activities.

When visiting Da Lat, whether it's a rainy or sunny day, this is the perfect place to explore. Its unique and novel architecture offers many interesting experiences. There are also plenty of spots for taking Instagram-worthy photos.

You for sure have never seen a building like this before in your life. An amazing construction with lots of details or better only details to experience. I have never seen something like that and I saw most of the houses which Architect Gaudi has built in Barcelona. If you want to be amazed, then don't wait and go
This building has unique and weird architecture for a real hotel (though mainly for tourists).The architecture is out of this world and you should definitely check it out at least once. :)

A must visit when you go Dalat especially if you like to see something unusual, artistic and conceptual.  Fun place to go with every corner of the place instagrammable.  All the route you take is an adventure, the stairway to heaven within the premise gives you a good view of Dalat city. The underwater theme at the central interior is beautifully spectacular as well.
The house is unusual, with unique architecture, high-quality rooms, and the staff here are very enthusiastic.
The style is unique, the architecture is distinctive, the prices are affordable, and the rooms are absolutely gorgeous. It's a fantastic experience!
Loved it!!! Great unexpected fun place!!! Would recommend for all whoVisit this magical place called Da Lat

Definitely worth a visit. Crazy House was truly the highlight of our visit to Da Lat. It is especially fun to visit with small children. They can enjoy climbing and clambering over all the absurd structures built here. There is something to see or discover everywhere. A new section is under construction, so there will be even more to see in the future. Also, take a moment to listen to the explanations from the staff. You will hear a lot of interesting facts.

On a somewhat rainy day in Da Lat, our family decided to visit the Crazy House. Everyone loved it. We enjoyed the nuttiness of it all, how every area felt different, at times joyful, others spooky, always extraordinary.This is definitely worth a visit in Da Lat. Laughs, wonder and puzzlement guaranteed.
Absolutely visit this place. Reasonable entry fee and a real one of a kind attraction. Architecture meets art in a wonderful way.
If you have the time, be sure to take the time to go and take a look here. It is very special and is in the top 10 craziest buildings in the world for a reason.
There is no such thing as leaving Dalat without going to the crazy house.

I planned to stay here,but cancel the plan,so just visit this place,it is so crazy,the design is not normal,but unique,worth a visit.you will find something funny inside.but be careful,some place quite dangerous,narrow path and bridges a bit high.there is a cafe behind the house,but no time to visit.cuz busy to take many photos here like a crazy man.some rooms occupied,so be more polite,dont disturb the guest.you can see some photos and infos about the founder in certain room.besides,there are souvenir shops on site.but never bought anything,some are not related to this venue.you can take grab to here,i took grabbike both way,very smooth,and cheap.
It's truly a crazy house, a designer whose ideas, when you enter, make you think only a crazy person could come up with them.

Didn't really expect much but was genuinely surprised by Crazy House, really worth a visit! Criss crossing across the narrow bridges between buildings, looking around the aqua themed rooms, views from the top. Fun and interesting way to spend an hour or hour and half in Dalat.
A truly super unique location! Really beautiful and unique.
Loved this wacky place, which is expanding, and a tourist destination. Would love to have stayed. Can get busy with coach loads of tourists
Great experience, very interesting, kids running around, nice coffee at the end, great view. Great
There are many Western travelers.
This place must be one on your bucket list! Amazing rooms, comfortable beds. Great staff, nice breakfast!! You can’t stop taking pictures of this place, just mind blowing!!!
We visited Crazy House in August 2019. It was a really cool, creative, and surprising place. Admission is 50,000 dong. I recommend it, especially for children, although we enjoyed it too.
The design and decoration of the houses are totally out of our imagination. Very recommended. Some parts of the building are still under construction.

Entrance fee 40,000 dong. Nice to walk around for 1 hour. The place is still under renovation and more is being built. Just be careful because there is no security here and there are no screams of missiles.

I really love the design of this homestay; it's very unique and completely different from the vintage style of other homestays in Da Lat. Every time I come to Da Lat, I stay here and rent a motorbike to explore the city.

The Crazy House was incredible. I only wish we knew it was possible to book a room for an overnight stay. Very Gaudi, this place had stairs, crossways and tunnels leading to amazingly styled rooms and architecture. A definite must go place.
Fun experience! Lots of stairs through surreal corridors. Also great fun for children (you go very high up, so slightly older).
This place is very unusual, with a unique design. Everyone in my family was very excited, but I was quite nervous because I'm afraid of heights, so I wanted to experience it.
From the outside you're never going to guess what's inside! It was way more than I expected. Very cool experience :)
Really enjoyed the crazy house, with every path leading to somewhere else. Amazing architecture as well as views. A must visit in Da Lat!
The whole thing reminds me of Gaudí's Barcelona, ​​but a bit more unstructured. A nice place to visit for just 2 euros per person.

We visited this place in early July on the recommendation of the Lonely Planet Travel Guide and was not disappointed.If you are in Dalat this is a must visit tourist attraction for the unusual architecture who ever came up with the idea of this place must have taken LSD.Think of Antoni Gaudí meets Andy Warhol!
I really liked this house.  Although some part of house are not totally safe, it was worth visiting. Do not miss it if you visit Da Lat
The architecture is quite unique, and the view is beautiful. The rooms are also not too expensive. It's very crowded with tourists. A place worth visiting.
The architecture is unique, and the high vantage point offers a panoramic view of Da Lat. The entrance fee is a bit expensive (in my opinion, a 20,000 VND fee is reasonable for such a visit).

I absolutely loved it.   I want to live in a house like that!!!!! We were lucky enough to meet the lady behind it all, as she was guiding her workers building the sky garden.  Although we spent longer than the recommended 30minutes.  And we didn't know beforehand that you can stay there, and not unreasonable rates.

We went on a free tour. In my opinion, the house's history is far-fetched, as if it was built that way by accident. In fact, during the tour, builders were finishing up the rooms there. It's interesting to see the concept, but the whole tour story is hyped up for tourists.
We visited it as part of a tour of Dalat. It was absolutely stunning! So many different staircases and walkways, all connected. What a fantasy!

This really is a crazy all over the place house. Well worth a look although a bit too crowded with local tourists for our taste. A maze of tunnels, bridges and not a straight wall to be seen - and it just keeps on growing!!

This design is fantastic! We even stayed one night in a bamboo house. It was small but perfectly formed, and breakfast was even provided. There were many tourists during the day, but it was very quiet at night. There were no tourists at 8 am, making it perfect for exploring on your own.
Must see!  Unlike anything we have ever seen in our travels!  We went on a rainy day, so the crowds were minimal.
Interesting architecture of an unusual hotel. Sunshine and rain alternate. Viewing platforms. Unusual atmosphere.

This was the first time I'd ever stepped into such a strange house; the decor was crazy but incredibly captivating—it truly lived up to its name, "Crazy House"! I promise to visit again next time, Crazy House!
Awesome place, so fun to explore. Rooms so cosy and warm but still spacious, staff friendly and helpful. Exploring and/or staying here is an experience you won't forget
Had such a good time for such a low price! Wish I could come back when it’s complete but even under construction it’s definitely worth a visit.
Beautiful, unique, and one-of-a-kind!!! The architecture is eye-catching, with a hotel and homestay, a dance floor, and it's absolutely crazy—you'll find out just how crazy it is when you get there!!

Strongly RECOMMENDED! Very bold and colourful! We could not resist taking many photos! 😁Crazy House also got hotel accomodation too..  Too bad, we did not stay there..But good bonus point is that Chicken Church is located nearby. Really saved our times!
The "Crazy House" in Dalat is a must-see, with exceptional architecture, and guest rooms are also available.

It was really crazy going up and down around and turns in this funny concept house. I like the rooms with animal concept but i find it too noisy to stay there.... but I enjoyed having my photos taken in d shark's mouth.. u really have to be creative hahah to really enjoy the crazy house... when u go there make sure u wear a comfortable shoes... narrow steps.. and narrow pathway bridge.. also nice place to have pictures of funny friends acting out too... cool place to clear u head. Get mesmerized and bring out the kid in you! Hahaha

I felt suspicious when I read this was going to be "guadi-eske" but it delivered an experience just different enough to not be a copy cat. You feel like being in a magical forest. All the pathways going everywhere make it that much more awesome. If you bring children, make sure you keep an eye on them because the walkways aren't always super safe.

An interesting house, a Vietnamese architect who studied in Moscow and designed the house with Gaudi, Disney and Salvador Dalí in mind. In our opinion, it is definitely worth a visit. Entrance fee 50k
Worth the price (40k/person) for an activity that's a bit different from what we usually do.

It's really specially, you can take many beautiful photos here. Unluckily we only have a one day tour for dalat, so we dont have enough time. If I have chance I will spend few days in Dalat next time rather only a one day tour.

It's like a walking around a maze, that also goes up in the sky, well nearly, you get as high as the buildings. It also has a garden where it has also a small shop that sells drink and swole food stuff. Whatever paint they used in the garden on the structure there it glows in the nighttime and it looks very strange. The the whole place has a childish atmosphere, it makes even the adults want to climb everywhere and joining in with the children.

It’s fascinating to see someone’s crazy vision come to life. The architect still lives here and I believe she is 79! Amazing to see this crazy design right opposite a great lunch spot - Le Chalat. D30,000 entrance I believe. Some bits can be slightly dangerous but just be sensible and you will be fine. I’m a complete scaredy cat and I was absolutely fine walking around everywhere. Wouldn’t spend too much time here but nice to pop into.
One of Da Lat's most attractive tourist destinations. Its unique and impressive architecture will definitely bring the family back.

When visiting Dalat this is something to put on your list of places to visit. The Crazy House is listed as one of the world’s ten most “bizarre” buildings. Its design resembles a giant tree, incorporating sculptured design elements representing natural forms such as animals, mushrooms, spider webs and caves.Be sure to visit the Crazy House when coming to Da Lat. It's a great way to spend an afternoon.Brennan and Mary
This place makes you feel like a child again, honestly is fantastic and interesting to see what a mind is capable of
Definetly worth a visit. I would not rent a room there but to view it makes sense.High imagination on the part of the builder. Go in the morning too many visitors in the afternoon.

Even though this place has become very touristy, a visit is a must. You'll be amazed by all the architecture and the decor of the rooms (yes, you can sleep there). It's a pleasure to wander through this crazy house. I encourage you to go.

A truly bizarre house, as its name suggests, an unusual but really fun place to see and do. Entry is 50,000 dong per person. One small drawback: we visited while the house was still under construction (an extension), so there was a part we couldn't see! But it's a fantastic experience!
Great palace in Dalat to visit for fun .you can explore ,loved the design and maze like walks as every path will lead to somewhere fun and interesting.
Bring your camera - very photogenic. Try to arrive early as can get busy with bus loads of tour parties.

The Crazy House in Da Lat doesn't occupy a large area, but the stairs inside are very steep and narrow. The elderly sigh at the sight of it and can't climb up and down, but the young people find it very exciting and worth a visit.

Great place to visit as it is fun and kinda reminds me of a giant kiddie's explore play place for adults. Loved the design and maze like walks as every path will lead to somewhere fun and interesting. Favourite room is the underwater one, especially love the shark mural on the floor that is cool.Really worth a visit to just go crazy like the house and let your imagination run wild with how you want to take your photos. If you climb to the top of the highest building, there is a breath taking view of the city.Be mindful of the stairsways though as some are rather steep so walk with caution and hold the rails.I especially like the room designs but not sure if i wld mind staying there since its a tourist attraction so people will be coming to look at you sleep from what i read. Dont miss out on the coffee and brownies sold at the little tuck-shop at the entrance. Really yummy. Quite a happy and interesting place that should not be missed.
It's incredible, very fun for kids and adults. We enjoyed it a lot; it's one of the nicest attractions in Da Lat.
This place is very unique and you can definitely lose yourself in it. It is in the top 10 most craziest houses in the world and it is well deserved. They are even building more to it now.

We visited this hotel as part of a tour of Dalat. It's truly unique. From the outside, it looks like a fairytale house from a Disney cartoon. The interior is equally unusual: passageways, labyrinths, multi-level architecture, and intricate paintings on the ceilings and walls. The rooms, while unusual, have everything you need. The owner has put a lot of effort into creating such a unique masterpiece. It's definitely worth a visit, but I doubt I'd want to stay there. What kind of vacation would it be if there were always crowds of curious tourists milling about?

The Crazy House, located in Da Lat, Southern Vietnam, resembles a mix of a castle and a cave from the outside. It's quite spacious, with two floors and interconnected corridors. There are even rooms available, but reservations need to be made years in advance. Inside, some areas are decorated like an underwater world, while others are uniquely themed, combining multiple concepts. It's extremely crowded, and tickets are required to enter.
You just have to visit the Crazy House It’s an architectural masterpiece It has to be seen to be believed But be very careful if you have young children. Some of the staircases are narrow and high
Excellent in its own unique way. Well worth a visit. What an amazing imagination the architect has. Go early as it can get very busy as tourist buses arrive.

When we were traveling to Dalat, we knew this was one of the city's main attractions. And the "upside-down hotel," as it's also called, didn't disappoint. You can take photos here at every turn; the building looks unusual and amazing. I definitely recommend a visit. Entry is 40,000 DKK.

The Crazy House Hotel/Museum is truly a very unusual/crazy place. We went there on a tour, but I had no desire to stay overnight. I've never seen anything like it before. It's deservedly one of the top 10 most unusual hotels in the world. New rooms are currently being built. There's an entrance fee. The top of the hotel offers a great view of the city. There's a cafe selling freshly squeezed juices.

A kind of Sagrada Familia, similar as well in the design as in the long term work. This house is not crazy, it is a beautiful hotel. Unfortunately we did not sleep in, only visited it. It seems that the architect has decided to merge buildings and Nature giving a symbiotic place where trees ,  animals, sharks, etc ... merge with the hotel building. Very interesting work still ongoing on thousands square meters.To visit absolutely  when in Da Lat!
Definitely worth a visit! The rooms are cool and perfect for a few days, but I didn't see any guests. There's a great observation deck on one of the buildings, a worthwhile spot!
Awesome, definitely worth checking out. Hundertwasser, Gaudí, and Dali on a trip! 😄 Truly something special! Also a great activity for when it rains!
It's a great place for taking photos, best to go in the morning or at dusk. When I went, it was still relatively off-season, but it was still

The kids were thrilled and scrambled all over it. The fish balls in the frog were incredibly delicious! I really like places like this too. Tickets are 50,000 per adult, but my 8- and 5-year-olds got in for free.

Went there today and can not understand negative reviews at all. If you are travelling to Da Lat you should see this place - it's great!Definitely a crazy house. Reminds me in some ways on Gaudi, the spanish Architect who is famous for the "Sagrada Familia", "Casa Batllo", "Park Güell" and many more.Back to the crazy house:It was actually much bigger than expected and it took me about 2 hours walking through the area (took a lot of photos). I think I have seen the most - going through the house was very much like beeing in a maze.They are still doing construction work to make it bigger.But you shouldn't bother about it. And don't bother about the street vendors outside too - this is an asian tourist place...You can have food and drinks inside if you want.
A house with very unusual architecture 🤔! Definitely kitsch!! But very funny! Rooms are available for rent, but those with vertigo should avoid it, as everything is elevated...except the garden!
50.000VDN for entry. There is very much to see, many rooms to see and roads to take. You can even look into some of the rooms they have in the hotel, very cool.
A place to visit if you are in Da Lat.Is a really nice place because is a strange house, like a gnome house, and is totally crazy!

I've been here many times, and I have to say, the architecture is bizarre and unlike anything else. Every detail reflects the owner's personality and dedication to this house. Perhaps it's because of its strangeness that people call it the Crazy House. Another impressive thing is that the staff wear costumes similar to those worn by witches in fairy tales. The imaginative architecture is fascinating. The small, lush green garden is also very interesting.

The best thing we have seen in Da Lat! It is a fascinating piece of art! I wish I had more time to spend there.  We spent about 1 hr, but it was not enough. Our guide was in a hurry to take us to the top of the hill Lang Bien.We were really disappointed! The 4x4 car  was very unconformable, I got sick for the road and the gasoline smell.On the top ... really nothing interesting, only lot of tourists.The view should have been charming, maybe 60 years ago, now you see only greenhouses plans.

Every time I vacation in Vietnam, I make sure to visit Madame Nga's house. It's like a living organism, with something new every year. Visit it, you won't regret it. I wouldn't stay there overnight, though; I think it's a bit uneasy at night.
really quirky.  lots of fun discovering all the tree houses and the amazing designs. the work in it is astounding. One visit would be enough tho.

Probably one of the most psychedelic things I've ever seen with my own eyes! At the very top of the building, or rather on the approaches to it, there's a very strange sensation – like a slight affect. %) Overall, a must-see in Dalat!!! Words are hard to describe the beauty of this place!

An amazing structure you have to visit when you are here. It became very special for us when we had an unexpected meeting with the founder and now 77-year-old architect. Even our guide had never experienced anything like this before.

I thought this would be a small wonky house but it’s huge and really interesting to visit, the lady architect had a vivid imagination and you can stay here as they have rooms to rent. It’s a great place for kids to visit and lots of passages and places to play hide and seek.
The maze of the hotel rooms is intriguing, spent a few hours exploring the rooms and turrets. It is beautifully built, but parts are still under construction. Very impressive!
Went for a beer in this place a d ended up walking around a maze for 29min. Good fun and the music was great. View from the balcony is top too.  Attacks backpackers and western travelers more.
An unremarkable tourist destination.

A tourist trap. There's no soul. There's nothing.
Better go to the nearby churches and leave some money there.
Unnecessary!

It's all social media hype. The woman originally wanted to leave a unique gift for her child and grandchild, so she built this place. It's a complete waste of time; the entrance fee is unnecessarily high. Taking photos is a separate achievement; the space is too small and there are too many visitors.

ATTENZIONE! ⚠️

A real tourist trap. Unfortunately, I can't describe it any other way. The entrance fee isn't worth a single dong, and it's a complete waste of time!
A total tourist trap. Unfortunately, I can't describe it any other way. The entrance fee isn't worth a single dong and is a complete waste of time!
I'm not sure what I'm going to see.
Visited, didn't stay in hotel. Ugly, boring (most of it is gift shops), dangerous to walk up (especially in rain. Waste of time

I couldn't have imagined this even in my worst nightmares. The route to the room was through utility rooms with dirty linens, a storage room, and a dark, deserted courtyard. The tiny room lacks any amenities: not a single bedside table, bedside lamp, closet, air conditioning, or any other appliances. Everything is very bad. There is nothing artistic or anything that suggests a unique hotel. Even for unpretentious guests, it is too poor and poor.

I went alone... I do not recommend it for solo travelers.

Because of the steep and narrow stairs and the crowds, I couldn't even go inside and just took a few photos from the outside.

It's not worth it at all.
The entrance fee is expensive, and it's not worth visiting. I expected better.

It was truly terrible. The ticket staff were unhelpful. I had called ahead to book tickets and they said I just needed to go to the counter and ask for an invoice, but when I got there, the staff didn't issue an invoice. I will never come back. It was awful.
The restrooms were so smelly that the stench permeated the entire area during the tour; it was unbearable, and we had to leave halfway through.

If there are children in the family, it is recommended to visit to foster children's innocence, but I absolutely do not recommend it for adults.

It is a place that is covered with concrete buildings that do not fit the center of the residential area, so it does not feel friendly.
It's not clear at all

We didn't stay at the hotel; we came with a group on an excursion block. It's a strange place and a complete waste of time. I'd like to know: are the tour organizers forced to bring groups here? It would have been better to have a coffee than to look at "this."

A complete waste of time.
Strange "art," in my opinion, completely tasteless; I didn't even want to take a single photo. It's an unpleasant and strange place. It's psychologically depressing. It would have been more comfortable to simply spend this time strolling around the city. I strongly recommend it.

I visited because it was within walking distance of my accommodation, but

this place is really…

This was a very bad experience. I love unusual hotels. My family and I booked the "Pumpkin" room, checked in, dropped off our bags, and went for a walk around the hotel. We saw that all the rooms were available, and we were the only guests. We liked another room and wanted to change our reservation. We went to reception and offered to change our reservation. They told us we couldn't do that. Then, after a short conversation, they told us we could pay extra in cash for a different room, and we agreed. However, upon checkout, they charged us an additional 500,000 because one of us had used the restroom in the first room we'd originally booked. One of the staff even brought back a trash bag from the bin with toilet paper inside.
Arguing was pointless; the staff were extremely rude, didn't speak well, blocked our car, scared our small child, and were very aggressive.
I've never seen such a lack of service before; it was terrible.

Regarding the accommodation itself: since this is a tourist hotel, be prepared for crowds of tourists to stomp up the stairs and batter your room door open, starting at 9 a.m., and people to peer in through the windows. It truly was a madhouse, in the worst sense of the word.
Nothing much to see
The parking attendant was very rude.

I was a tour guide taking guests to the villa on May 22nd at 8:30 AM. The staff made comparisons between different types of tourists, prioritizing Korean tourists while showing contempt for others (the staff said that Korean tourists need to present identification while Korean tourists don't). The staff member loudly challenged me to call management to report the incident. It was an event I never imagined – the staff would have such a terrible attitude towards customer service.

Going to see this is a waste of money and time. It seems like a crazy thing to do.
Why would anyone go?
The food was bland and the service was disappointing.

As a museum, it's a questionable place. Yes, the structure is interesting, but everything is executed in a cringey way. It's crowded and claustrophobic, and the stairs are narrow and dangerous. I don't understand why this is a must-see in Dalat; it's better to have a coffee and look at the mountains.

Its a mad house rather scary house.
Please be aware that it has got v v narrow stairs (Up and Down both) at a good height so if u have fear of height pls donot visit this place ..also there is no navigation here so One has no idea where r u going OR you are missing out on some areas of this house. Also if u can manage high narrow stairs in prickly Sun then visit the place else better explore other places or take some rest at hotel itself to energise urself. Sorry but For us it wasnt worth it. Didnt like it at all. Also PLEASE MARK EXIT WAY ATLEAST..we literally had to look for it for half an hr and somehow went out from another restaurant gate ..

Visit the Mad House; entrance fee is about €3.50 per person. The house is a labyrinthine series of stairs built from fake tree trunks. It's spread over several floors, with various shops and cafes. Personally, I don't think it's worth the expense. Go elsewhere.

Pointless walking up and down stairs with a less-than-ideal view of the city
Absolutely not worth the money
Could not describe this as architecture. Was fully expecting a rusty rollercoaster to appear at any point.
I don't understand who likes this.
Wondering why this attraction is recommended that much … quite dangerous and you better not be afraid of height at all

I have a prepaid reservation since Jan 18. Upon check in I’m told they double booked my room on my second night somehow. They have another room available but they tell me I will have to pay half of the difference in price. I travel ALOT.  Never have I been to a hotel that makes an error double booking and doesn’t just offer you the available room they have without paying extra. Ridiculous. I also don’t understand why I am the one checking in a day earlier than the second booking yet I’m the one who has to move and pay more for it. I am not feeling well and I really needed to just rest  but now I can’t because I will be looking for another hotel. Horrible policy and service in my opinion. I’m really disappointed. Was really looking forward to my stay.

On August 15, 2020, my family visited Hang Nga Villa. We were all quite delighted with the fairytale-like colors and spent a lot of time taking pictures. Unfortunately, my wife forgot her handbag at the aquarium. When we went outside, we remembered we'd forgotten it, but when we turned back, it was gone within 5 minutes.
Checking the security cameras, we didn't see any other guests taking it; it could only have been taken and hidden by a guest or an employee. But the most upsetting thing was that we couldn't find anyone with authority at the villa, only a few staff members searching. We had to contact the local police to file a report and then give Hang Nga Villa their phone number. Even though it's a well-known tourist area, we couldn't find anyone to help us when we asked to speak to the owner or manager. It was frustrating.
The whole afternoon was spent searching, with only my wife and one assistant. Reviewed as an attraction only;
I would've liked to stay on one of their rooms, and then take the time to visit the complex after the mass amount of tourists leave....but they are strict with NO PETS ALLOWED. Yet I can visit the attraction during business hours with my dog!?!?!  That to me is so bizarre.
So, as an attraction, and during visitation hours, it's utter mayhem and thorough madness. Simply put, they permit way too many visitors all at once (no bookings or reservations are needed). Trying to navigate the very, narrow, and winding staircases (with very low barricades / balustrades) is insanely difficult and a bit dangerous. Plus, there's no direction in which way to travel on these stairways, and people traverse both ways, creating congestion points and precarious overtaking points. The owners need to take some control and manage the crowds and the directions people walk. (Noting that some of these walkways/stairs are fairly high up....a fall from these heights could be fatal). Honestly,  I think they need to take visits only by reservation,  decrease the number of visitors per session, and then increase the entrance price (to cover their expenses due to lesser visitors). Only 2 stars due to this overcrowding and lack of management....could be a 3 or 4 star place.
The attraction itself is really good and enjoyable to explore all facets of the place (notwithstanding the overcrowding at most times during operating hours). However, it's not suited to everyone's taste.
There are a multitude of maze-like walkways, suspension bridges, and stairways that lead to many different; sculpted sections, small nooks to sit, accommodations, gardens, and themed rooms. You could literally spend hours exploring the entire place. Unfortunately it is more visual than photographic (most things are crammed in too close), but there are some nice opportunities to take photos (selfie lovers & Instagramers seem to adore posing in many areas....and poses ...to the hindrance of others.) Video bloggers would probably love it more than photographers.
I didn't bother to investigate the whole place (too crowded), but I was lucky enough to visit this place way back in 2009....hardly any crowds back then.
Lastly, I was permitted inside with my dog (her dog clipping appointment was cancelled at the last minute) but it was unwise of me to do so, as it was difficult to manoeuvre the narrow areas and steps while carrying her with the masses of visitors.  It was only a last-minute thing as I left DaLat the day after.

The opening time is 8:30 AM, but my family arrived at 8:20 AM, in heavy rain. We had three young children and four elderly people, and the parking lot was more than 200 meters away. I wanted to be flexible so the children and elderly could take shelter from the rain, and I asked them to sell tickets and let us in earlier, but the staff said we couldn't enter until exactly 8:30 AM. Therefore, I was very disappointed with this visit. Hopefully, the staff will be more flexible in this situation next time.

It is a fascinating and interesting space. Various colors and connecting bridges link the indoor and outdoor areas, creating a harmonious blend with Da Lat's sunny weather, the sky, and the flowers and trees in the garden. The coloring of the indoor walls and floors is magnificent and fantastic. However, there are no safety signs or warning markers. As I was exiting through the final exit, the edge of the stairs was worn down; due to the slipperiness of my shoes and the stairs, I slipped and hit the floor. My arm was broken. The guides and building managers showed no interest in my accident. Only after my companions asked for disinfectant did they bring a first-aid kit. Barely clutching my injured arm, I went to the hospital with my companions. Despite the large number of tourists, there are no precautions or measures in place regarding safety accidents. I believe that safety measures are necessary, especially considering the entrance fee. A rapid response plan is also required in the event of an accident. The accident occurred in the area connected to the building entrance, next to the ticket booth, yet there were no staff members present. Of course, my own carelessness played a part, but the safety measures taken after the accident were very inadequate, and there appeared to be absolutely no proper response protocol. While maintaining the magnificent structures is important, I believe it is also necessary to be considerate of tourists who pay an admission fee to visit.

Hang Nga Villa in Dalat, also known as the Crazy House in Dalat, is often referred to by tourists visiting the city as the "bizarre house" in Dalat. It is one of the most unique architectural works in Dalat.

I heard about an incredibly flamboyant guesthouse near the Da Lat Market, so I went to check it out. Its bizarre exterior lived up to its reputation; you can spot it from afar. I'm not the type to stay in cheap accommodations, so I only admired it from the outside, but I still thoroughly enjoyed it.

What surprised me most was its original architectural style. It reminded me of Gaudí in Spain, or perhaps even more free-spirited and crazy. I heard that a female architect named Nga started building this place after studying architecture in Moscow, and I was simply amazed by her ideas. I heard that construction began in 1990, and expansions and renovations are still ongoing. There's something romantic about a work of art that never ends.

The inside of the building is like a giant maze, with rooms and passageways organized by themes such as dragons, forests, water, and rocks. Each room in the guesthouse is named after an animal, and it's interesting that sculptures of those animals are displayed.


Even from the outside, the intricate network of passageways and bridges is visible, crisscrossing the area like an underground waterway in a forest. It's easy to see why this building is inspired by the natural formations of the 400-million-year-old karst landscape. Furthermore, looking around, it harmonizes beautifully with Da Lat's natural environment, where 94% is pristine forest. Since this forest is home to 800 species of wild animals and 260 species of birds, perhaps Mr. Nga tried to incorporate this magnificent nature into the building.

However, as mentioned in reviews, the passageways are quite narrow, and there are places where passing is difficult. Also, there are many narrow staircases leading to the rooftop and places with low railings, which might be challenging for those with a fear of heights. Small children could easily get lost, so it's important to keep a close eye on them.

If you're traveling to Da Lat, I think this is a place worth seeing. The entrance fee is 80,000 VND, and it seems you can see everything in about an hour to an hour and a half. This hotel has a very unique design, but even if you're not staying there, you can visit the inside by paying an entrance fee (I think it was 80,000 VND, about 480 yen) at the entrance. There are quite a few stairs to climb and descend, and even though it's called "inside the hotel," there are many steep, narrow staircases on the outside of the building with very low handrails, so you need to be careful not to lose your balance. In fact, there may be some places that people with a fear of heights won't be able to climb. The passageways are narrow, so you'll need to be considerate of others as you pass each other. It's not an exaggeration to say that you should set aside about two hours for your visit. It's a rare building that you don't see very often, so it's worth seeing. It's very impactful. You might even feel a sense of fear, wondering how people don't fall from such heights! If you're going to Da Lat, be sure to include it in your itinerary!

Hang Nga Villa, also known as Crazy House, is truly one of the most unique destinations in Da Lat that I've ever visited. Right from the entrance, it's distinctly different from typical architecture: everything is winding and meandering, like a fairytale world with a touch of mystery. The further you go inside, the more surprised you'll be by the narrow corridors, the winding staircases, and the rooms designed without any rules – truly "lost in a bizarre dream."

Every corner of Crazy House is special, perfect for taking Instagram-worthy photos. Especially in the morning, with the gentle sunlight filtering through the gaps, the scene becomes even more enchanting. Besides sightseeing, there are also rooms for guests who want to experience an overnight stay in this unique architecture. However, rooms are limited, so reservations are required.

What I liked most was the bold creativity, completely different from other familiar places in Da Lat. However, there are a few things to note: the pathways are quite steep and narrow in many places, so caution is needed, especially when traveling with young children. Also, it can get quite crowded at lunchtime, so if you want to take good photos, you should go early.

The entrance fee is reasonable. If you love art and architecture and want to experience a unique space, Hang Nga Villa is definitely a place you shouldn't miss when visiting Da Lat!

There's an entrance fee—I can't tell you the exact price, as I was on a tour. Tickets can be purchased on site; there are ticket offices nearby.

You can also rent a room on the grounds—there are 11 in total, each with its own style. Keep in mind that on weekdays, tourists wander the grounds until 6:00 PM, so complete silence isn't guaranteed. If a room is available, you can peek inside, but you're not allowed to enter. If a room is occupied, don't even try to peek.

The grounds themselves are interestingly designed, with both a marine and a forest theme. There are many narrow, branching paths, unexpected passages, and exits. There's a small area with a pond and carp, designed to resemble a forest with mushrooms—it looks unusual.

Overall, it's interesting to stroll and explore, but it's best to do it on your own; the time allotted for the tours is too short, and it's easy to get lost.

On the downside, some of the paths are very narrow and have low railings and fencing; even someone of short stature like me would easily fall over them, which is quite dangerous.

Based on that sublime architectural philosophy, the Crazy House must be appreciated purely as a work of art.
