they sell stamps postcards envelopes paper for wrapping parcels its quite amazing really you can get so much post stuff in one place
We took a break in this building after walking around the city centre. It was amazing to see the post office in operation. A wonderful picturesque building with plenty of souvenir shopping places.

Nice budiling with old style architecture, good for souvenier shopping and getting a feel for pre war vietnam. It tend sto get crowded, and the shopping is quite standard, nothing unique. But you can go to check out the building and the post office which still sells stamps etc.
A few shops to look through but all selling the same thing. No really much to see. The building is worth a look.

Fascinating glimpse into history of old postal age, this is still a functioning post office we can send a letter or postcard from here. The old fashioned glue pots are still in use for sticking stamps to letters. There are two wings branching out from the main office, selling souvenirs, postcards but the prices are very high and the same products can probably be found elsewhere for a very better price. It is located next door to Notre Dame Cathedral, we can visit two cultural sites together.
Beautiful building in the French style. Don't take too much time for it. If you have to pass by, fine, but that's it. Inside is for tourists only.

The building was interesting (mildly) with some tourist stuff for sale, but I would not make a special trip to see this. Takes 5-10 minutes, unless you are already right here for the cathedral (which is closed for restoration right now), there is better stuff to see just walking around the streets.

It's on the list of places to visit so we did, nothing notable to report other than its a working post office with some nice architecture from a bygone era. With photos taken it was on to the next venue on the list.
It’s a post office....not much else to say really!A nice old building from an era time forgot, very close to Notre Dame too. Postcards are on sale, as stamps! It is still a working post office.
Building itself is quite nice but not much to really see. Its just a post office still in operation with souvenir stores scattered throughout
I wasn't very impressed. It was small. Architecture wise, for me it was just alright. The inside is very commercialized, just little tourists deskshops.
There is not much to regret if you couldn't visit this place. it is better than Independence Palace I HCMC as it is old French architecture.  Couple of quick photos is all you need.

A nice building, designed by Eifel, with the option of purchasing from the usual tourist stalls... considering there aren't many attractions in and around Saigon, you might consider a visit, but if you're far away, it's not worth going too far... It's a different story if you're a stamp enthusiast... from what I understand, you can purchase them here...

The building it’s self is architecturally amazing it’s a great building steeped in history. Realistically, I can’t see why you would spend more than 15 minutes here after taking a few pictures! Directly across the road from Notre Dam Cathedral so two for one - bonus There’s a few stalls inside selling tourist stuff at over inflated prices

Good to see and spend some time there. It’s beautifully renovated and one can imagine how it was 100 years ago. It was very hot inside, seems no air conditioning or fans. So a very authentic experience!
Just something to take a picture of, otherwise didn't make much of an impression. It's free so just go take a look.
Lovely place to visit, busy, pricey souvenirs but it's still a cool place to look at.  I found the people friendly and welcoming and there were dancers shooting videos out the front.
The building was lovely to look at and inside are many souvenir shops. It’s right across the road from the Notre Dame Cathedral.
Not so much a post office as a huge souvenir shop with many little spaces pushing Chinese made stuff. Not really worthwhile to spend much time on.
We see it only from the outside on a guided city tour. Beautiful old building from which... Journalists during the war called their stories in from. It operates today as a post office.

The post office is a famous tourist destination which did not disappoint.  It's a old French colonial designed building whose architecture is just beautiful. It is still a functioning post office and you can buy a Vietnamese designed postcard for between £1 and £3 to post to loved ones or to yourself! The seller's also offer the usual ornaments,  bags and posters as well as jewellery,  so definitely worth a visit. It gets busy and is very warm inside. A good point to note that it is literally opposite the beautiful Notre Dame Cathedral.
It was surprising to see that it was not only a historic building, but still functions as a post office.However, the majority of the building was occupied by souvenir shops, which were crowded.

The central post office of Saigon is a pretty building built by the French Post Office Administration between 1886 & 1891 located Place de la commune de Paris. Today it is still in operation but looks more like a museum for tourists , with souvenir shops .It is worth stopping for a few minutes and detailing the interior architecture with at the back of the gallery a superb portrait of Ho Chi Minh . See also the old telephone booths

A beautiful building on the outside and the inside. Right in the heart of the city too. You can still buy stamps and use the phone lines there if needed, plus there are vendors selling more touristy stuff as well. The maps on the wall I found really interesting about how Vietnam looked when the Post Office was built and operating in its heyday.

Username: Travel Our Style Simon and Catrina
Comment: The whole area around the post office is worth a visit, not only is the Notre Dame Cathedral of Saigon but many other French colonial buildings with some high-end shops and restaurants its worth a walk to the Saigon Opera House. If you look back you will see the old CIA mission where the last Helicopter flight out of Saigon took off from.

This was about a 20-minute visit, and honestly that’s all you really need. The history here is interesting — the building dates back to the French colonial period and still carries that classic architectural style. It’s a quick sightseeing stop: you can grab a photo, buy a few knick-knacks, or even mail a postcard home. I picked me up a couple cute fridge magnets. It’s not a full “experience,” but it’s a nice little piece of Saigon history and an easy add-on to any District 1 walk.
Very nice building that I rediscover for the 2nd time. 20 years ago I don't remember seeing so many shops , too bad it spoils the beauty of the place!

Easy to find next to the Cathedral. Popular with locals and tourists alike. When we visited on a Saturday morning there was a real atmosphere outside with music and people socialising.The building is a real throw back to days gone by. Cool architecture, it’s full of souvenir ‘stuff’, but there are some  rows of old wooden phone booths with clocks above showing the time in cities around the world. Buying and sending a postcard seems to be the most popular activity. Plenty of cards to choose from, write it, and then send it from the post office counter. It’s definitely worth popping into and will save not take you half an hour.
Amazing building and a must see in Saigon. Well worth visiting to possibly send postcard to loved ones back. Plenty of souvenirs to be bought at great prices.

A very nice building The postcards are on sale on site you can have them postage on site and you will see the employees at work it is quite nice Many tourists in this month of July there are shops on both sides we noticed on prices were higher than elsewhere in Vietnam

It is a very nice office, very well preserved, it is close to several places of interest, making it a place in the 1st district that is not far to go.Its architecture is very beautiful, a building worth knowing
This famous building and were impressed by the grand, colonial architecture. It felt like stepping back in time. It's still a functioning post office, which is cool.
Quite crowded, a zillion of tourists, souvenir shops. Many people buy post cards and stamps and mail them. Built by the French in the 19th century, beautiful building.

The two 40m high towers look far into the landscape and that was all we could perceive from the church.Nevertheless, we do not regret our way there!The reason: the building stands in the middle of a very charming urban ensemble, which apparently attracts numerous – especially younger – people in the evening.The tallest building is currently the ‘Old Town Hall’, built in 1908, which is not missing from any guided tour of the city.Also interesting for the visit is the gastronomy. There are restaurants, but the main part lies in the street gastronomy.  We, too, have mingled a bit with the younger people here. Although we are already of an older vintage, we were received very kindly. A good sign of the very casual atmosphere that lies above the whole scenery. Great!
Great architecture and still a working post office!Some very nice tourist outlets inside of the entrance with not too bad prices either. Great place to escape the heat of the city.
Much more interesting than imagined. Wonderful old French building. Many souvenir shops inside as well as postal facilities. Friendly staff.

Old post office which has exterior and interior architecture in the French style.It still operates as a post office but the two side lanes and part of the Central area are now set aside as shopping areas for souvenirs and the like.Interesting to visit and entry is free.
Fun to write a card to family at home. While doing some sightseeing this is a nice place for a quick stop and write a card. The letters arrived within 14 weeks from Vietnam to the Netherlands

This is a proper post office. The well-maintained colonial building still retains its function. Sure a lot of it is devoted to selling stuff to send, mainly postcards, but then you can write it, buy a stamp, and post it - voila instant souvenir. Deservedly busy and well worth a quick stop off.
The building - built by the French - is interesting both externally and internally but it’s really just a lot of tourist shops inside plus a post office. Worth having a look if you’re in the area.

Mandatory stop. The interior is very nice with its careful decorations, like its old booths for making calls.The mail service continues to perform its function. You can buy nice postcards at the souvenir shops and send them as souvenirs to your family and friends.The building is in the French colonial style designed by Gustave Eiffel.Inside is the portrait of Ho Chi Minh and the central vault is spectacular.

Must see place while in Ho Chi Minh City. Nice post cards to mail to family or friends.  Also, a nice cheap souvenir to take back with you.  Just a note, don’t expect your mailed post card to get to its destination quick.  We were told it could take up to two weeks or not arrive at all.  I completely gave up on my post card to get to my parents.  It arrived over two months after mailing it.
Very nice building, but the interior is a bit cluttered. Phone booths need to be restored inside.  Even if the visit is quick it is a must.

A large colonial building, it is the central post office of Saigon, created by Gustave Eiffel's architectural firm in the late nineteenth century. When we visited it we were full of tourists, inside and out, so it was also difficult to photograph the details without filming someone.... I was struck by the skylights on the ceiling, from which a lot of light comes, and the portrait of Ho Chi Minh that dominates from above, there are old post boxes, with clocks marking the time of different places in the world, many tourists were busy writing postcards to send, all around many souvenir stalls, and agencies that offered tours that took away charm. Out with a delirium of tourists to take pictures!

Worth calling in to see this place if you are passing. There’s not really a great deal to do other than take a few photos of the building and send a few postcards home. But it’s novel! Lots of tourists and very busy.
Very nice place.Emblematic of the city with a lot of movement where you find all kinds of products for sale and that is definitely mandatory in this city.

Extremely busy, especially on a hot, muggy day and lots of foreigners that have no etiquette who push and shove. But so nice to be able to buy souvenirs, knick knacks or postcards to send back to loved ones back home.

It’s a beautiful building and one of the top attractions of the city, but there were way too people inside. But make sure you buy enough stamps from here. I found out later it was very difficult to buy stamps in Vietnam’s other cities.

We checked out the Central Post Office - what an architectural gem! The grand facade is impressive, and those vintage telephone booths are super cute. It's a bit touristy, but worth a quick stop. We snapped some pics and enjoyed the old-world charm.

The Saigon Central Post Office is an absolute must-visit for anyone exploring Ho Chi Minh City. Stepping into this grand structure is like taking a journey back in time.The exterior boasts elegant yellow walls with intricate details, a stunning blend of history and art. Inside, the high-vaulted ceiling and the vintage wooden telephone booths create a nostalgic charm. The large portrait of Ho Chi Minh overlooking the main hall adds a significant cultural touch.What impressed me most was how the post office is still a functional space, with locals and tourists using it for postal services, buying postcards, or browsing the souvenir stalls. The staff were friendly and efficient, making it an enjoyable experience.I also loved the convenience of writing a postcard and mailing it right there—a personal and heartfelt way to share a piece of Vietnam with loved ones.The Saigon Central Post Office is not just a historic building; it’s a living museum of Vietnam’s past. Whether you're an architecture enthusiast, a history buff, or just someone looking to soak in the local culture, this place will not disappoint.

This is a nice, historic building to pop into while going around the city.  The interior is nifty and I loved the old phone booths and clocks of different time zones.  There are places to sit down and write a post card which you can then mail.  The place is busy and there are bunch of shops inside.  Worth a stop if you are in the area.

We popped in mainly for the architecture. The building is beautiful, but it was quite crowded with tourists. Sent some postcards home, though prices were a bit touristy. The ceiling and old phone booths made for nice photos. Quick 30-minute visit was enough.

This is a fascinating spot to visit, even with its constant buzz of tourists. It radiates a positive, uplifting energy and offers a brief escape to simpler, more nostalgic times. A truly memorable experience that’s worth braving the crowds for!

Very beautiful building, a lot of tourists when we got there. ptices are marked up as well so i would recommend going to other places for souvenirs. you probably do not need to stay here for long since there are limited things to see, but i would say just one time visit would suffice

we enjoyed wandering through its grand halls, admiring the intricate details. It’s a great spot for photos and sending postcards, but don’t expect much in terms of modern amenities. Still, worth a quick visit!
A beautiful building from the French colonial era. It is a small building, it sits next to the church.  they have souvenirs and postal cards. We also liked the flooring of the building.
The building is beautiful and there are many small items on sale, so it might be a good place to buy a little souvenir. We can send postcards to commemorate the event, but it was very crowded.

It may seem strange to review an experience from a Post Office 8,000 miles away, but after seeing things for myself, I feel it was something worth sharing. You can easily see the French influences on the building both inside and out. Beautiful and very busy, located in a popular area of town. With wall murals, wood phone booths, tile floors, and skylights. Built in the late 1880's, it has stood the test of time, and probably enjoyed a renovation or two. Paint, wood trim, floor, etc. all in photo- ready shape. Looking at the oversize Ho Chi Minh portrait on the back wall, it was easy to remember all the great historical events written in letters passing through this place! A free- of- charge activity with LOTS to look at. There are limited shopping kiosks located on the center floor and additional shopping in each wing. Best of all, you can still purchase postal services here!! Parcels and postcards can be mailed with postage available from the very helpful and informed clerks. The post office provided a nice indoor break from the city heat, a glimpse of local culture, and lots of HCMC history to feel a part of. I'm happy I included time for a visit.

The French colonial architecture of this post office took our breath away! We spent an hour admiring the stunning arched ceilings and vintage phone booths. The elderly gentleman writing letters for locals was such a touching sight.
Good stop on your way exploring district 1. The architecture is intrrinh, as is the murals. Good place to buy and write your postcards before mailing them. 15 - 30 minute visit.
This building has a nice architecture and one of the top tourist places in the city. One can also send postcards to their loved ones from here.

Not an exciting activity, but a pretty old building where you send some snail mail home. Be sure to combine it with book street, notre dame, and the opera house - all within a few blocks - and then you can feel like you’ve done most of the obligatory sites.

Saigon Central Post OfficeThe central post office representing Ho Chi Minh City.Built after the Musée d'Orsay in Paris.It's a colonial architecture post office.Created by French architect Villedieu,The steel frame design is renowned for the Eiffel TowerIt is by Gustave Eiffel.The glass-enclosedThe large vaulted ceiling was lovely.Mr. Ho Chi Minh's painting is also displayed.

The post office is located in the city center, but there are also souvenir shops and tour desks inside. The tour bus that takes you around the Things to do in the city leaves from the front of Saigon Central Post Office.

The atmosphere is similar to the Musée d'Orsay in Paris. Inside, it feels like a station, but it is a post office. The ceiling is brilliant. Like there were more tourists than people using it as a post office?

The central post office is opposite of the cathedral (but still under renovation.At glance I thought this is a train station due to gigantic clock. Inside you can enjoy old French architecture which were built in the late 19th Century.The post office stamps and postcards for you to sent it back home but i feel its too much souvenir shops.

This old Post Office is a French architecture. As this is tourist attraction, It was certainly crowded.Lots of shop sell souvenirs and other touristy stuff. You could sent a postcard back home from this place.

The square in front and to direct view to the cathedral is beautiful, although it is still under construction. Inside you can enjoy some old French architecture. Souvenirs are over price but the post office sells nice stamps and postcards

Great stop on our tour of Ho Chi Minh City. The architecture is beautiful and loved the interior as well. I purchased several small souvenirs in the shops inside and booked another tour at the booth inside. Everyone was friendly and not pushy at all.

1)The Authority has CLEVERLY Turn this into a MUST Visit Icon in HCMC.2)The place is flooded with both Overseas and Local visitors DAILY; as it is included in ALL (Local or Overseas) Tour package !!3)Most of the Original strutures being preserved; however many shops (artifacts. Jewellery. Electronics. Stamps....)catering mainly to tourists are set up. 4)The location is Very Good, next to another Icon, NOTRE DAME CATHEDRAL, Which was burnt down and still under renovation. The BOOK STREET is also next to it.5)Visit it and ENJOY History !!

This is a fabulous building teeming with tourists. You can see amazing architecture and decorations. Buy a postcard, check out the time in other countries and shop for some local gifts. It was worth a visit but won't take long.
Nice to look at, free to visit. The post office is still located inside the building, and you can also buy and write postcards. However, a large part of the building is used as a souvenir shop.

Built in the 1880s, the Central Post Office is a significant symbol of Ho Chi Minh City in view of its lovely Viledieu design. Not only could Visitors enjoy the magnificent view of the building, they could take the opportunity to send a postcard home at the place.
If you are in the area it is nice to take a look inside. Beautiful building inside and out. There is still a post office, many shops, nothing special.
A lovely historic building both inside and outside. The souvenir shop is worth a look and no harassment by the shop keeper.

It's one of those tourist spots which also serves as a functional building for the people. It's beautiful on the inside and on the outside. It was built by the French and can be observed through its beautiful architecture. Worth a visit

C ( 24.03.10 )It is a beautiful central post office building with a beautiful exterior, and the interior is also beautiful. It is not a long time, but it is worth a visit, and the interior is still working as a post office. Most of the spaces consist of shops selling souvenirs, and there is no need to stay for a long time.
I visited on a half-day tour. There are various souvenir shops. By the way, the Church of Our Lady is next door. (Currently under renovation and cannot be visited.)
The central post office is a building built under the French protectorate. We see old geographical maps there. The interior is still operational but completely occupied by souvenir shops.
There is plenty to see on the steps in front of the post office. Groups of people pose for a picture. The post office itself is a place to buy stamps and souvenirs

Quite a spectacular building. Inside is still a working Post Office and has a beautiful tall ceiling. There are lots of vendors inside and outside selling various things. You can also buy amazing pop up Birthday cards here

This is a post office constructed by the French. Open daily until 6 except on Sundays where it closes at 5 pm.Beautiful interior, and it is impressive that in addition to many commercial booths inside, a part of it serves actually as a post office. You will find a section where people sit on wooden benches writing up postcards to be sent to their special ones.Free entry, and worth a visit!

The Central Post Office in Saigon / Ho Chi Minh City is an amazing historical building and a popular stop off for tours, indeed I visited as part of a cruise ship excursion to Vietnam and we had around 20 minutes here to pop in and take photos. You could easily spend more time as the building houses not just an operating post office but also many market stalls with gifts and magnets at very decent prices.Our guide said the building was built by Gustave Eiffel's company in the late 1880s but it turns out that it was actually built around the designs of another French architect, Alfred Foulhoux. It's one of the top tourist attractions in the city and can be visited at the same time as the Notre-Dame Basilica and the Opera House (both are located close to here). It's free to enter and well worth having a look inside if visiting central Saigon.

Central post office is one of the most remarkable buildings in Saigon, along with the Opera House, Rex Hotel and few others dating back to colonial era.This iconic post office with huge Ho Chi Minh portrait on the opposite side dates back to late 19c, when it was built by French as a main channel of communication in these days. It's located next door, to the right from the main twin tower Notre Dame Cathedral. It's open daily and pretty busy with both, locals and tourists.
Great place to visit and see how it was in the day. It's worth visiting! Heritage Post Office I’d absolutely recommend sending a postcard home! must do

Here’s a reminder of how important post and telecommunications services were in the last century, especially during colonial times when Europeans were dispatched halfway across the world. The building itself is impressive, making a statement in the government quarter. While the interior has preserved phone booths and post office counters where you can send letters by old fashioned snail mail it’s essentially a big souvenir shop with stalls selling all sorts. Handy to see price tickets to base your bargaining on as you continue your travels.
Nice to seeGreat architectureSome nice giftsPick an amazing pop up post card and post it home. Nice for 30 minsClose to centreWorth a visit

Visited here on Dec-27,2023 and explored. Outside this post office are many road side stalls selling card cut out art pieces, traditional hats etc. Saw multiple hop on hop off buses stopping here for some time.Inside are souvenir stalls in the perimeter and benches for visitors to sit etc.A portrait of Ho Chi Minh himself adorns the rear wall of this post office.
It is an interesting building to see. Worth a quick stop if you are in the area. Lots of small shops and a working post office.

Go for it !! There you will find a post office from the colonial era, still in operation, with its old telephone booths... on the sides of the entrance hall, lots of small merchants selling things to make nice little gifts!! We bought 2/3 bits and pieces that we were missing for small gifts, before getting back on the plane.
The post office building, although very ruined and shabby, is truly another fine example of French colonial style. Very interesting.

The old post office building is one of the highlights of HCMC. It is located very close to the cathedral (which is unfortunately currently a construction site). Inside there is actually a post office, but also many souvenir shops. Just take a look inside.

The overall structure is still in great operational condition. There are so many souvenir vendors within the premises but most stands are crammed down hallways making it almost inaccessible at times. The front of the building is where the Hop-on Hop-Off bus tours start. Worth the visit

We have been to HCMC many times and have visited this place on several occasions though not every time. It is one of the iconic landmarks in District 1. But in our recent visit, we have dropped by this place again as my cousins were visiting the city for the first time.It was Sunday and the GPO was very crowded and packed with visitors, both locals and tourists. Many were queuing to buy and send postcards and parcels while others were shopping for local souvenirs. Inside the building was very warm and humid (it was drizzling outside but windy) and even for a short while, we were all perspiring and so we went outside. Hope they can improve the air ventilation here, maybe installing more fans, aircoolers or aircon.But it's nice and one can feel the historical aspect of this building as if time has stopped with many vintage and old setups, decors and furniture.

Lovely building, architecture is great and still functions as a post office, so you can send your postcards :). Great gift shops at sensible prices and lovely, helpful staff. Straight opposite the cathedral and “ little Paris” with lots to do and lovely cafes. I purchased a card from an elderly lady Street vendor outside the post office. Like a foolish tourist gave her a 100,000 d note instead of 10,000 d. The lady pulled my hand with my wallet, gave me back the 100,000 and pointed to a 10,000 note, who could ask for more. The people here are so helpful, even just looking lost on the street people will point in the direction of the next attraction.
A very nicely preserved building - still operational today. Nice interior.You could buy nice souvenirs.

A full functioning post office that is also a tourist must stop and souvenir shop.Beautiful building, all features you’d see in period documentaries.  With all us tourists milling around, people can still get on with their post office biz.

Neither me or my husband has seen something like that before. In our country there is no post office anymore. It's not even commun to send faxes as it practically don't exist anymore in our country. So it was nice to see this beautiful building and all the services that exists there. It aslmost take you back in time.

It’s a beautiful building but there isn’t that much to see. You can go inside for free. We chose to buy and send 2 post cards. The  2 post cards cost £0.52 and to post them back to the UK was £2.10 roughly. Worth a look if passing but nothing major to see inside other than a pretty ceiling and the ability to send mail!

The Central Post Office appears to have been built at the end of the 19th century during the French colonial period, modeled after the Musée d'Orsay. It's still used as a post office today, and its arched ceiling is magnificent. A portrait of Ho Chi Minh hangs at the back of the building. They handle mail dispatch, sell stamps, and offer souvenirs. From the outside, it looks more like a train station than a post office.
The place is a Post Office built during the French occupation. It also has a market where you get small and cheap souvenir items.
An essential visit for those who enjoy architecture and history. The building was constructed in the 19th century and continues to function as a post office to this day.
short visit will do but must go. the architecture is great and it's a working post office meaning people still go there to write letters and counters are all working

It is worth a look at this beautiful old building which has French renaissance architecture. The inside was abuzz with tourists writing on post cards to send home. It is across the road from the Notre Dame cathedral.
Was great to see history of Ho Chi Minh city post office still being used as a post office.  Also many small stalls with souvenirs and the usual tourist stuff.

A worthwhile stop on a 1st timers itinerary.  Beautiful tiles and the old phone boxes are really cool.  A lot going on here and really busy.  Sell a lot of souvenirs here also.  Just opposite the church which is surrounded in scaffolding.  Beautiful building and I think its worth a few minutes of your time to look and look through.
The central post office is a building from the colonial era.

One of the few places listed in Saigon's tourist guides, this characteristic colonial-style building features an iconic image of Ho Chi Minh City at the back of the room. It's a fairly inexpensive place to buy souvenirs. Please note: it closes at 6:00 PM.
a place to people watch, while appreciating a by gone era. a few photos here and there to complete the album
Great that post office is still the post office and you can buy your postcards, souvenirs from there. Friendly staff. Building is in great shape and that is awesome too.
The post office is active and then the rest is pure overpriced tourism but the building is good inside and out.
Nice idea that you go to the post office and send a card to family to see how long it takes to arrive, building very old and nice

Nice building from the outside, okay on the inside. Good selection of postcards and some souvenirs inside. I was walking around stopping at landmarks around the city and enjoyed this as a stop, but probably wouldn't go too out of the way to see it.
Saigon's central post office is an architectural masterpiece by Gustave Eiffel. I recommend visiting and going inside (it closes at 7 PM).
In this spacious building, you can see the French influence from the time of Indochina. It is still in use and houses many small and large shops.
Beautiful, classic. I visited with a friend, who recommended that they still offer letter-writing services here, so I gave it a try. The writer was very charming.
An architecture reminiscent of the colonial era; a very pleasant atmosphere for writing or browsing in the shops on the side wings.
The design is beautiful and classic. I was very impressed when I traveled from Hanoi. It would be even better if the doors could be opened a little higher.
Still functions as an active post office - a historical building at the center of the city from the French colonial era.

An interesting relic from the colonial era that is well worth a visit. Inside you can buy souvenirs but you will also find a nearby post office. The restored cabins of the time and the ceiling are beautiful.
Architecturally intersting building that. Lots of ice cream sellers at the entrance, they also have cool call booths and still have it set up like a postal office and sell stamps
Be sure to stop by this post office on your tour of the city. It is located on Notre Dame Square, which is unfortunately currently being restored. Beautiful postcards are for sale there.

The Central Post Office in Ho Chi Minh City is a French-style building and also serves as the office. Since we visited on the first day of the Lunar New Year, the post office was closed, and we could only take photos in front of it.
Facinating building. Many tourist stalls inside for a walk round. Several good photo oportunies both inside and out.

Architecturally intersting building that is centrally located. Lots of ice cream sellers at the entrance with tape recorded loops loops announcing their flavors . You will  quickly learn that that the Vietnameses words for Chocolate  and Vanilla are the same as in French
Beautiful architecture - luckily not being destroyed during the Amerian war in Vietnam - but there should not be shops inside
It's worth visiting at least once, but only if you have enough time. If you don't have much time in Ho Chi Minh City, I would skip it in order to see other things.
Old Grand Building with a Post Office and  a mall with lots of shops selling local artifacts . Good for Photohsoot .
Very relaxing interesting tour.. Chris is good tour guide. Funny guy and very informative. In ever go Vietnam, will join the tour co again
One of the most mentioned places to visit. They do not accept credit cards. A lot of tourists insiede & outside

It was indeed a busy place to be in. Interesting to see that schools actually brought their students there to  post letters! Other than the postal services, there were also souvenir shops available on both sides of the entrance. Interesting to browse those shops! Nice architecture too!
A must-see. Not only for the beautiful building itself, but also for its location, which allows you to see the cathedral right next door, and the nearby streets brimming with atmosphere and charm.

The Central Post Office is not a long visit...about 10 minutes is all you'll need.  The outside of the building is beautifully ornate and is surprising open on the inside.  The tile work on the floor is beautiful and the maps on the wall are interesting in their depictions.  A quick glimpse is really all you need.
It was looking beautiful from outside and was even more fascinating from inside. First look make it appear like some European railway station. Have good choices to buy memorabilia.
Saigon's old post office has beautiful architecture, making it a great place for tourists to visit and take photos. I highly
Beautiful building from the French occupation era. Lots of souvenirs inside. If you are in this part of Ho Chi Minh, worth a quick visit.

The actual post office building is beautiful and steeped in history. Visiting here was a definite highlight walking ourselves around HCMC. The only disadvantage were the surrounding hawkers who harassed us to buy stuff....all the same stuff you see everywhere. Unfortunately one of them cracked a coconut open, handed it to me and then demanded 150 000 VND for it. Had no choice but to pay as he got very angry at us. The authorities need to crack down on this. When vendors refuse to put prices on their wears, be warned that their intentions are not honest. Had the same.problemnatvthe market.
This building, dating back to the French colonial period and designed by Gustave Eiffel, is part of the city's architectural heritage.
The post office is really in fantastic condition and well maintained.  You really get the feeling that you are taking a step back in time.  A must visit location for any tourist in HCMC.
Beautiful architecture of the building and a great place to buy souvenir's for the folks back home.Do not forget to send a post card to yourself from here
We went after closing but could see inside at the doors. Beautiful constructict. Nice environment. Beautiful place.
Its small shopping place and Post office togther. You can spend like an hour here, can do shopping if you're not goign to Hanoi, or else Hanoi has better option for the same stuff to buy.
A working post office that has seen much history go by its doors. Really the architecture more than anything else. Worth seeing if in the area, not as a special trip.

I'm glad I went in. It's a landmark building and knowing it helps you find your way in Saigon.  Go in and buy a postcard, sit at the early 1920s desks and fill it out, mail it.  I didn't take a thought it anything, don't even know if one is available.  I just walked through the main lobby and admired the decorations on the wall and the enormity of the space.

Not only post office as they do have souvenirs. Google shown Sunday is close so wasn’t expect much to visit that day and luckily did try to get there which is open on Sunday and of course not everything are open still possible to do some stuff like sending parcels or postcard. Postcard price are same nearly everywhere over there and stamp depends on location you wish to send. Very historic building and do have travel agent inside. Very much lively and unique feeling inside. Just next to Notre Dame Cathedral and close to Open Air Bookshop.
What a stunning building both inside and out, we didn't actually use the services but just popped in to have a look around.
A visit is recommended. It has two souvenir shops with fixed prices, which can help you gauge how much to haggle for elsewhere.
Nothing exceptional, but the place is beautiful; you can see the active post office, resembling certain scenes from a movie. Worth a visit.
It's just walking distance from our hotel. Very nice seeing this and other sites just around. Nice architecture.
A nice place to discover when you're in Saigon.
Museum post office that's great for historical learning, great area for photos too! Not much attraction to see but it's also a functioning post office that we can send postcards out.
The place inside was clean and cool with some seats to rest too.  Can reached there easily seen from around that area.
They are wonderfully well made, reflecting an era of French colonialism. From here it is possible to buy and send letters or postcards all over the world.
Historically very significant.  Very nice colonial building in a very interesting and pretty area of Saigon.  Worth a visit

A fantastic French Colonial architecture building having Roman archesxand gilded glass windows. This building houses old telephone boxes and historicalPictures. I first visited in Dec 2019 and its a must visit place anytime a visitor is in Saigon now renamed Ho Chi Ming city.Visitors queue up to post letters home to get a much prized Date stamp bookmark on their letters. Right next in the square is the Notre Dame Cathedral another must visit place.

One of Vietnam's greatest treasures, and especially that of Ho Chi Minh City (formerly Saigon), is its ability to preserve, embrace, and now share its colonial history with the world. France had a strong presence from the late 19th to the mid-20th century, leaving its mark on the life, culture, cuisine, architecture, and customs of its people. The city's Post Office is a prime example. Designed by the renowned architect Gustave Eiffel, it is perfectly preserved and continues to serve its original function as a post office, one of the most important in the country, while also being a major tourist attraction. Inside, you can also find shops selling souvenirs and mementos of your visit. In the same square, you can also admire the Notre-Dame Basilica of Saigon.
Great place to visit but many gift store instead of document and stuff to discover. Can consider it as short trip visit in town.

This 19th Century post office takes one back in time. Still a functioning post office, a large photo of Ho Chi Minh stares down at all who enter. We bought a few post cards at the gift shop to the right to send to relatives. Worth a 10-15 minute visit.

French style building, landmark of Ho Chi Minh City. You will see a lot of tourists and people filming here. It is near other attractions. Easy to access, big, inside is more beautiful than outside. It sells stamps, postcards, souvenirs. You should be quiet inside.

The architecture is brilliant, high ceilings and ample day light  but all of this gets overshadowed by the number of people inside.Apart from stamps there are a lot of other things one can buy from the shopping stalls in here. Stamps being the one thing you should carry back if you are a Philatelic like my husband the other stuff was easily available all around Ben Thanh and other parts of the city and country as well.

Most visitors are here not for postal services, but to gaze at the beautiful architecture of this heritage building, both exteriors and interiors. Open 7 days a week, its location right next to the Notre Dame Cathedral is an added bonus.

Considering that nobody writes letters or telegrams these days, a  heritage post office of the 19th century beomes a center of attraction. The Saigon Post office is a super large post office where postcards, table tops, wall hangings, fridge magnets and other souvenirs are sold. Even if you dont buy anything from here, you can walk around and appreciate the beautiful French architecture that makes for good photo op background. Nothing much to do here but a beautiful place to refresh your memories. We were reminded of those good old days when writing letters was a thing.
The interior, with its metal beams built by Eiffel, is magnificent, featuring old telephone booths that are still in use. A must-see!
The post office is a beautiful building from the outside. It is also a bustling post office with lots of activity from the inside - a good place to send a postcard back home. You can buy them there.

Stopped off here for a quick look in March 2020 whilst in HCMC for the weekend. It remains, as always, an attractive looking building. Easily combined with a visit to the Cathedral (currently closed for renovation work) and Book street.
A very nicely restored old building that is still actually active as a post office;

I recommend renting an ao dai (Vietnamese traditional dress) at the SKYhub Global Tour Lounge, a Japanese travel agency located very close to the post office, and taking commemorative photos there. Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral is also next door, so it's a great place to visit.

We went to the Central Post Office after a day out at the War Remnants Museum, we both thought the Post Office was a lovely building inside and out, you could definitely see the French influence. It very interestig inside as it is still used as a post office today, but there are also some souvenir shops there too. Definetely worth a visit
An impressive colonial building, still in operation today as the main post office, located directly opposite Notre Dame.
Pretty but very noisy and lots of tourists, which is normal... Start of a walk in the interesting neighborhood

Stopped here as part of a tour of Saigon, I think most tours do. It’s a quick photo opportunity inside and out with large painting oh Ho Chi Minh on wall inside, tellers situated around ground area along with market stalls. Seats available if you just want to sit and take it all in.
The central post office is a stunning building, you can see the French influence.  Always very busy and buzzing with locals and tourists

Impressive and vast French influenced building in the centre of Saigon. Such a difference from the multitude of skyscrapers that now dominate Saigon. The inside is huge and dominated by a massive picture of Hi Chi Minh at the far end. Definitely worth a quick visit if in the city.
We got some great shots of the architecture of this french occupation period building. we loved seeing the old postal assistant who helped people that could not write send letters

Having a day in Ho Chi Minh City, all the tourist areas within a central walking distance of each other with the beautiful cathedral next door to this grand historical handsome functioning post office. High ceilings greet you as you walk in feeling like you have gone back in time. Surrounding streets full of chilled but relaxing places to eat , drink and shop.

Unlike other post offices in the country, this one has real character, and the French architecture is remarkable. Both the exterior and interior transport you back several decades! Numerous souvenir vendors are located in the central section, but the counters around the perimeter are dedicated to postal services, where you can, for example, buy stamps. The ceiling is superb—look up!
Great architecture but don't keep high hopes. Its over crowded with tourists with lots of souvenir shops inside on both side of the post office.
In centre of HCM so easy to go for 5 mins & have a look around, esp[ically for the youngsters who have never visited any Post Office or made Trunk calls.
While the post office is a beautiful architectural gem, and well worth a visit, it’s a little sad to see it filled with trinket shops! But “the train station” is well worth visiting!
Although it's a post office, tourists can admire the gorgeous interior and buy postal-related goods such as postcards and stamps as souvenirs.

This massive post office in downtown offers souvenirs and post office memorabilia. Upon our visit, recent graduates posed on the steps in cap and gown, so this is an important and photogenic place for locals.Offering stunning architecture and interior design, the building dates back to the late 1800s. Even if you’re not up for shopping, step inside for gorgeous photographs of the inside, and visit Notre Dame next door!

What a beautiful building. We also saw this with Michael Portillo , onhis railway adventure in Vietnam. If you have a guide like we did. You would learn a lot more than you think. Like the fact that there is still one old gent in there that will send letters for you by hand.
Worth seeing...they have maintained the original structure well by inserting a good number of little shops inside.

While it is still used as a post office, the building is even more eye-catching for its large, old map of Ho Chi Minh City and its beautiful interior. In addition to its postal functions, it also has a wide selection of souvenirs, including most of the items you would find in a typical local souvenir shop.
We went there as part of a tour and had the chance to wander around.  Basically you take a few photos inside (it is free to enter) and look at the ornate interior and exterior. Worthwhile to visit.
Lovely old building.Lots of little interesting stalls insideStill a working counter so able to send postcards homeWould like to have had a little longer inside

Situated next to the cathedral Post Office is well worth a fifteen minutes visit. Internally as well as externally it's architecture is admirable. There are souvenir shops inside apart from it still being a functional post office. So this attraction is free to visit.
Very nice to have this place as a must see, as here you can send the postcard-surprise to your home :)

The PO is a step back in time with old fashioned phone  booths, clocks listing times elsewhere in the world, heaps of really unusual souvenirs and the ability to buy post cards and post these with a unique post mark on them. The architecture is French and is immediately opposite the Notre Dame cathedral. It is kept in great condition in cream and green colours of yesteryear. Alongside the post office is a Book Street, with book shops and great gifts as you browse through. A leafy area to read and purchase beverages or a snack. Worth a visit!
This should be on your list.  It was designed by Eiffel... it is historical, it is a great way to write and send post cards to people who don’t even remember what it is like to receive one !!

This is still an active post office (even though there is a new modern one next door). The two wings as you enter are now tourist tat shops, however, I thought that their prices for T shirts were the best I encountered in HCMC.

We found this building attractive and well preserved.  Inside lots of interesting articles to see and the ceiling is very photographic.  Lots of fabulous post cards and prints very well priced and of excellent quality.
You should definitely see the old post office.
Beautiful, well-preserved architecture that reminds us of something. In addition to the post office, there are many shops selling souvenirs, jewelry, and postcards (of course, it's a post office).
So beautiful building = outside and inside, still used as post Office, can be small museum for the France architecture.

Several vendors have set up shop in the main post office, selling their souvenirs at exorbitant prices, of course. Pleasant air conditioning, benches, and various sights from the French period allow us to rest.

The main post office is still staffed today, and you can buy stamps there, along with a large portrait of Ho Chi Minh. It's a beautifully preserved or restored example of fin-de-siècle architecture, located directly opposite the cathedral. There are also two souvenir shops at the entrance and two benches inside for resting.

This building, dating back to the French colonial era, is a worthy architectural heritage site, but the fact that they're still operating a regular postal service inside is quite amusing. There's a souvenir shop in the center, bustling with tourists. However, surrounding it are postal counters for each service, where postal workers are going about their daily tasks. It was hot and there was no breeze, and there was no air conditioning, just fans spinning above your head. There are benches, so it's a good place to rest if you get tired of sightseeing.
Amazing architecture. Really enjoyed this beautiful building.  Unfortunately, didn’t have a lot of time here as on a tight schedule.

The French-style architecture is very distinctive. There are many souvenirs for sale inside the post office. I originally thought the post office had been converted into a museum, but it turns out that postal services still exist. You can still buy stamps there.
Worth a quick look if you are there, nice building. Not somewhere you would spend long, but a good place to see.
This is like walking into a piece of history but one that is alive. It is a juxtaposition of a monument and still a working post office

Nice architecture, but there's not much to see within the post office.The colours are really great inside and the lighting is fabulous.There is some famous building near the post office such as the cathedral, Nguyen Hue, the opera, the statue of Ho Chi Minh and Bitexco.There's a small market inside so you can pick up some souvenirs etc.
There's not much to see within the post office, but it is worth visiting for the nice architecture. There's a small market inside so you can pick up some souvenirs etc.
A fantastic building, with phone booths and even an operator. It feels like stepping back in time.

This former post office has many original features still in place. It is now home to small souvenir shops. Unlike in the markets you are free to handle the goods and take time to look without being hassled.
If you're nearby, go in and take a look. It's cool to see what it looked like. It's very beautiful. It's cool to see that they still use some of it for mail handling.
Interesting architecture. The fact that it still operates until today makes it more amazing. Could try and send few postcard home too. Souvenirs shop has many interesting goods to offer.
Very good location. Luckily Not many tourists on the day we came. It seems a new renovation so less attractive.
This is a very beautiful colonial architecture from the French colony. A must visit place in HCM. You can find numerous local souvenir items here and even send postcards to family and friends.

Such a beautiful building, wonderful learning its history. The building is really well maintained, and it beautifully clean inside and outside.The decor inside is also beautiful! They have lovely trinket shops inside as well for some souvenirs. Although they tend to be more pricey than the other main market in the city (but I suppose it depends on where you want to go).Definitely worth a visit when in the city.
A beautiful building, especially when it is dark. The lighting is beautiful. We also went inside for a bit. Inside, you step back 50 years in time.

Although still a functioning post office, much of the non-back-office space is rented to and or used by souvenir vendors. The 19th century French-colonial architecture is the main reason for coming to this attraction.

Among the many colonial architectural relics, this building is a prime example, preserving the atmosphere of the former colonial era. While the arched ceilings and old telephone booths are reminiscent of buildings in Paris and London, the old maps on the ceiling and the world clocks in the telephone booths offer a glimpse into Saigon's past as one of Asia's leading international cities.

A well-maintained 19th-century neoclassical building. Worth seeing are the lobby with paintings depicting the historical map of Cholon and South Vietnam; the counter room with its green enameled iron supporting structure and the mosaic in the background featuring a portrait of Ho Chi Minh
Beautiful, immense architecture that takes us back to the Saigon of yesteryear. Make a detour if you're not too far away.
I've always loved French-style buildings and visited many places, including the French Consulate on Le Duan Street, but I was still truly "overwhelmed" when I actually spent time at this Post Office.
worth a visit cos it looks like a train station. Open long hours and offers a parcel  packing service - very efficient
The building is very beautiful and still partially functions as a post office, but most of it is geared towards tourists with souvenir shops and counters for booking tours.

Near Notre Dame and behind it is the street with various bookstores, so it's worth spending 10 minutes of your time. Our guide didn't explain much, but we enjoyed seeing it, so if you're in the area, pop in.
A very nice building at the centre of Ho Chi Minh, with lots of souvenirs shops. It is not very big, but worth a quick visit.
One of the most symbolic buildings of old Saigon.
Although it seems a bit ironic to see various shops selling souvenirs under the larger portrait of Ho Chi Minh, the space is very imposing and worth a quick look.
This building is a nice example of the neoclassical colonial style. It is totally free and full of original artifacts

If you are looking for a view of what 🇻🇳 looked like in its colonial days, then a visit to this place will tell you. Outside the post office is a beautiful old church that has been restored to its former glory, ST. Mary's, it think its called. Unlike western colonists who would have destroyed any forms of the culture they conquered, the Vietnamese have chosen to preserve their heritage, and this should be admired.A great tribute to the government.

Fantastic well maintained building, although there are many stalls inside selling tourist stuff it is still a working post office.You can write your postcards here buy your stamp and post them all inside a cool building with stunning French architecture.Free entry and despite it being busy worth a look.
A beautiful building worth seeing, although there's probably not much to see here. There's also a chance to buy souvenirs and send postcards.

Lovely entrance and inside is beautiful.  Didn't look much like a post office on entry as there were a lot of souvenirs for sale but further in there were writing desks available to sort out your postcards, very nice and free to enter.
Went there during sunser (around 5pm) and it was amazing. The sunlight gave the building the most amazing shine and if that isn't beautiful I dont know what is anymore. Love photography? Go.
Came here to look around at the grand building and we actually managed to send a postcard home too which was cool. It’s funny to think that this is still an operating post office

Walked here on way back from war remnants museum. Sent a postcard home and bought a few trinkets. Great architecture and you can still see the wooden phone booths. Definitely worth a trip provided you don’t land at the same time as a group of school children who all insist on writing their cards at the counter! Aw well you can always go next door for a burger if you’re fed up with noodles.
What a grand old building.  Try send a card or post card for the fun of it, quite the experience to see the stamps and process to get it going.

It is a no fee entrance because Central Post Office is still a working office. Inside is impressive big and there are a lot of small  shops for tourists .Also there are on wall 2 big painted maps of Saigon Cambodgia and Saigon area .Building was build by Gustav Eiffel before of 1900.

A very pretty building, a beautiful structure with a lovely interior, a very pretty portrait in the background, a very old post office with counters like in a bygone era, and old telephone booths. There are also souvenir shops; nice to see.
The architecture in this place is amazing. It is very impressive and well worth a visit. The souvenirs you can buy here are fantastic!
The building looks impressive, its metal structure designed by G. Eiffel. The interior of the post office is impressive, with a painting of Ho Chi Minh hanging in the center.

The post office is located in the city center, on Paris Commune Street, District 1. This is one of the most beautiful classical architectural buildings in Saigon, built in the decade 1886-1891. The Saigon Central Post Office is a fabulous old building constructed in the second half of the 1800s with Gothic, Renaissance, and French influences. It has a beautiful arched roof and two wonderful old maps on the walls. The furnishings are from a bygone era, with wooden phone booths and counters.  Lots of interesting shops inside and chance to do old-time mailing of postcards.

Of course, what has surprised me now is that the General Post Office of Ho Chi Minh City is still performing its original functions, rather than becoming a government agency or a museum. And quite successfully. Of course it's a piece of France. It's easy to imagine a building somewhere on the Champs-Elysees. It is said that the author of the design of this structure is the famous French architect Gustave Eiffel, who designed the Eiffel Tower and the Statue of Liberty... According to other versions, the Saigon Central Post Office was designed by architect Vildieu. It turned out that everyone was right: this structure was first designed by Gustave Eiffel, and later the architect Villieu and his assistant Foulhouh designed and rebuilt the building. So now there's a renaissance and a little bit of goth and classic and, I think, French Baroque. Nuclear architectural mix. And then. You'll be there, consider the architectural details carefully. They are worthy of being admired separately as special works of art.

Visiting the Saigon Central Post Office evokes a mix of awe and nostalgia. As you step inside, you're greeted by the grandeur of the soaring vaulted ceilings and the elegant arches, which instantly transport you back to the colonial era. The intricate details of the architecture and the historical maps on the walls tell stories of a bygone time, sparking curiosity about the city's past.The atmosphere feels alive yet tranquil, with the faint sounds of visitors admiring the space and locals conducting everyday postal business. The charm of the old wooden phone booths and the large central portrait of President Hồ Chí Minh adds a layer of cultural pride and history.It's a place that feels both timeless and vibrant, leaving you with a sense of wonder at how history and modernity coexist so seamlessly in this iconic landmark.

The Central Post Office, in Ho Chi Minh City is a beautiful building and an architectural marvel. It was built in the late 19th century, during the French colonial rule of the country. It is still functioning as a post office and it also houses a number of other shops, such as souvenir and tourist type stores.

In large cities such as Saigon, French architecture from colonial times is still visible. The colossal main post office is a striking example. This is not just any neoclassical building from the end of the 19th century. The richly designed hall of the post office is very large and has a quality that you will hardly find in France. Characteristic is that the building is very light with beautiful windows and that the building is still in use as a post office. The paneling, lamps, counters and telephone booths are still completely intact.

Situated in the heart of Ho Chi Minh City is the Central Post Office.  this is a must see experience if this is your first or even your second visit to HCM.  this is a working post office where locals can pay their bills, process mail, make phone call in old fashion phone booths and browse the many booths for trinkets. The interior includes many market stalls with a variety of gifts and souvenirs at reasonable prices.The post office is one of the top tourist attractions in the city and can be visited at the same time as the Notre-Dame Cathedral  across the street and the Opera House. Adorning the main wall is a huge portrait of Ho Chi Minh.  Admission is free and well worth having a look inside if visiting central Saigon.

Saigon Central Post Office is a post office located in the downtown area of Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. It was constructed between 1886 and 1891 when Vietnam was part of French Indochina. The building was designed by Alfred Foulhoux and is often erroneously credited as being the work of Gustave Eiffel or a collaboration between Foulhoux and Hanoi-based Auguste Henri Vildieu. The building is a tourist attraction and counts with Gothic, Renaissance, and French influences. The Saigon Central Post Office is located near the Saigon Notre-Dame Basilica, the city’s cathedral.You get all the souvenirs, lots of things like paintings & a variety of beautiful artefacts of  Vietnam. A place to visit if anyone is in Hochiming City.

For tourism:Lovely & very charming old post office, beautiful architecture and good for photos. I’d absolutely recommend sending a postcard home! (! Write your postcard out, visit the woman with the stamps (she was the last counter on the right hand side for us) dab them on the wet sponge & pop the postcard in the overflowing basket on the counter. ( it cost about £2 to send each postcard to the UK- 3 stamps need to go onto 1 postcard)For sending parcels overseas:Seems overwhelming at first but is very straight forward, we had been collecting things along our travels and needed to send it home to free up space. We took a big bag of things to the post office, headed straight to the back of the room (to the picture of Ho Chi Minh) where you will find a counter called ‘parcel and items packaging’- you need to fill out forms of what it inside, the quantity and the value of the items, the bag will be weighed & packaged ready to go.As of Oct 2023 we send a parcel around 6.8kg and it cost us around 4 million dong ( about £130) The staff were so helpful & their English is brilliant.

Sit down and enjoy the ambience of a bustling post office.Granted, it's not for everyone. It's opposite the church, so only 1 minute away.
Nice place to visit and buy souvenirs. WE even bough some postcards and posted to the home country.

Constructed between 1886-1891 and depicting classic elements of Gothic, Renaissance and French colonial styles, it was designed by French architect Alfred Foulhoux.... The Building features a unique, bright yellow exterior framed with white trim.... Curved windows are elegantly framed with green shutters... a large clock is slotted prominently at the main entrance.... The stunning, spacious interior has tall, domed ceilings with metal arches and a gorgeous patterned tile floor.... this is easily one of the busiest sites of this bustling city

This is one of very few buildings left in the city centre from the French colonial period. Many others has been either destroyed during the war or knocked down to build new modern hotels or apartment blocksThe post office was designed by Eiffel who of cause also designed Eiffel Tower in ParisYou can write a postcard and send it to your home country. They have a good selection of souvenirs Post office opened 7 days a week !

I must say that the Saigon Central Post Office is truly a unique and beautiful attraction. The architecture of the building is amazing, and it feels like you have stepped back in time. The interior is equally impressive, with vintage telephone booths and intricate mosaic tiles on the floor. It was a wonderful experience to anyone visiting Ho Chi Minh City.
A very picturesque and atmospheric place. Lots of tourists, souvenirs. Be sure to send a postcard from the old postal palace.
Fun to wander through. You can buy and mail postcards from one of the counters inside. I loved the colors and design.
A beautiful place where you can really feel the atmosphere of the colonial era; it's a bit strange. You'll find postal services there, but also shops and travel agencies.
This is well worth a visit to see the architecture, it’s also as it says is very central and easy to find. Not quite so easy to negotiate the traffic to reach it!

Was not sure of this when it was suggested but when we went I understood it better and it is a beautiful building.  Over the road was the cathedral and from outside we could see the building where the Americans were lifted off from
One of the best historical building in good condition. Inside has some cultural shopping centre. Can shop a varieties of Vietnamese craft there.

Look at it from the outside forstm then nter... you'll be amazed... They kept it priatine but perfectly restored. See what used to be the telephone cabins from where you could call the whole world through an operator ;)

I love to write letters so this was definitely on my agenda.  What a beautiful building.  And don't buy any souvenirs until after you visit here -- their prices are SO MUCH better than the vendors at the Ben Thanh Market!  I purchased some propaganda posters at the market and the exact same posters were 50% less at the post office!  Plus, the post office has a fabulous selection of post cards for purchase.  Definitely stop in.
A superb building from the French colonial period.

A quick stop at the attraction. The building is very pretty, as is the hall inside. We quickly bought our stamps and left. We were there in the early evening, and Mr. Nho, the official translator, was no longer there. We had to ask around because we didn't know if he was still alive. We were told he wasn't as popular there as he is back home (Hello?? As if working at 93 was normal, or even if anyone there was that old... besides, he's the only one who holds that position... I was a bit indignant at that statement 😅).
This post office is just worth the walk to get there It is so beautiful. Lots of room and just a working place but still like an attraction on its own.
Interesting place for a stop and some photos, They have cool call booths and still have it set up like a postal office and sell stamps etc.

I bought a postcard they were selling there and wrote a letter to Japan. Instead of dropping it in a mailbox, you just place it in a red basket on the counter. Western tourists were coming in one after another, sending letters. Email is nice, but there's something nice about a letter, even if it's late. You need to leave space because you're putting four stamps on it. The building is wonderful.
Beautiful French-style architecture, a working post office, a very interesting place from which you can send lovely handmade postcards all over the world.
Great place to visit when you go to Ho Chi Minh City. Lovely architecture, shops. Recommended for all.

Our tour guide took me and my sister in law to this place. A historical building still maintained to this date. Small shops inside. You can buy postcards and stamps and have them mail it for you. Although there’s not much to see, I think this is an important piece of history for Vietnam.
Magnificent!

In the heart of the city, it is an easy walk from the large hotel chains.  Surrounded by many good restaurants an other sightseeing spots in District 1.  Always great to send home postcards from HCMC.

Right by Notre Dame Cathedral so do both in one go.  Although the Cathedral is still under construction.  The Post Office is a nice building.  Like stepping back in time and full of tourists.  Well kept.  You don't need long.  Souvenir shops inside too.  A must whilst visiting the city.
Our guide explained that this beautiful building was designed by Eifel and that the magnificent floors were the originals. It was worth the stop

The post office has a nostalgic, old-fashioned Saigon feel. Upon entering, there were quite a few visitors, both domestic and foreign. I was a little disappointed I couldn't take pictures, but that also gave me many photos to cherish. You should definitely spend an afternoon here; it's very pleasant and relaxing after a stressful day.
A beautiful building and working post office. You can buy a postcard and send it back to your home country for a low price.
Was surprised by how many shops were also in the historical building. Later we could kick ourselves for not buying some things     and used our experience to measure prices elsewhere.
The post office was built in 19th century by French. This was beautiful architecture in Saigon. Inside, many stores for souvenir. Nice to see.

While Ho Chi Minh City may not be a place full of tourist attractions, its central post office is certainly the most remarkable one. The intact colonial building is a legacy of French Saigon with huge historic city and area maps. The encompassing portrait of Uncle Ho brings visitors back to present. Quite an interesting choice to meander around.
A very beautiful historic building with a stunning facade.

Beautiful place to hang out , spend some time here ... You book get city tour upon a bus .. which cost around 150.000 dong and you can also take pictures of notre basilica Jesus . Good place it is . Small market over here and toilet/washroom also available at 3000 dong

It is a well maintained building.  You can do a bit of shopping in here.  This is the old building and it has a shopping complex.  The actual post office is in a building on the side of the old Central Post Office building.  .
My favorite. Next to Notre Dame cathedral. beautiful and fantastic tiles inside. Sit down and write a postcard and mail it from here.
This place is a must go in you are in saigon. The place was originally designed and built by the French.
Great architecture and interesting information in particular the old telephone booths with signs depicting where you could call
Realy a nice building and historical very interesting. We are suprised about the architect of this building.
my friend takes me to this place, it is cool place to visit when you visit ho chi minh city, i was surprised by the vintage building in here
Entering the Saigon Central Post Office felt like stepping into a scene from a European movie. The space was vast and historic.
It feels great being here; everything brings back the old Saigon atmosphere. I occasionally visit the Post Office to explore and take pictures.
I still come here occasionally; the design features a wide dome, a beautiful space, and many tourists visit.
Gorgeous architecture, you must check it out, and it's across from some lovely gardens and the Notre Dame cathedral
A beautiful example of colonial architecture. The maps inside are superb. A must-see. A fun little detail: lots of young children practicing their English with the tourists.
This is a fascinating place to observe the workings of the Post Office: receiving and sending mail. In this fast-paced life, cherish these meaningful moments of slowing down.
A must-see location when you visit Ho Chi Minh City. Its central location, distinctive French design, and rich historical significance make it a truly beautiful and historic landmark.
The post office building still retains its old appearance, making it one of Saigon's most worthwhile historical landmarks to visit. The interior is beautiful and full of artistry.
Its unique architecture and artistry make it one of the most famous historical landmarks in Saigon. It's a popular tourist attraction.
It's been so long since I last visited this place. During the pandemic, it was closed, and now, even with limited time, at least I can wander around and take a look.

Although I've passed by this place quite a few times, I've been too busy to pay much attention. This time, I had nothing to do, so I wandered around and took in more of the surroundings. Normally, I don't notice anything special when I pass by, but now I see that the architecture and interior decor of the post office are really beautiful. No wonder so many tourists used to visit.
The architecture is incredibly unique. Inside, you'll see a very wide dome. The central post office is both modern and classic. It's a popular check-in spot for many young people in Saigon.

Have used the Central Post Office since 2006 and found the artefacts to my liking.  You need lots of patience in sending Post Cards to the U.S.  Putting postage on the envelope is a challenge, and you need at least one month (October) if you want the Cards to arrive before Thanksgiving.  I have to saythe hand-made cards never fail to impress .
A must-see attraction when visiting Saigon, Ho Chi Minh City. The building is very beautiful and has a unique architecture that adds to the beauty and elegance of the city.

One sunny afternoon, I happened to pass through District 1 of Ho Chi Minh City and suddenly realized that even though I'd lived in the city for over 20 years, I'd never once visited the Saigon Central Post Office. So I decided to stop by. It still had the same old architecture, but it exuded a timeless beauty that stirred my emotions.

The magnificent construction, sided by "Notre Dame Cathedral", will certainly catch your eyes while visiting Ho Chi Minh City. This is the old Saigon's "Central Post Office", built in the last years of the 19th century, after a project by French architects Alfred Foulhoux and Auguste Henri Vildieu. As a remnant of the French colonial period in Indochina, it has characteristics of the Gothic and Renaissance architectural styles, as well as French influences. Its artistic facade, internal domes and vaults, arched windows and wooden shutters are a perfect mix of Western and Asian styles. A giant clocks hangs above its main gate. Inside, check the ancient red phone booths and President Ho Chi MInh's portrait on the back wall of the central hall. Be ready for crowds and vendors, as there many stalls selling souvenirs, lacquerware and all the trinkets you may imagine, even "postcards and postage stamps"! Amazing! Visits to the "Central Post Office" and the awesome neighboring "Notre Dame Cathedral" can be easily combined. Enjoy!
My wife and I stopped by this historic and unmissable place as soon as we arrived in Ho Chi Minh City

Was amazed to see a senior citizen who used to function as letter writer in those days. He still renders his services though it's more as a souvenir. There are ample of handicrafts and souvenirs to pick up from this place and also a great location for some fantastic photo shoots...a definitely must be there for tourists

Come and experience the old-world charm and nostalgic feel of Ho Chi Minh City in its heyday. It's a popular destination for tourists seeking to learn about the unique characteristics and culture of Vietnam.

We wanted to send some postcards. This place is next to the Notre Dame Cathedral, a cool book market and a McDonalds, but the post office was very cool for what it was. Several great souvenir stands, photogenic and massive inside. Check it out!
Very quaint old post office.  Grab some postcard sand mail it to your friends or even to yourself as a fond memory.
It doesn’t take long to tour the Post Office but well worth your time. A land mark that must be experienced.
this post office is very beautiful. Especially  in the dome.you can send letter to your home here. there is also a giftshop here

Last trip in Ho Chi Min city, our family stayed at a hotel in the center of city. We walked around the downtown, with a stop at this post office to take photo and buy some gifts. It is a beautiful place .,.

On the tourist bucket list for HCMC. Designed by Gustav Eiffel of the Paris Eifel tower fame. Functional postal department is there to send parcels and post cards. Part of the area is  selling souvenirs. Worth a visit and is right next door to Notre Dame Cathedral of Saigon.Hop on hop off buses stop here. Also visit the book sellers street next door. You can send international Speedpost parcels to your home country from here. They  have standard size boxes and will pack for you. Need to have everything open for the staff to check you are not sending anything illegal.
Lovely architecture that is worth the visit; a piece of old Saigon. Buy a post card and stamp to commemorate your experience.
Amazing ceiling, located in 'Little Paris'.  This building designed by Gustav Eiffel. Close to the Notre Dame.  Worth a visit.
you can also buy souvenirs in this place, jewelry postcards, stamps and more. you can explore the corridor and make shopping deals.

Although designed by Gustav Eiffel - yes the one you're thinking of - more than 100 years ago, this stunning building is still in use for its original purpose - plus souvenir selling. Still, don't miss the beautiful vaulted ceilings, the incredible tiled floor and the antique maps showing the mail and telegraph routes through French colonial Vietnam. You probably won't stay long. We didn't. But I can say that I've actually been twice since I love the place so much.

People come here to send postcards and stay for the beauty of the architecture and the old-world charm of this building designed by Gustave Eiffel, as well as the memories of the colonial period. Incidentally, you can also browse the small shops on the sides.
Stunning architecture. Close to Notre Dame Church. A good place to pick up some unique souvenirs at reasonable prices.
Nice spot to buy a few bits and bobs to take home. The main attraction is the architechture of the building, great place to get a cool photo. A must see in Ho Chi Mihn City.
