The post office is a historic, very well-preserved building that still serves its original purpose and is lovingly designed. It features telephone booths, clocks displaying the times in various world cities, small shops, and of course, numerous post office counters.

Except that the Architect and builder of the building was Alexandre Gustave Eiffel, that's enough!!! The visit is free, it's open until 7:00 PM on weekdays, but it costs a lot if you want to mail a postcard back to your country.
An amazingly beautiful building inside and out.Our guide helped us a tremendous lot while explaining  the  and architecture.
Saigon's main post office is still in operation. They also sell souvenirs. You can buy a postcard with stamps there and send a greeting home.
Stunning building built by the French - has unique interior - full of atmosphere - worth a quick visit
The architecture of the building is very impressive and good to get some local stamp for stamp collectors

Grand architecture inside & out!  Well worth visiting.Highlight was meeting the 90 year old man who translates letters...we saw him on UK tv before we cam to Vietnam and got to talk with him on our visit! Awesome!!
A beautiful building, truly nostalgic, and it's actually still a real post office, even if souvenir shops predominate. Great architecture and lovely windows.

It's a must to visit this site, you got clear view information in the past of Saigon.Nice area and I love most the building structure, left by French colonization.Good spot for photos, and you have chance to buy wide range of postcards, on the go postage at the same time, isn't it wonderful!! Well recommended to visit, next door is McD if you want to grab a quick meal.

Good place to see local products and buy something, coffee for example, but look carefully before buying the price varies a lot from one post to another, haggling aside of course.visit to this peculiar market is essential. To spend hours viewing it in detail. Eating is a good idea.

There's not much to see in a city destroyed by war and almost entirely rebuilt, so a visit to the functioning post office is definitely worth it. A covered bazaar offers plenty of opportunities to buy souvenirs at good prices, especially for those who don't like bargaining.
A magnificent building opposite Notre Dame. As beautiful inside as it is outside. It's worth taking a look.
love the interior of post office a busy place constructed between 1886 to 1891 no entrance fees here
Worth a visit for the architecture and to people watch. Also a good place to post your cards at the desk, watch them being franked and sent of their way.
Lovely interior and well worth a detailed look. Take your time and read what is posted around. The fixtures and fitting are quite complete.
The Architecture is stunning and thanks to Michale Portillo's "Great Asian train journeys"on TV I doubt we would have visited a post office; but this is no ordinary post office. It is a must see.

Very beautiful post office in the middle of Saigon, worth a visit, the iron structure was designed by Gustave Eiffel, who made the Eiffel Tower in Paris. Fun to see locals and tourists sending packages and postcards to family and friends.

This was one of the highlights for me for the whole trip.  Built in 1886 and completed in 1891 the Central Post office is a classic example of French Colonial architecture at its best.  Sweeping main room and the old private phone closets this is still a working post office in addition to one of the best tourist stops in Saigon.  Truly beautiful and majestic.  Definitely a worthwhile stop.   Have a great visit!

Pure nostalgia – a place that makes you feel like you've stepped back in time. Not just the place itself, but also some of the people working in the post office. We watched a man of about 80 years old writing letters to what we assume were relatives.
Not often can you see a central post office these days. But this one's well-maintained and a real vintage place. MUST GO if you're in HCMC. Photogenic as well.
This was the first attraction we visited in Saigon and were very amazed with the architecture and design of the place.

This is a must to see in this city.  The architect was Gustave Eiffel and it is still a fully functioning post office.  You can also see people sitting around acting as scribes to write letters for people which is most interesting.  Don't bother with the two side wings as you enter the building they just sell a lot of tourist tat but just admire the inside and outside of this wonderful space.  You also from outside get an excellent view of Notre Dame Cathedral.
The Central Post Office is like a step back in time - beautiful old building which is still in full use today
Great walking distance if you stay in distrikt 1 must visit when you are in Vietnam Saigon history bulding
Not just a post office! The building itself is worth seeing, simply enormous...
Very informative interesting experience and wholesome tour excellent Guide Hoa. Love every details of all our places visited. Thank you Hoa
Such a magnificent place this is. Truly one of the places you would not want to miss if you are visiting HCM.

This was like walking into a railway station with all original telephone booths etc still in situ.  Also the old gentleman "letter writer" was sitting there ready to help people who years ago wouldn't have been able to read or write.  Apparently he sits there everyday still.
Interesting location, historically significant. Popular with tourists, sometimes overcrowded. Easily accessible, centrally located.
Shades of colonial life. Impressive building in good repair. Still a PO but many shops and a good cafe. See also the Cathedral next door and many iconic buildings in the same area.
The post office is a fantastic  building with a lot of character and should not to be missed. A must see, especially for architecture junkies.

The central post office was designed by French architect Villedieu and built in 1891.In district 1 it’s now mainly a tourist attraction, with a Western style structure and Eastern decoration.It still has all original features and the floor tiles are amazing.Outside is a really nice French style cafe too, lovely food and cold beer.
A very beautiful building. You can buy lovely postcards and envelopes. Very close to the cathedral.
This is an interesting building built by the French during their occupation of Vietnam. The architecture resembles a European train station.Definitely worth a visit.
No longer just a Post Office, there is so much going on. Interesting architecture and a lovely building. One of the places to visit in HCMC!
Don't forget to stop by here, it's worth it, not only to buy some nice stamps,
Beautiful building to visiting photograph,be warned,this is a tourist hot spot and gets VERY busy around lunch time. Full of tacky trinkets but worth the trip to see inside.

A very impressive building with telephone booths and switchboards. It feels like stepping back in time. Also impressive is the 80-year-old man who still reads the mail aloud to illiterate people every day or helps them write applications or letters.
Well worth a walk through for the historical and architectural features. Souvenirs and postal requirements all available, but without any pushy sales people.

It's worth seeing right next to the Mini Cathedral of Notre Dame. The old building was built during French rule. It's now a post office. Inside, there's also a currency exchange office and a souvenir shop. I recommend it.
A French colonial building, designed by Gustave Eiffel and completed in 1890. Very beautiful both inside and out. Still used as a post office. Located next to the also beautiful Notre Dame.
A visit to the post office is a must... a very pretty vaulted ceiling, retro telephone booths... a great atmosphere, under the watchful eye of Uncle Ho!
Nice Place to get souvenirs and Mail done at one place. Nice old postcards. I wish they had more cards with motorcycles, since there are so many here in HMC.
A real post office. Lots of tourists. Here you can send a postcard or a parcel to yourself or friends. Buy souvenirs. Relax. Recommended.
A beautiful, imposing building, both inside and out. The ticket counters are still just like in the old days. There are still old telephone booths. Beautiful to behold.
Excellent! 👌
Very interesting to see, a lot of nice souvenirs to buy, old telephone booths and the portrait of Ho Chi Mihn in the middle. Nice atmosphere.
A wonderful place, beautiful architecture, I recommend it to everyone visiting Ho Chi Minh.
The exterior is modern, but the interior is beautiful as well.
Magnificent architecture. This place is stunning. A great family outing. It has a colonial feel. Truly, truly, truly beautiful.
The building itself is worth a visit as is the old gentleman who has sat in his seat for most of his life helping translate letters, write messages, etc.  A must visit.

This historic post office building in the city center welcomes you with a very pleasant atmosphere. Inside, you have the chance to buy beautiful business cards and send them to your loved ones – a lovely souvenir. There's also a market inside where you can shop. Shops selling souvenirs, coffee, local clothing, hats, and even pearls – the goods are high quality, so you should definitely make time to shop here.

This colonial-era building remains strikingly beautiful. It is well-maintained despite the harsh weather conditions typical of these tropical countries. Built in the style of Eiffel's designs, it is grand inside with its well-preserved features. Inside, you'll find numerous offices and typical ticket windows, remarkable telephone booths, and some lovely murals.
Easy to find and such a grand old building! Lots of other grand buildings in the same street just take a stroll!

This post office is an incredible step back into colonial times (except of course for Ho's picture.  And towards the back is a "letter writer" now in his late 80s or early 90s who still bikes through Saigon traffic to come in every day as he has for decades to write letters for people.  He alone is worth the visit.
Interesting building the old post office now a indoor craft style market. Very busy all the time well worth a visit. Great architecture of its time.
This is a lovely building well worth a visit. We sat inside and took a minute to look at the ceilings and architecture. Its full of souvenir shops which were interesting to poke around in.

Constructed between 1886-1891 by  French Colonialists, this delightful and impressive work of art possesses Renaissance and French inspired architectural details. While inside the still functioning post office, note the intricate marble floors, the old telephone booths and clocks which reflect differing time zones. The soaring arched ceiling and its arched windows lend an additional  graceful beauty. The exterior is painted in golden yellow with white trim and green windows, shutters and molding detail. The plaster embellishment above the door is one of the most memorable features, with a large clock below. Attractive wrought iron bracing under the entry's overhead protective cover and lamp posts all contribute to this very pleasing architectural achievement.  Numerous overpriced souvenir vendors are located on or near the property, which stands across the road from Notre Dame Cathedral.

Ho Chi Minh City or Saigon 🇻🇳 I really made the most of this city. We started the day visiting the Saigon Central Post Office, the largest in the country, where I went to drop off my postcards. Just for family, so don't say, "What about me?" Hahaha, it's expensive. It was built between 1886 and 1891 and is an example of French colonial architecture. The most rewarding thing was that the person I mentioned in several stories was there!!! Mr. Duong Van Ngo is a gentleman who has spent most of his life translating letters, from Vietnamese to English or French and vice versa. He is the last survivor in this southern city who continues this work, for which he has earned recognition in the Guinness Book of World Records for Vietnam for the longevity of his career. 🙏🧘🏽‍♂️🙏
This is the biggest post office in Asia for sure. So nice and big. I feel like I'm in Europe when I'm inside. So classic and rare. Central district 1
French architecture with Egyptian influences and of course an Asian twist. Great building. If your a stamp collector it’s one stop shopping at great prices
Easy walk from our hotel Alagon Zen in district 1.beautiful original architecture with original telephone booths...non operational..sell trinkets post cards postage stamps and mail on the spot..

It takes you back in time. The roman style architecture is beautiful. The wood, is so old, looks as new and polished as was built yesterday. The services are quite easy to use. The poscard start as less as 10000 dong and posting to india, was 15000 dong. Must visit, cuz just a few functional post offices left now.

The Post Office is a magnificent building!  We met a ninety year old man who had worked as a writer and interpreter his whole life.  He comes to the Post Office frequently just because he likes being there and enjoys being around people.

A building of interest for its architecture. It's no longer a post office; I believe there are agencies inside that organize tours (I'm not sure, as I went on New Year's Day and they were closed). It takes about 10 minutes to visit, and it's next to the Notre Dame Cathedral in France. Interesting.

As part of a walking tour of Ho Chi Minh City we visited this huge post office. Built over 100 years ago it houses a great deal of history and it’s a super interesting building especially indoors.  The ceiling is spectacular, adorned with much elegance.There are a large number of vendors selling souvenirs and other items, Quite a market place considering it’s a post office. It also does serve as a regular post office and the line was pretty long as many locals and tourists use it. One of the vendors was selling stamps collectible stamps she’s very appropriate.Outside are some very nice statues
A fantastic building that architecture lovers should definitely check out :-)
Fine example of architecture and memories of time gone by.Inside there are maps and pictures showing the history of a fine building

It's a very large post office with counters arranged in a circle. I was able to send my mail without having to wait in line for too long. There were also several souvenir shops inside the post office, and the selection of goods was quite good. The prices were reasonable, but not incredibly cheap. It might be a good place to stop for a short break when you're tired from walking around.

The fact that you are given a basket that you can take around to put your souvenirs in, with no hassle from anyone pressuring you to buy, and bearing in mind their prices are no more expensive than the market, makes this altogether a really nice experience. Plus, of course, the architecture is really nice and way out of the league of any in the UK. You can even  buy postcards and sit and write them out before asking the kind gentleman for stamps.

Been recommended as one of the most attractive place in Saigon and that's true. We were really impressed by the architecture. There are a lots of shop with the local stuffs such as non la, and some interesting books about Vietnam.

This is a vintage post office built in 1800s, but they still operate this post office to send letters around the world. They also sell postcards, merchandise, and many others post office thingy. Full video, watch my YouTube channel, mangansekyorek
A very interesting experience; between the tourists taking photos, the vendors approaching you, the locals waiting, and the expats posting letters, it's quite amusing to watch.
Atmospheric but functioning Post Office. Full of souvenir tat but also some good buys if you look for them.
The post office in HP Chi Min City. Nice, big, and cool. Close to Rotterdam Church. Good.
Well worth a visit and the surroundings.   There is Vietcruise tours there which are worth looking at if you want to see beyond the city centre
Inside, the time in various countries around the world was displayed. It was also crowded with tourists, but it was a beautiful building.

The Central Post Office is really a testament to Ho Chi Minh's stature in this country.  The people seem to revere him, referring to him to as either 'Father Ho' or 'Uncle Ho' by different guides.  There is also a terrific painting of him that is central to the building's interior.The architecture itself is magnificent.Definitely worth visiting.

A working post office.  French colonial style with old type kiosks. Huge building with ornate decor. Various stalls inside selling usual souvenir type goods . Free to visit and next door to Cathdral.  It's recommended as a place to see . We enjoyed it.
Great Central location near the notre nadame cathedral, a worth while place to visit and great place to buy reasonably priced tourist souvenirs
Very beautifully preserved remnant of French colonial times. Worth a visit on the hop on hop off bus
Well worth a visit, it’s located close to plenty of lots of other sites and they’re all walkable. Enjoy, it’s a beautiful city

In my opinion this is a must for anyone that loves architecture and history as the building is just a huge expanse that as some say does resemble a train station from the bygone days of steam train travel.Have a look for Mr Duong Van Ngo, an octogenarian who we saw when there writing letters for people in french and English.There is a huge portrait of the city’s founder as well as the two old maps as you enter. It is immediately opposite the cathedral so easy to find.Do visit the Hard Rock Cafe nearby - at the end of book alley.Definitely worth a visit and recommended.Enjoy.
I think the atmosphere here is amazing. Looks so historical. It reminds me of France 🇫🇷 in the 90s.
A superb building well worth a visit, especially since it's located right next to Notre Dame Cathedral in Saigon.

Didn't think a Post Office would be an interesting thing to see, wrong! Go there! Take pictures! Wish we had Post Offices like this in the UK, instead of the austere, drab, anonymous, frankly miserable holes-in-the-wall we are expected to use
A wonderful venue that showcases real history and the present! If you're lucky, you might even bump into a retired postmaster who's over 90 years old.
Well worth a visit. The whole place was buzzing. You can buy postcards, get them stamped and take a selfie of the whole deal,...

A real discovery, you wouldn't expect a post office that performs its normal duties, but at the same time, it's a living museum bustling with visitors. A beautiful building with a souvenir shop inside, but you can't have everything.

To be honest, Ho Chi Minh City doesn't have many tourist attractions. While it's not a tourist spot since it's a post office, it feels like one. The building is beautiful, and you should definitely go inside. They also sell souvenirs, so buying stamps or something as a memento might be a good idea. I used to make phone calls from here often.
This building, in perfect condition, bears witness to the French occupation during the colonial period, with splendid period maps on the walls.
Beautiful, albeit very touristy. The building and the many post office counters are still worth visiting, though.
A post office from the colonial era. It reeks of the Third Republic. Superb, astonishing, amazing. A must-see!
Imposing post office, straight from the colonial era, complete with old telephone booths and counters!

La “Poste centrale de Saïgon“ that I grew up under its wings is still functioning like a well-oiled machine, even on Sundays! I can see the pride and joy in every attendant’s eyes when they serve the public. The majestic building has not lost its charm, its beauty is even more enhanced with the welcome addition of the pedestrian book lane bordering it. Brings back the sweet memories of my youth in Old Saigon. Amazing grace!

Stunning architecture meets daily utility. While the French colonial design is a must-see for photos, the interior remains a fully functional, efficient hub for mailing postcards or finding unique souvenirs.
Nothing special about this place, no historical significance other than an old post office building. Inside filled with vendors, more like a shopping place. Skip if you don’t have much time.

Just got home from there. Bought a lot of items from the central area to support my community. Lady in white shirt, hair bun, and glasses up charged me. When she totaled them up, she added two unwanted post cards and an extra 30k to my bill. I should've paid 810k but it ended up being 925k. If you're buying a lot, I'd suggest adding them up yourself. That was my mistake.

I went here on a Saturday to post a parcel, because regular postal shops close at 12 on a Saturday. My items were poorly packed in a larger than necessary box. They straight up lied about not being able to send the parcel by surface to drive up the price. To top it off they only accept cash. Needless to say I decided not to send my parcel from here.

Waste of time to come here if you actually want to use the post office facilities.

They are liars! I payed 65k for 2 post card’s stamps, logically they should give me four 15k stamps. But they only give me 2 stamps. When I notice this and went back to them, they denied it; even the male employee said he hadn't see me just now. What a joke.

Even though Vietnamese currency has many zeros, please make sure you calculate it carefully. Don't get your money stolen!

Mailing postcards or parcels through Vietnam Post has been very unreliable for me. Most of the time, I did not receive postcards or packages from family and friends, and many of the ones I sent never arrived either.

Several of my parcels were returned with reasons that were given but did not make sense to me. My packages were opened and not properly repacked.

After a year of repeated issues and disappointment, I can’t recommend their postal service at all.

It’s a working post office. They turned it into a tourist attraction due to its nice architecture. It’s worth visiting is you’re around. They have nice post cards. I sent one to my mum for 30kVND and I’ll see if/when it actually arrives 🤣

Update: It’s been about a month and the card hasn’t arrived yet. I wasn’t expecting it to anyways so no disappointment from my side 🤷🏻‍♀️ But I saw people sending so many cards at the same time. I’d suggest you be careful. It feels like a waste of money.

Update: Just going through my old reviews, I’ve realised it’s been 9 months and the post card I sent never got delivered. If you are going to send one to someone, get the card and post it when you are back to your home country or give it in person. Otherwise, it’ll never be delivered. Upon further reflection, I should’ve been able to tell they never even intended to post them. They’re not bothered about the stamp and they pile all cards to a box, probably for someone to bin them at the back. Shame.
Just to note - My friends from other countries used the same service and their once never got delivered either. It’s pennies when you think about it, but I don’t like their attitude.
Nice and beatiful historical building. But we had a really bad shopping experience there. Seller is rude and impatient.

Just one star!  Not for the beautiful historic building but for the absolutely disrespectful behavior of the operators.  The most famous place in this building is deserted.  It was the regular place of Dương Văn Ngộ.  He was an institution in this house and helped shape the reputation of this department for generations.  I visited the post office every time when I was in Saigon for several years, only looking forward to seeing him and talking to him. I really loved this old man and his story of life. I knew he had already died, but I visited his place again just now and was sorely disappointed.  I expected that his picture and his story would be found at this place to remember and honor him.  Shame on you, post office!

The treatment of the postal employee at counter 4 was not nice at all, as I stood for 15 minutes and she did not pay attention to me and took those who came after me with a smile as if I was not standing in line, knowing that I was the first at the time and she was talking on the phone without interest. I hope that the management monitors its treatment of customers. Is this concern for tourists? An experience that will not be repeated 👍🏼

"1 STAR for Saigon Central Post Office (Bưu điện trung tâm Sài Gòn). Horrible start to the day. Said they opened at 7:30 AM, waited 40 minutes, and STILL no one there. Showed up at 8:15, and finally, someone appeared. Big letdown, would not recommend relying on their opening times. Lost 40 minutes of my life"

Most expensive place I've been almost $150 USD to send a small package. Post cards where fine, but do not send packages, it's a scam. What I'm sending isn't even worth $60. Decided to just trash items I bought because I couldn't carry them and sending was too much. Crowded and not fun to many people yelling and no prices posted anywhere. Cash only, so bring lots of dong
TOURIST TRAP. BEWARE. I,VE PAID AND POSTED 3 DIFFERENT CARDS AND NO ONE REACHED IT'S DESTINATION YET (1,5 MONTH AFTER THE JOURNEY)

After all the hype of sending a postcard to your loved ones, I sent one during my trip to Vietnam and it’s been more than 10 days the recipient has not received it yet.

It’s heartbreaking for me, I feel helpless as there is no way of contacting them. I was very excited as it was my first time sending a postcard to someone.

If anyone can help please please guide me.

I read through all the reviews over here and I am giving you my experience of the post office. Given, the architecture is great and the post office has its own take you back in time kinda a vibe.
But when you try to mail something, the post office employees are barely of any help. Giving you as minimal information and focused on extorting money, they even go so far as to making fun of you in the regional language instead of talking to you or helping you. They are unbelievably mean and useless.
If the employees are that busy, one can always put up a board with instructions on where to go/how to mail, given that it is such a big tourist attraction. But seems like the bare minimum is too much for city.
Hopeless. People.

The building is nice and has a lot of history in this city, the greeting cards in the office are also nice and there are many choices of souvenirs for family souvenirs. I tried to buy greeting cards and tried to send them to family in my country and it was also expensive for just a greeting card and shipping. I asked the staff there "how long will it take for this greeting card to arrive in my country and they answered 2/3 weeks" okay but until now there has been no news from my family they have not received anything from Vietnam and this has been more than 1 month, I feel it is just a scam to Tourists buy greeting cards and send them to their respective countries. I thought they didn't send it until now and it will never arrive in my country I’m sad for it. I already paid for all the shipping and bought the card

I have read several reviews about the postal workers being very harsh and unfriendly, but I still decided to try sending a postcard. And sure enough, the staff was very rude. Even when showing the price with a calculator, they threw the calculator at me, which startled me. Think twice if you want to send mail here, because the staff here doesn’t respect others.

We wanted to send a 5kg package to europe. Been here days before, the lady gave us a price of 1.6m VND. After making the package the price suddenly Was 2.3m VND. And in the vietnamese Post Office it is Cash only ??? Feels like a Tourist trap even in an official Institution. What a shame to leave this beautiful country with that last impression.

I sent a package at Vietnam Post Office & the old lady, white shirt, black hair at desk 2 did not give me my correct change. Since she was hurrying me out on purpose, I did not have time to check the change. it was my fault for not checking the change but usually people in Saigon are trusting, not this lady.
When I got out the building I noticed the change was short. When I went back to speak to her about, she pretended she did not know English and laughed at me. I tried to be polite and not cause attention but eventually I let her have the change because she straight up ignored me. I believe in karma and robbing customers is one of the worst things you can do.

I gave her 2,009.000 Vietnamese dong.
The cost was 1,529.000 Vietnamese dong.
My change was suppose to be 480.000 dong but she only gave me 80.000

She stole 400,000 Vietnamese dong from me.

Be careful here because they will try to scam you. I recommend to not buy anything here and get postcards and send mail somewhere else.

Had the worst experience ever.
Wanted to send postacards to my country, so happy and excited while writing them. The problem came when I tried to send them, the woman on the postal office was so rude, didnt explain what I needed to do, treated me so bad, ignoring me.
Ended up looking for help somewhere else, and a lovely man helped me.
I dont know the woman’s name, but she should not be in customer service when she has such a bad attitude. Mid 50’s, short black hair.
Do us a favour and look for someone nicer to treat tourists, its hard for us to understand what we have to do.

Don’t exchange money here - they gave us a ripped $20 note for our Cambodia visa that is literally useless to us - no where in Cambodia will accept it, we had to rush to an ATM and pay the remainder in VND for our visas. They hid it amongst lots of notes with the rip at the bottom so we wouldn’t see it. Sneaky.

I have a sent a post card from Saigon post office to India with complete proper addressing in September 2024. It’s been 6 months i have not yet received the post letter . I feel it’s just a scam to buy post cards from them . And the staff in the Saigon post office are ever arrogant . Be careful when you visit them

Not a fan. Most of the staff here are amazing and friendly. The guy at the desk on the right is a rip off merchant. He pulled my box apart and tried to say I couldn’t send shoes, biscuits or coffee to Australia. Finally I got a supervisor and confirmed I could. But then I got told I had to pay both way postage in case Australian Customs returned the box, which never happened so I paid twice for nothing. He tried to say it was Australian Customs. The box turned up today with the shoes, biscuits and coffee. Be wary and know your country’s customs rules and stand your ground. Better yet, don’t let them open your package. Or go elsewhere.
Had a very bad experience. The staff at the customer’s service counter was really really rude. She scolded me
Great place but the people working there are AWFUL treating customers

gave a 200k note for 2 stamps so expected 132k change. we normally check our change every single time, but being an official establishment, we expected this to be the one place you would be safe from getting scammed. she gave me back a 10k note instead of a 100k. hope she enjoyed that £3 tip. what a terrible reflection of the scamming culture in this country.

Nv noi t anh du t noi t viet, that su k ton trong khach, dm

Postcard you guys sent will NEVER BE DELIVERED! Dun waste time & money go be cheated!!!

We went inside the post office with an ice coffee. Then, two security guys asked us get out there immediately, because they don't want water dropped from ice on the floor. There is no notifications and any boards warned us to not bring ice drinks inside the post office. Anyone had similar experience? Or just us, Vietnamese.

Awful, they lied about how much it would be to send a parcel back and almost doubled by the time we went to pay. They were very rude and unhelpful (apart from the gentleman packaging) and made you feel very unwelcome (especially the 2 ladies at domestic and international parcels).
Nothing special at all. Very hyped for no reason. Overpriced and hot inside. Nothing you must se if you don’t have your way past it.

As a functioning post office, it's horrible.  I called their hotline, the automated machine asked me to wait on the line and then it hung up on me.  There's no lines, so people are pushing and shoving (even foreigners).  I speak some Vietnamese, but I can't read.  When I asked them about prices, they told me to go look at their website.  I told them I can't read Vietnamese, they told me to go look at their website, again. Instead of repainting the building, they should have focused on other priorities.  #Vietnam.
Out of 8 postcards I sent from here to three different countries only one has arrived after four months of waiting. Very disapointing. The builing itself was nice to visit.
Could not rate lower. Hopeless and disorganised. Took nearly 2hrs to help me post two postcards and one sml package. Not sure anything of it will actually arrive at it’s intended addresses if at all.
Just an old building with a functioning post office in it. How is this a tourist attraction??? You’d be done in 3 minutes.

I hope I can give lower than 1 star. Basically, I sent a mail at here by using the fastest international service which the employee told me that it will take 4 working days for the receivers to receive. Right now, it has been 6 working days and when I checked the status with my tracking number,  it has not been delivered and still in Vietnam.  Don't use postal service at here. I think using American business like Fedex or others will be much much better , especially if you need to send something in emergency.

Bought a postcard to send to my family overseas. No pens on the tables so I asked a group of 5 people working behind the help desk if there is a pen available, they said no as one of them grabbed a pen on the table and moved it out of sight.
Horrible service.

Don't buy stamps there. They're supposed to be an Official Post Office but they make extra money by scamming people. I've asked 15 stamps to send abroad (France). The woman, very unprofessional, made me pay 19.000, despite the real price is 15.000. it's a shame we cannot trust an official office in Vietnam. Everywhere, they scam people.
A bar that take at least 30 mins to prepare your beer. Not cheap but super lousy service bar in District 1. Best of all, beware of the mosquitoes!

Mailed 4 Christmas cards on December 19th 2019 to friends in the USA while I was in HCMC on vacation.  I was told by the girl working the counter that delivery would take 2-3 weeks to get to my friends in California but as of January 25th 2020, none have been received.  Needless to say, I am very disappointed and would not recommend the HCMC Central PO for anything that you want delivered.  If you want to give them your money and get nothing in return, they have that down.
I pay 120 000 Dong for 4 stamp at 6k each and 4 stamp at 15 000. Why I’m still surprised to be overcharged by this country? But from the post, that’s amazing !

Surly staff, not good service. 3 to 4 weeks to send a postcard normal tarrif, or 800,000 dong to send in 3 to 4 days (35 USD).
By comparison Fedex will send you a 4 kilo package for the same price.

So bad services. My friend sent me a letter when he was in Saigon, on the day he came back his country. And I hadn't received it after 2 months. I came here to ask them and they said they could not help me with stamp letter. So why didn't they ask him choose better service? They just said him pay 4000₫ and put in the basket. Very irresponsible
We sent postcards to 3 different countries 3 months ago and none of them ever arrived

Since when vn not allow to send Samsung phone but Nokia is fine. They said that my friend can't pickup the phone because the phone is China? Samsung is korea god dam it. And how do they know what phone is in the backage if they not open it to see what inside. This is bs.
I am sent postal to my family in Mexico and USA . Like 4 weeks . For that day to naw my family no get postals . Just was money and time for anything. Poor service, lair post office
Terrible postal service. Extremely over priced, slow, inefficient and late.
Staff at Vietnam post are incredibly rude.
Very bad service, staff didn't explain the stamp instruction
Boring. bunch of shops selling the same junk as everywhere else. nothing to do and isn't worth your time.

Terrible post office I cannot get packages delivered they keep saying they are here when they're not.  You e-mail them from their website and they don't respond.  Its the worst post office in the world
Horrible post office. The actual postal service is a scam. I had sent a post card but it never reached.
Its been a month until now no post cards are coming 👎🏻👎🏻👎🏻 …
if i could give 0 starts i would, opening times not accurate, super unfriendly staff and overpriced!!!!!!
Not special enough to endure all the mean Vietnamese people that we dealt with. Save your time.
Boring, skip this
Refuse to look up mail by name or address
Be careful, snatch city.
good
post
Postcards will never arrive. My family waited for months and it’s very sad as it’s not cheap and generally a big disappointment

Follow up of my previous review Dec. 2025. It is now March 15, 2026, 4.5 months since I mailed my 5 postcards. Not even one has arrived. I wrote to Embassy in DC and they said that it will take months or IT WILL GET LOST ON THE WAY. LOST?? Why blame somebody else? I told them that they have to deal with the issue because there are so many people sending postcards. What happened to the postcards in the plastic basket? I think it's easy to throw them in the garbage rather that we drop them in a mailbox. Extra work. Tourists spent too much money. Mail at your own BIG RISK. Zero Star for that Post Office.

My first review on Dec. 2025.
Bought postcards and 3 big stamps for each postcard ( 1 small stamp could have been nicer) and drop them in that plastic basket in window 1. Has anybody received postcards you sent. Been 6 weeks since I sent them and have not received a single postcard. Sent postcards from Cambodia and in 12 days they all arrived.
My rating based on non arrival of postcards.
Sent a postcard on December 19, 2025. As of today the postcard is nowhere to be found. What kind of operation is this? Lies, up charges, and no guarantee.

Strongly advise against it! Zero organization! It's every man for himself! The staff aren't helpful; they just see you're Western and send you to the next window, until you've gone around the whole way and no one will tell you where to send a package or letter, how, or how much. We ask with a translator, but no answer! Except "cash only"!!! It's impossible to know where to buy empty envelopes without a card and stamp. Even the shopkeepers nearby send you elsewhere. Feeling like idiots, we left. Because eventually, the same two colleagues kept sending us back and forth between us, but without any answers.

A monstrous crowd with absolutely no organization, no tickets, nothing. And given the mess they make with the letters, I wonder how the actual mailing process works.

!!!Warning!!! Do not send your postcards from this post office because the postcards and stamps are more expensive there than elsewhere. Choose a smaller post office elsewhere in the city.

The actual price of a stamp is 15,000 VND. For international mail, you need two stamps (30,000 VND total).

I bought my postcards for 12,000 VND at Nguyen Hue Bookstore.

Apart from that, yes, the place is very nice.

The cars are always parked there 24/7, making it impossible for the technology cars to pick up and drop off passengers. Technology cars always pick up and drop off passengers in parallel rows, so they always get fined. Now, when customers go there to buff their cars, their trips are always canceled.

The security guard at night is very rude and takes the car outside to take money for himself. I also had this happen when I went for a drink at Highlands next door. Many people have complained but a big post office doesn't have any corrections, it's really bad.

PLEASE DON'T PARK YOUR CAR HERE! BEFORE I PARKED, MY WINDOW WAS INTACT AT 7:55 AM ON SATURDAY, JANUARY 31, 2026. WHEN I PICKED UP MY CAR AT 4:15 PM THE SAME DAY, MY WINDOW WAS BROKEN EVEN THOUGH I PARKED IN THE MOST EMPTY SPACE POSSIBLE! THE STAFF DIDN'T COMPENSATE AND EVEN SPEAK IN A CLUELESS TONE, CALLING ME A "MONEY-MAKING GIRL". You should park your car in a shopping mall, even if it's a little more expensive, for safety. These parking lots are full of thugs and swear a lot. The basement parking is super steep and narrow. First and last time going there!

I was here 15, 10 years ago, and now, in the spring of 2024. All that remains of the Post Office is a majestic building. The interior has been converted into commercial space, making it impossible to send a postcard or letter. The sign advertises that stamps are processed at two of the 10+ windows, but in reality, only one window is open. The line is enormous, the operator is busy on her phone. I stood there for 10 minutes, and during that time, she didn't let a single person through. She was constantly busy with a single parcel to Vietnam and arguing with the Vietnamese senders. By that time, there were already more than 10 people in line with ordinary postcards. I left and returned 40 minutes later – the same situation. Only one window for mailings, even more people with postcards. You can easily count 20 people in the photo. It was scorching hot, and the employees showed zero reaction. The other windows were either empty or busy with their phones, with no customers. It was insane. The impression was ruined.

Come see the building from the outside. Take a quick peek inside. I don't see the point in going in; it's a pointless crush inside, and the souvenirs are overpriced (the same magnets, postcards, and notebooks are 25-40% cheaper on the nearby bookstore street).
I actually saw that the coins sold in the post office were game coins! How funny! 🤭 Look out for VIP …
Terrible service, they didn't call the customer but delivered the customer's order; the worst service in the universe.
The mail counter staff were very rude, yelling at me when I didn't understand and asked questions.

Very bad experience with the female staff at the checkout counter this way (as pictured) on the morning of October 12th at 8am, extremely unprofessional attitude towards customers. The previous customer made her upset and she took it out on us, and her attitude was very rude, will never come back again.

Worker here is incredibly rude, i came to post an item and have tourettes and she was laughing and giving me dirty looks cause she didnt understand my disability at all. Disappointing for someome who works within a very packed tourist place that serves diverse people. Her face says it all.

I came to post an item and I have a disability called Tourettes, but the staff here was incredibly rude, laughed at me and looked at me with disdain. Very disappointing for someone who works within a busy tourist place that serves diverse people. 🤮🤮

It was noisy, busy and crowded, not fun at all
And be careful!!!!!! Do not send postcards, packages and letters to Israel!!

The service is almost like a government job. But the working attitude is arrogant and rude. From Hai Ba Trung to the big city post office. But with a LEGAL RECORD it took nearly 2 hours. The way of working and talking is rude. The style of clothing is like the guy in the market. (counter number 8). The attitude is like the son of a boss coming to work.
Probably 1.2 bad guys brought in by their family. Affecting the whole collective of a machine.
These people must be brought out, and only after they have had contact will they know what life will be like. Don't have that kind of work. If you are ignorant, go home and enjoy it. If you work, do it properly.
11/21/2022 / 13:15 🤫

We were looking forward to sending postcards, but it was definitely too crowded.

You're also often spoken to in English by children who say they want to practice their English.

The mothers secretly film the conversation and upload it online (TikTok). You're not asked if you want to.

We observed this practice with many foreigners. The children approached them one after the other, and the mothers filmed everything. A very unpleasant experience.

It's currently under construction (as of January 2025), so you can't see it. The exterior is littered with things like an apartment construction site. You can visit once construction is complete. I also recommend going to a different location than the post office.
If there were 0 stars, I would give 0 stars for the attitude of the counter staff. Not nice in person or in character.

Don't go out of your way to pay and write a letter; they're playing with people's hopes. After a few months away from our families, we decided to send five letters, and only two arrived. We asked the guy who gave us the free tour for some contact information to complain about, and his response was that many letters never arrive and that they don't make the effort to search for them or care. It's unfortunate that something that could be very beautiful can be like this.

The staff's attitude is rude, they look down on Vietnamese people, and they don't respect customers. You should review your attitude and how you treat all the staff of this shop. They are equally rude.
The Department said it is not responsible for processing the application :))

The place is very nice, but when it comes to sending a postcard…
Oh my god, the woman at the counter where you hand over the letters for international mail was incredibly rude. They don't explain anything. It turns out you have to stick on seven stickers, but she didn't give me seven, and since I didn't have enough, she charged me more. What really got me was that she kept ignoring me, and when she did "help" me, she was incredibly rude. I honestly left there feeling awful, with a severe anxiety attack. It's a horrible memory.

Do not send anything from here, including postcards. They will all disappear. I have experienced this three times. They will just tell you irresponsibly that they do not know and are not responsible. In short, this is a place that scams money. So everyone's impression of sending things from Vietnam is that things will disappear, even small things like postcards. Moreover, their staff have a very bad attitude. It is just a pretty building on the outside.

POST OFFICE STAFF MAY BE STEALING CUSTOMERS' PARKING MONEY
======
Yesterday, June 1, 2022, I went to Saigon Central Post Office to send documents to Hanoi. When I arrived, I parked my car in the parking lot on the left side of the post office from the street, but was refused because there was no space. Then I had to find a nearby parking lot to keep my car (costing 10k).

After sending the documents at the counter, the staff gave me a parking ticket, but I said there was no parking in the parking lot she pointed to, so I didn't take it. I asked her specifically where that parking lot was, and she told me to ask the security guard.

I asked the security guard where the post office parking lot was, but he pointed to the parking lot where I had just been turned away. I told him that the parking lot seemed full and refused to keep my car, then he told me to be precise, the post office parking lot was full, he spoke in a contemptuous tone, not believing what I said. 

Then I walked to the parking lot he pointed to confirm if it was the post office parking lot that initially refused to keep my car, then it was true. I walked back to the post office and met the security guard again. He was sitting on the chair playing on his phone, not bothering to stand up to talk to me. I said the parking lot he was talking about was the parking lot that refused to keep my car. He was still sitting, glanced up at me and said "Okay, leave it to me to handle it". I asked him what he was going to do, then he said "then YOU told me, I will handle it for YOU now". But he still sat playing on his phone, leaving me standing there looking at him for about 10 seconds without saying anything. I had to go so I couldn't meet the manager directly to report this issue. I am very angry about the service attitude of this security guard. The way he used rude words and the way he solved the problem for the customer. I think that since he stood outside the door, he would most likely be able to observe the situation of the post office's parking lot, more or less he would know clearly. Maybe I am wrong, but is there a time when the security guard does not let the post office's customers park their cars so that he can keep the cars of other customers to collect money for himself? With a central post office that is over 100 years old, I hope the management does not let this happen, but just needs to correct the attitude of the security guard so that he can please the customers coming and going./.
Do not send a postcard. It's a great scam, they never arrive! 5 cards sent, no receipts.

I wanted to send a package to Germany today. It weighed three kilos and was supposed to cost 1.4 million VND.
I then changed my mind.
I wasn't offered any shipping options, such as by ship, plane, or express.
What a shame.

The post office in the center of Ho Chi Minh City does not accept transfers, does not swipe cards, only accepts cash. 
The 4.0 era calls for a lot but does not improve those things!

Ah yes, the building is lovely and historic... but don't make the mistake of buying postcards inside and stamps (rather expensive) from the incompetent clerks at the counters... then entrusting them to this post office for delivery (a huge rip-off)... they'll never arrive... 4 postcards bought... stamped and mailed... never received by the recipients... (unless the delivery is done on foot from Saigon to France) but well, after almost a month in Vietnam... you learn to live with the chronic incompetence of some people and the often appalling services... Many examples could be given.

I just called this post office today at 1:38 p.m. on August 21, 2020, and met a female employee with a southern accent. I don't know if she was a transaction employee or a customer service employee, but she spoke very arrogantly and spoke in a vague way. I called to explain the problem I wanted to ask, but the employee's tone only asked question 1 and had no subject or predicate, not at all professional in the service industry. She looked at the customer as a beggar, showing a bureaucratic and arrogant attitude. I suggest that the BDTP review the attitude of the employee.

The person from the Post Office made an appointment to meet for work, but the person who made the appointment only said Saigon Central Post Office, I didn't know that it included many functional blocks. I went in and asked the guard at the gate of Cong Xa Paris in a soft and polite tone (if you say Central Post Office, who would know where else but this gate for the first time) "Miss XX, department YY has an appointment for me...", before I could finish speaking, the man spoke back as if cursing at me, the service attitude was really terrible, I still remember one sentence "Hey, do you know who I am, a robber or thief, let me in?". Honestly, with that attitude towards customers, who do you do business with? Is there anyone who steals or steals that needs to be asked like that? I think the Central Post Office should have a way to correct the attitude of its employees, especially those who are likely to meet the first customers, which are the security guards. Best regards and never see you again!
The post office is nice but a bit boring, the security guards are not friendly to visitors

Dear post office management, I sent 3 express envelopes (January 2, 2021) and registered to receive newspapers delivered to my home, but until today (January 12, 2021), I still have not received a reply. I checked many times through the switchboard and it said it had been successfully delivered to me, but in fact, I have not received any reply letter. For the past 10 days, I have been very tired to contact and have to stay at home to wait for the mail that cannot go anywhere. Now I asked again and they said that my 3 replies are still at the post office where I sent them. The post office staff were indifferent, not responsible or even apologized, and seemed annoyed with me. I contacted to ask when I could receive the reply so that I could arrange to be at home to receive it, but the post office staff seemed annoyed with me and blamed the mail delivery. I do not understand how to work like this. I am very upset about this issue, especially the service attitude and working style of the post office staff. Email. My letter is urgent and important. I accept the fee to send it, but I am really not satisfied with the way the post office works like this. I hope the management will help me resolve this matter. Sincerely thank you.
Central post office 1 of the largest city in the country but the tellers are terrible, rigid, lack knowledge, work like robots. The experience couldn't be worse.

The security guard rudely prevented the tourist from taking pictures. He did not give any reason for the ban. This is an act that tarnishes the image of the Post Office in particular and the face of the Party, Government and People of the City named after Uncle Ho.
Beware of scams from nearby sellers, a trader offers to exchange 10 euros of coins because they can't do anything with them and in fact there are 8 euros and a 10 Thai bat coin.

The way they work is terrible; the working hours are incorrect. It's past 8 AM and the criminal record department hasn't started working yet. They say they close at 7 PM, but they're nowhere to be seen by 6 PM. Please review this.

Business is incredibly slow; my criminal record check is taking over three weeks to complete, even though I have a city residence permit. They don't answer my calls either. I don't know if they're government employees or some kind of overbearing boss.

Tan Hiep Tien Giang post office's postman is so weird.
Delivery is late and they still blame the phone number, and when they deliver the goods, they see that the store is closed so they don't deliver.

#Customer: So from the day the goods arrived on January 12th to now January 17th, it's been 5 days and they still refuse to deliver. Who will do business like that?

#Postman: If you don't like it, then don't send it. I won't deliver the goods later.

Oh my god, this is the first time I've seen a postman talk like that.
The package is worth millions, not tens of thousands.

P/s: It's not delivered to the recipient, but they dare to report successful delivery on January 15th, the recipient's name is Phuoc.
Oh my god 🙏🙏🙏
Give the insurance phone number to contact, call 100 times and no one picks up. Just get rid of that phone number, people live far away and can't work.

Unreliable post office
Getting a criminal record certificate took 7 months and I still haven't received a response, costing 200,000 VND and only getting an appointment slip.

The post office does business with a really bad attitude. I really don't like this when visiting the post office.
Bad staff.
I am dissatisfied with the call center employee who spoke in a commanding tone and hung up abruptly, showing no respect for the customer.

How come I sent the item for collection but the post office didn't send the money back??? Same thing twice...what kind of business is this...just giving the phone number for show...if there's anything, call them and let them know
Sending a letter from Taiwan to Vietnam. It only takes one day to get from Taiwan to Saigon, and then 12 days to get from Saigon to Thai Nguyen. It's so slow, so slow, so slow!
The post office operates without putting customers first; I asked them to send a package to Thu Duc district but they refused.
More than 1 week have not received the goods. Called but didn't pick up. If you pay the full fee and ask for shipping, you won't accept it???? Ignorant
The staff arbitrarily charged extra money and worked carelessly.
Aside from its role as a post office, it's no different from Ben Thanh Market.

Username: _Make Up “Triệu Lệ Dĩnh” Trần Ngọc Hiền
Comment: What the hell is this provincial post office, it gives the wrong phone number. I can't call any post office. So discouraging.
The building is beautiful, but they don't send postcards to Russia. That's crazy.
There's nothing to see, it's dirty... I just got bitten by mosquitoes
Aren't you exploiting your employees too much?
The parking lot staff's attitude is too bad
Calls that go unanswered indicate poor customer support.

Great
How to book?
Unpleasant work attitude
Business is doing terribly.
bored
Bottom maintenance

Beautiful building, souvenirs for sale, you can visit Notre Dame at the same time. You certainly don't need to book a tour; get a map and do everything on foot, that way you see more. It is definitely doable and saves you the cost, because €23 is too much!

And yes. it is just a post office located opposite the church.. Went there and that's pretty much of it. Every Monday to Friday, there's this uncle sitting there helping people to write post card in French. Something unique :) No payment but just a small token will do.
It is really just a Big building functioning as a postoffice. Nothing special really. We were quite dissapointed when er got there.

Other than the beautiful French Colonial Architecture there is not much else to see. It is a working post office. The kids were bored as they are not interested in the architecture. There is a gift shop and you can get collectable stamps. If I was a local I wouldn't use this post office as it is so busy with tourists.
More crappy stuff to buy inside the post office - nothing to see that you cannot see on the street.  We thought is was a total waste of time.

Some might appreciate the architecture, I think that's the only thing that makes it worth going to.  Other than that, it's a post office.  They do sell trinkets and souvenirs there, but they are overpriced and you can get them cheaper in the market.  Shipping is quite expensive in Vietnam.  Don't expect US Post Office prices here.

The main post office in Ho Chi Minh City is a tourist attraction that you can see in a quarter or half an hour. Go inside the building and see how the postal service works alongside all the souvenir sales, interesting! Then you go outside and sit on a bench and study the people passing by, among others, the bride and groom like to be immortalized on the spot outside the building which is next to the cathedral.
Not much to see, nothing special apart from the European architecture and the size of the building. The post office is still in operation, and you can find tourist items in the center of the hall.
Lots of people and very crowded. Be wary of your belongings. However a very beautiful example of local history.

I have no idea why this is considered one of Saigon's top sights. It looks "nice" from the outside (colonial architecture, etc.), but the interior is completely unspectacular, and after two minutes you can just leave...
There is absolutely nothing exciting to see here - the walls are historic, but otherwise it seems to only serve the purpose of selling souvenirs.
After 3 min there is nothing to see. Nice building. If you want to send a parcel than you spend more time. Otherwise bit of a tourist trap

The church is included in the tour. Just one section of it feels European. The area in front is spacious, making it a great meeting point. You can buy stamps and make international calls, although I doubt you'll need to use them these days. It's convenient for currency exchange, but since there are ATMs everywhere in Vietnam, you might not even need that.
Near the cathedral. You can take 10mn to go there and have a look when you are around but I wouldn't go there especially to visit it because it is not so special.
Like most post offices in Indonesia, for example the big post office in Lapangan Banteng Jakarta...
Very busy, lots of vendors trying to sell souvenirs inside, the usual shouts of "Madame you buy......." Kind of ruined the atmosphere
But it's not much more than that.
outlook seems okay to take some inset worthy photo. but there is nothing much inside, except of those souvenir that you might want to purchase.

Outsidethe post office I caught a taxi to take us back to the hotel. He insisted I sit in the front which I did. The driver was on his phone a lot. Half way to the hotel he stopped and another driver took over and the original driver asked me to pay him. I opened my wallet and didn't have small bills. I told him you guys sort it out later. I didn't realised that the driver touched my wallet very quickly. He didn't dropped us off at the hotel he let us off before the hotel corner so we had to walk 100 meters. The driver had taken 500000 dong was missing equivalent to $33 dollars he stole of me. Warning to others don't sit in the front seat don't open your wallet in the front seat I gather he would have been recognised by the hotel staff so he avoided to drop us off
How is this one of the top things to do? It is just an old Post Office with gift shops inside. Were we at a different place? If you're used to old buildings, you won't be impressed
The post office is big, and with few souvenior shops, atm and etc. Nothing much to explore. The exterior design is nice and just opposite to cathedral.
I didn't like it; I don't think it's a major attraction.
More of a large souvenir shop now than anything else.  Worth a look when walking around taking in the city, close to the Notre Dame Cathedral.
If you into buring buldings this is the place for you.Beware of pickpocketing and other scums."Motobike?""Cigarettes?""Other staff you dont need?"...

Nice building. Inside it is still a post office. And of course souvenir shops..It is closed to the opera house and Notherdam cathedral.. Take one hour.. Finish them all and move on.. Not worth more than that.
The architecture is quite nice but nothing much else to see. Useful if you are thinking of sending postcards. This is usually part of a city tour .
With the Saigon opera, this is one of the few French-era remnants. It has an air of faded 'grandeur' and is a wonderful testimony of what a post office was before the DHL-UPS-Fedex era.
The post office is an example of left-over French architecture. It's worth a quick look and a peek inside at the big clock, but that's all. Not a very interesting place.
This is just a 4 minute photo stop; no more.This place is now a modern post office annex tourist shop; no more to be found here.Wouldn't waste the precious time at the Post office,

Don't get the fuzz about this place, went pass it twice looking for it. It is a normal old house nothing special at all.It is next to the cathedral so there is no reason why you shouldn't give it a visit. But don't expect the world

Nothing much to see here, you can buy your tickets for O show and it is across the notre dam cathedral, pretty centric and safe to walk around. If you want to to take a taxi only take the white ones with people wearing ties, they go by meter and are very cheap.
A historic building, built during the French occupation. In my opinion, it doesn't feature any noteworthy architecture. Inside, collectors can purchase interesting stamp sets.
It's hard to miss; the central post office remains a very beautiful building. However, it's nothing more than a mountain of tourists.

Wow. A post office. Honestly what's to expect? The architecture on the outside is beautiful especially with a church right across the road but the inside is just a post office. Don't expect some amazing view through time of how mail is transported. It's a post office. They send your crap to one place and receive it there. You'd have more fun watching paint dry. I don't discourage going to this place as the architecture is great and the surroundings of a church and many cafes is great but don't waste your time expecting anything to come of a post office.
We walked past it during our exploration of Ho Chi Minh. Took photos and walked on.
This was interesting but not really something you plan to do; it's more something you have to do like going to the DMV

This was a beautiful building with lots of history. The gift shops inside were well stocked with a good selection to choose from. I captured lots of pictures of the impressive building. Not on my top ten list of things to see but we are all different. It was part of my tour. It was very hot inside and out which made me anxious to leave. Good buzzing vibe . Lots of tourist.
Still a working post office : stalls selling souvenirs and local artefacts . That's it - doesn't cost anything and provides a refuge if it is searingly hot or a heavy downpour.
The building is beautiful but at the end of the day, its just a large post office. I wouldn't bother going again.
Slightly better than the cathedral, but again, the visit is rather short and there's not much to see!

While it is a lovely building there is not a lot apart from that.  Would give it a skip unless it is on a day-tour which it is included. I was happy as I had to post something and the staff were very helpful and professional. Needed to use bathroom and had to pay!  Listed as 4,000 was asked for 5,000 - its these little things that leave the visitors with a bad sense of hospitality.  Shame as most places and people are simply wonderful.
Look there was nothing that interesting here. It is an old building but nothing to get excited about.

When I read the description in Trotter, I had high expectations for this post office... Unfortunately, this building was nothing special... I almost walked right past it at first. In my opinion, there is nothing special about it.
Nice inside architecture but don't pay someone to go and see it . Just stop by on the way to your other sites.
A beautiful building but spent less than 5 minutes inside just taking a few pictures. Not a must see. It's opposite the cathedral but both are closed during lunch time.
Nothing much really.. But nice structure. Just next to the Cathedral as well so we went. Saw a couple having a prenup shoot there.
Let's say the building is beautiful to look at and inside there is the central post office which is actually particular to see considering you are in Vietnam...but that's all

I visited this post office in the interest of actually posting a letter. When I noticed it was being treated as a tourist spot I tried to figure out why. I failed to see what was so interesting about it. The architecture is fascinating, but otherwise it is just a regular post-office.

We were taken here by our guide, but in a sort of "well it's next to the Notre Dame cathedral" vibe. There wasn't much he could say about this place, and we eventually ended up chatting whilst watching people doing their postage stuff. Unless you find it fun to see people do postage, I wouldn't make a trip out of this place.
The Old Post Office is a beautiful old building and possibly an attraction for tourists with a special interest in post offices.
Well... It is a beautiful building in itself, but if you have just arrived in Vietnam (from Europe), it is not something you really have to see. Our guide was clearly proud of this building, though.
I have taken the photo and I do not what I should do next. If I have limited time, I will go to other place

Previously, the exterior paint had a certain charm, having faded to a degree that gave it a sense of age. However, the repainting has transformed the building into something strangely out of place. This is perhaps even

It's located right off the main street. The cathedral is right in front of it, and there are lots of tourists. Guidebooks often list it as a must-see spot, but the inside is dimly lit, the air conditioning isn't working, and for me, a few minutes was enough. It's close to Dong Khoi Street and the exterior is beautiful, but I don't think it's worth including in your travel plans.

In my Lonely Planet guidebook for Vietnam, this post office was described as an amazing building, but I walked right past it. Furthermore, the interior is beautiful, but I have definitely seen more beautiful buildings. The green steel structure doesn't stand out, just like the two maps and mosaic painting. If you look closely and examine them, they are certainly beautifully made. Additionally, I found the layout a bit too touristy for my taste, with the two shops and many ATMs.
Worth a visit if you need to send something..
I really don't know why this is a thing
Cool old architecture, but that's it...
Even more tedious than Notre Dame
Don't add to your bucket list
Colonial building for architecture fans
A beautiful building
God
Point of interest? Really?
Visit to the central post office
Based on the reviews, we went to see the famous Central Post Office expecting a "Wow!" effect... But the result: NOTHING.

It is a bit disappointing; there is not much to see. However, you can send your postcard at your leisure, which you can also buy here. Furthermore, you can buy some souvenirs and withdraw money (many different types of banks). Currently, it is covered in scaffolding for renovation.

First thing you'll notice is the new paint job. Orange-yellow. Bad idea. It's meant to look like the distinctive yellow color you'd see on the beautiful pictures of Hoi An. But someone got their color palettes mixed up.Inside the architecture is nice. It's no masterpiece, but compared to what else is in Saigon, it's... better. A huge bust of Uncle Ho looms down over you. But here's what upsets me. From everywhere in the Post Office, you can hear someone playing a traditional Vietnamese xylophone. No doubt to sell it as a souvenir. But do you have to play 'Yankee Doodle Dandy' and 'Happy Birthday'? Seriously? In one of the most iconic landmarks in HCMC, the best choice of song you can find is crappy cheesy western melodies. It makes me sad.The choice of souvenirs also makes me sad, but this is the same with every single tourist attraction in VN. Crap, crap, crap, everywhere.Try post something and you'll come across surly, short tempered staff. Foreigners get treated better than locals, but it's still a disorganised, chaotic, pushy system. If you must, come in, take a quick picture and leave. Go and enjoy a coffee somewhere nice.
It was under renovation, and I wondered what the exterior color would be like...
The post office more looks like a train station. There is construction on the outside due to which you cannot capture the entire picture. It's average place nothing great.
I didn't personally enjoy this place one bit. It's a frickin post office!.. Not a tourist attraction

No offense, but it is no big whoop. It is a working post office. Outside is beautiful, inside is barely worth a mention. Can't really mail much here without being jostled by the local people...5-10 min visit is all you really need...

The Central Post Office was built by the French to resemble a railway station. It is a noisy bustling place looked on by a picture of Ho Chi Minh. Good for buying stamps and posting letters. Don't go out of your way to visit but as it is opposite the cathedral worth a short stop.
Unless you need to actually post something i wouldn't go out of my way to see it, as its just a post office. Cool building and opposite the Notre Dame Cathedral but 'its just a post office'.
The place is an old structure central post office which is still functioning. there is no historical information written about the place. i didnt learn anything about the place history at all

The building itself is quite beautiful, French architecture, but there's not much to see in the place itself. If you go, take the opportunity to send a postcard to someone. Notre Dame Cathedral is located directly across the street.

Visiting post offices abroad is always an exciting experience, and one of the places I like to visit in a new city. This particular post office is large and even has a souvenir shop. They sell Vietnamese postcards, so you can buy them on the spot and mail them right away. However, there's no air conditioning, and it's incredibly hot. The ventilation is also poor, and the heat prevented me from staying there for long. What a shame.

Nothing special, 3 minutes is enough. The rest is just selling souvenirs, like in many parts of Ho Chi Minh City. The good thing is that it's near Notre Dame Church, so you can make the most of your trip.
nothing here to see. except to purchase a few souvenir. which are available in plenty at ben tham market only 5mins from here

I think taking a picture of the building would be enough. (A local was taking wedding photos there.) Inside it's a normal post office, but there's a row of souvenir shops along the wall near the entrance. I felt they were a bit overpriced, so I didn't buy anything.
many guide books refer to this place as a must see and truly i can not see why. It is a boring place.

Not much to do or see inside, deffinately not one for the kids! Nice architecture & interesting to see how communications used to be & yes it's quaint that it's still working today but it holds your interest for about 5 minutes.
I visited it on my own, without a guide. It was just a typical post office hall, with maybe some paintings, and a bunch of souvenir stalls for tourists. It was a complete waste of time.
This building is a must-see – both inside and out. Moreover, the structure is very well preserved.
This is a European style building so not very exciting if you come from Europe! It's moderately interesting as it's attractive and different from elsewhere but don't make a special trip.
Sorry, I couldn't see the interest in this. Yes, it's a working post office...but what else was so amazing? Unless you want to make an international call or send a letter, no need to stop here.

As I've read about the post office and was headed for the cathedral (just across the street from the post office) I decided to pop in. Wasn't worth it, you can buy overpriced souvenirs and the architecture is not impressive for anyone who has been travelling Europe.

There is really nothing to see unless you come from a very small country town that does not have a building with a first floor. Our tour guide even had trouble trying to sell it to us as an attraction.
The building is a pretty example of the architecture but i am not sure   it is somewhere to go as a destination, 10-15 minutes at most

Nothing interesting or amazing. It's just an old French/European Style building, once a train station, turning into a giant Post Office. You'll walk in take a couple of photos and walk right out. It's not impressive. Inside has a small souvenir shop that sells lots of things you can buy anywhere in Ho Chi Minh city.

Built by the French and looks like a French railway station on the outside..inside is a working post office that also sells tourist tat..nothing else to say really except you go in and you think ahh its a post office lol.

Saigon doesn't offer much and this is one of those things that is just a let down. Trip advisor wants me to write more but I honestly cant think of anything else to say about this place, its a post office - good if you want to post something I guess?
I am not sure why this is a tourist attraction. A large, wide building but only the central part is used for the post office. In any other city it would be a railway station. Not much to see really.

It's on every Ho Chi Minh City travel agent's agenda. It's absolutely not worth the attention. So, we went and looked at it, an old renovated building. The Central Post Office in Moscow should, in that case, be a real delight.

This place has a beautiful interior and facade but literally my group walked in.. walked around for 5 minutes. saw the outrages prices of some books and then walked out and looked at the cathedral.. You shouldn't make a special trip just for this, but if you are in the area.. it is nice to see.

The European design of the Central Post Office is interesting, but would be ordinary in any European town.  The history behind it and the design being done by Gustave Eiffel are great trivia facts.  However, the site is underwhelming.

Of course, the post office's function is no longer what it used to be. Although calling other cities and countries from Vietnam is most cost-effective from post offices. So the central post office does serve some communication function. However, the vast majority of the space is still occupied by retail. Whether it's the shops on the beach or the airport duty-free shop, it's quite crowded. And, of course, it's more expensive.

The building is great and the general area is historical. The central post office is an actual working post office. There is a shop right at the center of the main floor where they sell stamp collections. They claim to be the official shop for selling stamps. In reality they seem to be a rip off. I paid over 420,000 Dong (20 USD) for a few stamp books and found that the "private" shops and street vendor sell the very same stamps for much lesser.Avoid this shop.
What can you say about an old post office in a crazy city I cannot think of why you would visit other than walking past

The building reminds me of a main railway station in NY or Paris (of course in a different scale). Inside is just a really poorly illuminated hall with various counters for sending postcards and mail. A huge picture of Ho Chi Minh is hung facing the far end against the entrance, reminding you of a communism country. The inside of the building is really rundown with some cheap souvenir shops and one in the center of the hall. Strange set up but not worth the time going inside. If you get really short on time, just take a photo of the building outside. That's sufficient.

The building was grand and structurally pretty. Great big interiors that showed a glimpse of the buzzing post office in its hey days. But I was horrified to see that in the ancient coins souvenirs the counter was selling, they had put in a One Cent US coin and labelled it 1/2 Dong. And in another souvenir book, they used a Korean won coin in the same manner. Shocking!
I don't know why it's listed as a tourist attraction. Nothing special, just a quick glance to say you've visited.

Don't plan to spend much time here. It's a pretty building and the inside is nice too even though it has been "modernized". It seems like it is still a functional post office. I guess the only really interesting piece here is an old man that shows up every day- he used to work for the post office before retirement but still shows up every day to help people picking out stamps or that want to talk about the post office. They call him the "living history". I spent about 10 min here- that was enough.

It is indeed a beautiful building and it is worth stopping in for five minutes if you are passing by but it is just a post office so unless you need to send a letter or are an architecture enthusiast you probably wouldn't be missing much if you skipped it.
What can I say. It is a beautiful building designed by Eiffel [of the Eiffel Tower in Paris]. The exterior and interior are very handsome, but when all said and done, it's just a building.
Don't go out of your way to see this building. It's ok, but it's busy and filled with tourists and souvenirs. If you need to buy stamps and send some mail then you might as pay it a visit.
